From April to May and September-October, expect 9-26°C: excellent period, food fairs. From November to March, expect 0-13°C and 22-34% rain: damp cold, snow rare, fog frequent on the plain. Highlights: Piazza Maggiore (heart of the city, Basilica San Petronio = the world's 5th largest Catholic church, unfinished façade, contains the world's largest sundial on the floor), Fountain of Neptune (Giambologna 1566), 2 Towers (Le Due Torri, Bologna's visual signature: Asinelli 97m 12th century = Europe's tallest habitable leaning tower, 498 steps to the panoramic top, and Garisenda 48m heavily leaning, closed for renovation since 2023), 40 km of covered porticoes (UNESCO 2021, the world's longest portico system, the longest stretching 3.8 km to the Sanctuary of the Madonna di San Luca = hill with 666 arches), University quarter (Via Zamboni, student atmosphere, 17th-century palaces), Quadrilatero Market (restaurant and delicatessen zone, butchers and cheesemongers), Archiginnasio (1st university building 1563, carved wooden anatomical theatre), Seven Churches of Santo Stefano (interconnected church complex, ancient atmosphere). Excursions: Modena 30 min away (UNESCO Cathedral, traditional balsamic, Ferrari Maranello), Parma 1h away (ham, parmigiano), Ravenna 1h away (UNESCO Byzantine mosaics), Florence 35 min by high-speed train. Try tagliatelle al ragù (the real "bolognese" = with tagliatelle, never with spaghetti, denounced as a tourist aberration), tortellini in brodo, mortadella di Bologna, lasagne alla bolognese, tortelloni. Bologna airport (BLQ).
verone:en: Verona, city in Italian Veneto: 260,000 inhabitants at the foot of the pre-Alps, 100 km from Venice. UNESCO 1999 for its exceptionally preserved Roman and medieval old town. World-famous for Romeo and Juliet (Shakespeare's play, around 1597, set in Verona, but the "Juliet's house" is a 20th-century tourist invention, the Capulet/Montague families are fictional). June is my favourite month (9.0/10): 18-28°C, 36% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heavy heat (20-30°C, suffocating humidity on the plain). From September to October, expect 11-25°C: excellent period, harvests. From November to March, expect 0-13°C and 20-33% rain: damp cold, fog. Highlights: Verona Arena ("Arena" 1st century, the 3rd largest Roman amphitheatre after the Colosseum and Capua's, very well preserved, venue of the Verona Opera Festival open-air in June-August, one of the world's largest opera festivals, capacity 15,000 spectators with exceptional natural acoustics), Casa di Giulietta ("Juliet's House", medieval house bought by the city in the 19th century, balcony added in 1936 for tourism, Juliet statue whose right breast fans touch for luck, walls covered in love notes-graffiti), Piazza delle Erbe (ancient Roman square, market since Antiquity, medieval atmosphere), Piazza dei Signori with statue of Dante (who lived in Verona in exile), Castelvecchio (14th-century fortress with crenellated medieval bridge, museum), Basilica San Zeno (12th century, one of the masterpieces of Lombard Romanesque art), Santa Anastasia Church and Duomo, Lamberti Tower (84m panoramic view), Castel San Pietro (sunset panorama over the city). Excursions: Lake Garda 30 min away (Sirmione = Catullus's Roman peninsula, Bardolino = wine), Mantua (UNESCO, Renaissance Gonzaga city), Venice 1h by train. Try risotto all'amarone, pastissada de caval (Amarone-braised beef), Verona pandoro (Christmas cake born here), Valpolicella and Amarone wines (special vinification by drying the grapes). Verona-Villafranca airport (VRN).
turin:en: Turin, capital of Italian Piedmont: 850,000 inhabitants in the north-west, first capital of unified Italy (1861-1865). Baroque and industrial city (worldwide HQ of Fiat and Lavazza), Italian chocolate capital (gianduiotti), at the foot of the Alps (spectacular view of the Italian Alps on clear days). June is my favourite month (8.7/10): 16-27°C, 40% rain, ideal temperatures. July drops to 6.9/10 due to heat (19-30°C). From April to May and September, expect 7-24°C: excellent period. From November to March, expect -2/12°C and 19-29% rain: continental cold, frequent fog (the Piedmontese "nebbia"), snow possible but rare on the plain. Highlights: Mole Antonelliana (Turin's visual signature, 167m tower built 1863-1889 originally as a synagogue, became secular, houses the National Cinema Museum = one of the world's largest cinema museums, panoramic lift to the top), Palazzo Reale (UNESCO, palace of the kings of Sardinia then Italy, 16th-19th century), Duomo (Cathedral of Saint John the Baptist, houses the Holy Shroud = Shroud of Turin, rarely displayed, last shown 2015, next planned 2025), Egyptian Museum of Turin (the world's 2nd most important after Cairo, 30,000 artefacts including the near-intact tomb of Kha), Piazza Castello with Palazzo Madama, Piazza San Carlo ("Turin's drawing room" under arcades), Galleria Subalpina and Galleria San Federico (19th-century shopping arcades), Quadrilatero Romano district (ancient Roman historic centre), Parco del Valentino (the great park with mock medieval castle 1884), Lingotto (former Fiat factory converted by Renzo Piano, test track on the roof, scene from The Italian Job 1969). Excursions: Sacra di San Michele (11th-century perched abbey, inspiration for Umberto Eco's Name of the Rose), Langhe and Barolo 1h30 away (UNESCO, vineyards, Alba white truffle in autumn), Lake Maggiore 1h30 away. Try bagna cauda (garlic-anchovy sauce), agnolotti del plin, vitello tonnato, gianduiotti dark chocolate-hazelnut, Barolo and Barbaresco wines. Turin Caselle airport (TRN).
palerme:en: Palermo, capital of Sicily: 670,000 inhabitants on the north-west coast of the island. Cosmopolitan city with a heavy history (Phoenicians, Greeks, Romans, Arabs 831-1072, Normans 1072-1194, Spaniards 1282-1860, Italians since 1860). Spectacular architecture = Europe's unique blend of Arab + Norman + Sicilian baroque. UNESCO 2015 for its Arab-Norman itinerary. June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 20-27°C, 12% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (23-30°C, sometimes 38°C with sirocco wind from the Sahara). September stays excellent (8.6/10). From November to March, expect 9-20°C and 30-44% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Palermo Cathedral (mixed Arab-Norman-baroque architecture, royal tombs including Roger II and Frederick II), Palatine Chapel (UNESCO, inside the Palace of the Normans, Arab muqarnas ceiling + Byzantine mosaics + Norman flooring = absolute masterpiece, one of Europe's most beautiful churches), Monreale Cathedral 8 km away (UNESCO, Byzantine mosaics covering 6500 m² = the largest mosaic surface in the world after Hagia Sophia, cloister of 228 individually carved columns), Quattro Canti (baroque crossroads), Ballarò market and Vucciria (Eastern atmosphere inherited from the Arabs, street food), Capuchin Catacombs (8000 mummies 17th-20th century lined up, including the little girl Rosalia Lombardo who died 1920, perfectly preserved by secret embalming), Teatro Massimo (3rd largest opera house in Europe after Paris and Vienna, final scene of The Godfather III). Excursions: Cefalù 1h away (UNESCO cathedral, beach), Mondello 15 min away (urban beach), Erice and Trapani 1h30 away, Agrigento 2h away (UNESCO Valley of the Temples). Try arancini (fried rice balls, original to Palermo), pasta con le sarde, sfincione (thick Sicilian pizza), cannoli, granita. Palermo-Falcone airport (PMO).
bilbao:en: Bilbao, Spain's 4th city and capital of the Basque Country: 350,000 inhabitants, industrial city converted into a cultural capital since the opening of the Guggenheim Museum in 1997 (Frank Gehry architecture, the "Bilbao effect" = global urban catalyst). August is my favourite month (8.6/10): 17-25°C, 30% rain, mild temperatures ("verde" for its wetter climate than Mediterranean Spain). July stays excellent (8.5/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 15-25°C, festivals (Aste Nagusia mid-August, patron saint festival, 9 days of partying, cow run, txosnas Basque tents with sidra and pintxos). From November to February, expect 6-15°C and 47-55% rain: mild for Spain, humid oceanic climate (the Basque Country gets 1200 mm/year). Highlights: Guggenheim Museum Bilbao (Frank Gehry 1997, deconstructivist titanium architecture, "Puppy" giant floral sculpture by Jeff Koons at the entrance, one of Spain's most visited museums), Casco Viejo (old town, Las Siete Calles = 7 medieval streets, Santiago Cathedral, Plaza Nueva with its arcades), Mercado de la Ribera (Europe's largest covered market, Art Deco), Funicular de Artxanda (3 min to Mont Artxanda 226m, panoramic view over Bilbao), Vizcaya Bridge at Portugalete (UNESCO, the world's first transporter bridge 1893). Basque Country excursions: San Sebastián 1h away (Donostia, La Concha beach), Guernica 30 min away (bombed by the German air force in 1937, immortalised by Picasso), San Juan de Gaztelugatxe 35 min away (islet with hermitage and 241 steps, Game of Thrones "Dragonstone"). Try pintxos ("Basque tapas" on a slice of bread for 1.5-3€/piece, bar-hopping in Casco Viejo), bacalao al pil pil, txuleta (1kg rib steak), txakoli (local sparkling white wine poured from above), Basque cider.
tenerife:en: Tenerife is the largest of the Canaries and the most diverse: El Teide at 3700m separates the dry climate of the south (Costa Adeje, Los Cristianos) from the wet climate of the north (Puerto de la Cruz, La Laguna). August is my favourite month (9.2/10): 21-26°C, 10% rain, the period when the Anaga Laurisilva keeps its photogenic mists. From December to March, expect 15-21°C and 19-31% rain on the north side. Always wearable, but the south stays almost always sunny (200% rain difference depending on which side of the island). El Teide can get snow 3-4 months a year, sometimes the cable car closes for wind. La Laguna (UNESCO) keeps a real urban student life. The Santa Cruz carnival in February-March is the world's 2nd biggest after Rio. Tourist peak with raised prices. May-June and September-October are the best price/weather compromises.
gran-canaria:en: Gran Canaria, the miniature continent: Maspalomas dunes in the south, pine forests at altitude, Atlantic to the west. July-August dominate (9.6/10) with 21-28°C and 0-1% rain. Also the most touristy European period. May-June and September-October are my real compromise: nearly identical weather, reduced crowds, prices easing. The south (Maspalomas, Playa del Inglés) stays dry almost year-round thanks to the Cordillera Central blocking the clouds. From December to March, expect 16-21°C and 6-11% rain. Always mild but less reliable on the north side (Las Palmas wetter than Mogán by 30%). The Roque Nublo at 1800m can get snow in winter, surprising for the Canaries. Las Palmas keeps a real urban life all year, unlike the southern resorts.
lanzarote:en: Lanzarote was shaped by César Manrique, the artist who imposed low-rise white planning: no skyscrapers, no garish billboards. Near-desert dry climate. August is my favourite month (9.0/10): 18-23°C, 5% rain, steady trade winds. The whole year stays mild (12-23°C, 12-21% rain), it's the most stable island in the Canaries. Timanfaya (near-lunar volcanic landscapes), the Jameos del Agua and the Cueva de los Verdes are the must-sees (Manrique creations). Famara in the north-west has become a global surf and bodyboard spot. La Geria for vines planted in the black sand. Smaller island than Tenerife, but very diverse: 2-3 days minimum recommended. The calima (Saharan sand wind) can hit 3-5 times a year with a sudden ochre sky and 28°C.
fuerteventura:en: Fuerteventura is the driest and windiest of the Canaries: little relief to block the clouds, but also little to stop the wind. August is my favourite month (9.7/10): 18-28°C, 1% rain, sea at 22°C. The south (Sotavento, Costa Calma) has become a global kitesurf spot thanks to the steady thermal trade winds from May to September. The PWA holds a World Tour stop here every summer. From November to March, expect 12-22°C and less predictable wind. Always wearable, but the swimming season ends for Europeans. Hotel prices halved vs summer. The calima (Saharan sand wind) can hit any time of year with reduced visibility and a sudden 28-35°C. Phenomenon more frequent in recent years (climate change). Corralejo in the north remains the liveliest base, El Cotillo quieter.
san-francisco:en: San Francisco plays on the cool of its microclimate: California Current + Pacific = 12-22°C all year. June-September are my favourite months (8.3-8.4/10): 12-19°C, 0-4% rain. Karl the Fog (the characteristic coastal fog) rolls into the bay at the end of the day even in midsummer (Mark Twain: "the coldest winter I ever spent was a summer in San Francisco"). From December to March, expect 8-14°C and 29-36% rain: this is the wet season, often grey skies. Highlights: Golden Gate Bridge (cycle from Sausalito late afternoon), Alcatraz (book 2-3 months ahead in peak season), cable cars on Powell-Hyde, Lombard Street (the most twisting street), Coit Tower, Painted Ladies at Alamo Square, Mission District for the murals. Districts: Mission (Latino), Castro (historic LGBT, parade late June), Haight-Ashbury (60s hippie), North Beach (Italian). Try sourdough at Boudin (since 1849), Mission burrito at La Taqueria, Dungeness crab. Napa and Sonoma Valley 1h30 away for the vineyards.
miami:en: Miami is the tropical gateway of the USA: Latin (60% Hispanic, mostly Cuban), beaches, Art Deco, Cuban culture. March-April are my favourite months (8.5/10): 20-28°C, 33-42% rain, sea at 24°C, end of the tourist high season. From June to October, it's hurricane season: 65-81% rainfall, oppressive humidity (90%), real hurricane risk (category 4-5 possible, Andrew 1992 left a lasting mark). From December to February is the full high season: 18-26°C, 36-41% rain, hotel prices x3-x4, packed South Beach beaches. Highlights: South Beach and its Art Deco District (800 buildings from the 30s, Ocean Drive), Wynwood Walls (global street art), Little Havana (Calle Ocho, Domino Park, cigars at El Crédito), Vizcaya (Italian palace on the bay). American Spring Break March = party atmosphere. Everglades National Park 1h away (alligators, airboat tour), Key West 4h by car (Hemingway House). Try Cuban sandwich, ceviche, stone crab claws (season Oct-May at Joe's), arepas, key lime pie.
las-vegas:en: Las Vegas is the gambling and entertainment oasis in the heart of the Mojave Desert: 40 million visitors/year, 150,000 hotel rooms. April is my favourite month (9.5/10): 14-27°C, 5% rain, ideal temperatures for the pools and desert excursions. From June to August drop to 3.9/10 due to extreme heat (24-41°C, sometimes 47°C, 2nd hottest US city after Death Valley): going outside becomes painful, but hotels are air-conditioned. From December to February, expect 4-14°C: dry cold, freezing evenings, but good hotel prices. From October, expect 15-28°C: another excellent period (9.3/10). Highlights: The Strip (Las Vegas Boulevard, 6 km, 15 themed mega-resorts: Bellagio, Venetian, Caesars, Wynn, MGM Grand). Cirque du Soleil shows (8 different permanent shows), music residencies (Adele, U2 Sphere). Old Vegas on Fremont Street for the historic side. Excursions: Grand Canyon West (4h round trip), Hoover Dam (1h), Death Valley (2h, dangerous in summer), Red Rock Canyon (30 min). Flights from Paris via NY or LA. All-you-can-eat buffets (local institution). Prices fluctuate enormously (weekend x2-x3, conventions).
chicago:en: Chicago is America's 3rd city, capital of the Midwest, a major architectural city (where the skyscraper was invented in 1885). July is my favourite month (8.6/10): 19-27°C, 40% rain in storms, festivals (Lollapalooza late July, Taste of Chicago), terraces and Lake Michigan beaches. From August to September, expect 16-26°C: still pleasant temperatures, foliage from October. From December to February, expect -5/3°C: the "Windy City" earns its name, freezing winds off the lake, blizzards possible, days down to 9h of light. But Christmas markets (Christkindlmarket) and Millennium Park ice rink. From April to May, expect 4-18°C and 39-46% rain: variable transition. Highlights: Cloud Gate (the "Bean") at Millennium Park, Willis Tower Skydeck and 360 Chicago Tilt for the view, Art Institute of Chicago (Hopper, Picasso, Seurat), Architecture River Cruise (genuine must), Navy Pier. Districts: Wicker Park, Logan Square, Hyde Park (Obama). Try deep-dish pizza at Lou Malnati's, Chicago hot dog, beef sandwich. Comedy at Second City (Bill Murray, Tina Fey).
boston:en: Boston is the most European of the major American cities: cobblestones, red bricks, Harvard university in Cambridge. August stays my favourite month (8.8/10): 18-28°C, 34% rain, parks in full swing, Cape Cod beaches accessible. July is also excellent (8.6/10) with the 4th of July (fireworks over the Charles River). September-October are my real sweet spots: 9-23°C, spectacular New England foliage (peak mid-October, drive Vermont route from Boston). From December to March, expect -5/8°C: dry cold, frequent snow (blizzards possible), Boston Marathon on the 3rd Monday of April (Patriots' Day). Highlights: Freedom Trail (4 km, 16 historic sites of the American Revolution), Faneuil Hall and Quincy Market, Beacon Hill (historic district with gas lamps), Harvard and MIT in Cambridge (campus visit), Fenway Park (the oldest active baseball stadium, 1912). Districts: North End (Italian cuisine, Mike's Pastry for cannoli), Back Bay, South End. Try clam chowder, lobster roll (at Neptune Oyster), Boston cream pie. Cape Cod and the islands (Martha's Vineyard, Nantucket) 2h away in summer.
seattle:en: Seattle is the Pacific Northwest city: Microsoft, Amazon, Starbucks, grunge (Nirvana, Pearl Jam), rain. July-August are my favourite months (9.0-9.3/10): 14-24°C, 11-16% rain, sky finally clear after the rainy winter, Mount Rainier visible from the city. From November to March, expect 2-10°C and 52-60% precipitation (justified reputation: 940mm/year but mostly as continuous fine rain over 6 weeks, not heavy storms). From April to May, expect 4-18°C: skies gradually clearing. Highlights: Pike Place Market (first Starbucks 1971, fishmongers throwing salmon), Space Needle (184m, signature of the 1962 Expo), Chihuly Garden and Glass (Dale Chihuly's glass works), MoPOP Museum (pop music), Gas Works Park for the view. Districts: Capitol Hill (LGBT, alternative), Fremont ("Republic of Fremont", eccentric), Ballard (Scandinavian). Try the salmon, oysters, coffee everywhere. Mount Rainier (4392m) 2h away, Olympic Peninsula and its rainforest 3h away, Vancouver 3h away. Direct flight from Paris.
washington:en: Washington DC is the federal capital: 700,000 inhabitants, but 6 million in the urban area. Monumental city centre around the National Mall. June stays my favourite month (8.6/10): 18-29°C, 41% rain, but also rising humidity. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to stifling humid heat (21-31°C, feels like 38°C+). From December to February, expect -2/10°C: dry cold, occasional blizzards. March-April is cherry blossom season (around the Tidal Basin, Japanese gift from 1912, peak around 1-7 April, Cherry Blossom Festival), major tourist peak. September-October are my real sweet spots: 10-26°C, clear sky, foliage. Highlights (free): National Mall with Lincoln Memorial, Washington Monument (562 steps), Capitol, White House (booking via congressman), Smithsonian Museums (19 museums all free: Air and Space, American History, Natural History, African American History), Holocaust Memorial Museum, Arlington Cemetery. Georgetown for the charming side. Try half-smoke at Ben's Chili Bowl. Efficient metro. 4h by train from NYC (Acela), 1h from Baltimore.
nouvelle-orleans:en: New Orleans is one of the most singular American cities: French/Spanish/African/Creole heritage, jazz born here (Louis Armstrong), Cajun cuisine, Mardi Gras. October is my favourite month (8.6/10): 20-27°C, 28% rain, end of the stifling heat, festivals (Voodoo Festival late October). From June to September, expect 24-31°C: hot, humid, real hurricane risk (Katrina 2005, Ida 2021). From December to February, expect 8-20°C: mild, ideal for visiting without crowds. Mardi Gras (variable date February-March, 2 weeks of festivities, peak on the Tuesday before Lent) = absolute tourist peak, prices x4-x5, massive crowds on Bourbon Street. Highlights: French Quarter (Vieux Carré), Jackson Square and Saint Louis Cathedral, Bourbon Street (touristy), Frenchmen Street (local jazz, frequented by residents), Garden District (antebellum houses, Anne Rice's tomb), St Louis Cemetery No. 1 (Marie Laveau's tomb, voodoo). Try beignets at Café du Monde, gumbo, jambalaya, po'boy at Domilise's, étouffée. Plantation tours 1h away (Oak Alley, Whitney Plantation for the enslaved-people perspective).
toronto:en: Toronto is Canada's largest city: 6.5 million in the urban area, one of the world's most diverse (50% foreign-born). July-August are my favourite months (8.7-8.8/10): 18-26°C, 32-36% rain, terraces, festivals (Caribana late July, TIFF early September), Toronto islands by ferry for swimming. From December to March, expect -6/3°C and 31-41% precipitation (often snow): cold with lake-effect snow, but the PATH (29 km of underground tunnels) connects all downtown buildings. September-October are my real sweet spots: 8-22°C, Ontario foliage, Niagara 1h30 away. Highlights: CN Tower (553m, EdgeWalk for the brave), Royal Ontario Museum, Distillery District (Victorian brick), St. Lawrence Market, ethnic neighbourhoods: Chinatown, Little Italy, Greektown, Koreatown. Sport: Maple Leafs (NHL), Raptors (NBA), Blue Jays (MLB) at Rogers Centre. Niagara Falls 1h30 away (the Canadian side more impressive), Niagara-on-the-Lake for ice wines, Algonquin Park 3h away for nature. Direct flight from Paris in 8h.
montreal:en: Montreal is the 2nd largest French-speaking city in the world after Paris: island at the confluence of the Saint Lawrence, mix of French/English/multicultural. July is my favourite month (8.7/10): 18-27°C, 39% rain, festivals (Montreal International Jazz Festival early July, Just for Laughs mid-July, Osheaga music early August), terraces everywhere. From December to March, expect -12/4°C and 39-44% precipitation (often snow): a real winter with -20°C and blizzards possible. The underground city (RÉSO, 33 km of tunnels) makes total sense. Quebec Carnival 3h away. September-October are my real sweet spots: 7-22°C, spectacular foliage at Mont-Royal, Mont-Tremblant 1h30 away. Highlights: Old Montreal (Place Jacques-Cartier, Notre-Dame), Mont-Royal (Belvédère Kondiaronk for the view), Jean-Talon Market (North America's largest), Plateau Mont-Royal (characteristic external staircases), Mile End (Jewish bagels). Try bagel at St-Viateur or Fairmount, smoked meat at Schwartz's, poutine, tourtière, maple syrup (sugar shacks in March). Efficient metro.
bangkok:en: Bangkok is the Asian megalopolis par excellence: 11 million inhabitants, extreme contrasts between Buddhist temples and glass towers. December-February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 23-33°C, 13-22% rain, this is tropical winter, more bearable humidity. From June to October, it's the monsoon: 67-90% rainfall, violent late-afternoon downpours (often short), flooding possible in some districts. From April to May, it's crushing heat (27-35°C, feels like 42°C+), Songkran (Thai New Year mid-April) with water battles in the streets. Bangkok never scores better than 6.9/10: too hot-humid a climate to reach excellent. Highlights: Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew (Emerald Buddha), Wat Pho (46m reclining Buddha), Wat Arun, floating markets (Damnoen Saduak touristy but photogenic, Amphawa more local at weekends), Chatuchak market (15,000 stalls at the weekend). Khao San Road for backpackers, Sukhumvit for luxury and expats, Chinatown for street food (Yaowarat). Try pad thai, tom yum, mango sticky rice. Direct flight from Paris in 11h.
taipei:en: Taipei is the capital of Taiwan: 7 million in the urban area, dominated by Taipei 101 (508m, 2nd tallest tower at its construction in 2004). April is my favourite month (7.9/10): 19-26°C, 48% rain, mild temperatures before the monsoon. From May to September, it's the hot and humid season: 47-70% rainfall, max humidity (90%), typhoon season. From December to February, expect 14-20°C and 39-45% rain: mild but greyish (persistent fine rain). Highlights: Taipei 101 (observatory on the 89th floor), Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall (changing of the guard every hour), Longshan Temple, Bao'an Temple, Beitou (hot springs by metro). Night markets are the Taiwanese signature: Shilin (the largest but touristy), Raohe (more local), Ningxia (traditional cuisine). Try beef noodle soup (national dish), xiaolongbao at Din Tai Fung (Taiwanese origin), bubble tea, stinky tofu, mango shaved ice. Yangmingshan National Park to the north for hot springs and cherry blossoms (March). Jiufen and Shifen 1h by train for the perched villages.
osaka:en: Osaka is Japan's third city: kuidaore (eat until you go bankrupt), known for street food and a laid-back character. May is my favourite month (8.1/10): 15-23°C, 37% rain, terraces at Dotonbori. From June to mid-July, it's the tsuyu. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to humid heatwave (24-33°C). From October to November, ideal temperatures and momiji possible (Mino Park). March-April = sakura. Districts: Dotonbori (the neon signs, Glico Man), Shinsekai (old Tsutenkaku tower, retro atmosphere), Namba (shopping), Umeda (skyscrapers), Shinsaibashi (luxury). Osaka Castle (concrete reconstruction but panoramic view). Kuromon Market for street food. Try takoyaki (octopus balls), okonomiyaki (Japanese omelette), kushikatsu (fried skewers). Osaka is also the base for Kyoto (15 min by train), Nara (45 min, sacred deer at Tōdai-ji), Kobe (30 min, the beef). Universal Studios Japan for the fans (Wizarding World of Harry Potter, Super Nintendo World).
hiroshima:en: Hiroshima is one of Japan's major historic destinations: the city rebuilt after the atomic bombing of 6 August 1945 (140,000 deaths in the long term). May is my favourite month (8.2/10): 13-22°C, 30% rain, mild temperatures before the monsoon. From June to mid-July, it's the tsuyu (rainy season, 46% rainfall). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to humid heatwave (23-31°C). September-October are my real sweet spots (8.0-8.1/10): 14-28°C, clear skies, momiji starts in November. From December to February, expect 0-11°C: dry cold, often clear skies, reduced crowds. Highlights: Hiroshima Peace Memorial (UNESCO, former Industrial Promotion Hall preserved in ruins), Peace Memorial Museum (very emotionally challenging, testimonies, melted objects, 200¥), Peace Memorial Park (cenotaph, dome), Hiroshima Castle (rebuilt 1958). Miyajima 45 min by train + ferry: floating torii of the Itsukushima shrine (UNESCO, signature of Miyajima), sacred deer, Mount Misen accessible by cable car. Try Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki (different from Osaka, layered), local oysters (winter season), momiji manjū. 1h30 from Osaka by Shinkansen.
okinawa:en: Okinawa, subtropical archipelago in southern Japan: 160 islands, 1.5 million inhabitants, distinct culture (Ryūkyū kingdom until 1879), American military bases (75% of US bases in Japan are here). October-November are my favourite months (8.0-8.2/10): 21-28°C, 37-45% rain, sea still at 26°C, end of typhoon season. From June to September, it's the hot/humid season: 45-75% rainfall, oppressive humidity (24-30°C), frequent typhoons (about ten per season, can paralyse inter-island ferries for several days). From December to March, expect 15-21°C: mild but often cloudy, sea at 22°C (still swimmable for Europeans). Highlights: Naha (capital, Shuri Castle former Ryūkyū royal palace UNESCO, burned down in 2019, reconstruction underway), Kokusai-dori (shopping street), Cape Manzamo. Islands to explore: Ishigaki (Kabira Bay beach), Iriomote (subtropical jungle), Taketomi (traditional village by bicycle), Miyako (beaches among Japan's most beautiful). Diving world-class spots (Yonaguni with its mysterious underwater ruins). Try sōki soba (pork noodles), gōyā champurū (sautéed bitter cucumber), awamori (rice alcohol). Direct flight from Tokyo (2h30) or Osaka (2h).
phnom-penh:en: Phnom Penh, Cambodian capital, holds the painful memory of the Khmer Rouge (1975-1979) who killed 1.7 million people. January-February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 23-33°C, 11-12% rain, dry season, acceptable humidity. From May to October, it's the monsoon: 79-93% rainfall in violent downpours (often late afternoon). Phnom Penh never tops 7/10: constant tropical climate. From April to May, it's crushing heat (26-35°C). Essential sites (memorial): Tuol Sleng (S-21) former school turned into a torture prison by the Khmer Rouge, Choeung Ek Killing Fields 15 km away (mass graves memorial). Visit both sites in one morning (very emotionally taxing). Royal sites: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda, National Museum (Khmer sculptures). Riverfront (Sisowath Quay) at sunset. Central Market (Phsar Thmei, Art Deco 1937). Nightlife on Bassac Lane. Try amok, lok lak, num pang. 6h by bus from Siem Reap, 5h from Ho Chi Minh City.
siem-reap:en: Siem Reap is the base for visiting Angkor Wat, the world's largest religious complex (400 km²). January is my favourite month (6.9/10): 22-31°C, 7% rain, this is the dry season, acceptable humidity. Siem Reap never tops 7/10: constant hot tropical climate. From May to October, it's the monsoon: 76-91% rainfall in violent downpours, but greener landscapes (rice paddies, flooded Tonlé Sap), crowds reduced by 60%, prices halved. From April to May, it's crushing heat (26-35°C) before the monsoon: trying for temple visits. Angkor sites: Angkor Wat (very popular sunrise but packed, alternative sunset at Pre Rup), Bayon (faces), Ta Prohm (overgrown by jungle, Tomb Raider), Banteay Srei 1h away (the most delicate). Angkor Pass: 37$/day, 62$/3 days, 72$/7 days. Pub Street in town for nightlife (touristy). Tonlé Sap and floating villages (Kompong Phluk vs Chong Khneas more cliché). Try amok (fish curry), lok lak.
luang-prabang:en: Luang Prabang is the former royal capital of Laos, UNESCO listed: 33 temples, French colonial architecture, the Mekong river. December is my favourite month (9.2/10): 17-28°C, 11% rain, clear skies, golden light on the gilded roofs. November to February are the high season: 16-31°C, little rain. From May to October, it's the monsoon: 35-90% rainfall, but green landscapes. From April to May, it's stifling heat (24-35°C) and agricultural pollution (slash-and-burn). Essential sites: Tak bat ceremony at dawn (procession of 200 monks in saffron robes collecting offerings, around 5:30-6am, very silent, do not disturb), Mount Phousi (300 steps, 360° view at sunset), Wat Xieng Thong (the most beautiful temple, 1560), Royal Palace Museum. Kuang Si Falls 30 km away (turquoise water, swimming possible, bear sanctuary). Night market textile/handicrafts. Mekong cruise to the Pak Ou caves. Direct flight from Bangkok or Hanoi.
yangon:en: Yangon (formerly Rangoon), former capital of Myanmar (Burma): 5 million inhabitants, downgraded from capital status in 2005 when Naypyidaw was created artificially (administrative capital 320 km to the north). Yangon remains the country's economic and cultural capital. Marked by the military coup of February 2021 (dark era, international sanctions, travel advice unfavourable). From November to February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 20-32°C, 5-30% rain, the ideal dry season. Myanmar never tops 6.9/10 (hot tropical climate). From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 23-38°C (sometimes 42°C), impossible to visit in the middle of the day. From June to October, it's the monsoon: 68-99% rainfall, flooding possible, oppressive humidity. Highlights: Shwedagon Pagoda (Myanmar's spiritual signature, 99m gilded stupa covered in 60 tonnes of gold and 4500 diamonds at the summit, glittering at sunset from afar, foreign access 5$ + obligation to remove shoes and to cover bare legs/shoulders), Sule Pagoda (in the heart of downtown, cardinal landmark), Bogyoke Aung San Market (Scott Market, British colonial heritage 1926), Colonial quarter (19th-20th century British architecture in shabby-chic decay), Lake Inya and Lake Kandawgyi (public parks), Botataung Pagoda (riverside). Major Myanmar excursions: Bagan 1h flight (3000 stupas and temples on the plain, hot air balloon at sunrise), Mandalay 1h flight, Inle Lake (one-legged Intha fishermen). Important: unstable political situation since the military coup of February 2021. Many countries advise against travel.
colombo:en: Colombo, economic capital of Sri Lanka on the west coast: 750,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 5.6 million in the urban area (Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte = official administrative capital next door). Sri Lanka emerged from a bloody civil war in 2009 (26 years between the Sinhalese majority and the Tamil LTTE minority), reconstruction since with massive Chinese investment (artificial Port City designed in the sea, 2014-2030). January-December are my favourite months (6.9/10): 23-30°C, 43-58% rain, this is the relative dry season. Colombo never tops 6.9/10 (humid equatorial tropical climate year-round). From December to March, it's the tourist high season: 23-31°C, sea at 28°C. From May to September, it's the south-west monsoon: 88-97% rainfall in Colombo (but the eastern monsoon = dry season for the east coast Trincomalee/Arugam Bay), stable temperature 25-29°C. From October to November, it's the inter-monsoon: 82-92% rainfall. Highlights: Galle Face Green (long seaside promenade, local atmosphere at sunset, street vendors, kites, many Sinhalese families, Indian Ocean view), Galle Face Hotel (one of East Asia's oldest hotels 1864, British colonial heritage, Veranda bar an institution), Pettah (chaotic central bazaar, spice merchants, vegetable market, Eastern atmosphere), Jami Ul-Alfar Mosque in Pettah ("Red Mosque" 1908, signature red-and-white Islamic brickwork façade), Wolvendaal Cathedral (1749, Dutch colonial heritage), Independence Memorial Hall (independence memorial 1948), National Museum of Colombo (national museum, traditional masks, Buddhist statues), Lotus Tower (350m TV tower inaugurated 2019 financed by China, the tallest construction in South Asia, tourist panorama), Gangaramaya Temple (eclectic Buddhist temple with various Asian dependencies), Cinnamon Gardens (chic residential district of the Colombo elite). Excursions: Kandy 3h away by spectacular train (UNESCO, last royal Sinhalese capital), Galle 2h by train (UNESCO, fortified Dutch town 17th century), Negombo 1h away (urban beach + airport), Mount Lavinia 30 min away (Colombo suburb, accessible urban beach), Yala National Park 5h away (leopard park, one of the highest densities in the world). Try kottu roti (national dish, chopped paratha with vegetables-meat-spices), egg hopper ("appa" fermented rice pancake with fried egg), fish curry in coconut milk, string hoppers (vermicelli), pol sambol (coconut chutney), local Ceylon tea (the country is one of the world's biggest tea producers), local Lion Lager beer. Colombo airport (CMB).
buenos-aires:en: Buenos Aires, Argentine capital: 15 million in the urban area, "South America's Paris", massive European heritage (Italians and Spaniards), tango, beef, Maradona/Messi. January-December are my favourite months (9.1/10): 21-28°C, 26-27% rain, austral summer, 14h days, terraces packed in Palermo, asados (Argentine BBQs). From June to August (austral winter), expect 8-16°C and 19-26% rain: mild for winter but cool (Argentinians feel it as cold), tango and opera season (Teatro Colón). From April to May and September-November are the best shoulder seasons: 11-24°C, jacarandas in bloom in November. Major districts: San Telmo (old bohemian quarter, antiques market on Sundays, open-air tango shows), La Boca (colourful Caminito, Boca Juniors' Bombonera stadium, touristy but worth seeing), Recoleta (chic, cemetery where Eva Perón rests, MALBA Latin American art museum), Palermo (Soho/Hollywood subdivisions trendy, restaurants, nightlife), Puerto Madero (rehabilitated docks, modern zone), Centre (Avenida 9 de Julio = "the world's widest avenue" 16 lanes, Obelisk, Casa Rosada, Plaza de Mayo). Tango: milongas (tango bars to dance, La Catedral more local), tourist shows (Café Tortoni). Try asado (parilla La Cabrera, Don Julio), empanadas, choripán, dulce de leche, alfajores, Malbec wine. Football: River Plate vs Boca Juniors = the most passionate Superclasico in the world. Tigre 1h away for the Paraná delta by boat. Direct flight Paris-Buenos Aires in 14h.
cusco:en: Cusco is the former Inca capital at 3400m altitude: the altitude hits on arrival (short of breath, possible headache, difficult first night). July is my favourite month (8.0/10): 4-18°C, 10% rain, this is the dry season, clear skies, but also tourist peak. From June to August, expect 4-18°C with 7-25% rain: the ideal dry season for the Inca Trail trek (booking required 6 months ahead via licensed agencies). From December to March, it's the rainy season: 85-95% rainfall, the Inca Trail closes all February for maintenance. Machu Picchu remains accessible year-round. Highlights: Plaza de Armas (cathedral, Compañia de Jesús), Coricancha (Inca temple turned Dominican convent), Sacsayhuamán (Inca fortress with polygonal stones), San Blas (artistic district), Mercado San Pedro (fruit juices, alpaca). Sacred Valley 1h away: Pisac (Sunday market), Ollantaytambo, Maras (salt pans), Moray (Inca agricultural terraces). Acclimatisation: 2-3 days before any effort, hydration, coca tea. Lima-Cusco flight 1h. Try ceviche, lomo saltado, cuy (guinea pig, traditional dish), pisco sour.
lima:en: Lima is paradoxical: capital of one of the world's sunniest countries (the south), but itself in a coastal fog belt (the garúa) for five months a year. No significant rain ever. February is my favourite month (9.0/10): 21-26°C, blue sky at last, southern beaches (Asia, Punta Hermosa) accessible. From June to October, it's the garúa: thick fog morning and evening, constantly grey skies, 14-21°C. The sun makes brief appearances around midday. This is when Miraflores and Barranco take on their most melancholic face (very good for Vallejo and Peruvian literature fans). Cusco and Machu Picchu, at altitude, have their dry season at exactly this time (June-August = Inca tourist peak). Many travellers use Lima only as a transit base. The gastronomic scene (Central, Maido) remains the main reason to stop.
la-paz:en: La Paz is the (administrative) capital of Bolivia: 3650m altitude, the airport (El Alto) is at 4150m, the world's highest. The altitude hits immediately: shortness of breath, headache, disturbed sleep. August is my favourite month (7.7/10): 1-16°C, 11% rain, Andean dry season, clear skies. From May to August is the dry season: cool but stable temperatures. From December to March, it's the rainy season: 60-69% rainfall, some Salar de Uyuni and Death Road tracks impassable. Highlights: Mi Teleférico (urban cable car network, the world's largest, 30 km, unique panorama), Witches' Market (Mercado de las Brujas, traditional llama foetuses), Plaza Murillo, Iglesia de San Francisco, Moon Valley (erosion landscapes 30 min away). Excursions: Salar de Uyuni from Uyuni (10h overnight bus, 3-4 days by 4×4 with basic accommodation, 200-400$), Lake Titicaca and Copacabana (3h by bus, 3812m altitude), Death Road by mountain bike from La Cumbre (4670m → 1180m, agencies in La Paz for ~80$). Try salteñas (traditional turnover), api con pasteles, llama steak.
cartagena-col:en: Cartagena de Indias, coastal city in Colombia: 1 million inhabitants on the Caribbean coast, founded in 1533 by the Spaniards. UNESCO 1984 for its fortified historic centre (the most complete and well-known fortification system in South America, built to protect galleons laden with gold and silver against pirates including Francis Drake in 1586). January-March are my favourite months (6.9/10): 24-32°C, 9-16% rain, this is the dry season. Cartagena never tops 6.9/10 (hot-humid tropical climate). From December to April, it's the tourist high season: 24-32°C, 9-47% rain. From May to November, it's the rainy season: 47-85% rainfall, but brief downpours, stable temperatures (25-32°C). Highlights: Ciudad Amurallada (fortified old town, maze of colourful alleys with flowered balconies, Plaza Santo Domingo, Plaza San Pedro Claver, Plaza de la Aduana), Castillo San Felipe de Barajas (16th-18th century, the largest Spanish fortress in the Americas, complex of underground tunnels), Saint Catherine Cathedral (16th century), Las Bóvedas (former military casemates converted into craft boutiques), Getsemaní quarter (formerly working-class quarter, now gentrified and bohemian, street art, trendy restaurants), Convento de la Popa (17th century, on the 150m hill, panoramic view). Excursions: Rosario Islands by boat 1h (28 coral islands, national park, turquoise beaches, snorkelling), Volcán de Lodo El Totumo 50 km away (mud volcano, bath in therapeutic mud), Playa Blanca (on Isla Barú, paradise beach). Try arepa de huevo (coastal signature), ceviche, pescado frito, lechona, sancocho de pescado, agua de panela. Direct flight from Paris via Bogotá. Cartagena combined with 2 days Cartagena + 2 days Bogotá + 3 days Tayrona = classic Colombia circuit.
aruba:en: Aruba is part of the ABC islands (Aruba-Bonaire-Curaçao): 32 km long, 110,000 inhabitants, autonomous Dutch dependency, outside the hurricane zone (southern Caribbean). February-March are my favourite months (8.6/10): 25-29°C, 34-38% rain, steady trade winds, sea at 27°C. From September to December, expect 26-32°C with 28-65% rainfall: only period when showers are frequent (but often brief). From December to April, it's the American tourist high season: hotel prices x2-x3. The whole year is viable since it's outside the hurricane zone: a major advantage vs other Caribbean islands. Highlights: Eagle Beach (regularly ranked Caribbean beach #2-3 after Grace Bay), Palm Beach (livelier, hotels along the shore), Arikok National Park (40% of the island, desert landscapes with cacti, rock paintings, Natural Pool, Conchi), Oranjestad (colourful Dutch capital), Baby Beach (calm lagoon in the south), California Lighthouse, Alto Vista Chapel. Activities: kitesurf and windsurf at Fisherman's Hut (steady trade winds), diving (SS Antilla wreck), 4×4 in Arikok. Currency: AWG (florin) but USD accepted everywhere, papiamento (local creole) + Dutch + English + Spanish spoken. Try keshi yena, pastechi, balashi (local beer). Direct flight from Amsterdam (8h) or via Curaçao.
curacao:en: Curaçao is the largest of the ABC islands: 444 km², 160,000 inhabitants, capital Willemstad UNESCO listed for its Dutch colonial architecture (pastel façades). Outside the hurricane zone. March is my favourite month (8.6/10): 25-29°C, 32% rain, steady trade winds, sea at 26°C. From November to January, expect 26-30°C with 49-73% rainfall: only really wet period. From February to October, stable dry climate. The whole year viable. Highlights: Willemstad (UNESCO, Punda and Otrobanda separated by Sint Anna Bay and the Queen Emma floating bridge), Christoffel National Park (Mount Christoffel 372m, highest point), Shete Boka National Park (wild coast, blowholes), Hato Caves, Curaçao Sea Aquarium. Beaches: Playa Kenepa Grandi (Grote Knip, ranked among the most beautiful in the Caribbean), Cas Abao (beach and diving), Mambo Beach (lively), Playa Lagun. Activities: diving (world-renowned, accessible directly from the beach, Tugboat wreck), snorkelling. Curaçao Liqueur (Senior distillery visit, liqueur made from Laraha orange peels), Klein Curaçao by boat (deserted island). Try stoba (stew), funchi (polenta), keshi yena, blue Curaçao cocktail. Dutch + papiamento + English + Spanish. Direct flight Amsterdam (10h).
saint-martin:en: Saint Martin is the most divided island in the Caribbean: 53 km² between French in the north (Marigot) and Dutch in the south (Philipsburg/Sint Maarten), free border crossing. March-April are my favourite months (8.4-8.6/10): 23-28°C, 27-38% rain, sea at 26°C. From August to October, it's full hurricane season: Hurricane Irma in September 2017 (category 5) devastated the island. Many buildings remain under reconstruction, some beaches have changed shape. From June to November, expect 24-29°C and 33-70% tropical showers: sun between showers, but real hurricane risk to monitor. Maho Beach remains the Instagram image: A340/A380 aircraft passing 20m above the beach on approach to Princess Juliana Airport. French side: Grand Case (gourmet), Orient Bay (long beach, naturist section). Dutch side: casinos, duty-free shopping, Philipsburg for cruise passengers.
saint-lucie:en: Saint Lucia is a volcanic island in the Eastern Caribbean: 600 km², 180,000 inhabitants, traditionally Francophone (creole influenced by French despite British independence in 1979). Marked by the Pitons (UNESCO, 2 conical peaks of 700m and 800m). March-April are my favourite months (8.5/10): 22-29°C, 41-46% rain, sea at 26°C. From June to November, it's hurricane season: 60-73% rainfall, hurricane risk (Tomas 2010 left a mark). From December to May, expect 21-28°C: tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3. Highlights: Pitons (Gros Piton 798m hikeable in 2-3h, Petit Piton 743m for mountaineers only), Sulphur Springs at Soufrière ("the world's only drive-in volcano"), Diamond Botanical Gardens and waterfalls, Anse Chastanet (luxury resort and beach), Marigot Bay ("the most beautiful bay in the Caribbean" according to James Michener). Beaches: Reduit Beach (Rodney Bay, lively), Anse des Sables (Vieux Fort), Anse Cochon (snorkelling). Soufrière = base for exploring the Pitons. Castries = capital, cruise port, market. Saint Lucia Jazz Festival in May (one of the most famous in the Caribbean). Try green fig and saltfish (national dish), salt cod accras, cassava bread, local rum. Saint Lucia is very renowned for honeymoons (romantic resorts like Sandals).
antigua:en: Antigua and Barbuda is an independent state in the Lesser Antilles: 280 km², 100,000 inhabitants. 365 beaches on Antigua, that's the marketing promise ("a beach a day"). March is my favourite month (8.4/10): 24-28°C, 51% rain, sea at 25°C. From June to November, it's hurricane season: 58-73% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Irma 2017 devastated Barbuda by 90%). From December to May, expect 24-29°C: American and British tourist high season. Highlights: Nelson's Dockyard (UNESCO, 18th-century British naval base restored at English Harbour, the world's only remaining Georgian working dockyard), Shirley Heights (panoramic view over English Harbour, Sunday evenings with barbecue and steel band), Devil's Bridge (natural rock bridge to the east), Pillars of Hercules. Beaches: Half Moon Bay (crescent-shaped), Dickenson Bay (lively, hotels), Pigeon Point Beach, Darkwood Beach. Saint John's = capital, cruise port, market. Antigua Sailing Week late April (one of the world's most prestigious regattas). Barbuda 90 min by ferry: even quieter, Frigate Bird Sanctuary (5000 nesting birds). Try fungie (polenta, national dish), pepperpot, conch, ducana. Currency: Eastern Caribbean Dollar (XCD), USD accepted. Direct flight London/Frankfurt/Atlanta.
dublin:en: Dublin has a very wet oceanic climate: it rains 1 day in 3 all year. June-August are my favourite months (7.2-7.3/10): 13-19°C, 45-48% rain, 17h days, festivals (Bloomsday on 16 June, Dublin Pride in June). From October to March, expect 4-11°C and 41-48% rain: often grey skies, days down to 8h of light in winter, but this is pub season (Temple Bar stays touristy, prefer traditional pubs like The Long Hall, Toner's, Mulligan's). Saint Patrick's on 17 March is the tourist peak: prices x3, the city goes wild for 4 days. Trinity College and Book of Kells (booking required), Guinness Storehouse and its Gravity Bar, EPIC Museum (Irish emigration) are the classics. Wicklow Mountains 1h by bus for hiking, Galway 2h30 by train for the west coast. May and September are the weather/crowd compromises.
helsinki:en: Helsinki has two faces: bright and warm summer, freezing dark winter. June-July are my favourite months (7.6/10): 12-20°C, 35-41% rain, midnight sun (almost 19h of daylight), public saunas with dips into the Baltic (Löyly, Allas Sea Pool), Suomenlinna (UNESCO fortress) by 15 min ferry, festivals (Flow Festival mid-August). From December to February, expect -4/2°C and 40-47% precipitation often as snow: only 6h of daylight end of December. Christmas markets and Helsinki Christmas Village season. Aurora borealis rare in Helsinki (too far south), you need to head up to Rovaniemi (Lapland) or Levi. Helsinki Cathedral, Temppeliaukio (church in the rock), Design District for Finnish design. Helsinki stays expensive: restaurant meals 25-40€. Tallinn 2h by ferry (Estonia-Finland crossing). May and August are the best crowd/weather compromises.
tallinn:en: Tallinn, Estonian capital: 450,000 inhabitants on the Gulf of Finland, the best-preserved medieval old town in northern Europe (UNESCO, 13th-16th century). Hyper-digital Estonia (e-residency, e-government global model since 1991). June is my favourite month (8.2/10): 12-20°C, 37% rain, 18h days in June (no full night at the summer solstice = "valged ööd" white nights), terraces. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 14-21°C, very many Baltic cruises (Helsinki 2h by ferry, Stockholm 16h by overnight ferry). From November to March, expect -3/4°C and 34-50% precipitation often as snow: Tallinn under the snow is spectacular (snow-covered roofs, Christmas market on Raekoja plats considered one of Europe's most charming), days down to 6h of light end of December. Highlights: Old Town (UNESCO, divided into Toompea upper town and Vanalinn lower town): Raekoja plats (Town Hall Square, Christmas market since 1441), Alexander Nevsky Cathedral (Russian Orthodox on Toompea), Kiek in de Kök Tower, Kohtuotsa platform (signature panorama over the red roofs and the sea), St Olaf's Tower (Oleviste, 124m, wooden lift, view), Pikk Hermann (Toompea fortress, Estonian flag raised every morning). Kalamaja district (former fishermen, now trendy, old wooden houses, Telliskivi creative city), Patarei (sinister former Soviet prison). Excursions: Helsinki 2h by ferry (popular day trip), Lahemaa National Park 1h away (forests, Baltic manor houses). Try verivorst (Christmas blood sausage), kohuke (cottage cheese bar), Vana Tallinn liqueur.
cracovie:en: Krakow is the former Polish royal capital, spared by the war, one of Central Europe's most beautiful cities. June-August are my favourite months (8.2-8.5/10): 14-25°C, 37-48% rain, terraces packed on Rynek Główny (Europe's largest medieval square), Jewish festivals in July (Festival of Jewish Culture in Kazimierz). From December to February, expect -2/6°C and 38-43% rain or snow: Christmas markets on the market square, magnificent city under the snow. Auschwitz-Birkenau 1h30 by bus (booking required with guide in peak season, do not do it as a same-day round trip), Wieliczka salt mines 30 min away (UNESCO), Wawel Castle, Kazimierz (former Jewish quarter now trendy). Try a pierogi at Pierogarnia Krakowiacy, a żurek (Polish sour soup), a vodka at Wódka Café Bar. Krakow stays very cheap: traditional meals 5-10€, beer 1.50-2€. May and September-October are the best tourism/weather compromises.
varsovie:en: Warsaw was rebuilt after 85% destruction in 1944: the UNESCO old town is an identical 1950s recreation. June-August are my favourite months (8.3-8.5/10): 14-25°C, 37-46% rain, terraces packed, free Chopin concerts at Łazienki park (Sundays at noon), festivals (Warsaw Summer Jazz Days). From December to February, expect -2/5°C and 37-42% rain or snow: continental cold, Christmas markets on the market square. Concert season at the Wielki Theatre. Warsaw Uprising Museum and POLIN (History of Polish Jews) are the must-visits to understand the city. Praga district east of the Vistula for the alternative side, Wilanów for the baroque palace. Warsaw stays one of Europe's cheapest capitals: traditional meals (pierogi, żurek, bigos) 6-12€. Krakow 2h30 by Pendolino high-speed train.
le-cap:en: Cape Town is regularly ranked the world's most beautiful city: Table Mountain rising sheer, Cape Point, vineyards 30 minutes away (Stellenbosch, Franschhoek). Austral Mediterranean climate. January-February are my favourite months (9.2-9.3/10): 17-25°C, 8-10% rain, long days, sea at 18-19°C (Atlantic side cold, False Bay side 21°C). From June to August (austral winter), expect 10-17°C and 34-40% rain: damp, often grey skies, ferry and cable car to Robben Island and Table Mountain often cancelled for wind. The Table Mountain cable car closes regularly for weather conditions: have a plan B. Cape Agulhas 2h30 away is the true southern tip of Africa. Southern right whales visit Hermanus from June to November (1h30). Safety: townships need a guide, the city centre and Camps Bay stay safe. Stellenbosch and Franschhoek for the wine route (Pinotage, Chenin blanc). Boulders Beach for the Cape penguins.
zanzibar:en: Zanzibar, Tanzanian archipelago in the Indian Ocean: 2 main islands (Unguja the larger, called by confusion "Zanzibar", and Pemba), 1.3 million inhabitants, Afro-Arab cultural mix (Sultanate of Oman 1698-1856), historic capital of the slave and spice trade. July-August are my favourite months (8.3/10): 22-28°C, 50-51% rain, this is the "dry season" (kusi south-east wind). From June to October, it's the tourist high season: 22-30°C, 50-59% rainfall in brief showers, sea at 26°C. From March to May, it's the long rainy season ("masika"): 67-92% rainfall, many hotels close, rough sea. From November to February, it's the "hot season" (kaskazi north-east wind): 24-32°C, 41-75% rainfall in brief afternoon showers, sea at 28°C, warmer for swimming. Highlights: Stone Town (UNESCO, capital, maze of alleys, characteristic carved wooden doors with 800 doors recorded, House of Wonders, former slave market turned Anglican cathedral memorial Slave Market), Forodhani Gardens (night market every evening, skewers, soups), Spice Tour (30 min away, clove/cardamom/vanilla/cinnamon plantations, Zanzibar remains the world's largest clove exporter). East coast beaches (the most beautiful, but big tides ±3m exposing 1 km of sand at low tide): Paje (kitesurf), Jambiani (quiet), Pingwe. North coast beaches (no tide effect, ideal for swimming): Nungwi (lively), Kendwa (Full Moon Party). Activities: swimming with turtles at Mnemba Atoll, diving on Mnemba Reef, dolphin watching at Kizimkazi. Try Zanzibar pizza (local street food), spiced pilau, octopus curry. Logistics: Zanzibar 1h30 from Dar es Salaam by ferry + 30 min by plane (direct flight Paris or via Doha/Istanbul).
sydney:en: Sydney, Australia's largest city: 5.3 million inhabitants, famous bay with the Opera House (Jørn Utzon, 1973) and the Harbour Bridge. December is my favourite month (8.6/10): 18-25°C, 34% rain, austral summer, 14h days, packed beaches (Bondi, Coogee, Manly). From June to August (austral winter), expect 7-18°C: mild but frequent rain, cool swimming (sea at 17°C). Period when humpback whales migrate offshore (June-October, viewing from Cape Solander or North Head). September-November are my real sweet spots: 10-23°C, jacarandas in bloom in November (Kirribilli especially), New Year's Eve in Sydney = world-renowned fireworks over the Harbour Bridge. Highlights: Sydney Opera House (interior tour or opera/concert), Bridge Climb (Harbour Bridge climb), Royal Botanic Garden, The Rocks (historic district), Bondi to Coogee Coastal Walk (6 km along the cliffs). Manly by ferry from Circular Quay (30 min, one of the world's most beautiful ferry rides). Blue Mountains 2h away (Three Sisters, hiking). Try meat pies, vegemite, flat white (born here), barramundi, kangaroo steak. Sydney New Year's Eve draws 1 million spectators.
melbourne:en: Melbourne, Australia's cultural capital: 5 million inhabitants, sporting city (Australian Open in January), world-class coffee culture, alleys covered in street art. February is my favourite month (8.9/10): 15-25°C, 21% rain, austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 7-14°C with 37-42% rain: cold by Australian standards, short days (9h of sunshine), but also the ski season at Mount Buller 3h away. December-March are the tourist high season. Melbourne is famous for its "4 seasons in one day": bring a jacket even in midsummer. Highlights: Federation Square, Hosier Lane (the most famous street art), Queen Victoria Market (since 1878), Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG, 100,000 capacity), St Kilda (urban beach, penguins at the Pier at dusk). Trendy districts: Fitzroy (Brunswick St), Brunswick (Sydney Rd), Carlton (Lygon St Italian quarter). Great Ocean Road 1h30 away (12 Apostles, 250 km panoramic road), Yarra Valley 1h away (wines, Domaine Chandon), Mornington Peninsula 1h30 away (hot springs, wines). Try flat white in the laneway cafes (Brother Baba Budan, Patricia, Seven Seeds), Vietnamese pho in Richmond (Victoria St). Melbourne and Sydney = friendly historic rivalry.
auckland:en: Auckland is New Zealand's largest city: 1.7 million inhabitants (1/3 of the country), "City of Sails" (1 sailboat for 4 inhabitants), built on 50 dormant volcanoes. January-February are my favourite months (8.2-8.3/10): 18-23°C, 39-40% rain, austral summer, swimming at the East Coast beaches (Mission Bay, Takapuna). From June to August (austral winter), expect 10-16°C with 42-48% precipitation: mild for the southern hemisphere but damp. Auckland has a "4 seasons in one day" climate typical of New Zealand. Highlights: Sky Tower (328m, the southern hemisphere's tallest structure, SkyJump for the brave), Auckland Domain and War Memorial Museum, Viaduct Harbour (port redone for the America's Cup), Devonport (12 min by ferry, village charm). Excursions: Waiheke Island 40 min by ferry (wines, beaches, restaurants), Rangitoto Island (young 600-year-old volcano, hike to the summit), Hobbiton 2h30 away (Lord of the Rings set at Matamata), Coromandel Peninsula 2h away (Cathedral Cove, Hot Water Beach to dig your own hot pool at low tide). Try hangi (Maori cuisine cooked in the earth), pavlova, hokey pokey ice cream, NZ wines (Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc). Auckland is generally the starting or ending point for New Zealand road trips.
porto:en: Porto has experienced a tourist explosion since 2010 thanks to massive low-cost flights. The city keeps its charm despite it all: Ribeira on the Douro, metal bridges, port wine cellars. July-August are my favourite months (9.1/10): 16-24°C, 8-10% rain, milder temperatures than southern Portugal thanks to the Atlantic. From November to March, expect 7-17°C and 40-50% rain: rainy Porto par excellence (the city gets 1200mm/year, as much as Brest). This is the indoor season: port wine cellars in Vila Nova de Gaia (Sandeman, Taylor's, Graham's), Livraria Lello (which inspired Harry Potter). São Bento Station (azulejos), Cais da Ribeira at sunset, Clérigos Tower, Foz do Douro by tram for the sea. Douro cruise to the vineyards 1-3 days. Try a francesinha (monster Porto-style sandwich) at Café Santiago, tripas à moda do Porto for the brave. May-June and September-October are the best price/weather compromises.
madere:en: Madeira, the "island of flowers": Portuguese volcanic island in the Atlantic, 1000 km from mainland Portugal, mild subtropical climate year-round. July-August are my favourite months (9.2/10): 19-26°C, 10-12% rain, sea at 22°C, stable temperatures. From December to February, expect 13-19°C and 31-35% rain: the north side stays wet almost year-round (UNESCO laurisilva), the south (Funchal, Calheta) is markedly drier. Highlights: Funchal (capital, old town Zona Velha, Mercado dos Lavradores market), levadas (irrigation channels turned into trails, 2500 km in total: 25 Fontes, Caldeirão Verde, Caldeirão do Inferno), Pico do Arieiro (1818m, 7 km hike to Pico Ruivo 1862m, ideal sunrise above the clouds), Câmara de Lobos (fishing village immortalised by Churchill), Cabo Girão (Europe's 2nd highest cliff, 580m, glass skywalk), Porto Moniz (natural pools in the lava). Monte cable car (gardens) and wicker toboggan ride down (carros de cesto, unique experience). Try espetada (beef skewer), bolo do caco, poncha (rum-lemon-honey), Madeira wine (dry Sercial to sweet Malvasia). May-June and September-October are the real sweet spots prices/weather. Direct flight Paris-Funchal in 4h.
tolede:en: Toledo, in central Spain: 85,000 inhabitants, former capital of Visigothic then Muslim then Christian Spain (capital until 1561 when Philip II moved it to Madrid), UNESCO 1986. "City of the 3 cultures" (Christian, Jewish, Muslim coexisting from the 8th to the 15th century). June is my favourite month (9.2/10): 17-30°C, 13% rain, ideal temperatures before the July-August heatwave. September stays excellent (9.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-35°C, but also extreme crowds at midday (Toledo = classic day trip from Madrid 30 min by AVE train, 8 million visitors/year for 85,000 inhabitants). From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to extreme heat (21-35°C, sometimes 42°C, the Meseta plateau interior). From December to February, expect 2-14°C and 18-23% rain: dry winter cold, but Toledo in winter = spectacular medieval atmosphere without crowds. Highlights: Cathedral Saint Mary (French Gothic 13th-15th century, one of Spain's most impressive, altarpieces, sacristy with 18 Grecos), Alcázar (perched fortress, Renaissance military architecture, Republican defeat in 1936 = episode mythologised by Francoists), Jewish quarter (Judería) (Santa María la Blanca synagogue 12th century with Moorish arches, del Tránsito synagogue 14th century with Sephardic museum, one of the only two preserved medieval synagogues in Spain along with Cordoba), Iglesia de Santo Tomé (Burial of the Count of Orgaz, El Greco masterpiece 1586), El Greco House-Museum (Cretan artist who died in Toledo in 1614), Cristo de la Luz Mosque (10th century, one of the only mosques preserved in Christian Spain), Alcántara Bridge (Roman) and San Martín Bridge (medieval), Mirador del Valle (signature panorama over the fortified city encircled by the Tagus). Try mazapán de Toledo (marzipan since the 12th century), Toledo-style partridge, queso manchego.
malte:en: Malta is the EU's smallest member state: 316 km², 540,000 inhabitants, 3 islands (Malta, Gozo, Comino), thousands-of-years history (Phoenicians, Romans, Arabs, Normans, Knights of the Order of Saint John 1530-1798, British 1814-1964). June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 21-27°C, 3% rain, sea at 23°C, ideal weather before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (24-30°C) and extreme crowds: Malta concentrates 3 million tourists/year for 540,000 inhabitants, hotel prices x3, packed beaches. From December to March, expect 11-17°C and 21-37% rain: mild for Europe, ideal climate for visiting culture without crowds, European golfers wintering. September is also excellent (9.1/10). Highlights: Valletta (UNESCO capital, founded by the Knights in 1566, St John's Co-Cathedral with Caravaggios, Grand Master's Palace), Mdina ("Silent City", former fortified capital), Hal Saflieni Hypogeum (UNESCO, 4000-year-old underground temple, book 3 months ahead, only 100 visitors/day), Megalithic Temples of Hagar Qim and Mnajdra (UNESCO, 5000 years old, predating the pyramids), Marsaxlokk (fishing harbour with colourful luzzu boats, Sunday market). Gozo 25 min by ferry (quieter island, Citadella in Victoria, Marsalforn salt pans, Dwejra Bay). Comino and its Blue Lagoon (turquoise water, snorkelling, accessible by boat from Malta/Gozo). Sliema and St Julian's = modern beach resorts. Try pastizzi (cheese/peas pastry), rabbit stew (local rabbit), ftira, Marsovin/Meridiana wine.
cordoue:en: Cordoba is the Mosque-Cathedral (Mezquita), 856 columns of pink marble and jasper, one of Europe's most impressive monuments. May is my favourite month (9.4/10): 14-28°C, 16% rain, Fiesta de los Patios early May (UNESCO, 2 weeks, private patios open to the public, jasmine and carnations everywhere). July drops to 3.9/10 due to extreme heat (22-38°C, sometimes 47°C, one of Europe's hottest cities): impossible to visit during the day, life inverted (going out after 23h). From December to February, expect 5-18°C and 22-24% rain: mild during the day, ideal for the Mezquita without crowds. Juderia (former Jewish quarter), Alcázar of the Christian Kings and its gardens, Roman Bridge over the Guadalquivir, Medina Azahara 8 km away (caliphal city in ruins, an absolute must). Salmorejo and flamenquines are the local specialties. March-April and October-November are the only real sweet spots. Seville 50 min by AVE train.
genes:en: Genoa (Genova), capital of Liguria in Italy: 580,000 inhabitants, former powerful Maritime Republic (11th-18th centuries, rival of Venice) that colonised the western Mediterranean. Italy's most important port, birthplace of Christopher Columbus (1451). UNESCO 2006 for its Palazzi dei Rolli (42 palaces of the rich Genoese families 16th-17th centuries). July is my favourite month (9.3/10): 21-28°C, 27% rain, ideal temperatures. August stays excellent (8.9/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 10-29°C. From November to March, expect 5-15°C and 35-44% rain: mild for northern Italy. Highlights: Old town (Centro Storico, one of Europe's largest medieval old towns, maze of "caruggi" = narrow alleys sometimes 1m wide, unique atmosphere mixing chic and working-class), Palazzi dei Rolli (UNESCO, including Palazzo Rosso and Palazzo Bianco = Strada Nuova museums on today's Via Garibaldi, paintings by Van Dyck, Rubens, Caravaggio, Veronese), San Lorenzo Cathedral (12th century, black-and-white striped Genoese-style façade, houses the Sacro Catino, a vase considered to be the Holy Grail according to legend), Acquario di Genova (Europe's 2nd largest aquarium after Valencia, Italy's #1 attraction for families, designed by Renzo Piano, a local son), Porto Antico (old port redeveloped by Renzo Piano for Columbus's 500th anniversary in 1992, Bigo panoramic crane, Galata maritime museum), Piazza De Ferrari (modern centre with fountain), Boccadasse (former fishing village on the east coast, postcard-perfect, renowned ice cream), Spianata Castelletto (19th-century public lift to a panorama over the city), Staglieno Monumental Cemetery (19th-20th century funerary sculptures, one of Europe's most impressive). Excursions: Cinque Terre 1h30 away (UNESCO, already covered by BDW), Portofino 30 min away (ultra-chic village), San Fruttuoso (abbey accessible only on foot or by boat), Camogli (fishing village with pastel houses). Try pesto alla genovese (the original, Ligurian DOP basil), Genoese focaccia (different from Bari, thinner and oilier), trofie al pesto, farinata (chickpea pancake), pansoti, Cinque Terre wines. Genoa airport (GOA).
bari:en: Bari, capital of Italian Puglia and Italy's 9th city: 320,000 inhabitants on the Adriatic coast. Major port for crossings to Greece and Albania, strategic stopover for visiting Puglia (Alberobello UNESCO trulli, Lecce baroque, Matera). June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 20-29°C, 18% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (22-31°C, sometimes 38°C with sirocco). September stays excellent (9.0/10). From November to March, expect 6-18°C and 28-36% rain: mild for southern Italy, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Bari Vecchia (labyrinthine fortified old town, narrow alleys, atmosphere more Sicilian than Italian, women making orecchiette by hand on boards in front of their houses on Via Arco Basso = signature Bari scene), Basilica of Saint Nicholas (Basilica di San Nicola, 11th-century Romanesque, houses the relics of Saint Nicholas of Myra brought back by 47 sailors of Bari in 1087 from present-day Turkey, place of Orthodox and Catholic pilgrimage as Saint Nicholas is the origin of Father Christmas), Saint Sabin Cathedral (12th-century Romanesque), Castello Normanno-Svevo (Frederick II 13th century), Lungomare (one of Italy's longest seaside promenades, 13 km), Petruzzelli Theatre (Italy's 4th largest theatre). Essential Puglia excursions: Alberobello 1h away (UNESCO, village of trulli = conical dry-stone houses signature of Puglia), Matera 1h away (UNESCO, troglodyte city of the sassi inhabited continuously for 9000 years, European Capital of Culture 2019), Polignano a Mare 30 min away (perched village on cliffs with signature coves), Ostuni (white city), Lecce 1h30 away (baroque), Castel del Monte 1h away (UNESCO, octagonal Frederick II castle), Trani 30 min away (signature Romanesque cathedral by the sea), Gargano (peninsula to the north, Vieste). Try orecchiette (ear-shaped pasta), focaccia barese, sgagliozze (polenta fries), panzerotti (stuffed turnover), burrata (originating in Puglia), Primitivo and Negroamaro wines. Bari airport (BRI).
lecce:en: Lecce, capital of the Salento in southern Italian Puglia: 95,000 inhabitants nicknamed the "Florence of the South" or "Florence of the baroque" for its exceptional baroque architectural richness. Unique style of baroque leccese = extravagant sculptural ornamentation carved in the local stone ("pietra leccese" = soft golden limestone that hardens with time, ideal for fine sculpture). September is my favourite month (8.9/10): 20-28°C, 27% rain, ideal temperatures after the heatwave. June-July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to heat (19-32°C, frequent sirocco). From April to May and October, expect 11-23°C: excellent period. From November to March, expect 7-19°C and 26-42% rain: mild for southern Italy. Highlights: Basilica Santa Croce (17th century, Italy's most exuberant baroque façade, sculptures by Cesare Penna 1582-1646, considered overloaded by some critics, sublime by others), Piazza del Duomo (square closed by the Duomo + Campanile + Bishop's Palace + Seminary, all baroque), Roman amphitheatre (1st century, partly buried under Piazza Sant'Oronzo, capacity 25,000), Saint Oronzo Column (recovered from the Via Appia in Brindisi), Palazzo dei Celestini, San Matteo Church (unique ovoid baroque), Rosario Church, Castello Carlo V (16th century), Faggiano Museum (unusual: an owner who was digging to repair his pipes uncovered 2500 years of urban history under his house, which he turned into a museum). Essential Salento excursions: Otranto 45 min away (cathedral with 12th-century Tree of Life mosaic = masterpiece, Aragonese castle), Gallipoli 45 min away (fortified old town on a bridged islet, beaches), Santa Maria di Leuca 1h away (the southern tip, cape), Salento coast = the "Maldives of Salento" for its turquoise-water beaches (Pescoluse, Punta Prosciutto, Marina di Pulsano = white sand, crystal water rivalling the Caribbean). Try pasticciotto leccese (small shortcrust cake with pastry cream, local signature), rustico (béchamel-mozzarella-tomato turnover), ciceri e tria (chickpeas with pasta), Negroamaro wine, coffee with ghiaccio (served over almond-milk ice).
matera:en: Matera, European Capital of Culture 2019, is one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities (9000 years): the Sassi (UNESCO), troglodytes carved into the limestone tuff. June is my favourite month (8.7/10): 17-29°C, 27% rain, golden light on the Sassi at sunset. July drops to 6.9/10 due to heat (21-34°C, sometimes 42°C, southern Italian interior). August stays good (8.2/10) but hot and crowded. From November to March, expect 3-15°C and 19-32% rain: mild, ideal for visiting without crowds, singular winter atmosphere on the Sassi (Pasolini shot The Gospel According to Matthew in 1964 here for its biblical look, Mel Gibson The Passion of the Christ in 2004, Mary Magdalene in 2017). Highlights: Sassi di Matera (Sasso Caveoso more raw, Sasso Barisano restored), Cathedral, Casa Grotta (reconstructed troglodyte house), Cripte rupestres (Byzantine troglodyte chapels). Murgia Timone Belvedere on the other side of the canyon for the panoramic view. Cave hotels in the Sassi (Sextantio Le Grotte della Civita, Locanda di San Martino). Try peperoni cruschi (dried peppers), pane di Matera (IGP), cavatelli, lampascioni. Bari 1h away by bus.
cinque-terre:en: Cinque Terre is 5 UNESCO fishing villages clinging to the cliffs of the Ligurian Riviera: Monterosso, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola, Riomaggiore. July is my favourite month (9.1/10): 18-26°C, 24% rain, sea at 22°C. August excellent (8.8/10) but overtourism: 3 million visitors/year across 5 villages totalling 4000 inhabitants, the famous Sentiero Azzurro Manarola-Riomaggiore aka "Via dell'Amore" closed since 2012 (reopening underway), many villages limit access. From November to March, expect 3-13°C and 39-48% rain: many hotels and restaurants close, trails sometimes closed for landslides (Sept 2012, Oct 2018 were devastating). May-June and September-October are the real sweet spots. Cinque Terre Express train serving the 5 villages = the most practical solution (5€ per trip or 18.50€ day pass). Hikes: Sentiero Azzurro (50€ Cinque Terre Card permit), higher Sentiero Verde-Azzurro. Try pesto genovese, focaccia, Monterosso anchovies, sciacchetrà (straw wine). La Spezia 10 min by train for cheaper, quieter hotels.
zurich:en: Zurich, Switzerland's largest city: 430,000 inhabitants, 1.5 million in the urban area, the country's economic and financial capital (worldwide HQ of UBS, Credit Suisse until the 2023 merger, Swiss Re, Zurich Insurance), regularly ranked among the cities with the highest standard of living in the world. On the shore of Lake Zurich with views of the Alps on clear days. August is my favourite month (8.3/10): 15-25°C, 40% rain, ideal temperatures. June-July stay excellent (8.1-8.2/10). From April to September, it's the tourist high season: 4-25°C. From November to March, expect -2/8°C and 41-46% rain: winter cold, possible snow (Zurich gets 30-50 cm/year cumulatively), frequent fog over the lake ("Hochnebel" thermal inversion), Christmas market on Bahnhofstrasse. Highlights: Bahnhofstrasse (one of the world's most expensive shopping avenues, 1.4 km, Swiss banks, luxury watches, jewellers), Old town Altstadt (medieval alleys on both banks of the Limmat, in the city centre, very well preserved), Grossmünster (12th-century Romanesque cathedral with 2 towers, site of the Zurich Protestant Reformation by Huldrych Zwingli in 1519, panoramic view from one tower), Fraumünster (9th-century convent, stained glass by Marc Chagall 1970), Saint Peter (Europe's largest clock face, 8.7m diameter), Kunsthaus Zurich (one of Switzerland's most important art museums, David Chipperfield extension 2021), Landesmuseum (Swiss national museum), Lake Zurich and Quai promenade (in summer swimming in the lake from the historic Badis = public baths, Seebad Enge the most famous), Niederdorf district ("Dörfli", medieval nightlife quarter), Kreis 5 and Zurich-West (former industrial districts converted to trendy, gastronomy, art, Frau Gerolds Garten = cult urban garden, Viaduktbögen under viaduct arches), Uetliberg (871m, Zurich's home mountain, train from HB in 25 min, panorama over the city and lake to the Alps). Excursions: Lucerne 1h away, Rhine Falls 45 min away (Europe's most powerful at Schaffhausen), Lake Constance 1h away, Stein am Rhein. Try Zürcher Geschnetzeltes (sliced veal in cream-mushroom sauce, signature Zurich dish), rösti, half-and-half fondue, raclette, Birchermüesli (invented in Zurich by Dr Bircher-Benner 1900), Sprüngli Swiss chocolate (no S, the most prestigious in Zurich). Zurich airport (ZRH), one of Europe's most modern, 12 min by train from the centre.
geneve:en: Geneva, Switzerland's 2nd city and European HQ of the UN: 200,000 inhabitants in the city, 600,000 in the urban area. International capital (HQs of WTO, WHO, UNHCR, ILO, ICRC), world capital of luxury watchmaking (Patek Philippe, Vacheron Constantin, Rolex at Plan-les-Ouates), private finance capital. On the shore of Lake Geneva with views of Mont Blanc on clear days. July-August are my favourite months (8.9/10): 17-27°C, 29-33% rain, ideal temperatures, swimming in the lake from the Bains des Pâquis, a Genevan institution. From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 13-27°C. From November to March, expect 0-10°C and 36-47% rain: winter cold, possible but rare snow on the plain (Geneva is in a zone protected by the Jura to the west), bise sometimes glacial north wind, Place Bel-Air Christmas market. Highlights: Jet d'eau (Geneva's visual signature, 140m high, 500 litres/second, visible from anywhere in the city, created in 1886 as a hydraulic factory pressure-relief valve, became a tourist icon in 1891), Old town (one of Europe's largest old towns, cobbled alleys, Saint Peter's Cathedral 12th-century Romanesque-Gothic — site of the Calvinist Reformation, seat of John Calvin 1536-1564 — panoramic towers, archaeological excavations 3rd-century basement), Maison Tavel (Geneva's oldest house from the 12th century, urban history museum), Place du Bourg-de-Four (oldest square), Reformation Wall (1909, tribute to the Reformers Calvin/Knox/Bèze/Farel sculpted on 100m), Palais des Nations (UN European HQ, guided tour 17 CHF, the world's largest intergovernmental meeting space), International Red Cross and Red Crescent Museum (opposite the Palais des Nations, moving humanitarian view), CERN at Meyrin (European Organization for Nuclear Research, where the Higgs boson was discovered in 2012, free guided tours by booking), MAMCO (modern contemporary art museum), Patek Philippe Museum (one of the world's largest watchmaking collections, free on the 1st Saturday of the month). Excursions: Mont-Salève by cable car 5 min from Veyrier (Geneva's home mountain on the French side, 360° panorama over the city and the Alps), Lausanne 35 min by train, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc 1h away (Aiguille du Midi cable car 3842m), Annecy 30 min away (Europe's purest lake), Yvoire medieval lakeside village. Try longeole (Geneva sausage with cumin), lake perch fillets, half-and-half fondue, longeole, local Geneva Beer brewery. Geneva-Cointrin airport (GVA) 7 min by train from the centre, one of Europe's most practical.
lucerne:en: Lucerne (Luzern), Swiss city in the heart of central Switzerland: 80,000 inhabitants on the shores of Lake Lucerne (Vierwaldstättersee, Switzerland's 4th largest lake, one of the world's most picturesque at the foot of the Alps). Considered among the most beautiful cities in Switzerland, tourist paradise with preserved historic architecture and spectacular alpine setting. August is my favourite month (8.1/10): 16-25°C, 48% rain, ideal temperatures. June-July stay excellent (7.9-8.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 9-25°C, heavy crowds (Asians mostly in the watch boutiques, Pilatus and Rigi cable cars congested), boat trips on the lake. From November to March, expect 0-9°C and 42-50% rain: winter cold, frequent fog over the lake ("Hochnebel"), possible snow, but skiing accessible 1h away (Engelberg).
