The reference hipster city of the USA since the 2000s ("Keep Portland Weird", emblem of the American slow living and craft beer movement), the most extensive urban parks in the country. July is my favourite month (9.9/10): 14-28°C, only 7% rain (Mediterranean summer climate typical of the Pacific North-West, dry and temperate summers, mild and rainy winters). August stays excellent (9.5/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-29°C. From November to March, expect 2-11°C and 58-61% rain: mild but greyish (Portland has a reputation for being one of the rainiest cities in the USA, which is exaggerated: Seattle, Mobile, Miami actually receive more annual precipitation — but Portland does indeed have more rainy days). Highlights: Powell's City of Books (the largest independent bookshop in the world, 1.6 million books over 5 floors, 6300 m²), Pearl District (former industrial quarter converted into a trendy zone), Forest Park (one of the largest urban parks in the USA, 2000 hectares of forest), Washington Park (Rose Garden = 10,000-rose garden, Japanese Garden = one of the most beautiful Japanese gardens outside Japan according to Japanese rankings), Portland Saturday Market (since 1974, the largest outdoor craft market in the USA), International Rose Test Garden (Portland is the "City of Roses"). Craft breweries: Portland is one of the world capitals of craft beer (70+ city breweries, 250+ in the metro), notably Deschutes, Cascade, Hopworks, Breakside. Excursions: Mount Hood 1h away (3429m, volcano, year-round skiing at Timberline Lodge), Multnomah Falls 30 min away (188m, one of the most famous waterfalls in the Pacific North-West), Columbia River Gorge, Willamette Valley (renowned Pinot Noir wines). Try Voodoo Doughnuts (doughnut shop), food carts (one of the best US street food scenes, 500+ carts), salmon, IPA. PDX airport (regularly ranked the best US airport).
savannah:en: Savannah, in Georgia: 145,000 inhabitants, founded in 1733 by James Oglethorpe (1st Georgian city planned on a grid), most beautiful colonial city of the southern USA according to many rankings, 22 shaded squares (tree-lined squares) structuring the historic centre. April is my favourite month (8.7/10): 15-25°C, 25% rain, end of spring with azaleas and magnolias in full bloom, famous Virginia oaks covered in Spanish moss (Tillandsia usneoides) hanging from the branches. From March to May and October-November are the best seasons: 11-26°C, mild climate. From June to September, it's the cyclone season with heavy heat: 44-72% rainfall, oppressive humidity, real hurricane risk. From December to February, expect 8-21°C and 27-30% rain: mild for the south, ideal for visiting. Highlights: Forsyth Park (central park, signature 1858 fountain, picnic under oaks), Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist (French Gothic, stained glass), SCAD Museum of Art (contemporary art in former railway depot), Mercer-Williams House (setting of the novel/film Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil by John Berendt 1994/Clint Eastwood 1997 = Savannah exploded touristically after this cult book), Bonaventure Cemetery (cemetery 10 min away on the edge of Wilmington River, southern Gothic atmosphere, popularised by the same book), Wormsloe Historic Site (cult oak avenue 2.4 km, Instagram point). River Street and City Market (tourist zones on the edge of the Savannah River), Tybee Island 30 min away (beach). Try shrimp and grits, fried chicken (at The Olde Pink House), pralines (Leopold's Ice Cream). 2h from Charleston by car (the 2 cities can be combined in a Lowcountry road trip).
vancouver:en: Vancouver is the most accessible city for Europeans on the Pacific coast of Canada: Pacific on one side, mountains (North Shore Mountains) on the other. July is my favourite month (9.1/10): 15-22°C, 15% rain, finally clear skies, swimming at Kits Beach and English Bay, hiking at Grouse Mountain. From November to March, expect 1-9°C and 55-65% rainfall: city reputed to be the rainiest in Canada (1200mm/year), but also when Whistler (2h away) opens its ski season. May-June and September are the best compromises. Highlights: Stanley Park (1000 acres, larger than Central Park, totem poles), Granville Island (public market, microbreweries), Capilano Suspension Bridge (70m above the canyon), Gastown (Steam Clock, the first quarter), Grouse Mountain (cable car for the view). Districts: Yaletown (luxury), Kitsilano (hippie/yoga), Commercial Drive (Italian). Try sushi (Vancouver = the North American sushi capital), salmon, ramen at Marutama, Korean BBQ in Coquitlam. Vancouver Island (Victoria) 1h30 by ferry, Whistler 2h by car. Direct flight from Paris in 9h.
quebec-ville:en: Quebec City, capital of the province of Quebec (Canada): 540,000 inhabitants, founded in 1608 by Champlain, the only fortified city in North America north of Mexico (UNESCO 1985). 95% French-speaking (major difference from the more bilingual Montreal). June-August are my favourite months (8.0/10): 6-25°C, 41-53% rain, mild temperatures after the long winter, festivals (Festival d'été de Québec mid-July, one of the largest in Canada). From September to October, it's the spectacular foliage: 5-20°C, red maples, one of the most beautiful regions in the world for this season. From December to March, expect -14/0°C and 37-46% precipitation (often abundant snow, Quebec City receives 3m of snow/year): it's the Quebec City Carnival (late January-mid-February, 3 weeks, the largest winter carnival in the world, Bonhomme Carnaval parades, canoe races on the frozen Saint Lawrence), Ice Hotel (30 min away, hotel rebuilt every winter since 2001, possibility of sleeping there), skating on the Saint-Charles river. Highlights: Old Quebec (UNESCO, divided into Upper Town on Cap-Diamant and Lower Town on the riverside), Château Frontenac (1893, signature Fairmont hotel of the skyline, the most photographed hotel in the world according to some rankings), Plains of Abraham (1759, French defeat against the British, end of New France), Citadel (still in service, daily changing of the guard), Place Royale and Petit-Champlain district (the oldest commercial street in North America), Fortifications (4.6 km of intact ramparts, walkable on foot). Excursions: Montmorency Falls 15 min away (84m, taller than Niagara), Île d'Orléans (agricultural countryside), Charlevoix 1h30 away. Try poutine, tourtière, maple syrup, cipaille.
mont-tremblant:en: Mont-Tremblant, Quebec ski resort in the Laurentides: 9000 inhabitants at 200m altitude (Mont-Tremblant summit reaches 875m), 130 km north of Montreal. One of the most famous resorts in eastern Canada and North America, renowned for its pedestrian village rebuilt in 1991 (Intrawest) with very successful pseudo-European architecture. July is my favourite month (8.2/10): 16-25°C, 49% rain, but Mont-Tremblant is above all a winter destination. From June to September, expect 5-25°C: active summer season (36-hole golf Le Géant and Le Diable, hiking, mountain bike, swimming Tremblant lake). From December to March, expect -15/2°C and 41-47% precipitation often as snow (4-5m/season): this is ski high season. Pistes: 102 pistes, 14 lifts, vertical drop 645m (modest compared to the Alps but excellent for North America), 4 sides (South main, North, Soleil, Edge). Characteristic conditions: extreme dry cold (-25°C in January-February, frozen breath on the face within minutes), fresh snow nearly guaranteed (severe continental climate), icy patches typical. Audience: Quebecers in the majority, Northeast Americans (Boston, NY), Europeans seeking a North American experience. Tremblant Pedestrian Village: 50+ restaurants and shops, lively nightlife, resurrected European village atmosphere. Hotels: Fairmont Tremblant (5*), Hôtel Quintessence (5*+ on the lakeside), Westin, 150-800 CAD/night. Try poutine, maple syrup, tourtière, Quebec microbrewery beer (La Diable). Logistics: Mont-Tremblant 1h30 from Montreal by car (highway 15 north then 117), Mont-Tremblant International airport (YTM, seasonal flights).
lake-louise:en: Lake Louise is the most photographed altitude emerald lake in Canada: turquoise water (glacial sediments), Château Lake Louise Fairmont in the background, Victoria glacier at the back. At 1730m altitude in Banff National Park. July-August are my favourite months (8.0/10): 9-20°C, 37-46% rain, access to Lake Agnes Tea House, Plain of Six Glaciers, Big Beehive hikes. From December to March, expect -14/0°C: ski season. The Lake Louise Ski Resort (4200 hectares) remains one of the largest in North America. The lake freezes completely in winter for skating. From November to May, the Canadian Rockies stay under snow. The summer crowds (up to 15,000 visitors/day at the lake) require a paid Parks Canada bus system from Banff. Banff town 50 km to the south, Calgary 2h by car.
gold-coast:en: The Gold Coast is the Brisbane coast: 70 km of beaches, high-rise buildings on the seafront, theme parks (Movie World, Sea World, Wet'n'Wild), world-class surf. November is my favourite month (8.6/10): 19-26°C, 38% rain in brief showers, sea at 23°C. From December to March, expect 21-28°C with 43-57% rainfall: subtropical wet season, real cyclone risk (up to 1 per year). Irukandji jellyfish possible (rare but serious). From June to August, expect 13-21°C and 19-23% rain: this is the local austral winter, ideal for surfing at Snapper Rocks or Kirra (the most consistent waves in the world) and hiking in the hinterland (Lamington National Park, Springbrook). Surfers Paradise is the tourist heart but also the loudest: for calm, Burleigh Heads, Coolangatta, Currumbin. Schoolies Week in November (Australian young graduates partying) fills hotels and the atmosphere. To plan around if travelling with family.
cairns:en: Cairns, in tropical north Queensland, is the access base for the Great Barrier Reef (UNESCO, the largest coral reef in the world, 2300 km, visible from space). August-October are my favourite months (8.7/10): 18-29°C, 23-31% rain, dry tropical season. From November to April, it's the wet season: 40-75% rainfall, possible cyclones (historic category 5), Irukandji jellyfish (very dangerous, swimming forbidden or in stinger suits). From May to October, it's the ideal tourist season: clear sky, calm sea, maximum visibility for diving. Highlights: Great Barrier (day-trip boat 150-250$, diving or snorkelling at Hastings Reef, Michaelmas Cay, Agincourt Reef), Daintree Rainforest (UNESCO, the oldest tropical forest in the world, 180 million years), Cape Tribulation (where the forest meets the reef), Kuranda by Skyrail (7.5 km cable car above the canopy), Atherton Tablelands (plateaus, waterfalls, crater lakes). Cairns Esplanade Lagoon (free artificial pool by the sea, secure swimming year-round). Cairns has no swimmable beaches in town (sea 30 min away). Try barramundi, mud crab, kangaroo. Direct flight from Singapore, stopover Sydney.
perth:en: Perth is the most isolated metropolis in the world (Adelaide 2700 km away, Bali closer than Sydney). Dry Mediterranean climate with winter rain. December is my favourite month (9.8/10): 18-28°C, 6% rain, 14h days. From June to August, expect 10-21°C and 43-50% rainfall: this is austral winter, as wet as a Breton winter. Cottesloe and Scarborough are the reference beaches, the ferry to Rottnest Island (quokkas, cycling, snorkelling) is a Sunday classic for locals. Margaret River 3h to the south (wines, surf, gourmet) deserves 2-3 days. King's Park stays one of the largest urban parks in the world. January-March are also excellent (9.2-9.5/10), with sea at 22°C. Flights from Europe via Singapore or Doha (~24h door-to-door).
darwin:en: Darwin, capital of the Top End: gateway to Kakadu National Park (UNESCO) and Litchfield National Park, extreme tropical climate. July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 21-30°C, 3% rain, this is the dry season (Dry Season), ideal for visiting. From May to October, it's the Dry Season: constant sky, bearable temperatures (22-32°C), all sites accessible. From December to March, it's the Wet Season (tropical monsoon): 71-86% rainfall, daily storms, heavy temperatures (26-32°C, feels-like 40°C+ with humidity), real cyclone risk. Many Kakadu trails closed in wet (Jim Jim Falls, Twin Falls inaccessible). April and November = build-up/build-down, oppressive humid transitions. Highlights: Kakadu National Park (3h away, Aboriginal rock art at Ubirr and Nourlangie, crocodiles at Yellow Water Cruise), Litchfield National Park (1h30 away, swimming in natural pools: Florence Falls, Buley Rockhole, Wangi Falls), Mindil Beach Sunset Market (Thursday-Sunday in dry season), Crocosaurus Cove ("Cage of Death" for swimming with a crocodile). Asian cuisine very present (proximity Indonesia). Flight from Bali in 2h30 (closer than Sydney). Population 130,000: human-scale city.
hobart:en: Hobart, capital of Tasmania: 250,000 inhabitants, the most southern city in Australia, cool oceanic climate, Mount Wellington (1271m) overlooking the city. January is my favourite month (8.5/10): 13-22°C, 26% rain, austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 4-13°C: cold with sometimes snow on Mount Wellington (rare in town). March-April are my real sweet spots: 11-20°C, reduced tourist crowds, foliage in Tasmania. Highlights: MONA (Museum of Old and New Art, one of the most avant-garde contemporary art museums in the world, access by catamaran ferry from the centre), Mount Wellington (road or Pinnacle Track on foot), Salamanca Market (Saturday morning, historic market on Salamanca Place), Battery Point (19th-century historic district). Tasmania excursions: Cradle Mountain and its Lake St Clair National Park (Overland Track 6 days), Bay of Fires (east coast, beaches with spectacular orange rocks), Wineglass Bay (Freycinet National Park), Port Arthur (former UNESCO penal colony). Try Tasmanian salmon, oysters, scallops, Tasmanian whisky (Sullivans Cove). Direct flight Sydney/Melbourne in 1h30. Tasmania is becoming a major food&drink destination.
rotorua:en: Rotorua is the New Zealand geothermal capital: 75,000 inhabitants, geysers, hot springs, mud pools, permanent sulphur smell. Also the capital of Maori culture in North Island. January-February are my favourite months (8.1-8.3/10): 14-23°C, 39-41% rain, austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 5-13°C and 39-43% rain: cold and wet, but the hot springs come into their own (steam in the cold). Highlights: Te Puia (Pohutu Geyser up to 30m, Maori marae, kiwi house), Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland (Champagne Pool, Lady Knox Geyser at 10:15am every day), Hell's Gate (mud pools), Polynesian Spa (thermal springs lakeside), Whakarewarewa (living Maori village), Hangi and Maori concert (traditional cuisine cooked in the earth + dance). Outdoor activities: Redwoods Forest (canopy walk Redwoods Treewalk, world-renowned mountain biking), Mountain biking (Whakarewarewa Forest, one of the best mountain-bike spots in the world). Wai-O-Tapu 30 min away, Lake Taupo 1h away (the largest lake in NZ, volcanic crater), Tongariro Alpine Crossing 1h30 away (one of the best hikes in the world, 19.4 km). Try hangi, kiwifruit (originating here at Te Puke 1h away). Near-permanent sulphur smell: you get used to it.
wellington:en: Wellington, the New Zealand capital: 215,000 inhabitants, the windiest city in the world ("Windy Welly", average annual wind 27 km/h), intense café culture, cultural scene. January-February are my favourite months (8.1-8.3/10): 15-20°C, 26% rain, austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 9-13°C and 41-43% rain: mild but humid and windy, days at 9h of light. Highlights: Te Papa (National Museum, free, one of the best museums in Oceania), Mount Victoria (view over the bay), Cable Car up to Kelburn and the Botanic Garden, Cuba Street (bohemian pedestrian street, cafés), Weta Workshop (special effects from Lord of the Rings, The Hobbit, Avatar: 30-min visit). Wellington Movie Tour on the Lord of the Rings sets. Zealandia (eco-restored sanctuary, NZ wildlife). Marlborough vineyards 3h away (by ferry to South Island, Sauvignon blanc capital), Kapiti Coast 1h away, Martinborough wineries 1h30 away (Pinot Noir). Inter-Islander Ferry Wellington-Picton (3h30, Cook Strait, one of the most beautiful crossings in the world). Try flat white, fish and chips, hokey pokey. Wellington has the highest density of cafés/restaurants per inhabitant in the world.
christchurch:en: Christchurch, capital of Canterbury in South Island: 380,000 inhabitants, the most "English" city in New Zealand, tragically marked by the September 2010 and February 2011 earthquakes (185 deaths, 50% of the centre destroyed). Reconstruction still ongoing. January-February are my favourite months (8.3/10): 13-21°C, 25-28% rain, austral summer. From June to August (austral winter), expect 4-12°C: dry cold, snow possible on the Port Hills. Highlights: Cathedral Square (Cathedral under restoration since the earthquake, 10-year project), Cardboard Cathedral (Shigeru Ban, temporary cathedral 2013), 185 Empty Chairs Memorial (memorial to the earthquake victims), Botanic Gardens, Christchurch Tram. Quake City (museum about earthquakes). Excursions: Akaroa 1h30 away (Banks Peninsula, former French colony, Hector dolphins), Arthur's Pass 2h30 away (panoramic road towards the west coast), Kaikoura 2h30 away (whale watching, whales year-round), Mount Hutt and other ski resorts in winter. Christchurch is the classic starting point of South Island road trips (towards Queenstown via Wanaka, or via the west coast and Franz Josef Glacier). Direct flight from Sydney/Melbourne.
bay-of-islands:en: Bay of Islands, in the New Zealand Northland: 144 islands, turquoise water, historic base of the founding of New Zealand (Treaty of Waitangi 1840 between Maori and the British Crown). January is my favourite month (8.4/10): 17-24°C, 39% rain, subtropical austral summer (warmer climate than Auckland). From June to August (austral winter), expect 10-16°C with 37-43% rain: mild for NZ. Highlights: Waitangi Treaty Grounds (the country's founding place, marae, museum), Russell ("first capital", ferry from Paihia), Paihia (tourist centre), Hole in the Rock (boat excursion, pierced rock arch), dolphin tours (dolphins year-round), Cape Reinga 3h away (the most accessible northernmost point of NZ, where Maori souls leave the world). 90 Mile Beach (actually 90 km, accessible by 4×4 bus on the sand). Activities: sailing (catamaran day trip 100-150$, 3-day excursion 500$), diving (wreck of the Rainbow Warrior sunk by France at Matauri Bay), kayak. Try snapper (local fish), kiwi fruit, hokey pokey ice cream. Bay of Islands = tropical NZ destination for New Zealanders (January school holidays). Direct flight Auckland-Bay of Islands (Kerikeri) in 35 min, or 3h by road.
taupo:en: Lake Taupo is the largest lake in New Zealand: 616 km² (twice the size of Singapore), formed by the eruption of the Taupo super-volcano 1800 years ago (one of the largest of the last 5000 years). January is my favourite month (7.9/10): 13-22°C, 33% rain, austral summer, swimming in the lake (water at 18-20°C in summer). From June to August (austral winter), expect 4-11°C with 40-44% rain: cold, skiing possible at Whakapapa and Tūroa on Mount Ruapehu (2 largest resorts in NZ). Highlights: Huka Falls (powerful waterfall, 220,000 litres/second), Craters of the Moon (free geothermal area), Tongariro National Park (UNESCO, 3 active volcanoes: Ruapehu, Ngauruhoe = Mount Doom from Lord of the Rings, Tongariro), Tongariro Alpine Crossing (19.4 km, one of the best hikes in the world, do with point-to-point shuttle), sky dive Taupo (the cheapest skydive in NZ, 200-400$). Hot Water Beach at the lake, Maori rock carvings at Mine Bay (only accessible by boat). Trout fishing world-renowned (rainbow and brown trout). Taupo = central base to explore Tongariro National Park. 1h from Rotorua, 4h from Auckland, 5h from Wellington.
zakynthos:en: Zakynthos (Zante), 3rd largest Greek Ionian island: 40,000 inhabitants over 410 km² south-west of Kefalonia. Nicknamed "the flower of the Levant" by the Venetians. Signature image of Greece: Navagio Beach ("shipwreck beach", isolated cove with rusted shipwreck stranded 1980, white limestone cliffs 200m, indescribable turquoise water). May is my favourite month (8.9/10): 16-24°C, 19% rain, sea at 21°C. June-July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (21-33°C) and the extreme crowds on Navagio (access restriction since 2018 for landslide risks). September stays excellent (8.8/10). From November to March, expect 10-19°C and 26-46% rain: mild but rainy, many hotels close. Highlights: Navagio Beach (the most photographed beach in Greece, accessible only by boat from Porto Vromi or Saint-Nicolas, swimming since 2022 strongly discouraged due to landslide risks, aerial panoramic platform above, one of the most viral beaches on Instagram), Blue Caves (sea caves at Cape Skinari, water of supernatural blue, accessible by boat), Marathonissi (Turtle Island) (turtle-shaped islet in Laganas Bay, protected zone for the nesting of loggerhead turtles Caretta caretta = protected species, Zakynthos marine reserve), Zakynthos Town (Chora rebuilt after the 1953 earthquake which destroyed 95% of the island, Solomos Square in tribute to the Greek national poet Dionysios Solomos born here in 1798), Cape Keri (wild cliffs south-west, sunset panorama), Porto Limnionas (magnificent cove, cliffs and turquoise water). Loggerhead turtles = nesting May-August on Laganas Bay, observation possible but NEVER walk on the beach at night (eggs destroyed by footsteps), swimming with a turtle forbidden. Try pasteli (sesame-honey), ladotyri (local cheese), retsina (resined wine). Zakynthos airport (ZTH).
thessalonique:en: Thessaloniki, 2nd city of Greece and capital of Greek Macedonia: 800,000 inhabitants in the urban area on the northern Aegean Sea. Founded in 315 BC by Cassander, capital of the Eastern Byzantine Empire for centuries, cosmopolitan city pre-1923 (40% Sephardic Jews, 30% Turks, 30% Greeks before the population exchange of 1923). UNESCO 1988 for its 15 Palaeochristian and Byzantine monuments. September is my favourite month (9.1/10): 18-28°C, 17% rain, ideal temperatures after the summer heatwave. From June to August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heavy heat (20-33°C, sometimes 40°C). From April to May and October, expect 10-24°C: excellent period. From November to March, expect 2-17°C and 16-33% rain: continental cold, rare snow. Highlights: White Tower (Lefkos Pyrgos, signature visual of Thessaloniki, 15th-16th century Ottoman tower on the seafront, panorama at the top, museum of city history), Aristotelous Square (the great central square facing the sea, designed after the great fire of 1917, Greek Haussmann model), Saint Demetrios Church (Agios Dimitrios, Palaeochristian basilica of the city's patron saint, crypt with Roman martyrium), Hagia Sophia Church (Agia Sofia 8th century, Byzantine copy of the Hagia Sophia of Constantinople), Rotunda of Galerius (4th-century Roman mausoleum converted into a Byzantine church), Arch of Galerius and Palace of Galerius (4th century, Emperor Galerius resided here), Ano Poli quarter ("upper city", the only Ottoman quarters that survived the 1917 fire, cobbled alleys, panoramic view), Byzantine ramparts (4 km), Archaeological Museum (Macedonian artefacts), Byzantine Museum (one of the best in the world on Byzantine art). Excursions: Mount Olympus 1h30 away (2917m, home of the ancient Greek gods, 2-day ascent), Vergina-Aigai (UNESCO, Macedonian royal tombs including that of Philip II father of Alexander the Great), Meteora 3h away, Mount Athos (visit reserved for Orthodox men only). Try bougatsa (semolina pastry), koulouri (sesame bagel), souvlaki, ouzo, tsipouro (Macedonian eau-de-vie). SKG airport.
naxos:en: Naxos, the largest of the Greek Cyclades: 22,000 inhabitants over 430 km² in the heart of the Cyclades. More traditional and less touristy than neighbouring Mykonos or Santorini, greener (interior mountains, Mount Zas 1004m highest point of the Cyclades), beaches among the most beautiful in the Aegean (golden sand). June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 21-28°C, 6% rain, sea at 22°C, meltemi wind that cools things down. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-30°C) and the meltemi wind (north wind at 50-70 km/h in peak, ferries often cancelled, rough sea for swimming). September stays excellent (9.3/10). From November to March, expect 10-17°C and 22-41% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many hotels close, island almost deserted (guardian of Cycladic tradition). Highlights: Portara (monumental marble gate of the unfinished temple of Apollo 6th century BC on Palatia islet linked to the port by a dyke, one of the most famous postcard images of Naxos, view at sunset), Chora of Naxos (old town inside the 13th-century Venetian Kastro, labyrinthine alleys, white houses), Filoti and Apiranthos (altitude villages in the mountains, traditional stone architecture, white marble everywhere — Naxos is the ancient "marble mountain" of the Cyclades), Renowned beaches: Agios Prokopios (one of the most beautiful beaches in Greece according to several rankings), Agia Anna, Plaka (the longest 4 km, wild), Mikri Vigla (kitesurf, windsurf), Alyko (cedars unique in Greece). Excursions: Lesser Cyclades (Koufonisia, Schinoussa, Iraklia = trio of preserved islands south of Naxos, 1h ferry), Mykonos and Santorini 1h30-2h by ferry. Try kitron (lemon liqueur unique in Greece), graviera (cheese), Naxos potatoes (the most renowned in Greece). Naxos airport (JNX, domestic flights) or ferry from Piraeus 5h.
paros:en: Paros is the good Cycladic compromise: less photographed than Santorini, livelier than Milos, easy transport. June stays my favourite month (9.6/10): 22-27°C, 5% rain, sea at 21°C, crowds still reasonable. Naoussa in the north (port on the seafront, restaurants on the jetty) and Parikia (capital, ferry, Panagia Ekatontapyliani) concentrate the activity. Antiparos just opposite by 10-minute ferry for the cave and the calm. From July to August, expect 24-29°C, 1% rain, and the tourist invasion: meltemi (north wind) blows hard, ideal for kitesurf at Pounda. From November to March, expect 11-19°C and 20-37% rain: classic Cycladic hibernation, many tavernas and rooms close. May and September-October are the real sweet spots: weather still summer, crowds reduced by 50%, lower prices. Plan 4-5 days to fully enjoy.
kefalonia:en: Kefalonia (Cephalonia), the largest Greek Ionian island: 35,000 inhabitants over 780 km² south of Corfu. Made famous by the Louis de Bernières novel Captain Corelli's Mandolin (1994) then the 2001 film with Nicolas Cage. Rebuilt after the devastating 1953 earthquake (95% of the island destroyed, one of the worst earthquakes of the 20th century in Europe). June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 21-27°C, 11% rain, sea at 22°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and the crowds (Italians, British in the majority). September stays excellent (8.8/10). From November to March, expect 9-16°C and 33-48% rain: mild but rainy, many hotels close. Highlights: Myrtos Beach (the most famous in Kefalonia, one of the most photographed beaches in Greece along with Navagio, white cliffs 200m, unique turquoise water thanks to the light reflecting on the clear bottom, panoramic point from the road above), Antisamos Beach (where the film Captain Corelli's Mandolin was shot, near Sami), Melissani Cave (underground lake discovered 1951, turquoise water lit by a hole in the vault at solar noon, 30-min crossing by boat), Drogarati Cave (stalactites/stalagmites, sometimes concerts inside for its exceptional acoustics), Argostoli (capital, port, restaurants), Fiscardo (one of the only villages spared by the 1953 earthquake, preserved Venetian architecture, smart port), Assos (picturesque village on a peninsula with 16th-century Venetian fortress), Mount Ainos (national park, endemic Kefalonian fir, highest point 1628m). Excursions: Ithaca (Ulysses' return island according to Homer, 30 min by ferry from Sami), Lefkada, Zakynthos. Try kreatopita (meat pie), aliada (garlic puree), thyme honey, Robola wine (local AOC white grape, ideal with fish). Kefalonia airport (EFL).
lefkada:en: Lefkada (Leucas), Greek Ionian island connected to the mainland by a bridge: 23,000 inhabitants over 320 km², the only Greek island accessible by car without a ferry (canal and bridge from Akarnania). Renowned for its turquoise beaches on the west coast that rival the Caribbean. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 16-26°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C. August stays excellent (9.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 14-26°C, sea at 22-25°C, hotel prices x3, ferries to neighbouring islands stormed. From November to March, expect 1-12°C and 35-50% rain: mild but windy (Lefkada can be freezing with the north wind in winter), many hotels close. Highlights: West coast (Lefkada's signature, the most famous beaches: Porto Katsiki = "the most beautiful beach in Greece" according to several rankings for 20 years, vertical white cliffs 100m, access by 80 steps), Egremni (4 km beach, damage from the 2015 earthquake made access difficult, stairs destroyed, swimming since 2022 by boat only), Kathisma (the most accessible, beach bars), Pefkoulia, Mylos, Agiofili near Vasiliki. Vasiliki (world capital of windsurf and kitesurf, the evening thermal wind "Eric" ideal for beginners), Nidri (port for ferries and excursions to the islands), Cape Lefkata ("Sappho's leap", the poet Sappho is said to have jumped here in 600 BC for unrequited love), Nidri Waterfalls. Excursions: Skorpios private islands (formerly belonging to Aristotle Onassis, bought by a Russian billionaire), Meganisi, Kalamos, Kefalonia and Ithaca 30 min by ferry from Vasiliki. Try ladopita (olive oil pie), Lefkada salami (spicy), Vertzami wine (local red grape). Logistics: Aktion Preveza airport (PVK) 30 min by car, accessible directly by bridge from the mainland.
kos:en: Kos, Greek island in the Dodecanese off the Turkish coast: 33,000 inhabitants over 290 km², birthplace of Hippocrates (ancient doctor, creator of the oath) in the 4th century BC. Renowned for its beaches, its Greco-Roman heritage and its European charter atmosphere (British in the majority). June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 22-27°C, 4% rain, sea at 23°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-30°C, sometimes meltemi wind) and the crowds (British in the majority, hotel prices x3, congested beaches). September-October stay excellent (8.6-9.4/10). From November to March, expect 11-20°C and 28-42% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many hotels close. Highlights: Asclepieion of Kos (sanctuary of Asclepius 4th century BC on 3 terraces, place of Hippocrates' medical teaching, ancient school of medicine founded here, 4 km from the port), Old Town of Kos (city centre, 14th-century Knights of Saint John castle, ancient agora, Hippocrates' Tree = 700-year-old plane tree said to have seen Hippocrates teaching under its shade, more legend than fact), Embros Therma (sulphurous hot springs 50°C flowing into the cold sea, free natural swimming, access by 10-min path), Beaches: Tigaki (12 km), Paradise Beach (Bubble Beach, volcanic gas bubbles emerge from the sand), Kefalos (Camel Beach), Mastichari (kitesurf), Nikolas at Plakas (one of the most beautiful beaches of Kos), Lambi (urban), Zia (altitude village for the sunset). Excursions: Bodrum (Turkey) 1h by ferry (former Halicarnassus, Mausoleum of Halicarnassus one of the 7 ancient Wonders), Nisyros (active volcano, neighbouring island), Kalymnos (climbing), Pserimos. Try krasotyri (local cheese), Greek baklava, Kos ouzo. Kos airport (KGS) with charters from Europe.
milos:en: Milos is the geological Cyclade: 70 beaches, white cliffs, volcanic landscapes. Less frequented than Santorini or Mykonos, but that's changing fast. June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 21-27°C, 3% rain, sea at 22°C, prices still reasonable. Sarakiniko (lunar white landscape), Kleftiko (caves accessible by boat), Tsigrado (climbing to descend) are the most famous sites. Klima fishing village with its colourful syrmata (former boat sheds). From July to August, expect 24-29°C with 1-3% rain: Greek tourist peak (Greeks themselves holiday in August). From November to March, expect 10-19°C and 23-37% rain: many restaurants close, ferries reduced, classic Greek hibernation atmosphere. May-June and September-October stay the best compromises. Access from Athens: 3-4h fast ferry or 30-minute flight (but expensive).
hydra:en: Hydra (Ύδρα), Greek island in the Saronic Gulf 70 km south of Athens: 2000 permanent inhabitants over 50 km². No motorised vehicles allowed on the island since 1968 (royal decree to preserve the unique character of the village): only means of transport = mules and donkeys for luggage transport, walking paths, water-taxis to the distant beaches. Smart-bohemian atmosphere, refuge of artists and intellectuals (Leonard Cohen had his house here 1960-2007 where he wrote several albums). June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 22-30°C, 9% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. September stays excellent (9.3/10). July-August stay good (6.9-8.8/10) despite the heat (25-33°C) and the crowds (Athenians on weekends, Italians, Americans). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 22-26% rain: mild, many establishments close, island almost deserted (unique charm). Highlights: Hydra Port (horseshoe-shaped, one of the most beautiful Aegean ports, 18th-19th century mansion-houses in stone of the Hydriot shipowners who made fortune in maritime trade then contributed to the Greek War of Independence in 1821, unique atmosphere with donkeys waiting to transport luggage to the hotels), Mansions Tombazi, Voulgaris, Tsamados, Boudouris (some 18th-century palaces open to the public), Cathedral of the Dormition (17th-century with signature white marble bell tower), Hydra Historical Museum (the island's role in the Greek War of Independence), Lazaros Koundouriotis House (18th-century Hydriot shipowner museum), Leonard Cohen's House (private, view from outside, near the port). Beaches: Hydra has no sandy beach at the village (flat rocks with direct access to deep water), for real beaches take a water-taxi: Vlychos (sandy, 30 min on foot or 5 min by boat), Bisti (the most beautiful, turquoise water, access by boat only), Agios Nikolaos (extreme west, isolated), Mandraki (north-east, former shipyard converted into a beach). Paths: Hydra is a hiker's paradise (mountainous island, paths from the village to the Profitis Ilias and Eleni monasteries 2h away), cultural route to the fishing village of Kamini (15 min on foot). Saronic excursions: Spetses, Aegina, Poros by ferry 30 min to 2h. Try amygdalota (almond cakes signature of Hydra), saganaki, local sea urchin (fresh urchins), ouzo. Logistics: Hydra accessible only by hydrofoil from Piraeus Athens in 1h30 (Hellenic Seaways 30€/person, no direct flight, no bridge), Saronic ferry 3h.
samos:en: Samos, Greek island in the north of the Dodecanese archipelago 1.3 km from the Turkish coast (the closest of the Greek islands to a foreign coast): 33,000 inhabitants over 477 km². Greener than the Cyclades thanks to its abundant water sources (Nestlé Samos mineral water), mountains including Kerkis 1433m. Birthplace of Pythagoras (570 BC), of the philosopher Epicurus (341 BC) and of the painter Aristarchus. UNESCO 1992 for the sanctuary of Hera and the Tunnel of Eupalinos. June is my favourite month (9.5/10): 21-28°C, 13% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. September stays excellent (9.4/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-32°C). From November to March, expect 9-19°C and 33-46% rain: mild but relatively rainy (Samos receives more rain than the Cyclades thanks to its mountains). Highlights: Heraion of Samos (UNESCO, sanctuary of Hera 8th century BC, one of the largest sanctuaries of ancient Greece, modest remains but major historical site), Tunnel of Eupalinos (UNESCO, aqueduct tunnel dug into the mountain in the 6th century BC at the order of the tyrant Polycrates, 1036m long dug simultaneously from both sides and meeting with a gap of only 60 cm = major technical feat of Antiquity, accessible to visitors over 200m), Pythagoreion (UNESCO, ancient port at the current location of Pythagorio = 2nd town of Samos, ancient fortifications, birthplace of Pythagoras symbolised by the port), Vathy Archaeological Museum (capital of the island, monumental kouros statues), Vathy/Samos Town (charming capital in an arc on the port), Pythagorio (the most famous port, more touristy atmosphere), Tsamadou Beach (the most beautiful pebbles, naturist on part), Lemonakia Beach, Kokkari (fishing village in the north, the most picturesque on the island), Manolates (picturesque mountain village, panoramic terraces), Karlovasi (3rd town, ferry port to Athens), Pythagoras Springs karst caves. Excursions: Kuşadası Turkey 1h by ferry (then Ephesus UNESCO 30 min, the main reason for staying in Samos for many), Patmos (UNESCO, island of the apocalypse of Saint John), Ikaria (neighbouring island, one of the world's 5 "Blue Zones" with exceptional longevity of inhabitants 90+ years). Try Samos Vin Doux Naturel (sweet liqueur wine made from white Muscat à petits grains, AOC since 1971, the most prestigious of Greek sweet wines), souvlaki, moussaka, local feta, thyme honey. Samos airport (SMI).
lac-garde:en: Lake Garda ("Lago di Garda"), the largest lake in Italy: 370 km², 158 km circumference, in the shape of an inverted Y between Veneto/Lombardy/Trentino. More accessible and less jet-set than Lake Como, more family-friendly and sporty. June-August are my favourite months (8.6-8.8/10): 14-29°C, 36-37% rain, swimming in the lake (water at 24°C in midsummer), windsurf and kitesurf (steady wind Pelér from the north in the morning and Ora from the south in the afternoon). From November to March, expect 3-14°C and 24-36% rain: mild for northern Italy, Limone lemon trees still green, skiing possible 30 min away (Monte Baldo, Madonna di Campiglio, Folgaria). Regions: South (Sirmione, Desenzano, Peschiera) flat, warmer, livelier, easy access from Verona/Milan; North (Riva del Garda, Limone sul Garda) mountainous, alpine, watersports, narrower lake (fjord effect). Highlights: Sirmione (Scaliger fortified peninsula, hot springs, Grottoes of Catullus = Roman ruins), Riva del Garda (Trentino side), Limone sul Garda (lemon groves, its limonaie = stone greenhouses), Malcesine (cable car to Monte Baldo 1760m), Bardolino (red wines), Desenzano (lively port). Gardaland (the largest theme park in Italy, at Castelnuovo del Garda). Activities: sailing, windsurf, kitesurf at Torbole, Monte Baldo hiking, lake-route cycling. Try tortelli di zucca (pumpkin ravioli), risotto al tastasal (sausage), bigoli, Bardolino/Lugana wine, lake olive oil.
sorrente:en: Sorrento, smart resort on the northern Amalfi Coast in Italy: 16,000 inhabitants south of the Gulf of Naples, perched 50m on a volcanic tuff cliff. Privileged base for visiting the Amalfi Coast, Capri, Pompeii, Herculaneum, Vesuvius and Naples. Spectacular view over the Gulf of Naples with Vesuvius in the background. June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 20-27°C, 17% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. May and September stay excellent (8.1-8.6/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and the extreme crowds (Americans in the majority 50% of the clientele, British, hotel prices x4-x5, full restaurants, congested road). From November to March, expect 7-18°C and 38-55% rain: mild but rainy, many hotels close, ideal for calm. Highlights: Historic centre (pedestrian old town, Piazza Tasso = main square dedicated to the poet Torquato Tasso born in Sorrento 1544), Marina Grande (picturesque fishing port on the rocks below, accessible by stairs from the centre, seafood restaurants), Marina Piccola (modern marina, ferries Capri/Naples), Bagni della Regina Giovanna ("Baths of Queen Joan", natural pool on the rocks 4 km to the west, ruins of a Roman villa, swimming between rocks through a natural arch), Sorrento Cathedral (Duomo, 11th-15th century), Cloister of San Francesco (13th century with signature orange trees), Villa Comunale (public garden on the cliff edge, sunset panorama over the bay = local institution), Museo Correale di Terranova (18th-century palace with Neapolitan art), Valley of the Mills (Valle dei Mulini, sunken valley in the heart of the city with ruins of old mills overgrown with tropical vegetation, mysterious atmosphere). Essential excursions: Capri 25 min by hydrofoil (signature island, Grotta Azzurra, Anacapri, Villa San Michele, Faraglioni), Amalfi Coast 1h away by SITA Sud bus (vertiginous winding road, Positano-Amalfi-Ravello — already covered by BDW), Pompeii 30 min by Circumvesuviana train (UNESCO, Roman city buried 79 AD), Herculaneum 30 min away (UNESCO, similar but better preserved), Vesuvius (volcano ascent 1281m, panorama over the bay), Ischia and Procida 1h away by ferry (already covered by BDW), Naples 1h by train (Napoli culinary capital). Try gnocchi alla sorrentina (gnocchi tomato mozzarella basil), spaghetti alle vongole, pasta al limone, mozzarella di bufala, sfogliatella (Neapolitan pastry), limoncello from Sorrento (local production, IGP "limoni di Sorrento" lemons). Naples airport (NAP) 1h away by train.
ravello:en: Ravello, perched on the heights of the Amalfi Coast in Italy: 2400 inhabitants at 365m altitude above the Mediterranean. Exceptional panoramic view over the coast, refuge of European aristocrats since the 19th century (Wagner, Greta Garbo, Jacqueline Kennedy stayed here). July-August are my favourite months (9.1-9.4/10): 18-29°C, 15-22% rain, sea at 25°C below (300m descent), mild temperature at altitude (5°C less than at the Amalfi seafront). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 13-29°C, but also hotel prices x4-x5 (Ravello is one of the smartest villages in Italy, 5*+ hotels Belmond Caruso or Palazzo Avino at 800-3000€/night), book 6 months ahead. From November to March, expect 4-17°C: mild for the altitude, many hotels close, windy climate. Highlights: Villa Rufolo (13th century, hanging gardens with signature panoramic view over the coast, Wagner drew inspiration here for Parsifal in 1880, classical music open-air festival Ravello Festival June-September), Villa Cimbrone (12th century, higher and more spectacular, Terrazza dell'Infinito = unique panorama with stone busts facing the sea), Duomo of Ravello (11th century, mosaics, ambo), Centro storico (Piazza Duomo, walking alleys, stairs to Atrani and Amalfi 30-45 min descent). Amalfi Coast: Amalfi 15 min by bus (Duomo Sant'Andrea, Pontone), Positano 30 min away (pastel village on a cliff), Capri 1h by hydrofoil. Try limoncello (local production, Amalfi lemon orchards), pasta con vongole, fior di latte mozzarella, coast wine (Costa d'Amalfi DOC). Logistics: Ravello 1h30 from Naples by bus (mandatory parking, pedestrian access only).
siena:en: Siena, medieval city in Tuscany in Italy: 53,000 inhabitants 70 km south of Florence. UNESCO 1995 for its exceptionally preserved historic centre (one of the most intact medieval cities in the world, little modified since the 14th century). Historic rival of Florence (the 2 republics waged war from the 12th to the 16th century, until Siena's fall to the Medici 1555). Cradle of Sienese Gothic painting (Duccio, Simone Martini, Lorenzetti). June is my favourite month (9.3/10): 16-29°C, 22% rain, ideal temperatures. July-August stay excellent (8.7-8.9/10) despite the heat (19-32°C). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 7-32°C, extreme crowds in July-August, hotel prices x3 (American and Asian clientele in the majority, focus on the Tuscan experience). From November to March, expect 2-15°C and 27-39% rain: mild for Tuscany, few tourists, particular charm under mists. Highlights: Piazza del Campo (one of the most beautiful medieval squares in the world, shell-shaped on a slope, paved with red bricks divided into 9 sections representing the 9 magistrates, place of the Palio di Siena 2 days a year = horse race between the 17 contrade since 1633, the most violent and passionate event in Italy, free access in the centre of the square with 50,000 people pressed together), Torre del Mangia (88m tower of the 14th-century Palazzo Pubblico, panorama 503-step climb), Palazzo Pubblico (medieval town hall, houses the Museo Civico with the frescoes of the Allegory of Good and Bad Government by Ambrogio Lorenzetti 1338, one of the most significant works of political Middle Ages), Duomo of Siena (Gothic 13th-14th century, black-and-white striped façade, marble mosaic floor covering 13,000 m² telling the history of Christian humanity — the largest pavement in the world, only unveiled August-October), Battistero San Giovanni (Gothic baptistery with baptismal font by Donatello, Ghiberti, Della Quercia), Pinacoteca Nazionale (Sienese Gothic Duccio Maestà, Simone Martini, Lorenzetti). Tuscany excursions: San Gimignano 1h away (UNESCO, 14 medieval towers, "Manhattan of the Middle Ages"), Volterra 1h away, Montepulciano and Pienza 1h away (UNESCO, Vino Nobile wines and Pecorino cheese), Val d'Orcia (UNESCO, signature Tuscan landscapes, cult photographs), Crete Senesi (lunar clay hills), Florence 1h away, Pisa 1h30 away. Try pici (large fresh Sienese pasta), ribollita (re-boiled bread cabbage bean soup), pappa al pomodoro, panforte (spiced dried-fruit Christmas cake IGP from Siena since the 13th century), ricciarelli (almond biscuits), Chianti Classico DOCG, Brunello di Montalcino DOCG (one of the great Italian wines).
procida:en: Procida, the smallest of the three islands of the Gulf of Naples (Procida, Ischia, Capri): 4 km², 10,000 inhabitants, fishing island preserved from mass tourism, typical pastel houses of Marina Corricella. Italian Capital of Culture 2022 (the first time an island has received this title). July is my favourite month (9.4/10): 23-29°C, 8% rain, sea at 24°C, ideal temperatures. August stays excellent (9.1/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 20-29°C, but Procida stays much less frequented than Capri or Ischia (Italian tourists in the majority, Neapolitan weekenders, few foreigners), hotel prices x2-x3. From November to March, expect 8-15°C and 30-48% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many hotels close, island almost deserted (4000 permanent inhabitants). Highlights: Marina Corricella (signature fishing port, pink-yellow-blue pastel houses, one of the most famous postcard images of Italy, popularised by The Talented Mr Ripley 1999 and Il Postino 1994), Terra Murata (medieval citadel at the highest point 91m, panoramic view), Abbey of San Michele Arcangelo, Marina Grande (arrival port for ferries from Naples), Chiaiolella Beach (the most famous, volcanic black sand), Pozzo Vecchio Beach ("Postino Beach" where Massimo Troisi shot the film). Excursions: Vivara (islet linked by a bridge, nature reserve), Ischia 25 min by ferry (hot springs). Try spaghetti alle vongole, lemon cake (Procidan lemons, smaller than Amalfi), insalata di limoni (yes, salad of peeled Procidan lemons). Logistics: Procida 1h from Naples by ferry (departures from Beverello port), 25 min by hydrofoil. No car needed (island walkable in 1h).
alberobello:en: Alberobello, UNESCO village in the Italian Puglia in the Itria Valley: 11,000 inhabitants 50 km south-east of Bari. World capital of trulli = signature dry-stone conical houses of Puglia, historically built dry without mortar (14th-19th century) to escape the taxes of the Spanish Bourbons (peasants would dismantle the roofs before tax-collector inspection then put them back afterwards). UNESCO 1996 for 1500 preserved trulli spread across the Rione Monti and Aja Piccola districts. July is my favourite month (9.4/10): 21-31°C, 12% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. June and August stay excellent (9.0-9.3/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 10-31°C, big crowds in July-August (tourist coaches, hotel prices x3, the village of 11,000 inhabitants can hit 30,000 visitors/day). From November to March, expect 4-16°C and 29-36% rain: mild for southern Italy, few tourists, magical under rare but possible snow. Highlights: Rione Monti (the largest trulli district, 1030 trulli aligned over 5 hills, the postcard image of Alberobello, descent of the Via Monte San Michele stairs, become very commercial with souvenir shops on the ground floor of the trulli), Rione Aja Piccola ("small orchard", 400 trulli, more traditional and residential, still inhabited by locals), Trullo Sovrano (the only 2-storey trullo, 18th century, today a free museum showing the traditional interior of a 19th-century Puglian family), Belvedere Santa Lucia (panorama over Rione Monti from Santa Lucia square, signature photo), Trullo Siamese ("Siamese trullo", 2 trulli joined to a single roof according to the legend built for 2 brothers who fell out and divided the house), Sant'Antonio Church (rare church in the shape of a trullo, built 1926 respecting the local style), Casa Pezzolla (territory museum with 15 connected trulli). Itria Valley excursions: Locorotondo 10 km away ("most beautiful village in Italy" 2003, circular perched village with white houses and sharp pointed sloping roofs rare in the South), Cisternino (another "most beautiful village", specialty bombette = stuffed grilled wood-fire skewers), Martina Franca (18th-century baroque architecture), Ostuni 30 min away ("white town" perched), Polignano a Mare 45 min away (cliff village with coves, Cala Porto signature beach of Puglia). Caveat: Alberobello = a tourist village entirely focused on trulli commerce, traditional atmosphere largely vanished in Rione Monti, prefer early visits 7am before tourist coaches at 10am or in the evening after 5pm. Recommended visit half a day + a night in a trullo (unique experience = 80-200€/night). Try orecchiette (Puglian ear-shaped pasta), focaccia barese, fave e cicoria (broad bean wild chicory puree), bombette, burrata d'Andria, mozzarella, Primitivo wine, Negroamaro. Logistics: Alberobello 1h from Bari by car, Trenitalia + slow Sud Est train.
tropea:en: Tropea, beach resort in Calabria in southern Italy in the south-west, on the "Costa degli Dei" ("Coast of the Gods"): 6500 inhabitants. Perched on a 60m limestone-tuff cliff overlooking directly a white-sand beach and turquoise water, Tropea is regularly ranked among the most beautiful beaches in Italy ("Borgo dei Borghi 2021" = Most Beautiful Village of Italy elected by Italians). Known worldwide for its IGP red onion of Tropea (Cipolla Rossa di Tropea Calabria, the sweetest red onion in the world according to Italian chefs, exported throughout Europe). July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 22-29°C, 19% rain, sea at 25°C, ideal temperatures. June and August stay excellent (8.4-8.7/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 12-29°C, crowds in July-August (Italians in the majority, few foreigners thanks to Calabria's remoteness). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 38-48% rain: mild for southern Italy, many establishments close, unique atmosphere under sea mists. Highlights: Old town on the cliff (pedestrian centro storico, narrow alleys, square-belvederes on the cliff edge with spectacular panoramas over the Mediterranean and Mount Stromboli volcano visible 60 km to the north-west on clear days), Belvedere Piazza Cannone (main terrace, Instagram photo-stop), Tropea Cathedral (Duomo 12th-13th century Norman, houses Byzantine icon of the Madonna di Romania), Santuario Santa Maria dell'Isola (signature visual of Tropea, 11th-century Benedictine sanctuary perched on a rocky islet linked to the beach by a strip of land, access by 3-minute monumental stairs, 360° panorama from the summit, illuminated in the evening, the most photographed image of Calabria), Tropea Beach ("Spiaggia della Rotonda", fine white sand with turquoise water rivalling the Caribbean according to many magazine rankings), Neighbouring beaches: Marina del Convento, Marina dell'Isola (around the Sanctuary), Marasusa, Le Roccette. Costa degli Dei sites: Capo Vaticano 12 km to the south (preserved wild coves, Praia di Fuoco/Tonicello/Riaci beaches, panorama over the Aeolian Islands), Pizzo 30 km away ("capital of tartufo" = artisan chocolate ice cream filled with hazelnut-butter cream, to taste at Bar Dante), Aspromonte National Park 1h away. Aeolian Islands excursions: Stromboli, Lipari, Vulcano, Salina, Panarea, Filicudi, Alicudi = volcanic UNESCO archipelago accessible by hydrofoil from Tropea-Vibo Marina port (May-October), 1-day Stromboli excursion volcano ascent (924m almost permanently active) recommended. Try IGP Tropea red onion (taste raw on bread and oil, in jam, in original ice cream, in marmalade, fried as fried onions), Calabrian 'nduja (spicy spreadable charcuterie, signature of Calabria), pasta cu' la muddica (pasta with breadcrumbs), pesce spada (grilled swordfish), DOP bergamot of Reggio Calabria, Cirò DOC wine, Sicilian lemon granita. Lamezia Terme airport (SUF) 1h away.
ischia:en: Ischia, the largest of the three islands of the Gulf of Naples: 46 km², 65,000 inhabitants, still active volcanic island (last eruption 1302), renowned for its hot springs (the Romans were already going there in the 1st century BC). July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 20-24°C, 7% rain, sea at 24°C. August stays excellent (9.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 17-24°C, hotel prices x3-x4, overloaded ferries. From November to March, expect 5-11°C and 29-47% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many hotels close but the main thermal centres stay open (Negombo, Castiglione, Poseidon). Highlights: Castello Aragonese (on an islet linked by a bridge, signature panorama, paid access, 1 km of visit), Mount Epomeo (789m, highest point, 2h round-trip hike, 360° panorama Gulf of Naples-Vesuvius-Capri), Sant'Angelo (pedestrian fishing village at the extreme south, hot beach where you can dig in the sand and find boiling water), Giardini La Mortella (botanical garden of the composer William Walton), Forio (main western town), Lacco Ameno (signature stone "Fungo" mushroom). Famous thermal centres: Negombo (the smartest, Japanese gardens), Castiglione (facing the Castello), Poseidon (the largest, 22 terraced pools at Citara), Aphrodite Apollon at Sant'Angelo. Beaches: Sant'Angelo, Maronti (the longest), Citara (with Poseidon). Try coniglio all'ischitana (Ischia rabbit, traditional dish), bucatini alle olive, white wines Biancolella and Forastera (native varietals). Logistics: Ischia 1h by ferry from Naples (Beverello port), 50 min by hydrofoil.
elba:en: Elba (Elba), the largest island of the Tuscan archipelago in Italy: 32,000 inhabitants over 224 km², 3rd largest Italian island after Sicily and Sardinia. World-famous as the place of exile of Napoleon I (from 4 May 1814 to 26 February 1815, that is 9 months and 22 days, after his forced abdication at Fontainebleau), where he reigned as "emperor of the island of Elba" over 12,000 inhabitants with a personal guard of 1000 soldiers before his spectacular return to France for the Hundred Days. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 22-29°C, 8% rain, sea at 24°C, ideal temperatures. June and September stay excellent (8.6-9.4/10). August drops to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-30°C) and the extreme crowds (Italians on school holidays in the majority, hotel prices x4-x5, congested Piombino-Portoferraio ferries, packed beaches). From November to March, expect 8-17°C and 25-38% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many hotels close, island almost deserted. Highlights: Portoferraio (historic capital, fortified old town Cosmopoli created by Cosimo I de Medici 1548, picturesque port), Villa dei Mulini (Napoleon's official residence in Portoferraio, modest but preserved with period furniture, open for visits), Villa San Martino (Napoleon's summer residence 5 km from Portoferraio in the countryside, with art gallery added by Anatoli Demidoff in the 19th century), Medicean Fortresses Forte Falcone and Forte Stella (panorama over Portoferraio), Mount Capanne (1019m, highest point, "cabinovie" cable car in unusual open individual basket-type cabins, 360° panorama over the Tuscan archipelago + Corsica visible 50 km to the west on clear days), Capoliveri (south-east altitude village, medieval atmosphere), Marciana and Marciana Marina (west coast), Porto Azzurro (picturesque east-coast port with Spanish fortress), Rio Marina (east coast, former capital of iron extraction plundered by Etruscans then Romans then Medici). Beaches: Elba has 150 varied beaches (sand, pebbles, rocks, volcanic black sand) depending on the coast: Cavoli and Fetovaia (sand, turquoise waters south-west coast), Sansone (white pebbles, crystal-clear water north coast), Capo Bianco (unique white pebbles), Biodola (long beach, smart hotels), Lacona (the longest), Innamorata ("lovers' beach"), Padulella, Topinetti. Excursions: Pianosa and Montecristo (preserved islands of the Tuscan archipelago, limited access by booking), Capraia (wild island), Aeolian Islands Sicily 8h by ferry. Try sburrita (Elban cod soup), totani con patate (squid potato), schiaccia briaca (Aleatico Elba DOC wine cake), Aleatico dell'Elba DOCG (signature sweet wine of the island), Ansonica (local white wine). Logistics: Elba accessible only by ferry from Piombino in 1h (Toremar, Moby, Blunavy, 30-50€/person vehicle), no airport for commercial flights (small airstrip for private planes Marina di Campo).
pantelleria:en: Pantelleria, Italian volcanic island between Sicily and Tunisia: 7700 inhabitants over 83 km², closer to Tunisia (60 km) than to Sicily (110 km), considered Italy's most secret island. Nicknamed "the island of water and wind" in Arabic ("bint al-rayh" = daughter of the wind). The Italian elite has its summer residence here (Giorgio Armani, Sting, Madonna have houses here), deliberately preserved atmosphere. June is my favourite month (9.0/10): 21-25°C, 14% rain, sea at 21°C, ideal temperatures. May and September-October stay excellent (8.4-8.8/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-30°C, sirocco Saharan wind sometimes 40°C+). From November to March, expect 9-16°C and 26-37% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, but windy (Pantelleria is one of Italy's windiest islands, Saharan scirocco and north-west mistral), many hotels close out of season. Highlights: Lago di Venere ("Lake of Venus", crater volcanic thermal lake, sulphurous hot springs 35-40°C, therapeutic mud to apply on the body, signature of Pantelleria, free swimming), Specchio di Venere ("Mirror of Venus", another purer Venusian lake), Arco dell'Elefante ("Elephant Arch", rocky formation by the sea in the shape of an elephant drinking from the sea, signature visual of Pantelleria), Bagno Asciutto ("Dry Bath", natural volcanic sauna caves 50°C, exit and wrap in a towel), Cala Levante and Cala Tramontana (coves accessible only on foot, crystal-clear water), Faro Punta Spadillo, Cala Cinque Denti ("5 Teeth", signature rocky formations), Sesi archaeological centre (UNESCO in progress, 7000 prehistoric megalithic burials 1800 BC in volcanic black stone unique in the world), Dammusi ("traditional houses of Pantelleria" = architecture in black volcanic stone with white round domes to collect rainwater, medieval Arab-Berber heritage, architectural signature unique in the world, many converted into smart boutique hotels). Vineyards: Pantelleria is famous for its Passito di Pantelleria DOC (sweet liqueur wine made from Zibibbo Moscato d'Alessandria dried in the sun on basalt slabs, millennial technique, one of Italy's great sweet wines), UNESCO 2014 recognises the "vigne couchée pantesca" = viticulture technique in circular bowls dug into the rock to protect the vines from the Saharan wind, classified as intangible heritage of humanity. Pantelleria IGP Capers = among the best in the world (the other rival being Salina in the Aeolians). Activities: diving (crystal-clear water, ancient underwater archaeology), volcanic hiking (Mount Grande 836m, 360° panorama over Tunisia + Lampedusa visible on clear days), boat around the island (one of the only ways to access the wild coves). Hotels: Resort Sikelia, Tenuta Borgia, Khamma (smart boutique hotels in renovated dammusi, 300-1000€/night). Try couscous pantesca (Arab heritage), pesto pantesco (made from tomatoes and capers, different from Genoese pesto), insalata pantesca (potato tomato caper onion salad), Passito di Pantelleria DOC, Zibibbo wine. Logistics: Pantelleria airport (PNL) with flights from Palermo/Trapani/Rome (1h), ferry from Trapani Sicily in 5h.
pays-basque:en: French Basque Country, region in south-west France on the Spanish border on the Bay of Biscay: 300,000 inhabitants concentrated on the coast (Biarritz, Saint-Jean-de-Luz, Hendaye, Bayonne). The Basque Country is divided between 3 provinces on the French side (Labourd, Basse-Navarre, Soule) and 4 provinces on the Spanish side (Biscay, Guipuzcoa, Alava, Navarre). Strong cultural identity (Basque language euskara, dances, cuisine, red-green-white architecture of traditional etxe houses). August is my favourite month (8.5/10): 16-25°C, 35% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (8.4/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 14-25°C, big crowds (French and Spanish tourists, hotel prices x2-x3). From November to March, expect 4-13°C and 43-53% rain: mild but very rainy (Basque Country = 1500 mm/year, one of the wettest regions of France), unique atmosphere under mist on the Basque cliffs. Highlights coast: Biarritz (smart beach resort since Napoleon III who had Villa Eugénie built here in 1854 for his wife Eugénie, Grande Plage, European capital of surfing, Biarritz Surf Festival, 5* Hôtel du Palais), Saint-Jean-de-Luz (tuna fishing port, old town with Basque half-timbered houses, Place Louis XIV where the king married Marie-Thérèse 1660), Hendaye (extreme south-west of France, 3 km beach, Spanish border via the Bidassoa), Bayonne (Basque cultural capital, fortified Vauban old town, 13th-16th century Gothic cathedral, famous for its chocolate introduced to France by Sephardic Jews fleeing Spain in the 17th century and its Bayonne ham IGP), Anglet between Biarritz and Bayonne. Highlights interior: Espelette (red-white-green village, capital of Espelette pepper AOC since 2000, façades covered in dried peppers in September = signature postcard image), Sare and Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port (stage on the Camino de Santiago, starting point of the Camino Francés), Iraty Forest (the largest beech forest in Western Europe 17,000 hectares, hikes), Rhune mountain (905m, 1924 cog railway from Espelette, panorama over the Atlantic and the Pyrenees). Activities: surf (Biarritz, Anglet, Hossegor 30 min away on the Landes side — European surf capital), Basque pelota (national sport, jai alai at Cesta Punta), Pyrenees hiking, gastronomy. Festivals: Fêtes de Bayonne in July-August (the largest festival in France after the Nice Carnival, 1 million people dressed in white-red in the streets, since 1932 inspired by the Pamplona festivities), Biarritz Festival of surf, Quiksilver Pro France. Try piperade, axoa de veau, ttoro, IGP Bayonne ham, Ossau-Iraty AOC cheese, gâteau basque, Irouléguy AOC red wine, Patxaran (Basque sloe liqueur), txakoli on the Spanish side. Biarritz-Anglet-Bayonne airport (BIQ).
dordogne:en: The Dordogne (department + river) is the heart of the Périgord: 9000 km², 410,000 inhabitants, medieval castles, prehistoric caves, truffle and foie gras gastronomy. July-August are my favourite months (9.2/10): 17-28°C, 22-24% rain, ideal temperatures, golden swans on the rivers. From June to September, it's the high season: 14-28°C, hotel prices x2, country house rentals very sought after (many British). From December to February, expect 3-12°C and 41-43% rain: mild for France, good period for the Périgord black truffle (December-February season, markets at Sarlat/Sorges/Lalbenque). Highlights: Sarlat-la-Canéda (exceptional medieval city, Wednesday/Saturday markets), Vézère Valley (UNESCO, prehistoric sites): Lascaux IV (replica, the original closed since 1963 for preservation), Les Eyzies (National Museum of Prehistory), Font-de-Gaume and Combarelles (rare caves still open with original prehistoric art), Dordogne Valley with its perched villages (Domme, La Roque-Gageac, Beynac, Castelnaud). Castles: Beynac, Castelnaud (medieval rivals), Hautefort, Bourdeilles, Biron. Activities: descent of the Dordogne by canoe (Vitrac to La Roque-Gageac, 12 km, 25-35€/person). Sarlat market Saturday morning. Try foie gras, magret/confit de canard, black truffle, walnuts, strawberries, Bergerac/Monbazillac wine, neighbouring Cahors wine.
alsace:en: Alsace is the most Germanic French region: 600 km² between the Vosges and the Black Forest, 1.9 million inhabitants, vineyards, half-timbered villages, gastronomy of Alemannic tradition. July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 16-26°C, 38% rain, terraces in Strasbourg and Colmar, hikes in the Vosges. August stays excellent (8.6/10) with fewer tourists after the 15th. From November to March, expect 0-12°C and 39-44% rain: dry continental cold, snow in the Vosges (skiing possible at Champ du Feu, La Bresse). But it's the tourist apogee with the Christmas markets (Strasbourg, Colmar, Riquewihr, Kaysersberg) from the last Friday of November to 24 December = among the oldest and most visited in Europe, prices x2-x3, massive crowds. Highlights: Wine Route (170 km from Marlenheim to Thann, 70 villages, Riesling/Gewurztraminer/Pinot Gris), Most Beautiful French classified villages: Eguisheim (origin of the vine), Riquewihr, Hunawihr, Kaysersberg. Strasbourg (UNESCO, see dedicated review), Colmar (Petite Venise, Unterlinden Museum with Issenheim Altarpiece), Mont Sainte-Odile (panorama Alsace plain). Try choucroute (at Gilg in Riquewihr or Le Picotin in Strasbourg), tarte flambée ("flammkueche"), baeckeoffe, kougelhopf, bredele, Alsace wines. May-June and September-October are the best tourism/weather compromises.
antalya:en: Antalya, capital of the Turkish Riviera in south-west Turkey on the Mediterranean: 1.5 million inhabitants, 5th city of the country. Main tourist destination of Turkey (15 million visitors/year, ahead of Istanbul). Former Lycian-Roman-Byzantine-Ottoman port, preserved Kaleiçi fortified old town. June is my favourite month (9.2/10): 20-31°C, 15% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the extreme heat (24-37°C, sometimes 40°C+ with sirocco). May and September-October stay excellent (8.7-8.9/10). From November to March, expect 6-21°C and 17-39% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Kaleiçi (fortified old town labyrinth of Ottoman cobbled alleys, wooden houses converted into boutique hotels, Byzantine-Seljuk-Ottoman architectural blend), Hadrian's Gate (Üçkapılar, marble Roman triumphal arch 130 AD for Emperor Hadrian's visit), Yivli Minaret ("Fluted Minaret" 13th-century Seljuk, signature visual of Antalya), Hıdırlık Tower (Roman tower 2nd century, sunset panorama over the Mediterranean), Antalya Museum (one of the best archaeological museums in Turkey, exceptional Roman sarcophagi), Düden Waterfalls (waterfalls flowing directly into the sea, Lower Düden = unique in the world, view by boat), Konyaaltı Beach (long pebble beach to the west), Lara Beach (sandy beach to the east, 5*+ all-inclusive resort hotels Las Vegas style), Olympos 80 km away (Lycian remains in the jungle, beach), Mount Tahtalı (4350m cable car the longest in the world 2007, 2365m summit, view over the coast). Essential Turkish Riviera excursions: Pamukkale 3h away (UNESCO, signature white limestone terraces, Hierapolis Roman ruins), Cappadocia 8h by bus (UNESCO, fairytale fairy chimney landscapes, hot air balloons), Side 1h away (ancient Greco-Roman remains by the sea), Aspendos 1h away (the best-preserved Roman theatre in the world 2nd century, still used for concerts), Phaselis and Olympos (coastal Lycian cities). Try piyaz (white beans tahini sauce), tandır kebabı (lamb cooked in a jar), şiş kebab, lahmacun (Turkish pizza), baklava, ayran (salty yoghurt), Çay (Turkish tea in tulip glasses). Antalya airport (AYT), one of the busiest in the eastern Mediterranean.
cappadoce:en: Cappadocia, in central Anatolia (Turkey): unique geological landscapes ("fairy chimneys", volcanic tuffs sculpted by erosion), UNESCO troglodyte cities. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 15-30°C, 3% rain, ideal temperatures, clear sky for the hot air balloon flights at sunrise (the signature experience, 150-300€/person). From April to October, it's the high season: 5-31°C, hot air balloon flights workable (but capricious weather, 30-50% of flights cancelled in shoulder season). From December to March, expect -3/12°C with 25-37% precipitation often as snow: Cappadocia under the snow is spectacular (magical landscapes but balloon flights often cancelled, heated hotels but cool), prices divided by 3. Highlights: Göreme Open Air Museum (UNESCO, 9th-12th century Byzantine rock-hewn monasteries and churches with frescoes), Hiking valleys: Love Valley (phallic shapes), Red Valley, Pink Valley (at sunset), Ihlara Valley (14 km gorge), Underground cities of Derinkuyu (8 levels, up to 20,000 people could shelter here) or Kaymakli, Uçhisar (rocky fortress, panoramic view), Avanos (pottery on the Halys river), Pasabag (fairy chimneys with hats). Logistics: Istanbul-Kayseri flight (1h15) + 1h transfer to Göreme/Ürgüp. Unique troglodyte hotels (Museum Hotel, Sultan Cave Suites). 3-4 days minimum to fully enjoy. Try testi kebab (cooked in a clay jug), gözleme.
bodrum:en: Bodrum, smart beach resort in south-west Turkey on the Aegean Sea: 175,000 inhabitants. Former Halicarnassus = birthplace of Herodotus ("father of History", 484 BC) and site of the Mausoleum of Halicarnassus = one of the 7 Wonders of the ancient world (tomb of Mausolus king of Caria 350 BC, destroyed by earthquakes in the Middle Ages, visitable remains). Nicknamed "Turkish Saint-Tropez" for its smart-bohemian blend. May is my favourite month (9.1/10): 17-25°C, 15% rain, sea at 19°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. June-July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-34°C) and the extreme crowds (Russians, Israelis, Europeans, Istanbulites on yachts in the majority, hotel prices x4-x5). September-October stay excellent (6.9-8.7/10). From November to March, expect 9-20°C and 13-44% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, calm, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Saint Peter's Castle (Bodrum Castle, 15th century built by the Knights Hospitaller of Rhodes with stones recovered from the Mausoleum, houses the Museum of Underwater Archaeology = one of the best in the world, ancient wrecks: Uluburun 14th century BC, Yassıada 7th century), Mausoleum of Halicarnassus (foundation remains, explanatory panels, memorial atmosphere more than spectacular as destroyed), Ancient theatre (4th century BC, panoramic view over the bay), Bodrum Marina (port with luxury yachts, seaside restaurants), Bodrum Bazaar (Çarşı, oriental atmosphere), Bardakçı Beach (close to centre), Yahşi Beach, Gümbet (party tourist neighbouring resort), Türkbükü ("Saint-Tropez of Bodrum", smart, no beach but pontoons on the water, seaside restaurants), Yalıkavak (smart marina port, 5*+ hotels, luxury boutiques), Gümüşlük (picturesque western village, feet in the water at sunset), Bitez (windsurf), Ortakent. Excursions: Kos 1h by ferry (Greek island already covered by BDW), Pamukkale 3h away, Cleopatra Island (white sand unique in the world brought from Egypt by Mark Antony for Cleopatra according to legend). Try Turkish meze, balık ekmek (fish sandwich), kebab, raki (Turkish anisette), Turkish coffee. Bodrum-Milas airport (BJV).
fethiye:en: Fethiye, beach resort on the turquoise coast in south-west Turkey: 90,000 inhabitants. Former Lycian pirate nomads, covers the ancient Telmessos destroyed by an earthquake in 1957 then rebuilt. Renowned for its spectacular bay, the Ölüdeniz Lagoon ("Dead Sea" in Turkish, signature turquoise lagoon surrounded by pine forests, one of the most famous postcard images of the Mediterranean), and paragliding from Mount Babadağ (1969m, one of the most beautiful paragliding spots in the world). May is my favourite month (9.1/10): 15-26°C, 23% rain, sea at 20°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. June-July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (20-35°C). October stays excellent (8.8/10). From November to April, expect 7-22°C and 16-42% rain: mild for the Mediterranean. Highlights: Ölüdeniz Lagoon 14 km away ("Blue Lagoon", national park protected since 1986, unique turquoise water thanks to salinity blend, free public beach + private Belcekiz beach, limited access), Mount Babadağ (4900m cable car + 1700m zipline towards the summit 1969m, tandem paragliding with a pilot = the signature experience of Fethiye, 1500-2000 TL ≈ 80-100€ for 30 min flight with landing on Belcekiz beach, one of the spots ranked among the global top 5, optimal conditions May-October), Lycian rock-cut tombs (carved into the cliffs above Fethiye, 4th century BC, signature of the Lycian civilisation, free access), Butterfly Valley (Kelebekler Vadisi, accessible only by boat or extreme hike, beach with Jersey Tiger butterflies in June-September), Kayaköy ghost village 8 km away (former Greek village of Levissi abandoned in 1923 during the Greco-Turkish population exchange, 500 empty stone houses, memorial atmosphere, inspiration for Louis de Bernières' novel Birds Without Wings 2004), 12 Islands tour (1-day boat excursion in Fethiye Bay, snorkelling, swimming), Saklıkent Gorge 50 km away (18 km gorges, hike with feet in 8°C icy water even in summer), Tlos and Pinara Lycian ruins. Lycian Way ("Lycian Way", one of Europe's great long-distance trails, 540 km Fethiye-Antalya, ranked top 10 globally by the Sunday Times). Try dürüm wrap, balık ekmek (fish sandwich), gözleme (traditional pancake), şiş kebab, raki. Dalaman airport (DLM) 50 min away.
varanasi:en: Varanasi (Benares, Kashi) is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world (3000 years), holy Hindu city on the Ganges. February is my favourite month (8.8/10): 14-26°C, 6% rain, ideal temperatures. From November to March, it's the high season: 10-31°C, morning mist on the Ganges (atmospheric for the ghats). From April to June, it's the extreme heatwave: 24-39°C (sometimes 47°C), impossible. From July to September, it's the monsoon: 77-84% rainfall, the Ganges overflows, ghats flooded, navigation difficult. Highlights: Ghats on the Ganges (88 ghats over 7 km, stairs descending to the river): Dashashwamedh Ghat (the main one, Ganga Aarti ceremony every evening at dusk, 45 min very spiritual), Manikarnika Ghat (main cremation ghat, funeral pyres in continuity, photos forbidden), Assi Ghat (south, calmer). Boat trip at dawn (5am) on the Ganges = essential experience, see Hindus bathing and saying their prayers. Old town labyrinth of alleys around Vishwanath Temple. Sarnath 10 km away (where Buddha gave his first sermon, ruins, museum). Logistics: Varanasi is intense, dirty, chaotic — extreme cultural shock acknowledged. Hotels with Ganges view (Ganges View, BrijRama Palace). Try kachori sabzi, lassi at Blue Lassi, paan. Direct flight from Delhi (1h30).
udaipur:en: Udaipur is the "Venice of Rajasthan": romantic city surrounded by 7 lakes, the most love-laden and elegant of Rajasthan. February is my favourite month (9.4/10): 13-27°C, 1% rain, ideal temperatures, golden light on Lake Pichola. From November to March, expect 10-31°C: tourist high season, optimal climate. From April to June, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 22-37°C (sometimes 42°C), bearable thanks to altitude (598m) but punishing. From July to September, it's the monsoon: 47-72% rainfall, but green landscapes (rare for Rajasthan), full lakes. Highlights: City Palace (the largest palace in Rajasthan, 11-palace complex over 244m long, Mewar dynasty continuous since 728), Lake Pichola (sunset cruise), Lake Palace (white palace floating in the middle of the lake, Taj 5*+ hotel, non-guest access impossible), Jagmandir Island (another island-palace, accessible by boat), Saheliyon-ki-Bari (gardens of the maids of honour), Monsoon Palace (on the Sajjangarh hill, panoramic view at sunset). Eklingji and Nagda Temples 22 km away (millennial). Try dal baati churma, gatte ki sabzi, malpua. Octopussy from James Bond (1983) shot here (Lake Palace in the film). Direct flight from Delhi (1h15) or overnight train from Delhi/Mumbai. October-November and March-April are the real sweet spots tourism/weather.
kochi:en: Kochi (Cochin), port city of the State of Kerala in south-west India on the Malabar coast: 600,000 inhabitants. 1500-year port history trading with Romans, Arabs, Chinese, then successively colonised by Portuguese (1500-1663, Vasco da Gama died here in 1524 and was buried here until transfer to Portugal), Dutch (1663-1795), British (1795-1947). 2000-year Jewish community (Cochin Jews). January-February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 24-31°C, 18-23% rain, this is the ideal dry season. Kochi never tops 6.9/10 (very humid equatorial tropical climate year-round). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 24-31°C, sea at 28°C. From June to October, it's the south-west monsoon: 85-100% rainfall (Kerala is the 1st region to receive the monsoon in India, June = nearly permanent torrential monsoon), many hotels stay open but limited excursions. Highlights: Fort Kochi (historic peninsula, preserved colonial atmosphere blend Portuguese-Dutch-British, ideal for walking/cycling), Chinese fishing nets ("Chinese Fishing Nets" Cheena Vala, cantilever wooden structures brought by Zheng He in the 14th century, still active today, signature visual of Kochi at sunset over the bay), St Francis Church (1503, the oldest European church in India, original burial place of Vasco da Gama 1524), Santa Cruz Basilica (16th-19th century Catholic cathedral), Mattancherry Palace ("Dutch Palace" 16th-century Portuguese expanded by the Dutch, 17th-century Hindu mural frescoes), Paradesi Synagogue at Mattancherry (1568, the oldest active synagogue in the Commonwealth, Cochin Jews community today reduced to 5 people after emigration to Israel in 1950), Jew Town (antiques street unique atmosphere), Kashi Art Café (essential café-gallery Princess Street, art-literature scene of Kochi). Essential excursions: Kerala Backwaters at Alleppey 1h30 by train ("Venice of India", 900 km of navigable canals, 1-2 day cruise on a traditional houseboat "kettuvallam" converted = signature experience of Kerala, 100-300€/night for 2 people all included), Munnar 4h by bus (Western Ghats mountains, 1500-2700m tea plantations, unique hill landscapes), Periyar National Park (elephant reserve, rare tigers), Marari Beach (quiet beach 1h away). Major festival: Kochi-Muziris Biennale (the largest contemporary art biennial in Asia since 2012, December-March, multiple sites). Try meen moilee (coconut fish curry), karimeen pollichathu (banana-leaf grilled fish), appam (fermented rice pancake), puttu, masala dosa, kerala thali, local Robusta-Arabica coffee. Logistics: Cochin International airport (COK) 30 min away, direct flights from Dubai/Doha/Singapore.
alpe-dhuez:en: Alpe d'Huez, at 1860m altitude in Isère, is the sunny resort of the French Alps: 300 days/year of sunshine claimed. The Sarenne (16 km, 2000m vertical) remains one of the longest black pistes in Europe.
