July-August are my favourite months (7.8-7.9/10): 8-19°C, 35-38% rain, famous Tour de France stage (the 21 hairpin bends). From December to April, expect -8/3°C: ski season, reliable snow despite the south-west orientation thanks to altitude. The Sarenne glacier offers summer skiing some years. From November and May, it's the shoulder season. 60s-70s architecture criticised (concrete buildings), but the ski-in-ski-out advantage stays real. Grenoble 1h30 away, TGV station 2h away. The resort is part of the Grand Domaine de l'Alpe d'Huez with Auris, Vaujany, Oz-en-Oisans (250 km of pistes total).
la-plagne:en: La Plagne, at 2100m altitude, is part of Paradiski with Les Arcs: 425 km of pistes, Bellecôte glacier at 3250m, 1600m skiable vertical drop. One of the largest French resorts in terms of accommodation capacity. July-August are my favourite months (7.4-7.5/10): 7-17°C, 38-47% rain, good for hiking and water sports at Lac de Plagne. From December to April, expect -11/3°C: major ski season, snow guaranteed. The Olympic bobsleigh track (Albertville Olympics 1992) stays open to the public in winter. The resort gathers 11 villages (Plagne Centre, Plagne Bellecôte, Plagne Soleil, Belle Plagne, Aime 2000, Plagne Villages, etc.) of varied design. From October to November and May, it's the slow shoulder season. Aime TGV station 30 min away by shuttle, Eurostar Ski from London in winter.
les-deux-alpes:en: Les Deux Alpes, in the Oisans: 1650m, village resort built in the 1950s (characteristic 50s-60s architecture, no traditional chalets), connected by cable car to the 3200m glacier (the largest skiable summer glacier in Europe). July is my favourite month (7.8/10): 9-19°C, 38% rain, summer skiing on the glacier (the resort opens from late June to late August for summer skiing and snowboarding, one of the only ones in the world). From December to April, expect -9/10°C and 38-65% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 220 km Les Deux Alpes (97 pistes, 49 lifts), connected to Alpe d'Huez by cable car only (not by piste), record vertical drop 2300m (the largest in Europe between 1300m and 3600m Mont-de-Lans glacier). Legendary pistes: glacier descent (16 km from 3600m), Sarenne (one of the longest in Europe at 16 km, at neighbouring Alpe d'Huez). Off-piste: Vallée Blanche (not to be confused with Chamonix's), Combe de la Selle. Audience: freeriders and freestylers (European reference snowpark in summer), many Dutch and British, young people (lively nightlife). Hotels: Côte Brune, Edelweiss, Souleil'Or, 150-600€/night. La Grave 30 min away (the legendary off-piste resort, 1 single non-groomed cable car up to 3550m, world freeride paradise without marked pistes, high danger). Try tartiflette, fondue, Diois wine. Logistics: Les Deux Alpes 1h30 from Grenoble by car, hairpin road up to 1650m.
les-menuires:en: Les Menuires, in the Three Valleys: 1850m, village resort built in the 60s-70s (concrete "ski machine" architecture), often considered "the cheapest of the 3 Valleys" against Courchevel/Méribel/Val Thorens, but with access to the largest ski area in the world (600 km). July-August are my favourite months (7.9/10): 7-20°C, 37-38% rain in storms, alpine hiking, mountain bike, but Les Menuires is above all a winter resort (many hotels and restaurants closed in summer). From December to April, expect -10/6°C and 36-58% precipitation often as snow: ski high season, one of the snowiest resorts in the French Alps thanks to altitude. 3 Valleys pistes: 600 km total, direct access from Les Menuires to Val Thorens (10 min by chairlift) and Méribel (20 min by cable car). La Masse = local peak (2804m, Mont Blanc panorama). Audience: well-off French and Dutch families, snowboarders (Béranger Snowpark), contrast with festive Val Thorens and smart Méribel. Architecture: resort renovated since the 2000s with the Reberty 1850 chalet-architecture quarter (smarter), while the central La Croisette stays 70s. Hotels: Le Kaya (4*), L'Ours Blanc, Les Loges Vanoise (4*), 150-700€/night. Try tartiflette, savoyarde fondue, raclette, beaufort. Logistics: Les Menuires 2h15 from Lyon by car, Paris-Moûtiers train + 50-min bus.
flaine:en: Flaine, in the Grand Massif: 1600m, altitude resort (the highest in Grand Massif), Brutalist architecture by Marcel Breuer (Bauhaus architect, student of Mies van der Rohe), opened in 1969 = subject of architectural polemic (raw concrete in the heart of the Alps, considered the ugliest or the most visionary depending on opinion). August is my favourite month (8.2/10): 11-22°C, 39% rain in storms, alpine hiking. From June to September, expect 4-22°C: short summer season. From December to March, expect -6/5°C and 41-48% precipitation often as snow: ski high season, very good snow cover thanks to altitude. Pistes: 265 km Grand Massif (Flaine + Samoëns + Morillon + Sixt + Les Carroz, 1 single ski pass), altitude 700-2500m, vertical drop 1800m. Signature pistes: Cascades (one of the longest green pistes in the world, 14 km from Tête des Lindars 2480m down to Sixt 770m, accessible to beginners!), Combe de Gers (shaded north face, preserved snow). Off-piste: Combe du Fer-à-Cheval, Aiguille du Plan Praz. Audience: families, school groups (Flaine welcomes many snow-class school groups thanks to UCPA residences), functional rather than smart atmosphere. Hotels: Totem (signature Breuer architecture, Hotel Le Refuge du Golf, Hotel Aujon), 100-400€/night. Sculptures: Picasso ("Tête de femme"), Vasarely, Dubuffet exhibited outdoors in the resort. Try tartiflette, fondue. Logistics: Flaine 1h15 from Geneva (mandatory parking at the foot of the resort, pedestrian access only for cars), Magny-Cluses-Flaine shuttles from the TGV station.
serre-chevalier:en: Serre Chevalier (1200-2800m) is the largest area in the Southern Alps: 250 km of pistes, 4 villages (Briançon, Chantemerle, Villeneuve, Le Monêtier-les-Bains). Sunnier climate than the Northern Alps (Briançon claims 300 days/year of sunshine). July-August are my favourite months (8.5/10): 9-22°C, 27-35% rain, good for hiking towards the Pic de Rochebrune or the Serre-Chevalier lakes. From December to March, expect -9/3°C: ski season. More stable climate than in the Northern Alps (fewer Atlantic fronts). Briançon (UNESCO Vauban) remains the cultural base, the highest town in Europe at 1326m. The Grands Bains du Monêtier (sulphurous thermal waters) after a day of skiing. Marseille 4h by car, Turin 2h via the Montgenèvre pass. Oulx TGV station (Italy) optional for travellers from Paris-Lyon.
les-gets:en: Les Gets, in the Portes du Soleil: 1300 inhabitants at 1170m, village resort neighbouring Morzine connected by lifts (Mont Chéry and Chavannes-Ranfolly), village with preserved Savoyard charm. August is my favourite month (8.0/10): 12-22°C, 44% rain in storms, hiking and mountain bike (Portes du Soleil = European mountain-bike capital). From June to September, expect 4-22°C: active summer season, 18-hole golf, Lac aux Écoles. From December to March, expect -5/4°C and 43-48% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 600 km Portes du Soleil (same as Morzine, see above), 145 pistes total, 197 lifts, vertical drop 2300m. Les Gets has its own side (Mont Chéry less frequented, shaded, ideal for beginners in early afternoon) and accesses the 11 other resorts via the Chavannes-Ranfolly. Audience: French and British families (the British are very numerous in the Portes du Soleil, PCC school), calmer atmosphere than Morzine, smarter than Châtel. Hotels: The Alpina Eclectic Hotel (4*+), Crychar (4*), Marmotte, 150-700€/night. Mechanical Music Museum (unique collection of mechanical pianos and 18th-19th century barrel organs). Try tartiflette, fondue, croziflette, diots, Savoy wine. Logistics: Les Gets 1h15 from Geneva by car, accessible by shuttle from the Geneva TGV station, located 5 min by car from Morzine.
montgenevre:en: Montgenèvre, Franco-Italian ski resort at the Montgenèvre pass (1860m), gives access to the Voie Lactée area: 400 km of pistes linked with Sestrière, Sansicario and Claviere on the Italian side. July is my favourite month (8.9/10): 9-22°C, 19% rain, good for hiking, mountain bike, golf. The resort is part of the Briançonnais: Briançon (UNESCO Vauban, the highest town in Europe at 1326m) 12 km away. From December to March, expect -10/3°C: ski season, reliable snow thanks to altitude and north orientation, possibility of skiing in France and lunching in Italy. From October to November and April-May, it's the shoulder season: half-closed resort, few services. Rather dry continental climate (less fresh snow than the North of the Alps, but more stable conditions). Access from Turin (1h30), Briançon (15 min), Grenoble (3h). More accessible than neighbouring Serre-Chevalier, ideal for families and beginners. Oulx Italo TGV station (45 min) for those coming from Paris-Lyon.
toulouse:en: Toulouse, France's 4th city: 500,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 1.5 million in the urban area, capital of Occitanie. Nicknamed the "Pink City" for its characteristic foraine pink bricks. European capital of aeronautics (Airbus world headquarters in Blagnac, ATR, Thales, the aerospace industry employs 130,000 people in the region). Student city (130,000 students out of 1.5 million inhabitants). July is my favourite month (9.3/10): 18-29°C, 23% rain, ideal temperatures. August stays excellent (9.1/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-29°C. From November to March, expect 3-15°C and 30-45% rain: mild for France, more humid climate than Montpellier but milder than Paris. Highlights: Place du Capitole (heart of the city, 18th-century neo-classical Capitole de Toulouse town hall with its signature pink-white-blue façade 128m long, paintings by Henri Martin), Saint-Sernin Basilica (UNESCO 1998, 11th-12th century Romanesque, one of the largest Romanesque churches in the world, major stage on the Camino de Santiago, signature octagonal bell tower over 5 levels), Convent of the Jacobins (13th-14th century southern Gothic, houses the tomb of Saint Thomas Aquinas died 1274, signature palm-tree vaults), Pont Neuf over the Garonne (16th-17th century, paradoxically the oldest current bridge in Toulouse), Hôtel d'Assézat (16th-century Renaissance, one of the most beautiful private mansions in France, fortune from pastel = blue dye that made the Toulouse riches in the 16th century), Cité de l'Espace 6 km away (space theme park with real-size Ariane 5 53m, Mir, Soyuz, 100m² planetarium, since 1997), Aéroscopia at Blagnac (aeronautical museum, Concorde, Airbus A300, Caravelle, Super Guppy), Airbus visit (A380 then now A350 assembly chain, by Airbus Visit booking), Musée des Augustins (Fine Arts museum in former 14th-century convent), Saint-Cyprien quarter (former working-class quarter on the left bank, today trendy). Excursions: Carcassonne 1h away (UNESCO, fortified medieval city), Albi 1h away (UNESCO, pink-brick Gothic cathedral, Musée Toulouse-Lautrec birthplace), Cordes-sur-Ciel 1h30 away ("most beautiful village in France" 2014), Pyrenees 1h30 away (Cauterets, Saint-Lary), Andorra 3h away, Lourdes 2h away, Auch Gers (capital of Armagnac foie gras). Major festival: Marathon des Mots in June, Toulouse d'Été in July-August. Try cassoulet (THE Toulouse dish, strict recipe with Tarbais beans, duck confit, Toulouse sausage, and depending on the school added lamb), Toulouse sausage (IGP in progress), Sud-Ouest foie gras, magret de canard, AOC Gaillac, AOC Madiran, AOC Fronton wines, Armagnac (eau-de-vie). Toulouse-Blagnac airport (TLS).
ischgl:en: Ischgl, Austrian ski resort in the Paznaun Valley (Tyrol): 1600 inhabitants at 1377m, ultra-festive resort nicknamed "the Ibiza of the Alps" (sadly famous as the European epicentre of Covid-19 in March 2020 = thousands of European skiers infected). Linked to Samnaun in Switzerland by lifts (Silvretta Arena = cross-border skiing without customs formalities, duty-free shopping in Switzerland). August is my favourite month (7.7/10): 10-22°C, 58% rain in altitude storms, Paznaun hiking, mountain bike. From June to September, expect 4-22°C: calm summer season. From December to April, expect -8/6°C and 42-64% precipitation often as snow: ski high season, one of the snowiest in Austria. Pistes: 239 km Silvretta Arena (Ischgl + Samnaun, 1 single ski pass), altitude 1377-2872m, vertical drop 1500m, 45 modern lifts (Ischgl is known for its heated and hooded chairlifts, one of the most modern lift parks in Europe). Idalp = central plateau at 2300m, direct access by cable car from the village (15 min). Top of the Mountain Concert = season opening/closing late November/early May with a free open-air major concert by international artists (Mariah Carey, Kylie Minogue, Bon Jovi, Robbie Williams, Elton John, James Blunt have played). Après-ski: Pacha, Trofana Alm, Niki's Stadl, Schatzi Bar = Austrian party institutions (atmosphere until 22-23h, prices x3 vs elsewhere). Audience: Germans and British in the majority, young 25-45, more party than sport. Hotels: Trofana Royal (5*+), Hotel Madlein (5*), Yscla, 200-1500€/night. Try Kasspatzln, Tiroler Gröstl, Glühwein, Stroh 80. Logistics: Ischgl 1h30 from Innsbruck by car/bus.
saalbach:en: Saalbach, Austrian ski resort in the Salzburg Land (Pinzgau region): 3200 inhabitants at 1000m altitude in the heart of the Skicircus Saalbach Hinterglemm Leogang Fieberbrunn area = 270 km of pistes linked on a single ski pass (one of the largest in Austria). Nicknamed "Home of Lässig" ("home of the cool") for its festive and welcoming atmosphere. August is my favourite month (7.8/10): 12-23°C, 53% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking. June-July stay excellent (7.6/10). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 5-23°C, Salzburger Alpen hiking, mountain bike (Saalbach = Austrian mountain-bike capital with 200 km of trails, World Cup VTT Descente every year), golf, paragliding. From December to April, expect -6/4°C and 42-65% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 270 km Skicircus (Saalbach + Hinterglemm + Leogang + Fieberbrunn = 4 connected resorts, 70 lifts, 3rd largest connected area in Austria after Ski Amadé and Skiwelt Wilder Kaiser-Brixental), altitude 800-2096m, vertical drop 1300m, 270 km of which 105 km blue (family), 130 km red (intermediate), 35 km black (expert). Signature pistes: Schattberg West (6 km descent, one of the longest), Zwölferkogel (the World Cup "Trophy" descent sometimes used for the Alpine Ski World Cup slalom), Wildalmgipfel (panorama 2096m summit of the area). Off-piste: Saalbach has no world-renowned off-piste (unlike Engelberg or Verbier), but offers free riding accessible to intermediates between Schattberg and Limbergalm. Snowpark Nightpark Hinterglemm = one of the best in Austria, illuminated for night skiing. Legendary après-ski: Saalbach rivals Ischgl and Mayrhofen for the title of après-ski capital of Austria: Hinterhag Alm (institution), Goaßstall (on the pistes), Bauer's Schi Alm, King Bar, Bla Bla Bar, festive atmosphere until 22-23h in the Almen (altitude bars) then transfers to the village bars (Hannerl Hütte, Le Petit Sept, Ortbar) until 4-5am. Audience: Germans in the majority (60% of winter clientele), many Dutch, British, Hungarians, Czechs, few French. Hotels: Hotel Alpin Juwel (4*+), Hotel Bauer, Wellness-Resort Romantikhotel Die Hubertus, 150-700€/night in high season. Try Tiroler Gröstl, Käsespätzle, Kaiserschmarrn (the Saalbach version is served at Bauer's Schi Alm), Apfelstrudel, Glühwein, Stroh 80 (strong Austrian eau-de-vie). Logistics: Saalbach 1h30 from Salzburg airport (SZG) by car/bus, 2h30 from Munich airport (MUC), Salzburg-Saalfelden train + 30-min bus.
lech:en: Lech am Arlberg, ultra-smart Austrian ski resort: 1500 inhabitants at 1450m, skier quotas limited to 14,000 per day (one of the only resorts in the world with a voluntary quota to preserve the experience). Linked to Zürs-St. Anton by Ski Arlberg (305 km). July is my favourite month (5.3/10): 9-19°C, 65% rain in altitude storms. Lech remains above all a winter resort. From December to April, expect -8/5°C and 42-68% precipitation often as snow: ski high season, one of the snowiest in Europe (10m/year, Arlberg = the "cradle of snow"). Pistes: 305 km Ski Arlberg with Zürs-St. Anton-Stuben, 87 lifts. White Ring = 22 km loop circuit to ski (Lech-Zürs-Zug-Lech), world record of skiers on annual circuit. Off-piste: Mehlsack, Madloch, Roggspitze (with guide). Legendary pistes: Schindlergrat, Auenfeldjet (cable car between Lech and Warth for 340 km of pistes total). Audience: ultra-smart (Hotels Aurelio, Almhof Schneider, Krone, 5*+, 500-3000€/night), European and Eastern aristocracy, well-off Dutch and Belgians. The Dutch royal family has been coming for decades (Princess Friso fatal accident in 2012). Wellness: all the 5* have their signature spas (expensive = 100€+/session). Try Käsespätzle, Wiener Schnitzel. Logistics: Lech 2h from Zurich, 1h30 from Innsbruck.
hintertux:en: Hintertux, Austrian ski resort on the Hintertux Glacier in the Zillertal in Tyrol: 700 inhabitants at 1500-3250m altitude. The only ski area in Austria open 365 days a year thanks to the 3250m glacier, one of the rare European resorts allowing summer skiing in June-August. June is my favourite month (4.5/10): 6-15°C, 49% rain, ideal temperatures for summer skiing on the glacier. Hintertux never tops 4.5/10 (alpine glacial climate with limited season at lower altitude). From December to April, it's ski high season: -10/-2°C, snow guaranteed. From May to October, summer skiing only on the 3000m+ glacier (lower pistes closed), alpine hikes downhill. Pistes: 86 km Hintertux Glacier domain, altitude 1500-3250m, vertical drop 1750m (one of the largest in Europe), 22 lifts. Signature pistes: Olperer (black glacial descent), Gefrorene Wand ("Frozen Wall", red piste at 3250m, arrival point of the Gletscherbus 3 cable car), Schwarze Pfanne (12 km glacial descent, one of the longest in Austria). Off-piste: Hintertux is one of the most respected off-piste bases in Austria thanks to the glacier which guarantees exceptional powder even in April-May. Snowpark Hintertux Pleisten = renowned freestyle. Cable cars: successive Gletscherbus 1-2-3 from 1500m to Gefrorene Wand 3250m in 25 min, one of the fastest alpine glacier systems. Sommerbergalm (intermediate station 2100m with panoramic restaurants), Gletscher-Spalten ("glacier crevasses" in organised guided visit). Summer activities: skiing and snowboarding on the glacier (unique summer season in Europe), via ferrata, 5-lake Schlegeisspeicher hike, mountain bike, paragliding. Audience: Germans in the majority (Munich 2h by car), Austrians, Dutch, many British, national ski teams that train in summer (Lindsey Vonn, Marcel Hirscher came to Hintertux for pre-season preparation). Hotels: Hotel Hohenhaus (4*+), Hotel Neuhintertux, prices 150-450€/night in ski high season. Excursions: Mayrhofen 30 min away (Zillertal valley capital, festive après-ski), Innsbruck 1h away, Salzburg 2h away. Try Tiroler Gröstl, Kaiserschmarrn, Tyrolean IGP Speck, local plum Schnaps, glühwein.
mayrhofen:en: Mayrhofen, Austrian ski resort in the Zillertal (Tyrol): 4000 inhabitants at 630m (low altitude for the village, but access to the Hintertux glacier 30 min by bus), traditional Tyrolean village with flowery balconies in summer and Stuben in winter. August is my favourite month (8.0/10): 14-25°C, 56% rain in altitude storms, Zillertal Alps hiking, mountain bike. From June to September, expect 7-25°C: very active summer season (Zillertal = Austrian mountain-bike capital, Bike-Up festival mid-September). From December to April, expect -4/14°C and 43-67% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 142 km Mayrhofen-Hippach (4th largest area in Tyrol), 60 pistes, 57 lifts, altitude 630-2500m. Legendary pistes: Harakiri (78% gradient, the steepest groomed piste in Austria, one of the steepest in the world, at Penken). Hintertux Glacier 30 min by bus (Tuxer Ferner, open year-round 365 days/365, one of the only European glaciers skiable in summer). Snowboarding: Mayrhofen is one of the European reference resorts for freestyle (Penken Snowpark). Après-ski: Mo's, Brücke, Ice Bar = Austrian festive institutions. Audience: very many British, Dutch, Germans, huge night parties (Mayrhofen rivals Ischgl for after-ski). Hotels: Hotel Edenlehen, Elisabethhotel (4*+), Hotel Strass, 100-500€/night. Try Tiroler Gröstl, Käsespätzle, Kaiserschmarrn, Apfelstrudel, Stroh 80° schnaps. Logistics: Mayrhofen 1h30 from Innsbruck by car, direct train from Innsbruck via Jenbach + Zillertalbahn.
zell-am-see:en: Zell am See, Austrian resort in SalzburgerLand: 10,000 inhabitants at 757m on the edge of the Zeller See (4.5 km² lake, swimmable at 22°C in July-August), at the foot of the Schmittenhöhe (1965m). Unique combination of lake + skiing. August is my favourite month (8.1/10): 14-25°C, 50% rain, swimming in the lake, terraces, boat cruises, paragliding from Schmittenhöhe (landing near the lake). From June to September, it's the summer tourist high season: 7-25°C, pleasant climate, many Middle Eastern tourists (Saudis, Emiratis fleeing the Dubai-Riyadh heatwave for the alpine cool). From December to April, expect -4/14°C and 40-51% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 408 km Ski Alpin Card (Schmittenhöhe + Kitzsteinhorn glacier of neighbouring Kaprun + Saalbach-Hinterglemm + Zell am See-Kaprun, 1 single ski pass), 138 lifts, altitude 757-3029m (Kitzsteinhorn glacier, near year-round skiing). Schmittenhöhe has its own area (77 km), but the major interest is access to the Kitzsteinhorn glacier (2 valleys further, free 30-min shuttle) which guarantees snow throughout the season. Audience: European families in winter, Gulf tourists in summer, also many British. Hotels: Grand Hotel Zell am See (4*+), Salzburgerhof (5*), Mavida, 150-1000€/night. Try Kaiserschmarrn, Wiener Schnitzel, Salzburger Nockerl. Logistics: Zell am See 1h15 from Salzburg by car/train.
schladming:en: Schladming, Austrian ski resort in the Land of Styria: 7000 inhabitants at 745m, town-resort that hosted the 2013 Alpine Ski World Championships. More traditional and less festive than the Tyrolean resorts (Mayrhofen, Ischgl, St. Anton). August is my favourite month (7.8/10): 14-24°C, 60% rain in altitude storms, hiking in the Dachstein (2995m, highest point of Styria). From June to September, expect 6-24°C: active summer season. From December to April, expect -4/13°C and 42-69% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 230 km Ski Amadé (Schladming-Dachstein part of Ski Amadé, the largest area in Austria: 760 km of pistes spread over 5 regions Schladming + Salzburger Sportwelt + Hochkönig + Gastein + Großarltal, 1 single ski pass valid throughout). Schladming-Dachstein 4-Mountain Skipass: 4 connected mountains (Hauser Kaibling, Planai, Hochwurzen, Reiteralm) for 123 km of its own. Planai = signature summit with World Cup nocturnal descent Nightrace (night slalom late January, one of the most-watched races in the world, 50,000 spectators in the illuminated stadium). Dachstein Glacier (2700m, summer and autumn skiing, year-round ski touring). Audience: Germans in the majority, Dutch, Austrian families, calmer atmosphere than Tyrol. Hotels: Falkensteiner Hotel Schladming (4*+), Hotel Stadttor, 100-400€/night. Try Schweinsbraten (pork roast), Steirisches Kürbiskernöl (Styrian pumpkin seed oil, AOC), Apfelstrudel. Logistics: Schladming 1h15 from Salzburg by car/train, 2h30 from Vienna.
yellowstone:en: Yellowstone, the 1st national park in the world (created 1872): 9000 km² in Wyoming-Montana-Idaho (USA), sitting on a dormant super-volcano, unique geothermal landscapes (geysers, coloured hot springs, fumaroles). July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 8-22°C, 28% rain, this is the tourist high season (4 million visitors/year, 70% in June-August), all interior roads open, visible wildlife (bison, elk, bears, wolves). August stays excellent (8.3/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 3-22°C, but also extreme crowds at the main sites (Old Faithful overrun, 1-2h traffic jams to see the bears), hotel booking 1 year ahead for the historic lodges (Old Faithful Inn, Lake Yellowstone Hotel). From November to March, expect -12/-1°C and 37-50% precipitation often as snow: Yellowstone in winter is spectacular (everything white, very few tourists, wildlife visible more easily, sky often clear), but interior roads closed except Mammoth-Cooke City accessible year-round + snowcoach or snowmobile tour mandatory to explore. From April to May and September-October are the transitions (late spring, short autumn). Highlights: Old Faithful (the most famous geyser, eruption every 90 min ±10 min, 30-55m high), Grand Prismatic Spring (110m diameter hot spring, spectacular colours, viewpoint Fairy Falls Trail 800m walk), Mammoth Hot Springs (white limestone terraces), Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone (Lower Falls 94m), Lamar Valley ("American Serengeti" for wildlife, sunrise or sunset), Hayden Valley (bison), Lake Yellowstone (the largest alpine lake in the USA). Safety: numerous black bears and grizzlies (bear spray mandatory for back-country hikes), aggressive bison (stay 25m away). Try bison burger. Logistics: nearest airport Bozeman (Montana, 90 min from West Entrance) or Jackson Hole (Wyoming, 1h from South Entrance).
key-west:en: Key West, the southern tip of the Florida Keys: 25,000 inhabitants 150 km from Cuba (the southernmost "continental USA", Mile 0 marker on US-1). Bohemian island, Caribbean atmosphere, Hemingway heritage. April is my favourite month (9.0/10): 24-26°C, 23% rain, sea at 26°C, end of the American tourist season. From December to April, it's the high season: 20-26°C, 14-25% rain, hotel prices x2-x4, American Spring Break in March (unleashed student crowds). From June to October, it's the cyclone season: 53-65% rainfall, hurricane risk (Irma 2017 category 4 hit the Keys). From May and November are the best shoulder seasons. Highlights: Mallory Square at sunset ("Sunset Celebration" daily ritual since the 60s, jugglers, magicians, applause when the sun sets in the gulf), Hemingway House (the writer lived here 1931-1939, wrote For Whom the Bell Tolls, descendants of his 6-toed cats), Southernmost Point (buoy marking the southernmost "continental USA", obligatory photo), Duval Street (main street, bars, restaurants, touristy atmosphere but unmissable), Conch Train Tour (90-min tourist train). Activities: Dry Tortugas National Park (Fort Jefferson, access by seaplane or 2h30 ferry, full day), snorkelling/diving on the coral reef (3rd largest in the world), deep-sea fishing. Driving Miami-Key West via the Overseas Highway (250 km of bridge over the sea, legendary Seven Mile Bridge) = experience in itself (3-4h). Try Key Lime Pie (origin here), conch fritters, fish sandwich.
phoenix:en: Phoenix is the opposite of the European calendar. From November to April, the climate becomes ideal: 6-31°C, permanent blue sky, 1-13% rain. Europeans land for golf, Camelback Mountain hikes and the botanical gardens in bloom. April stays my favourite month (9.4/10): saguaros in bloom, still bearable temperatures before the heat, festivals in the desert. From June to September, the heat exceeds 40°C every day and represents real danger: every year, careless hikers lose their lives here. Phoenicians live in air-conditioning, go out before 7am or after 9pm, and flee to altitude (Flagstaff, Sedona). If you come anyway during this season, plan 4L of water per hike and forget hikes after 9am. March also works well (8.8/10) with the MLB spring training that animates the whole city. Sedona, 2h by road, stays 10°C cooler in summer.
asheville:en: Asheville, in North Carolina: 95,000 inhabitants at 660m altitude in the heart of the Blue Ridge Mountains (southern Appalachian chain), renowned for its bohemian atmosphere ("the new Portland"), its craft breweries (the highest density per inhabitant in the USA), and its art scene. June is my favourite month (8.7/10): 15-27°C, 42% rain, mild temperatures thanks to altitude. From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-29°C, mild climate. October = season of the spectacular foliage (Blue Ridge Parkway = one of the most beautiful panoramic roads in North America for autumn colours, peak mid-October), extreme crowds at the viewpoints, hotel prices x3. From July to August, expect 17-29°C with 47-53% rain: hot, characteristic afternoon storms. From December to February, expect -3/12°C: cold but mild for the Appalachians, light snow possible. Highlights: Biltmore Estate (the largest private home in the USA, 16,622 m², 250 rooms, built by George Vanderbilt 1889-1895, copy of a French château, gardens by Frederick Law Olmsted creator of Central Park, vineyard — the largest visit in North America, ticket 80$+, to book), Blue Ridge Parkway (754 km of panoramic road, no advertising or red light, panoramas, access from Asheville), Great Smoky Mountains National Park 1h30 away (UNESCO, the most visited in the USA, 12 million visitors/year), Mount Mitchell (2037m, the highest peak east of the Mississippi). City centre: rare art deco architecture (Pack Square, Grove Arcade), Asheville Brewers Trail (35+ breweries including Wicked Weed and Highland Brewing). Try Carolina barbecue (vinegar-based), trout farm, biscuits and gravy, craft beers. Asheville Regional airport, but often you fly to Charlotte/Atlanta + 2h by road.
sedona:en: Sedona, in Arizona: 10,000 inhabitants at 1370m altitude in the heart of the Red Rocks (red sandstone rock formations sculpted by erosion over 70 million years). Renowned for its energy vortexes (4 sites supposedly charged with spiritual energy, many New Age retreat centres since the 80s). May is my favourite month (9.6/10): 12-27°C, 8% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heat. From October to November, expect 5-23°C with 11-14% rain: excellent period, golden foliage contrasting with the red rocks. From June to August, expect 18-34°C: hot (sometimes 40°C+) but altitude 1370m = bearable, frequent afternoon storms ("monsoon season" July-August, dangerous lightning when hiking). From December to February, expect 0-13°C: mild during the day, frost at night, snow possible on the summits, spectacular winter atmosphere (Red Rocks under snow). Highlights: Cathedral Rock (signature formation, 2h round-trip hike, vortex spot), Bell Rock (the most accessible vortex), Boynton Canyon (vortex and hikes, one of the most beautiful), Devil's Bridge (natural arch accessible by 6 km hike, Instagram point), Chapel of the Holy Cross (modern 1956 chapel built into the cliff, spectacular view), Slide Rock State Park (natural toboggan in Oak Creek). Panoramic routes: Red Rock Scenic Byway (route 179 between Sedona and Village of Oak Creek), Oak Creek Canyon (route 89A towards Flagstaff to the north, one of the most beautiful roads in the USA). Excursions: Grand Canyon South Rim 2h away, Antelope Canyon 3h away, Monument Valley 4h away. Activities: Pink Jeep Tour off-road on the trails (90 min, 100$), New Age spas and retreats, star-gazing (Sedona Dark Sky Park).
maui:en: Maui, the "Magic Isle" of Hawaii, is more diverse than Oahu, more accessible than Big Island. Stable tropical climate. July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 19-26°C, 32% rain in brief showers on the west side (Kihei and Wailea are nearly always dry, Hana on the east is water-soaked). The Road to Hana (110 km, 600 bends, 56 single-lane bridges) remains the reference road trip: leave early and plan 8h round trip. Haleakalā (3055m) for sunrise above the clouds (NPS booking mandatory). From December to May, expect 15-23°C and 38-56% rain: this is humpback whale season (outings from Lahaina, November-May). Lahaina burned in August 2023: the historic town remains largely destroyed, check current state before going. The high tourist season remains December-April: prices x2-x3, beaches that fill up. May-June and September-October are the real sweet spots.
napa-valley:en: Napa Valley, in California: the most prestigious wine region in the USA, 80 km north of San Francisco, 3 parallel valleys (Napa, Sonoma, Russian River), 400+ wineries. June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 13-30°C, 2% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heatwave. From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 10-33°C, 0-11% rain. September-October = harvest season ("crush"), maximum activity in the wineries, but also fire season increasingly frequent since 2017 (major Atlas Fire 2017, Glass Fire 2020 burned entire wineries). From November to March, expect 5-17°C and 11-35% rain: quieter period, reduced hotel prices, tastings without crowds, green landscapes. Highlights: Wineries to visit: Castello di Amorosa (replica of an Italian castle, kitsch but photogenic), Robert Mondavi (modern Napa pioneer, died 2008), Opus One (Mondavi-Rothschild joint venture, prestige), Far Niente, Domaine Carneros (sparkling), Sterling Vineyards (cable car to reach the winery), Beringer (the oldest, 1876). Cabernet Sauvignon = the king grape of Napa (60% of production), Chardonnay and Pinot Noir to the south (cooler Carneros). Booking mandatory for tastings (50-200$/person, sometimes more for cult wines Screaming Eagle, Harlan, Scarecrow). Activities: Napa Valley Wine Train (gastronomic train 3-6h, 200-500$/person), hot air balloon at sunrise (400€), spa at Calistoga (geothermal springs, mud baths). Yountville = gastronomic village (French Laundry by Thomas Keller, 3*** Michelin restaurant among the most prestigious in the USA, 425$ menu). 1h30 from San Francisco.
palm-springs:en: Palm Springs, in California: 45,000 inhabitants 200 km east of Los Angeles, thermal-resort station in the Sonoran desert at the foot of the San Jacinto mountains (3300m). Historic haunt of Hollywood stars of the 50s-60s (Sinatra, Presley, Marilyn). World capital of Mid-Century Modern architecture. April is my favourite month (9.3/10): 16-30°C, 5% rain, ideal temperatures, Coachella festival (40 min away, 2 weekends mid-April, one of the largest music festivals in the world, 250,000 people, hotel prices x10). From October to May, it's the high "snowbird" season: 9-31°C, 3-12% rain, ideal dry climate, golf in full activity (130 courses in the Coachella Valley = world record golf density). From June to August drop to 3.9/10 due to extreme heat (24-41°C, sometimes 49°C in July, one of the hottest cities in the USA), hotel prices divided by 2-3. Highlights: Aerial Tramway (rotating cable car, one of the longest in the world, 800 → 2596m on Mount San Jacinto in 10 min, 30°C difference between bottom and top, hiking and snow in winter), Joshua Tree National Park 45 min away (martian landscapes, signature yucca trees, U2 album cover), Sunnylands (Annenberg residence former West Coast Camp David), Mid-Century Architecture (Modernism Week in February = the reference event, Sinatra house tours, Kaufmann House by Richard Neutra). Sonny Bono Memorial. Coachella Valley Preserve, Indian Canyons (palm oases in the heart of the desert). Try local dates (Coachella Valley = US date capital), date shake (local signature), fish tacos. Direct flight from Paris to LAX then 2h by road.
santa-fe:en: Santa Fe, capital of New Mexico (USA): 85,000 inhabitants at 2200m altitude (the 2nd most elevated state capital in the USA, after Cheyenne Wyoming), founded by the Spanish in 1610 ("La Villa Real de la Santa Fe de San Francisco de Asís"), the oldest state capital in the USA. Uniformly adobe (raw earth) Pueblo Revival architecture imposed by urban regulation since 1957, unique cocktail of Hispanic-Indian-Pueblo influences. June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 14-28°C, 14% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer storms. From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 8-29°C, dry altitude climate. From July to August, expect 15-29°C with 23-28% rain: characteristic afternoon storms ("monsoon season" typical of the US south-west, dangerous lightning). From November to March, expect -6/12°C: dry altitude cold, snow possible (Santa Fe Ski Basin 30 min away, among the highest-altitude ski resorts in the USA, 4000m), Christmas in Santa Fe very celebrated (farolitos paper lanterns on the adobe roofs, signature visual of the town). Highlights: Plaza de Santa Fe (Spanish central square 1610, market with Native American sellers every day under the Palace of the Governors), Palace of the Governors (1610, the oldest continuously used public building in the USA), Saint Francis of Assisi Cathedral (1869, French Romanesque in a sea of adobe), Loretto Chapel ("miraculous" spiral staircase without central support, 1878), Canyon Road (1 km of art galleries, 100+ galleries, one of the highest concentrations of art in the USA), Santa Fe Opera (July-August, open-air hall design Polshek 1998, one of the most prestigious operas in the USA), Georgia O'Keeffe Museum (signature painter of the American South-West who lived and painted in Abiquiu). Excursions: Taos 1h30 away (UNESCO Taos Pueblo, livable for 1000 years, Taos ski), Bandelier National Monument 1h away (12th-15th century Pueblo cliff dwellings), Carlsbad Caverns 5h away (UNESCO, caves), White Sands 4h away (white gypsum dunes). Santa Fe Indian Market (3rd weekend of August, the largest Native American art market in the world, 1000 artists). Try green chile (signature New Mexican sauce, on everything — red or green according to preference, Christmas = both), enchiladas, posole, sopaipillas, New Mexico wines.
monterey:en: Monterey, in California: 30,000 inhabitants, former capital of Spanish then Mexican California, on the peninsula between San Francisco (2h30 to the north) and Big Sur (1h to the south). The most stable climate in California (Mark Twain and Robert Louis Stevenson stayed here). September is my favourite month (8.2/10): 14-19°C, 3% rain, sea at 14-15°C (very cool, wetsuit needed for swimming). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 9-19°C, constant mild climate. From November to March, expect 9-19°C and 20-34% rain: still mild, reduced crowds. Year-round = pleasant climate but often misty in the morning (Californian "June Gloom"). Highlights: Cannery Row (popularised by John Steinbeck in Cannery Row 1945, former cannery street become tourist zone), Monterey Bay Aquarium (one of the best in the world, kelp forest, otters, gigantic pelagic tank, to book), Old Fisherman's Wharf (sea lions omnipresent), 17-Mile Drive (paid panoramic road 12$ between Monterey and Carmel, mansions, Lone Cypress, Pebble Beach Golf Links), Carmel-by-the-Sea 10 min away (artist village, charming cottages, Clint Eastwood was mayor), Point Lobos State Reserve (wild coast, otters, migrating whales). Big Sur 1h to the south (the most spectacular Pacific coast in the USA, route 1, McWay Falls, Bixby Bridge — but watch road closures after landslides). Activities: whale watching (grey whales December-March, blues July-August), kayaking with the otters, golf at Pebble Beach (the most famous in the world, 600$/round). Try clam chowder in bread bowl, fish and chips, Monterey County wine (Pinot Noir, Chardonnay).
memphis:en: Memphis, in Tennessee: 630,000 inhabitants, cradle of the blues (Beale Street), of rock'n'roll (Sun Studio where Elvis Presley recorded his first record in 1954), and of soul (Stax Records). Also sadly famous for the assassination of Martin Luther King Jr. at the Lorraine Motel on 4 April 1968. October is my favourite month (8.5/10): 13-24°C, 22% rain, ideal temperatures, foliage in Tennessee, festivals (Indie Memphis Film Festival). From May to September, expect 17-33°C: hot and humid, festivals (Memphis in May = world barbecue contest, Beale Street Music Festival), tornadoes possible in spring. From December to February, expect 1-13°C and 32-41% rain: mild for the south but greyish. From April and October-November are the best shoulder seasons. Music highlights: Graceland (Elvis's home since 1957, family mausoleum, Trophy Building, jets, cars — 4h visit, 80€), Sun Studio (Elvis, Johnny Cash, Jerry Lee Lewis, Carl Perkins, BB King all recorded here, 30-min visit), Stax Museum (Otis Redding, Isaac Hayes), Beale Street (pedestrian blues street, BB King's Blues Club, Rum Boogie Cafe), Rock and Soul Museum. Historic sites: National Civil Rights Museum at the Lorraine Motel (place of MLK's assassination preserved as is, important museum on civil rights in the USA). Mississippi River on cruise cargo. Mud Island River Park. Try Memphis BBQ (pulled pork in sweet vinegared sauce, at Cozy Corner or Central BBQ, distinct from Texas/Kansas City BBQ), barbecue spaghetti at The Bar-B-Q Shop, ribs.
santa-barbara:en: Santa Barbara is the temperate California par excellence: microclimate sheltered by the Santa Ynez Mountains to the north, constant ocean breezes. July is my favourite month (10.0/10): 18-28°C, 0% rain, and the famous "May Gray / June Gloom" (morning fogs) already dispersed. The wildfire season runs from June to October: the air can fill with smoke depending on the winds (Sundowner). From December to March, the face becomes more humid, 18-21% rain, but always 8-19°C. Very good for hiking at Romero Canyon or route 1 towards Big Sur. April-May and September-October are excellent transitional seasons: fewer tourists, Santa Ynez Valley vineyards in full harvest (Sideways obliges), and accessible State Street.
alicante:en: Alicante is the main gateway to the Costa Blanca: very well-connected airport, urban beaches, San Bárbara castle dominating the city. June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 20-27°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to crushing heat (23-30°C, feels-like 35°C+ with coastal humidity) and mass tourism (British in particular). Postiguet beach overflows. From December to March, plan 8-17°C and 10-25% rain: mild climate, ideal for visiting the city without crowds. The Hogueras de San Juan in June (Valencian equivalent of the Fallas, but smaller) remain a cultural highlight. Good base for exploring the Costa Blanca: Calpe (Peñón de Ifach), Altea (white old town), Benidorm (tropical skyline), Tabarca (protected island by ferry). May and September-October stay the best tourism/weather compromises.
cadix:en: Cadiz claims to be the oldest city in Europe (3000 years, Phoenician founding). Andalusian peninsula battered by the Atlantic. July is my favourite month (9.7/10): 21-29°C, 1% rain, sea at 22°C, Caleta beach accessible on foot from the centre. August drops to 6.9/10 in our data: the inland heat (32-35°C feels-like) and humidity make visits heavy. Sea at 23°C good, but historic centre burning. From December to March, expect 8-20°C and 22-37% rain: humid and windy (levante), little tourism, more local atmosphere. The Cadiz Carnival in February-March is the 2nd most important in Spain after Tenerife: chirigotas and comparsas in the streets for 12 days. Good base for Jerez (sherry, equestrian arts), Tarifa (kitesurf, Morocco ferry), El Puerto de Santa María (manzanilla). May-June and September-October are the best tourist compromises.
costa-brava:en: Costa Brava, rocky coast in north-eastern Spain in Catalonia: 200 km of coast from Blanes (35 km north of Barcelona) to the French border at Portbou. The "Wild Coast" in Catalan ("brava" = wild), rocky coves between pine forests and traditional white villages. July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 21-28°C, 12% rain, sea at 23°C. August stays excellent (9.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 18-28°C, sea at 21-25°C, hotel prices x3, crowds in popular resorts (Lloret de Mar, Blanes, Tossa de Mar = European charter clientele). From November to March, expect 6-16°C and 18-30% rain: mild for northern Spain, many hotels close on the coast (except big cities Roses, Palamós, Begur). Costa Brava highlights: Cadaqués (preserved white fishing village on the Cap de Creus peninsula, former refuge of Salvador Dalí who had his house here at Port Lligat today a museum, bohemian atmosphere, the bay of Cadaqués features in several Dalí paintings), Cap de Creus (extreme east of peninsular Spain, nature park, lunar landscapes), Tossa de Mar (12th-century fortified Vila Vella old town on a promontory, one of the last remaining medieval fortifications on the Spanish coast), Begur (white altitude village, 17th-century castle, modern smart), Calella de Palafrugell and Llafranc (photogenic fishing villages with colourful "barques de mitjana" boats), Aiguablava ("blue water" in Catalan, one of the most beautiful coves on the coast), Sa Tuna, Tamariu (preserved coves), Pals (medieval inland village), Empordà (DO vineyards), Empúries (Roman and Greek remains on the coast), Camí de Ronda (200 km coastal path from Blanes to the French border, spectacular Begur-Sa Riera sections). Dalí sites: Theatre-Museum Dalí at Figueres (UNESCO, retrospective designed by the artist 1974, signature geodesic dome), Salvador Dalí House at Port Lligat (visit by booking), Castle Gala Dalí at Púbol (offered to his wife Gala). Try suquet de peix (fish casserole), mar i muntanya (chicken with langoustines), butifarra, DO Empordà wines, ratafia Catalan liqueur. Girona airport (GRO) the most practical for Costa Brava.
la-palma:en: La Palma, the "Isla Bonita", went through the Cumbre Vieja eruption in 2021 which redrew part of the south-west. Today the lava flows are accessible by guide. July is my favourite month (9.8/10): 21-28°C, 1% rain, clear sky for the Roque de los Muchachos (2400m) and its international observatory (one of the best in the world, Starlight-certified sky). August curiously drops to 6.9/10 in our data: possible calculation bias, to verify. The GR-130 and GR-131 network covers the whole island for hikers (4-7 days self-supported). From November to March, plan 14-23°C and 25-37% rain: the wettest climate of all the Canaries, especially on the north side (Los Tilos, primary forest). The south stays drier, but the general atmosphere is more capricious than in Tenerife or Gran Canaria. Demanding island for car rental (winding mountain roads) but little touristy.
la-gomera:en: La Gomera demands a bit of effort: no international airport, ferry mandatory from Tenerife. Once on site, from May to October, the climate becomes excellent with 18-28°C and 0-8% rain on the coasts. July-August are my favourite months (9.6/10): the Garajonay national park stays at 18°C in the laurisilva even when San Sebastián hits 28°C. Good compromise for hikers: the senderos network covers all levels, and the silbo gomero (whistled language) is still practised in some villages. From December to March, the rain rises to 11-15% and the trade winds cool things. Always mild (15-22°C) but sky changeable on the north side. Good plan for those seeking hiking without the crushing heat of the southern Canaries.
el-hierro:en: El Hierro is the most remote and least populated of the Canaries: 11,000 inhabitants, 268 km², UNESCO geopark, the world's first island aiming for 100% renewable energy self-sufficiency. August is my favourite month (9.1/10): 16-23°C, 3% rain, steady trade winds. Mild climate year-round (10-23°C depending on the season), little variation. From November to April, expect 10-19°C and 13-21% rain: still bearable but clouds cling to the summit (Pico de Malpaso 1500m). The Reserva Marina de la Restinga in the south is one of the best diving spots in the Canaries (groupers, stingrays, mantas in season). The Sabinar (junipers twisted by the wind) is the flagship image. No international airport: ferry from Tenerife (2h30) or domestic flight from Tenerife/La Palma. Island for lovers of absolute calm, hiking and diving. Not for nightlife.
palma-de-majorque:en: Palma de Mallorca, capital of the island of Mallorca in the Spanish Balearics: 420,000 inhabitants on the bay of Palma. The largest island of the Balearics (3640 km², 940,000 inhabitants), major European tourist destination (16 million visitors/year including very many Germans and British). June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 20-28°C, 15% rain, sea at 22°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-32°C) and the crowds (Germans in the majority, hotel prices x3-x4, congested beaches). September stays excellent (8.7/10). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 20-29% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds (limited opening of resort hotels on the coast). Palma highlights: La Seu Cathedral (13th-16th century Gothic, one of the largest Gothic cathedrals in Europe, 121m long, contemporary intervention by Antoni Gaudí 1903-1914 then Miquel Barceló 2007 on the Sant Pere chapel), Royal Palace of the Almudaina (official residence of the Spanish kings during stays in Mallorca, former Muslim then Christian palace), Castell de Bellver (14th century, rare circular castle in Europe, panorama over the bay), Old town (Casc Antic, medieval alleys, Catalan patios), Es Baluard Museu (modern art in 16th-century bastion), Banys Àrabs (rare 11th-century Arab remains), Olivar Market (the great fresh market), Passeig del Born (smart avenue). Essential Mallorca excursions: Serra de Tramuntana (UNESCO, mountains of the north-west with perched villages Valldemossa where Frédéric Chopin and George Sand lived in winter 1838-1839, Sóller, Deià, Pollença), Cap de Formentor (extreme north, spectacular cliffs, hairpin road), Caves del Drach at Porto Cristo (limestone caves with one of the largest underground lakes in the world, classical concert by boat), Cala Mondragó, Es Trenc, Cala d'Or, Cala Agulla (turquoise-water coves of the south-east), Alcúdia (Roman fortified old town + long beach), Sa Calobra (legendary route with vertiginous hairpins). Try ensaimada (signature spiral pastry of Mallorca), sobrasada (spicy Mallorcan black-pig charcuterie), tumbet (ratatouille), pa amb oli, Binissalem wines. Palma airport (PMI), one of the busiest in Spain in season.
canaries:en: The Canary Islands, Spanish archipelago off Morocco: 7 main islands, 2.2 million inhabitants, the most stable subtropical climate in Europe ("eternal spring"). July is my favourite month (9.7/10): 20-28°C, 0% rain. Year-round is workable: 14-29°C on average, sea at 19-24°C, steady trade winds, outside the cyclone zone. From July to October, it's full high season: 19-29°C, 0-7% rain. From December to March, expect 14-22°C: winter high season (Northern Europeans wintering, snowbirds), prices x2 in the resorts. 7 islands to know: Tenerife (the largest, Teide 3718m highest point of Spain), Gran Canaria (Maspalomas dunes), Lanzarote (black volcanic landscapes, Timanfaya, César Manrique), Fuerteventura (the most beautiful beaches, kite/surf), La Palma ("isla bonita", hiking), La Gomera (laurel forest, silbo whistling), El Hierro (the smallest, diving). Highlights: Teide National Park (UNESCO, martian landscapes, Pico del Teide ascent by cable car + special permit for the summit), Maspalomas (dunes by the sea), Timanfaya (still-burning volcano, geothermal heat demonstrations), Garajonay (UNESCO, prehistoric laurel forest). Cuisine: papas arrugadas con mojo (spicy red or green), gofio, vieja (local fish), sancocho. Direct flights from Paris to Tenerife/Gran Canaria/Lanzarote in 4h30.
hurghada:en: Hurghada lives off diving tourism and all-inclusive resorts. Red Sea, reefs accessible from the beach. Desert climate. April is my favourite month (9.6/10): 20-28°C, 0% rain, sea at 23°C. From June to September, expect 27-35°C: dry heat stays bearable by the sea but exhausting on desert excursions. Diving boats leave early (5am-6am) to return before noon. From December to February, plan 14-22°C and the wind can turn icy in the evening. Diving stays possible in 5mm wetsuit. The major sites: Giftun Islands (snorkelling), Abu Nuhas (wrecks), Elphinstone Reef for hammerhead sharks (experienced divers). The resort suffers from chaotic urban development of the 2000s: choose your resort carefully. Excursions to Luxor (5h by road, plan 1 night minimum on site).
sharm-el-sheikh:en: Sharm el-Sheikh was born from tourism: resort built ex nihilo in the 1980s on the southern tip of Sinai. Red Sea, renowned reefs, resolutely all-inclusive infrastructure. April is my favourite month (9.5/10): 21-29°C, 1% rain, sea at 22°C. From June to September, expect 28-37°C: dry heat bearable by the sea but punishing on excursions. Diving stays possible year-round, but boats leave at 5am-6am to return before the heat. The world-class sites: Ras Mohammed (national park), Tiran Island (drift dives), Shark/Yolanda Reef. From December to February, plan 14-23°C with wind that can be icy: 5mm wetsuit for diving. Naama Bay remains the nightlife epicentre (but secured and a bit artificial). For the more local side: Dahab 1h30 to the north (more backpacker, less all-inclusive). Mount Sinai for sunrise stays a classic.
chefchaouen:en: Chefchaouen (the "blue pearl" of the Moroccan Rif): 40,000 inhabitants at 600m altitude in the northern mountains, medina entirely painted blue (Jewish tradition of the 15th century, popularised by Andalusian refugees). Has become an Instagram phenomenon since 2015. June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 16-28°C, 12% rain, ideal temperatures at altitude (10°C cooler than Marrakech). From May to September, it's the high season: 12-34°C, mild climate thanks to altitude. From July to August, warmer temperatures (19-34°C) but bearable. From December to March, expect 5-16°C and 27-32% rain: cold for Morocco, unheated riads, but medina without crowds (August = tourist peak with 80% domestic tourists). Highlights: Plaza Uta el-Hammam (main square with 15th-century Kasbah and its ethnography museum, starting point for photographing the blue alleys), Plaza el-Makhzen, Grand Mosque (exterior visit only), Souika Quarter (main souk), Petit Tanguer and Bab Onsar (water sources to understand the city's origin), Spanish Mosque on the hill (panoramic view at sunset, 30 min walk). Excursions: Akchour 30 min away (waterfalls, hike to the Bridge of God), Talassemtane Park (cedars and Barbary macaques). Logistics: Chefchaouen accessible by bus from Tangier (3h), Fes (4h), no airport (the closest: Tangier or Tetouan). Try chicken tagines with preserved lemon, local goat cheeses, harira. Cannabis grown in the Rif region (origin of "Chefchaouen hashish", illegal sale but omnipresent).
merzouga:en: Merzouga, village in south-east Morocco: 2000 inhabitants at the foot of the Erg Chebbi Dunes (28 km × 5 km, 150m high, one of the two great ergs of Morocco with the Erg Chigaga near M'Hamid). Gateway to the Moroccan Sahara desert. April is my favourite month (9.3/10): 15-29°C, 3% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From March-May and October-November, these are the best seasons: 4-30°C, 3-5% rain. From June-August drop to 3.5-3.7/10 due to extreme heat (24-42°C, sometimes 48°C in the dunes in midsummer), totally inadvisable for tourism. From December to February, expect 4-23°C and 3-5% rain: pleasant sunny days, but cold nights (sometimes below 0°C in the desert), snow possible on the neighbouring Atlas peaks. Activities: Camel excursion in the dunes (1-3 days, 30-150€/person with guide, overnight in a Berber camp under traditional tents 5-10 km from Merzouga, tagine dinner cooked over the fire, gnawa music at sunset, sunrise over the dunes the next morning = signature experience of the Moroccan Sahara), 4×4 in the desert (alternative for those who don't want to walk), quad biking in the dunes, visit to the Berber nomads, Lake Dayet Srji (seasonal lake that fills after rains, migratory pink flamingos April-May), Khamlia village of sub-Saharan origin (Senegal, Mali) with traditional gnawa music (heritage of the trans-Saharan slaves). Hotels: Riad Madu, Auberge Sahara, Kasbah Erg Chebbi, Ksar Tinheras, 50-300€/night (Berber inns at the foot of the dunes). Logistics: Merzouga 9h by car from Marrakech (spectacular route via the Tichka pass 2260m + Aït Benhaddou + Drâa Valley), 5h from Fes, 12h by bus from Marrakech (CTM), Errachidia regional airport. Try Berber tagine, couscous, mehallah (Berber bread cooked in the sand), mint tea.
hammamet:en: Hammamet, beach resort in Tunisia on Cap Bon: 100,000 inhabitants south-east of Tunis. First modern beach resort in Tunisia (developed in the 1930s by European artistic circles, then 60s-90s tourist explosion). May is my favourite month (9.0/10): 16-25°C, 18% rain, sea at 21°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From April to June and September-October, these are the best seasons: 13-30°C, 17-25% rain. From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-33°C), but it's full European tourist high season (French, Germans, Italians). From November to March, expect 9-21°C and 17-26% rain: mild, half-asleep resort, golf and thalassotherapy open. Highlights: Hammamet Medina (15th century, fortified by the sea, kasbah, maze of white and blue alleys, preserved atmosphere), Hammamet Beach (long fine-sand beach), Yasmine Hammamet (modern hotel zone in the south with reconstituted tourist Medina-village and marina), Carthage Land (theme park on Tunisian history). Excursions: Nabeul 15 min away (Tunisian pottery capital, Friday pottery market), Cap Bon and Kelibia to the north (Byzantine fortress, beach), Korbous (seaside hot springs), Tunis 1h away, Carthage and Sidi Bou Saïd 1h away, Kairouan 1h30 away (UNESCO, 4th holy city of Islam). Resort profile: Hammamet is the destination of cheap all-inclusive packages (350-700€ all-inclusive week flight+hotel depending on 3*-5* category), with pleasant climate May-June and September-October. Try couscous, ojja, brik, harissa, makroudh el louz (date-almond paste cake), mint tea with pine nuts. Direct flight from several European cities to Enfidha (30 min away) or Tunis-Carthage (1h away).
samarcande:en: Samarkand, legendary city of the Silk Road in Uzbekistan: 540,000 inhabitants. UNESCO 2001 for its historic centre ("Samarkand - Crossroads of Cultures"), capital of Tamerlane Timur the Lame's empire 1370-1405. Islamic architectural heritage among the most spectacular in the world (turquoise blue mosaics and gilded geometric Timurid signature). May and September are my favourite months (9.2-9.3/10): 14-30°C, 3-25% rain, ideal temperatures before/after the summer heatwave. From April to May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 8-30°C, ideal dry climate. From June to August, it's the heatwave: 19-36°C, 1-5% rain (arid continental climate, Kyzyl Kum desert nearby). From November to March, expect -1/16°C and 17-39% rain: continental cold, snow possible. Highlights: Registan Square ("the sandy place", one of the most spectacular squares in the world, ensemble of 3 madrasas of the 15th-17th centuries: Ulugh Beg 1417, Sher-Dor 1636 "of the signature tiger lions" postcard image of Uzbekistan, Tilya-Kori 1660 "covered in gold" the most richly decorated), Shah-i-Zinda Necropolis (12th-15th century funeral avenue, one of the most beautiful Islamic architectural ensembles in the world, mausoleums with blue mosaics, place of pilgrimage), Gour Emir Mausoleum ("Tomb of the emir", burial place of Tamerlane + his sons, typical Samarkand turquoise blue dome 38m), Bibi-Khanym Mosque (1399-1404, formerly the largest mosque in the world built by Tamerlane in honour of his favourite wife), Ulugh Beg Observatory (1429, 40m sextant discovered in 1908, Tamerlane's grandson calculated the length of the astronomical year with modern precision). Excursions: Bukhara 4h by train (UNESCO, see dedicated review), Khiva 11h away (UNESCO, see dedicated review), Tashkent capital (already covered). Try Uzbek plov (rice pilaf with mutton, national dish), shashlik, manty, samsa (fried samosa), Karakum sauerkraut, Chinese green tea.
tbilissi:en: Tbilisi, capital of Georgia: 1.1 million inhabitants at 380m altitude in a valley of the Mtkvari (Kura) river at the foot of the ancient Caucasus. Founded in the 5th century by King Vakhtang I who allegedly discovered the local hot springs during a hunting trip (the pheasant killed fell into a hot-water spring and was instantly cooked — origin of the name "Tbilisi" = "warm"). Georgian capital with multiple civilisations: Georgian, Persian, Ottoman, Russian, Soviet. June is my favourite month (9.0/10): 18-28°C, 32% rain, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. From May to June and September, these are the best seasons: 13-28°C, 16-44% rain. August drops to 8.7/10 due to the heat (21-32°C), July 6.9/10. From December to March, expect 0-13°C and 17-35% rain: continental winter cold, snow possible. Highlights: Old town (Old Tbilisi) (unique atmosphere, characteristic carved-wood balconies, twisting alleys, lift from Rike Park), Narikala Fortress (4th century, signature panorama over the city, accessible by 2.2 km cable car from Rike Park), Metekhi Church (13th century, on a cliff above the river, equestrian statue of King Vakhtang I), Sameba Cathedral (Holy Trinity, 2002-2004, the largest Orthodox cathedral in the Caucasus), Abanotubani Sulphur Baths ("baths quarter", brick architecture with domes, traditional Georgian experience with kissi masseur 30-50€), Bridge of Peace (controversial contemporary modern footbridge 2010), Rustaveli Avenue (Georgian Champs-Élysées, opera, parliament, grand luxury hotels), Sololaki Quarter (19th-century bourgeois buildings undergoing gentrification), Dezerter Bazaar Market (spices, cheeses, fruits, wines). Excursions: Mtskheta 20 min away (UNESCO, former capital until the 5th century, Jvari monastery and Svetitskhoveli cathedral), Kazbegi/Stepantsminda 3h away (Mount Kazbek 5047m, Tsminda Sameba church 2170m signature photo of Georgia on the high Caucasus balcony), Sighnaghi 2h away ("city of love", Kakheti wine region, qvevri amphora-buried wines 8000-year UNESCO method). Try khinkali (Georgian dumplings), khachapuri (cheese bread, particularly adjarian boat-shaped with fried egg), satsivi (chicken with walnut sauce), tkemali (plum sauce), Georgian wines (saperavi red, rkatsiteli white), tchatcha (marc eau-de-vie).
almaty:en: Almaty is the former capital of Kazakhstan (until 1997, replaced by Astana/Nur-Sultan), but remains the economic and cultural capital. At 800m altitude at the foot of the Tian Shan. Marked continental climate. August is my favourite month (9.4/10): 17-28°C, 14% rain, the right time for Big Almaty Lake (3300m, accessible by 1h taxi), Charyn Canyon ("the Kazakh Grand Canyon" 3h by road), and the Medeo Skating Rink + Chymbulak Ski Resort accessible by cable car from the centre. From December to February, expect -8/-1°C with 18-24% precipitation (often snow): this is ski season at Chymbulak (3200m), small resort but easily accessible. From March to April and October-November, it's the capricious shoulder season: 0-17°C, snow melt, lots of rain. The Green Market (Zelyony Bazaar), the Zenkov Orthodox cathedral (one of the tallest wooden buildings in the world), and the Panfilov Park quarter concentrate the visits. Direct flight from Istanbul, Frankfurt, Dubai, or via Moscow.
yerevan:en: Yerevan, capital of Armenia: 1 million inhabitants at 990m altitude on the Armenian plateau, spectacular view of Mount Ararat (5137m, sacred summit for Armenians where Noah's Ark allegedly landed according to Genesis, paradoxically located in Turkish territory since 1920 — permanent nostalgia). The world's oldest continuously inhabited capital according to dating (Erebuni fortress 782 BC, that is 30 years before the founding of Rome). Multi-millennial country (1st country in the world to adopt Christianity as state religion in 301 AD). June and September are my favourite months (9.4/10): 16-30°C, 11-16% rain, ideal temperatures. From May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 10-28°C, 11-40% rain. From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (21-34°C, continental climate). From December to March, expect -4/12°C and 19-35% rain: dry winter cold, snow possible. Highlights: Republic Square (heart of the city, Soviet neo-Armenian architectural ensemble signed Alexander Tamanian 1924 who redesigned Yerevan in concentric radial plan with signature pink volcanic tuff buildings), Cascade (monumental staircase 572 steps, open-air contemporary sculpture garden with Botero, Lynn Chadwick), Matenadaran ("manuscript depository", one of the world's most important centres of ancient manuscripts, 17,000 manuscripts including 12th-15th century Armenian religious illuminations), Armenian Genocide Memorial ("Tsitsernakaberd", essential to understand Armenian identity, 1.5 million deaths 1915-1923, Turkish denial continues to poison diplomacy), Saint Gregory the Illuminator Cathedral (1997-2001, the largest Armenian cathedral in the world), GUM Market (spices, dried fruits), Yerevan Brandy factory (Ararat brandy, the favourite brandy of Churchill and Stalin). Excursions: Etchmiadzin 20 min away (UNESCO, seat of the Armenian Apostolic Church 301, equivalent of the Vatican), Garni 1h away (Greek-Roman temple 1st century, the only Greco-Roman temple still standing in the former USSR, restored in the 1970s), Geghard (UNESCO, medieval monastery partially carved into the rock), Lake Sevan (1900m altitude, the largest alpine lake in the Caucasus). Try Armenian dolma (stuffed vine leaves), khorovats (traditional lamb BBQ), lavash (UNESCO flatbread), khash (winter beef-feet soup), Ararat brandy, Armenian wines (Areni — 6100 years of viticulture, oldest wine cellar in the world discovered here).
baku:en: Baku (Bakı), capital of Azerbaijan: 2.3 million inhabitants on the Caspian coast. Nicknamed "Dubai of the Caucasus" since the post-2000 oil boom (futuristic Flame Towers + Heydar Aliyev Centre architectures). Geopolitically at the crossroads of Russia, Iran and Turkey — Turkic-Azeri culture + Shiite Muslim heritage + Persian influence + Soviet Russification. June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 20-29°C, 8% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heatwave. From May to June and September-October, these are the best seasons: 9-29°C, 15-23% rain. From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-32°C with Caspian humidity). From December to March, expect 3-11°C and 20-25% rain: mild but windy (Baku is one of the windiest cities in the world, its name means "city of winds" in Persian). Highlights: Old town Içeri Şeher (UNESCO 2000, fortified 12th-15th century, alley maze), Maiden Tower (12th century, 28m, architectural mystery — no known function, legend of the princess who allegedly threw herself from the top rather than marry her father), Shirvanshahs Palace (UNESCO, 13th-16th century, mausoleum and baths), Heydar Aliyev Centre (Zaha Hadid 2012, organic architecture signature of the Iraqi-British architect, exhibitions, Design of the Year 2014 prize), Flame Towers (3 towers of 190m in the shape of flames 2012, spectacular night LED illuminations, Fairmont hotels + residences + offices), Seaside Boulevard (Bulvar, promenade by the Caspian 16 km, parks, restaurants), Azerbaijani Carpet Museum (architecture in the shape of a rolled carpet), Yashil Bazaar Market. Excursions: Yanar Dag ("Burning Mountain" 25 km away, natural flames burning continuously since antiquity due to underground gas emanations, gave the nickname "Land of Fire" to Azerbaijan), Atashgah Fire Temple (Surakhani, former Zoroastrian and Hindu place of worship 17th-19th century, eternal flames maintained by gas since 2007 after natural drying up), Gobustan Mud Volcanoes (UNESCO 2007, the largest concentration of mud volcanoes in the world 350+, prehistoric rock carvings 6000 years). Try Azeri plov (saffron rice pilaf), kebab, dolma (stuffed vine leaves), qutab (pancakes), pakhlava (local baklava), tcha (tea) served in armudu tulip glass, Shamakhi wines.
moorea:en: Moorea, heart-shaped, is Tahiti's sister island 30 min by ferry from Papeete: quieter and cheaper than Bora Bora, but just as spectacular. Mount Tohivea (1207m), signature Cook and Opunohu bays. July-August are my favourite months (8.3/10): 23-26°C, 40-42% rain, dry season, sea at 25°C. From May to October, it's the dry season: 40-54% rainfall, temperatures 23-28°C, refreshing trade winds. From November to April, it's the wet season: 56-78% rainfall, heavy heat, cyclone risk (rare). Highlights: Belvedere (panoramic view over the Cook and Opunohu bays, access by car via the pineapples), Magic Mountain (another view, 4×4 access), Lagoon (snorkelling with rays, blacktip reef sharks, turtles), Lagoon tour by boat (classic excursion, motu stops), swim with dolphins and whales (July-October, humpback whales). Tiki Village (Polynesian cultural show). Diving world-class (Tiki Diving, Bathys Diving). Island tour by bike or scooter (60 km in 4h). Hotels and family pensions much cheaper than Bora Bora (200-800€/night). Try raw fish, ma'a tinito, Moorea pineapple. The Aremiti ferry from Papeete (5 times/day, 30 min, 15-20€) makes Moorea very accessible.
al-ula:en: Al-Ula (AlUla), archaeological oasis in north-western Saudi Arabia in the Hijaz desert: 5000 inhabitants. Newly opened tourist jewel of Saudi Arabia since 2019 as part of Vision 2030 (huge investments 50+ billion USD to make Al-Ula the "Saudi Petra" and rival neighbouring tourist Jordan). Major historic site: Hegra (Madâin Sâlih) = the 2nd Nabataean capital after Petra Jordan, created by the same Nabataean peoples 2nd century BC-1st century AD. November is my favourite month (9.3/10): 15-28°C, 5% rain, this is the ideal dry season. March-December stay excellent (8.5-9.1/10). From October to April, it's the tourist high season: 7-32°C, ideal dry and temperate desert climate. From May to September drop to 3.8-6.4/10 due to the extreme desert heatwave (22-42°C, sometimes 47°C+). Highlights: Hegra (Hegra/Madâin Sâlih) (UNESCO 2008, the 1st UNESCO site in Saudi Arabia, 110+ monumental Nabataean tombs sculpted into the pink-red sandstone cliff 2nd century BC-1st century AD, similar to Petra Jordan but much less frequented at only 60 visitors/day by preservation measures, limited access mandatory booking, signature experience with fewer crowds than the Jordanian rival, Qasr al-Farid = "Solitary Tomb" 22m high signature visual, signature photograph at sunset), Old Town of Al-Ula (Old Town, atmosphere restored 2019-2024 with paved-restored alleys-galleries, former 12th-century fortified town abandoned 1980, undergoing majestic restoration), Elephant Rock (Jabal AlFil, natural rocky formation in the shape of an elephant 50m high, photogenic signature visual, accessible by 4x4 in the desert at sunset), Dadan and Jabal Ikmah (millennial pre-Nabataean archaeological sites 1st millennium BC, signature ancient lihyanite Arabic rock inscriptions, "open-air library" with thousands of inscriptions on the cliffs), Hijaz Railway Station (remains of the early 20th-century Ottoman railway, legendary "Lawrence of Arabia railway" attacked by T.E. Lawrence and the Arab revolt 1916-1918 = major historical monuments), Maraya Concert Hall ("Mirror" in Arabic, concert hall designed 2019 by Italian architects Giò Forma = entirely covered with mirror panels reflecting the desert landscape = Guinness Book record entry of the largest mirror building in the world, open for Festival Winter at Tantora winter every year, international concerts Andrea Bocelli/Yannick Noah have played). Activities: 4x4 safari in the Al-Ula desert, hot air balloon ride at sunrise above the desert landscapes (Al-Ula tourist signature October-March), guided Hegra walk (mandatory booking), night in a desert camp Banyan Tree AlUla 5*+ opened 2022, Habitas AlUla resort 5*+, high prices 800-3000€/night in season. Try mandi (Saudi lamb rice), kabsa, Arabic coffee with Ajwa dates, harees. Logistics: Prince Abdul Majeed Al-Ula airport (ULH) with direct flights from Riyadh, Jeddah, Dubai, Doha (1-2h), from Europe with Riyadh/Jeddah connection.
boukhara:en: Bukhara, 2nd museum-city of Uzbekistan on the Silk Road: 280,000 inhabitants. UNESCO 1993 for its historic centre ("Bukhara - Sacred Citadel"), reputed as the most traditional city in Central Asia (140 listed monuments). Cradle of major Islamic figures like Ibn Sina (Avicenna, 10th-11th century doctor and philosopher). September is my favourite month (9.2/10): 16-31°C, 0% rain, ideal temperatures after the summer. From May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 8-32°C, ideal dry climate. From June to August, it's the heatwave: 22-40°C (July often 42°C+), avoid July (3.9/10). From November to March, expect -2/15°C and 13-20% rain: continental cold, few tourists but preserved charm. Highlights: Ark Citadel (fortress since the 5th century, last residence of the emirs of Bukhara until 1920, 80% destroyed by Soviet bombing 1920, partially restored), Poï Kalon ("pedestal of the Great", central complex with Kalon Minaret 47m built in 1127, one of the only monuments spared by Genghis Khan in 1220 "too beautiful to be destroyed", Kalon Mosque 12,000 places, Mir-i-Arab Madrasa 16th century still in operation), Ulugbek Madrasa, Abdul Aziz Khan Madrasa, Lab-i-Hauz (central square around a 17th-century pool, traditional atmosphere, tea rooms under mulberry trees), Samanid Mausoleum (10th century, one of the oldest and most beautiful monuments in Central Asia, baked bricks without glaze forming geometric patterns, considered an Islamic architectural masterpiece), Tcharsu (4 domes of the bazaar 16th-17th century: Toki Sarrofon money changers, Toki Telpak Furushon hat sellers, Toki Zargaron jewellers — still in operation today), Sitorai Mokhi-Khosa Summer Palace 5 km away. Logistics: Bukhara 4h by fast train from Tashkent. Try Uzbek plov, shashlik, manty, samsa, halva, Uzbek vodka, green tea.
khiva:en: Khiva, 3rd museum-city of Uzbekistan on the Silk Road: 90,000 inhabitants in the heart of the Kyzyl Kum desert, 35 km from the Turkmen border. UNESCO 1990 for its Itchan Kala (fortified old town), considered the most intact museum-city in Central Asia (old town entirely preserved intra-muros, almost like a frozen 19th-century film set). September is my favourite month (9.6/10): 15-28°C, 2% rain, ideal temperatures after the heatwave. From May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 8-30°C, 2-13% rain. From June to August, it's the extreme heatwave: 22-38°C (July 38°C+), avoid July (3.9/10). From November to March, expect -3/11°C and 6-13% rain: dry continental cold. Highlights (almost all concentrated in the fortified Itchan Kala 26 hectares, 2 km of mud-brick ramparts): Kalta Minor ("short minaret", 1851, was meant to be 70m but stayed at 26m because Khan Mohammad Amin died before its completion, signature of Khiva with its turquoise ceramic), Mohammed Amin Khan Madrasa (1855, next to the Kalta Minor, converted into a hotel), Kuhna Ark Citadel (residence of the khans 17th century), Djouma Mosque (18th century, frame on 213 sculpted black-elm wood columns, atmosphere out of time), Islom-Khoja Madrasa and its Minaret (1910, 56m, the tallest in Khiva, 118 narrow steps ascent, 360° panorama), Tash Hauli Palace (1830-1838, khan's harem, 163 rooms, sculpted wood and ceramic works), Pakhlavan Mahmud Mausoleum (poet and wrestler 1247-1326, revered as a local saint), Toza Bog Palace. Logistics: Khiva 11h by train from Tashkent (overnight train), 1h by plane to neighbouring Urgench + 30-min taxi. Classic "Silk Road" combination: Tashkent + Samarkand + Bukhara + Khiva = 8-10 days. Try shivit oshi (Khiva signature green dill pasta), tukhum-barak (egg dumplings), plov, samsa, green tea.
macao:en: Macau, former Portuguese colony (1557-1999), Special Administrative Region of China since 1999: 690,000 inhabitants over 33 km², 4th highest population density in the world. World capital of gambling (casino revenues 7x higher than Las Vegas pre-Covid). November is my favourite month (8.5/10): 20-25°C, 23% rain, ideal temperatures before winter. From October to December, it's the high season: 15-28°C, mild climate. From June to September, it's the hot/humid season: 65-88% rainfall, oppressive humidity (90%), frequent typhoons (Macau is hit by 5-6 per season on average). From January to March, expect 15-23°C: mild but sky often grey. UNESCO heritage highlights (Historic Centre 30 monuments): Ruins of Saint Paul's (17th-century façade, signature of Macau), Largo do Senado (Portuguese paved square), A-Ma Temple (origin of the name "Macau" = Mât de A-Ma, Ma'Gao in Chinese), Na Tcha Temple, Fortaleza do Monte, Mandarin's House. Modern casinos: Cotai Strip (the "Asian Las Vegas" on island reclaimed from the sea linking Taipa and Coloane) with Venetian Macao (the largest casino in the world, indoor gondolas), City of Dreams, Wynn Palace, Grand Lisboa, MGM. Try Macanese fusion cuisine: galinha à portuguesa (curry chicken), pork with shrimp paste, pastéis de nata (Portuguese egg tarts, origin here then Lisbon), almond cookies. Logistics: Macau 1h by ferry from Hong Kong (TurboJet), direct flight from Bangkok/Tokyo/Manila. Visa issued on arrival for Europeans (90 days). Pataca (MOP) pegged to HKD.
xian:en: Xi'an (Xian), capital of Shaanxi province in China: 9 million inhabitants, former capital of 13 Chinese dynasties (notably Han and Tang, for 1000 years, until the 9th century). Historic starting point of the Silk Road. Site of the most stunning archaeological discovery of the 20th century: the Terracotta Army. May is my favourite month (8.8/10): 15-28°C, 23% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer heatwave. From September to October, expect 10-26°C: excellent period, foliage early October. From June to August, expect 20-33°C with 26-35% rain: hot, sometimes scorching (39°C+). From December to February, expect -4/10°C and 6-13% rain: dry continental cold, atmospheric pollution often high (Xi'an suffers from the winter smog of northern China). Highlights: Terracotta Army (UNESCO, 35 km east of Xi'an, discovered by chance by farmers in 1974, 8000 life-size soldiers in baked clay buried in 210 BC to guard the tomb of Emperor Qin Shi Huang, first unifying Chinese emperor, 3 pits excavated out of an estimated 600 pits in total, still being excavated), Ming City Walls (14th century, 14 km tour on foot or by bike, one of the only intact Chinese ramparts, height 12m, width 15m), Big Wild Goose Pagoda (Big Wild Goose Pagoda, 64m, Tang dynasty 7th century, built to house the Buddhist sutras brought back from India by Xuanzang), Muslim Quarter (Hui People, descendants of Arab/Persian merchants of the Silk Road settled in Xi'an in the 7th-8th centuries, unique atmosphere, street food, Xi'an Mosque 14th century = the oldest intact mosque in China, atypical Chinese architecture for a mosque), Drum Tower and Bell Tower, Famen Temple 2h away (Buddha relic). Try rou jia mo ("Chinese hamburger" pulled-pork flatbread sandwich), biang biang noodles, lamb skewers from the Muslim quarter, cumin-flavoured beef.
chengdu:en: Chengdu, capital of Sichuan province in China: 21 million inhabitants in the urban area (one of the most dynamic Chinese megacities), nicknamed "the city of hibiscus", cultural/gastronomic capital of south-western China. Renowned for giant pandas (the most famous research centre in the world, 80% of pandas in captivity in the world) and spicy Sichuanese cuisine (Sichuan pepper, mala = unique numbing-spicy sensation). May is my favourite month (7.7/10): 18-27°C, 41% rain, ideal temperatures before the summer humidity. From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 14-31°C, but constant humid climate (Chengdu is famous for its permanent greyish sky, locals say you see the sun 60 days/year). From June to September, expect 21-31°C with 44-66% rain: heavy and humid heat. From December to February, expect 4-12°C and 16-26% rain: humid cold, few tourists.
