July is my favourite month (9.8/10): 12-28°C, 12% rain, dry altitude climate (2100m), constantly blue sky. Ideal for hikes and mountain bike (50+ trails at Park City Mountain Bike Park). From December to March, expect -11/4°C: this is ski season. "The Greatest Snow on Earth" is the official slogan of Utah: the snow is dry and light here ("champagne powder") thanks to the dry continental climate and the Great Salt Lake effect. Park City Mountain (the largest US area, 7300 acres) + Deer Valley (ski only, no snowboard) + Canyons form a remarkable trio. Sundance Film Festival every January (former Robert Redford) fills the town for 10 days: prices x3-x4, bookings 1 year ahead. From April to May and October-November, it's the shoulder season: skiing closing, mountain biking not yet open. Direct flights from Europe via Salt Lake City.
breckenridge:en: Breckenridge, former Colorado mining town (1860, gold rush), keeps its historic Main Street. Cult ski resort with 5 mountains at 2900m altitude. July is my favourite month (8.0/10): 5-21°C, 47% rain in storms, hiking towards the Mohawk Lakes or Quandary Peak (4350m, one of the most accessible 14ers in Colorado). From December to March, expect -16/-2°C: ski season, dry snow guaranteed. The Imperial Express summit exceeds 4000m, the highest in North America. From April to May and October-November, it's the shoulder season: everything closes, ghost town. Difference vs Vail: truly historic Main Street (not a fake village), prices slightly less crazy (pass at 150-180$). The Epic pass gives access. Denver 2h away via I-70, same traffic jams on Sunday evening in winter.
cervinia:en: Cervinia (Breuil-Cervinia) is the Italian face of the Matterhorn, resort at 2050m altitude at the foot of the summit. Ski link with Zermatt in Switzerland via Plateau Rosa (3500m), one of the rare international ski areas. July is my favourite month (7.6/10, constantly cool alpine climate): 8-17°C, 40% rain in afternoon storms, the right time for the Lago Blu (lake reflecting the Matterhorn) and the hike to Plan Maison. From December to April, it's the major ski season: reliable snow thanks to altitude (most of the pistes above 2000m), summer skiing on the Plateau Rosa glacier until late August some years. From May-June and October-November, it's the shoulder season: summer skiing vs hiking not yet ready. Cervinia is the smart opposite of Courmayeur or Cortina: resort built for skiing, little Italian village soul. Aosta 1h by car, Turin 2h.
val-gardena:en: Val Gardena (Gröden in German), in the South Tyrolean Italian Dolomites, is one of the most charming ski valleys in the Alps: 3 Tyrolean villages (Ortisei, Santa Cristina, Selva di Val Gardena), Sella Ronda link (26 km loop on skis). July-August are my favourite months (4.6-4.8/10): 6-16°C at altitude, 63-65% rain, good for hiking towards the Tre Cime di Lavaredo and the Sassolungo. From December to April, expect -11/3°C: major ski season, reliable snow thanks to altitude and the continental Dolomitic climate. The Saslong World Cup (men's descent) every December. From May and October-November, it's the slow shoulder season. South Tyrolean (languages: Ladin, German, Italian). Cuisine fusion between Tyrolean and Italian (canederli, speck, strudel). Bolzano 30 min away, Venice 3h. Innsbruck airport 2h30 by road (Brenner pass).
madonna-di-campiglio:en: Madonna di Campiglio, in the Italian Trentino: 700 inhabitants at 1550m in the heart of the Brenta Dolomites (UNESCO 2009, signature Dolomitic landscapes), reputed as the "Italian Saint Moritz" for its smartness and prices. August is my favourite month (7.3/10): 9-19°C, 70% rain in altitude storms, Brenta hiking, via ferrata. From June to September, expect 5-19°C: active summer season (climbing, climbing in the Brenta = cradle of Italian climbing, Cesare Maestri). From December to April, expect -11/7°C and 26-44% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 156 km Skiarea Campiglio Dolomiti di Brenta (Madonna + Pinzolo + Folgarida + Marilleva, 1 single ski pass), connected to Dolomiti Superski by shuttle buses (1200 km of pistes in 12 Dolomitic resorts, 1 single pass). 3 Tre (cult late-December World Cup slalom on the Canalone Miramonti piste, one of the most famous pistes on the circuit). Pistes: Spinale, 5 Laghi, Pradalago, Grostè (3000m, Brenta panorama). Off-piste Brenta (with guide). Audience: Italian aristocracy (Milanese, Venetian), well-off Italian families, smarter than Cortina. Hotels: Hotel DV Chalet, Hotel Alpen Suite, Lefay Resort & Spa (5*+), 200-2000€/night in high season. Carlo Magno Hotel = historic palace. Try canederli, polenta, casunziei, speck, Trentino wines (Pinot Bianco, Müller-Thurgau). Logistics: Madonna 2h30 from Milan or Verona by car, 4h from Munich.
cape-cod:en: Cape Cod is the arm-shaped peninsula south-east of Boston, Massachusetts: 105 km long, 350,000 permanent inhabitants (multiplied by 3 in summer), Victorian shingle-clad houses, Atlantic beaches, historic lighthouses. July is my favourite month (8.8/10): 20-26°C, 31% rain, sea at 21°C (still fresh), ferries to the islands. August stays excellent (8.7/10). From June to September, it's the American tourist high season: hotel prices x3-x4, bookings 6 months ahead for the charming inns (Chatham Bars Inn, Wequassett). From December to March, expect -2/7°C and 35-39% precipitation often as snow: 60% of restaurants and hotels close, but calm winter atmosphere. Highlights: Provincetown (P-Town, at the northern tip, LGBT atmosphere since the 70s, ferries to Boston), Cape Cod National Seashore (60 km of wild Atlantic beaches), Cape Cod Rail Trail (40 km of cycle path on a former railway line), Lighthouses (Highland Light, Nauset Light, Chatham Light). Islands to discover by ferry: Martha's Vineyard (45 min from Falmouth, former retreat of the Kennedys and Obamas, smart Edgartown), Nantucket (1h15 or 30-min plane from Hyannis, preserved 19th-century whaling atmosphere). Whale watching from Provincetown (late May to October). Try lobster roll (at Mac's Seafood), clam chowder, fried clams (Whole Belly at Kream 'N' Kone), Wellfleet oysters. 2h from Boston by car.
outer-banks:en: Outer Banks ("OBX"), chain of North Carolina barrier islands: 320 km of long narrow sandy islands separating the Atlantic from the mainland, 60,000 permanent inhabitants (population x10 in summer), cradle of modern aviation (the Wright brothers performed the 1st controlled motorised flight at Kitty Hawk on 17 December 1903). June is my favourite month (8.7/10): 24-25°C, 31% rain, sea at 24°C. From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 19-28°C, hotel prices x4-x5, beach house rentals 2000-10,000$/week, packed beaches (but 200 km of beach = space for everyone). From July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (25-28°C) and beginning cyclone risk. From September to November, it's the active cyclone season (the US east coast is particularly exposed, the OBX are nicknamed "Graveyard of the Atlantic" for the thousands of historic shipwrecks + modern hurricanes). From December to March, expect 7-15°C and 28-38% rain: mild for the east coast, many establishments close. Highlights: Wright Brothers National Memorial (Kitty Hawk Hill, exact site of the 1st flight, 18m granite monument, reconstructed hangar), Cape Hatteras National Seashore (113 km of preserved beaches, the tallest brick lighthouse in the USA 63m), Cape Hatteras Lighthouse (1870, moved 880m inland in 1999 in the face of coastal erosion, 268 steps), Bodie Island Lighthouse, Currituck Beach Lighthouse, Jockey's Ridge State Park (the highest mobile dunes on the East coast, 30m, paragliding sandboarding), Roanoke Island and Lost Colony (the 1st English colony of America 1587, mysteriously disappeared, open-air theatre in summer), Ocracoke Island (preserved island accessible only by ferry, historic haunt of the pirate Edward Teach "Blackbeard"). Activities: surf (the US east coast = capital of surf outside Hawaii/California), kitesurf at Hatteras, paragliding, fishing pier, mustang wild horses (Corolla, descendants of the 16th-century Spanish horses). Try seafood (OBX-version clam chowder, oysters, soft-shell crab), hush puppies (fried bread balls). Norfolk airport (Virginia) 1h30 away or Raleigh-Durham 3h away.
acores:en: The Azores is the Portuguese archipelago of the Atlantic: 9 volcanic islands, spectacular green landscapes (hills, crater lakes, hot springs). July is my favourite month (8.0/10): 18-23°C, 46% rain, mild temperatures for hiking. Oceanic climate = rain possible all year (very changeable). From June to September is the relatively drier season (45-50% rain). From November to March, expect 13-17°C and 52-56% rain: Atlantic storms, rough sea, inter-island ferries sometimes cancelled. 9 main islands: São Miguel (the largest, see dedicated review), Pico (the volcano 2351m, highest summit in Portugal, 5h round-trip ascent), Faial (Horta marina with its Peter Café Sport for sailors from around the world), Terceira (Angra do Heroísmo UNESCO, bulls in the streets), São Jorge (cheese), Flores (waterfalls, one of the most beautiful), Corvo (the smallest, 400 inhabitants), Graciosa, Santa Maria (the warmest). Whale watching April-October (25 different species possible). Diving in the Azores among the best spots in the Atlantic (manta rays at Princess Alice, sharks). Logistics: Azores Airlines mandatory inter-island flights, 1 single ferry (Atlanticoline). Plan 10-15 days minimum for 3-4 islands. Direct flight Paris-Ponta Delgada (4h).
sao-miguel:en: São Miguel, the largest of the Azores (746 km²): Portuguese island in the Atlantic, 1500 km from Lisbon, green volcanic landscapes (European Hawaii). July-August are my favourite months (8.2-8.3/10): 18-24°C, 46-48% rain, mild temperatures, swimming in the Atlantic (water at 22°C). From December to March, expect 12-17°C and 52-56% precipitation: near-continuous rain (humid oceanic climate), but the greenest landscapes. Capricious weather island: 4 seasons in one day possible. Highlights: Sete Cidades (crater with green and blue lakes, panorama from Vista do Rei or Boca do Inferno), Lagoa do Fogo (lake in wilder crater), Furnas (volcanic valley with geysers, free hot springs Poça da Dona Beija or Terra Nostra), Cozido das Furnas (traditional dish cooked in the volcanic earth, to order the day before), Ponta Delgada (capital), Vila Franca do Campo (volcanic atoll offshore). Whale watching April-October (sperm whales year-round, blue whales March-June). Tea plantations Gorreana (the only European plantation, since 1883). Try Azores pineapple, São Jorge cheese, Pico wine, queijadas. May-June and September-October are the best tourism/weather compromises. Direct flight Lisbon (2h) or Porto.
funchal:en: Funchal is the capital of Madeira: Portuguese volcanic island in the Atlantic, 1000 km from continental Portugal. Mild subtropical climate year-round. July-August are my favourite months (9.4/10): 20-27°C, 10-12% rain, sea at 22°C. From December to February, expect 13-19°C and 31-35% rain: the north side of the island stays humid almost year-round (UNESCO laurisilva), the south (Funchal, Calheta) is markedly drier. The network of levadas (irrigation canals converted into paths) covers the whole island: 25 de Fontes, Caldeirão Verde, Levada do Caldeirão are the classics. The Monte cable car (gardens) and the wicker sled descent (carros de cesto) remain the flagship attractions. Câmara de Lobos (fishing village immortalised by Churchill). Madeira wine is tasted at all the bodegas in the centre. Pico Ruivo (1862m) accessible in 1h30 walk from Pico do Arieiro for sunrise above the clouds.
gozo:en: Gozo is the rural sister of Malta: 67 km², 30,000 inhabitants, 25-minute ferry from Cirkewwa. No airport. More peaceful, more local atmosphere. July is my favourite month (9.7/10): 24-26°C, 0% rain, sea at 25°C. The Azure Window, the signature arch, collapsed in 2017 during a storm: no longer search for it in the guides. The Citadella of Victoria (medieval citadel) remains the centrepiece, views over the whole island. Beaches: Ramla Bay (red sand), San Blas (accessible by path). Diving on the Blue Hole of Dwejra (collapsed too but still beautiful) and the HMS Trusty wreck. August drops to 6.9/10 due to the heatwave (25-26°C feels-like 35°C+). From November to March, plan 13-20°C and 23-39% rain: humid and windy, many taverns close, but the cliff walks (Ta' Cenc) stay superb. 2-3 days suffice as a complement to Malta.
valletta:en: Valletta, capital of Malta: 6000 inhabitants intra-muros (the smallest European capital), 100,000 in the conurbation-city. UNESCO 1980 = the first European city planned on a Renaissance grid, founded 1566 by the Knights of Malta (Order of Saint John of Jerusalem) after the Great Ottoman Siege of 1565 where the knights and inhabitants heroically resisted 40,000 Ottomans with only 6000 defenders. June is my favourite month (9.6/10): 19-29°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures before the heatwave. May and September-October stay excellent (8.7-9.4/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-32°C, frequent sirocco wind from the Sahara). From November to March, expect 9-19°C and 18-37% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds. Highlights: Saint John's Co-Cathedral (16th-century Maltese baroque, austere façade hiding an interior of exceptional baroque opulence, houses the Beheading of Saint John the Baptist by Caravaggio 1608 = the largest painting by Caravaggio and signed the only time in his life, painted while he was himself a fugitive in Malta before his exclusion from the Order), Grand Master's Palace (16th century, former palace of the Grand Masters of the Order, today residence of the President of Malta + parliament, armoury of the Order), Upper Barrakka Gardens (panoramic gardens with view over the 3 Cities on the other side of the Grand Harbour, noon/4pm cannon ceremony British tradition preserved since 1824), Lower Barrakka Gardens, Fort Saint Elmo (at the end of the peninsula, hero of the Great Siege 1565, houses the National War Museum), Auberges of the Tongues (8 historic auberges of the Order, one per geographic "tongue": Auberge de Castille = today Prime Minister, Auberge de France, Auberge de Provence = National Museum of Archaeology, Auberge d'Italie...), Manoel Theatre (1731, one of the oldest theatres still in operation in Europe), Saint Paul's Anglican Cathedral. Malta excursions: Mdina 30 min away ("Silent City", former fortified medieval capital, preserved atmosphere), neighbouring Rabat (paleochristian catacombs), Mosta (Rotunda dome 4th largest in the world 67m), Marsaxlokk (picturesque port, Sunday fish market), Blue Grotto (sea cave accessible by boat), Hagar Qim and Mnajdra (UNESCO, megalithic temples 3600 BC = among the oldest standing stone monuments in the world, older than the pyramids), Gozo 25 min by ferry (more rural neighbouring island, Citadel of Victoria, more beautiful beaches), Comino (tiny island with photogenic but congested turquoise Blue Lagoon). Try pastizzi (savoury pastries with cheese or chickpeas, signature of Malta), Fenkata rabbit stew (rabbit in red wine, national dish), ftira (sandwich on flatbread), Cisk lager (national beer), Kinnie (bitter orange and herb soda). Malta International airport (MLA).
iles-cook:en: The Cook Islands are a New Zealand archipelago of 15 islands in free association: 17,000 inhabitants, capital Avarua on Rarotonga (the main island). Independent state but New Zealand citizenship. November is my favourite month (8.2/10): 21-22°C, 38% rain, dry season. From May to October, it's the dry season: 36-56% rainfall, temperatures 20-25°C, steady trade winds. From November to April, it's the wet season: 51-66% rainfall, heavy heat, cyclone risk (Pat 2010). Highlights: Rarotonga (main island, 32 km circumference, Cross-Island Track 6h hike), Aitutaki 50 min by plane (spectacular turquoise lagoon with 15 motus, considered one of the most beautiful in the South Pacific), Atiu (limestone caves, local coffee), Mangaia (the oldest island in the Pacific). Activities: diving and snorkelling, kayaking in the Aitutaki lagoon, hiking on Rarotonga, seabird observation. Punanga Nui Market in Avarua on Saturday. Cook Maori community (Cook Islands Maori language). Cuisine: ika mata (raw fish in coconut milk, Cook version), poké (taro), grilled fish. Direct flight from Auckland in 4h (Air New Zealand) or Los Angeles. Very good value for money vs French Polynesia: half-price, more relaxed atmosphere. Currency: NZ Dollar.
polynesie:en: French Polynesia covers 5 archipelagos over 5 million km² (the size of Europe): 118 islands, 280,000 inhabitants. August is my favourite month (7.7/10): 23-27°C, 65% rain, dry season. From May to October is the relative dry season (steady trade winds, calmer sea), from November to April is the wet season (cyclones possible). The 5 archipelagos: Society Islands (Tahiti, Moorea, Bora Bora, Raiatea, Huahine, Maupiti, the most visited), Tuamotu (atolls, world-class diving Rangiroa and Fakarava), Marquesas (Hiva Oa where Gauguin and Brel are buried, Nuku Hiva, rocky cathedral landscapes, isolated and wild), Australs (Rapa, Raivavae, humpback whales), Gambier (Mangareva, black pearls, the most remote). Major activities: diving (Tuamotu atoll passes = among the best in the world, manta rays, hammerhead sharks, tiger sharks), surf (Teahupoo at Tahiti = wave of world reputation, Paris 2024 Olympics here), swimming with humpback whales (July-October in several archipelagos), hiking (Marquesas). Air Tahiti serves all the islands (Air Tahiti Pass 60-200€ between islands). Plan 3-4 weeks minimum to see 2-3 archipelagos. Direct flight Paris-Papeete via LA or via Tokyo. Try raw fish in coconut milk, ma'a tinito, fei.
nouvelle-caledonie:en: New Caledonia (Kanaky in Melanesian), French overseas community in the south-western Pacific 1500 km east of Australia: 270,000 inhabitants on 18,575 km² (Grande Terre 16,372 km² main + Loyalty Islands Lifou-Maré-Ouvéa + Île des Pins). Sensitive political status = France colonised the archipelago in 1853 under Napoleon III, lasting independentist-loyalist conflicts: 3 independence referendums 2018-2020-2021 rejected independence (but the 3rd contested as boycotted by Kanak independentists), May 2024 riots caused 14 deaths + Nouméa devastation, political dialogue to come 2025-2026 on self-determination. Coral reef = one of the largest barrier reefs in the world, 2nd after Australia's, UNESCO 2008. November is my favourite month (8.5/10): 19-26°C, 56% rain, this is the ideal austral spring. September-October-December stay excellent (7.9-8.4/10). From September to November, it's the ideal austral spring: 17-27°C, sea at 25°C, ideal temperatures. From December to March, it's the potential cyclone season: 21-30°C, 67-79% rain, moderate tropical cyclone risk. From June to August, it's the austral winter: 16-23°C, cooler 21°C waters, dry and cool, pleasant atmosphere. Highlights: Nouméa capital (100,000 inhabitants, French tropical Pacific atmosphere, Place des Cocotiers central square, Saint Joseph Cathedral 1893 neo-Romanesque, Tjibaou Cultural Centre 1998 signature Renzo Piano architecture in homage to the Kanak independentist leader Jean-Marie Tjibaou assassinated 1989 = major Pacific cultural signature), Anse Vata Beach (the main urban beach of Nouméa), Baie des Citrons Beach, Île aux Canards (5 min by shuttle from Anse Vata, snorkelling with reef accessible from the beach), Amédée Lighthouse (1865, 56m high, one of the tallest metal lighthouses in the world, accessible by boat, paradisiacal beach around), Île des Pins ("the most beautiful island in the world" according to many travel rankings, accessible 25 min by plane or 4h by ferry from Nouméa, Oro Bay turquoise natural pool signature visual of New Caledonia, Kanumera Bay, Saint Joseph Bay, accommodations rare and expensive 250-800€/night), Loyalty Islands (Lifou + Maré + Ouvéa + Tiga, 25 min by plane, more traditional Kanak atmosphere with dreh + nengone + iaai language), Ouvéa specifically = signature visual with its 25 km of white sand beach lining the turquoise lagoon = one of the most beautiful beaches in the world, Lifou Beach Easo + Trou de Bone, Maré Cliffs. Grande Terre interior: Grand Sud with nickel mines red-orange martian landscapes (New Caledonia = 4th world producer of nickel), East Coast traditional Kanak tribes, West Coast livestock and agriculture. Activities: diving and snorkelling (world-class, 2nd largest barrier reef, white-tip shark, manta rays, green turtles, dolphins), kitesurf and windsurf (steady trade winds), humpback whale safari (July-October, New Caledonia is on the migratory route of humpback whales, observation from the west coast), GR New Caledonia hiking. Try bougna (traditional Kanak dish = vegetables + meat + coconut milk cooked in banana leaf signature Melanesian culinary), chicken with coconut milk, lobster, raw fish in coconut milk, flying fox stew, Lifou IGP vanilla, local Manta + Number One beers, Ti'punch. Nouméa-La Tontouta airport (NOU).
noumea:en: Nouméa is the capital of New Caledonia (French overseas territory of the South Pacific): 100,000 inhabitants, "Nice of the Pacific" atmosphere, multicultural community (Europeans, Kanak Melanesians, Polynesians, Asians). November is my favourite month (8.5/10): 21-27°C, 43% rain, end of the austral spring, sea at 24°C. From May to November is the relative dry season: 33-47% rainfall, temperatures 17-26°C. From December to April, it's the wet season: 50-70% rainfall, heavy heat (24-29°C), cyclone risk (Niran 2021). Highlights: Anse Vata (main urban beach, lively), Baie des Citrons (other urban beach), Tjibaou Cultural Centre (Renzo Piano, dedicated to Kanak culture), Nouméa Market (Port Moselle, in the morning). Excursions: Île aux Canards (marked underwater path, accessible by 5-min shuttle), Ilot Maître (resort on islet), Amédée Lighthouse (day trip). The Caledonian lagoon is the largest closed lagoon in the world (UNESCO, 24,000 km²): diving and snorkelling on the 2nd largest barrier reef in the world. Loyalty Islands 1h by plane (Lifou, Maré, Ouvéa for the most beautiful beach). Grande Terre (main island): Yaté for nature, Rivière Bleue, Mont Panié. Try bougna (traditional Kanak dish), bichique. Direct flight Paris-Nouméa via Tokyo in 24h (Aircalin). Expensive (French Pacific island), but very beautiful destination.
turks-et-caicos:en: Turks and Caicos are a British overseas territory: 8 main islands, 38,000 inhabitants, beaches among the whitest in the world (Grace Bay regularly #1 in rankings). March is my favourite month (9.0/10): 24-26°C, 21% rain, turquoise sea at 25°C, end of the North American high season. From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 30-54% rainfall, hurricane risk (Irma and Maria 2017 devastated). From December to May, expect 24-27°C: American tourist high season, very high hotel prices (5-star 600-2000$/night). Highlights: Grace Bay Beach (12 km of beach at Providenciales, the signature of the archipelago), Chalk Sound (spectacular turquoise lagoon), Conch Bar Caves at Middle Caicos, Salt Cay (historic salt marsh island, migrating humpback whales January-March). Activities: diving and snorkelling (3rd largest barrier reef in the world), kitesurf at Long Bay, observation of humpback whales. Islands: Providenciales (the most touristy with main airport), Grand Turk (capital Cockburn Town), North/Middle Caicos (calmer), Salt Cay (the quietest). Economy: luxury tourism + offshore banking. Try conch (lambis) in all forms, hominy grits. Direct flight Providenciales from Paris via London-Miami. No income tax = favourite destination of American big shots.
niseko:en: Niseko is the most famous ski resort in Japan: 15m of snow per year (among the snowiest in the world), legendary powder ("Japow" = Japan powder), 2 million visitors per season, heavily frequented by Australians, Hong Kongers and Singaporeans. July is my favourite month (8.0/10): 17-25°C, 47% rain, mountain hiking, but Niseko is above all a winter destination. From December to March, expect -9/4°C and 47-79% precipitation (snow): ski high season, hotel prices x4-x5, Hirafu = main village full, booking 6 months ahead. From November and April are the shoulder seasons: piste opening mid-November, closing early May, pistes still skiable in spring. From May to October, station open for summer activities: mountain bike, golf, hiking towards Mount Yotei (1898m, the "Hokkaido Fuji"), rafting on Shiribetsu River. Resorts: 4 connected zones by Niseko United pass (Hirafu, Hanazono, Niseko Village, Annupuri), 2191 ha, 30 lifts, vertical drop 1000m. Off-piste legendary (back-country in cat ski or heli ski via local guides). Onsen characteristic of Hokkaido (outdoor rotenburo under the snow). Try Hokkaido seafood, miso ramen, mochi. Direct flight Tokyo (Sapporo Chitose) then 2h by bus or train + transfer (private services from airport).
hakuba:en: Hakuba, Japanese valley in Nagano prefecture (Japanese Alps): 9000 inhabitants at 700-1200m, town-valley that hosted the alpine events of the Nagano 1998 Winter Olympics. Composed of 10 distinct resorts connected by free shuttles with a single pass. June is my favourite month (7.6/10): 14-23°C, 58% rain in storms, Japanese alpine hiking, but Hakuba is above all a winter destination. From June to September, expect 9-29°C: calm summer season (above 1500m pleasant climate, in valley bottom 700m hot-humid). From December to March, expect -6/8°C and 51-73% precipitation often as snow (10m/season, among the snowiest resorts in the world, legendary "Japan Powder" climate): ski high season. 10 Hakuba Valley resorts: Happo-One (the main one, 26 pistes, former 1998 Olympic descent piste), Hakuba 47 and Goryu (snowboarders), Tsugaike (family, snowpark), Iwatake, Sanosaka, Norikura, Cortina (Mount Yari view), Hakuba Highland, Goryu Toomi. 1 Hakuba Valley pass for 200+ km of pistes. Off-piste legendary: Hakuba Backcountry attracts freeriders from around the world (Australians in the majority, they who popularised the Japan Powder), guides mandatory (fatal avalanches every season). Onsen characteristic: Mimizuku no Yu, Daionji, Hakuba Shio no Michi (rotenburo outdoor baths with view of the snow-covered summits). Audience: Japanese in the majority, Australians (40% of foreigners), Singaporeans, growing Europeans. Hotels: Hakuba Tokyu, Hotel Gracery, traditional ryokans (200-1500€/night). Try miso ramen, local soba, Nagano sake. Logistics: Hakuba 4h by train+bus from Tokyo (Shinkansen Nagano + 1h bus), 1h by car from Nagano.
nozawa-onsen:en: Nozawa Onsen, Japanese village ski resort in Nagano prefecture: 3500 inhabitants at 600m, one of the most traditional in Japan ("onsen village" literally). 13 free public hot springs ("soto-yu") managed by the inhabitants since the 8th century. August is my favourite month (8.1/10): 21-28°C, 53% rain, but Nozawa Onsen is above all a winter and bath destination. From June to September, expect 10-28°C: very calm summer season, traditional traditions (Dosojin Fire Festival on 15 January but preparation year-round). From December to March, expect -4/3°C and 53-79% precipitation often as snow (10m/season, among the snowiest resorts in Japan): ski high season. Pistes: 40 pistes Nozawa Onsen Ski Resort, altitude 565-1650m, vertical drop 1085m, 21 lifts. Off-piste: Nozawa offers excellent freeride terrain with access via Soyuyama and Yamabiko Field. 13 free public hot springs (onsen "soto-yu") in the village alleys, accessible to village residents and visitors (leave a small donation 100¥), temperature 40-50°C, separated by sex. The most famous: O-Yu (central, 19th-century traditional wooden architecture), Asagama-no-Yu (the hottest). Dosojin Fire Festival on 15 January (one of the 3 great fire festivals in Japan, men of 25 and 42 years in ritual duel to defend a giant wooden structure against villagers who try to set it on fire, spectacular). Audience: Japanese in the majority (Tokyoites on weekends), growing Australians, much more traditional atmosphere than Niseko (less English-speaking). Hotels: traditional ryokans (Yumoto, Nakajimaya), modern Auberges (Hotel Haus St. Anton), 100-500€/night. Try nozawana (traditional pickled vegetable), soba, Nagano miso ramen, Hokujo sake. Logistics: Nozawa Onsen 3h from Tokyo (Shinkansen Nagano 1h45 + 1h15 bus), 1h by bus from Nagano.
furano:en: Furano, town in Hokkaido 200 km north-east of Sapporo: 22,000 inhabitants in the heart of Hokkaido (north island of Japan), famous for its lavender fields in July-August (the largest in Asia, to see at Farm Tomita) and its renowned winter ski resort. July is my favourite month (8.1/10): 17-26°C, 48% rain, this is the full summer tourist season (lavender in full bloom from 20 June to 20 August, peak mid-July to mid-August, crowds of Japanese and Asian tourists for photos). From June to September, expect 5-26°C: summer high season (colourful flower fields Patchwork of nature, golf, cycling). From December to March, expect -11/3°C and 48-74% precipitation often as snow (12m/season, among the snowiest resorts in the world after Niseko, legendary "Japan Powder"): ski high season. Pistes: 23 pistes Furano Ski Resort (managed by Prince Hotels), altitude 235-1074m, vertical drop 839m, gondola access 8 min from the village. Off-piste Furano (with guide, crossroads of the Australians), Furano Backcountry very renowned. Asahi-dake 1h away (highest volcano in Hokkaido 2291m, ski touring). Try Furano melon (the most expensive melon in Japan, can reach 200€ for 1 superior quality melon), cantaloupe melon ice cream, white asparagus, Furano cheese (rare in Japan), Hokkaido sake. Audience: Japanese and Asians in the majority (summer lavender), Australians and Europeans in winter (skiing). Hotels: New Furano Prince Hotel, Furano Natulux Hotel, ryokans with onsen. Logistics: Furano 2h from Sapporo by train (from Sapporo Chitose Airport + Asahikawa train + Furano train), 1h from Asahikawa regional airport.
nagoya:en: Nagoya, 4th city of Japan: 2.3 million inhabitants in the heart of the Chubu region, industrial capital of the Japanese automobile industry (world headquarters of Toyota at neighbouring Toyota City, Mitsubishi, Yamaha). Often overlooked by tourists, useful stop between Tokyo (1h40 by Shinkansen) and Kyoto (35 min by Shinkansen). May is my favourite month (8.0/10): 14-24°C, 41% rain, ideal temperatures before the tsuyu monsoon June-July. From April to June, it's the tourist high season: 9-27°C, end of cherry blossoms in April. From June to mid-July, it's the tsuyu (rainy season). July-August drop to 6.7-6.9/10 due to the humid heatwave (23-32°C). From October to November, expect 7-22°C: excellent period, momiji (autumn colours). From December to February, expect 0-11°C and 18-24% rain: dry cold, snow rare. Highlights: Nagoya Castle (17th century, rebuilt after WWII bombings, keep with 2 gold dolphins on the roof "shachihoko" symbols of the city, undergoing reconversion to traditional wood since 2018), Atsuta Shrine (one of the most important Shinto shrines in Japan, guardian of the sacred sword Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi one of the 3 imperial treasures of Japan), Toyota Commemorative Museum (fascinating visit of the textile then automobile Toyota industry), Nagoya Science Museum (the largest planetarium in the world 35m diameter), Sakae Quarter (modern shopping centre, Oasis 21 floating architecture), Osu Quarter (traditional shopping street), Toyota Stadium (Rugby World Cup 2019, Football World Cup 2002). Excursions: Inuyama Castle 30 min away (the oldest Japanese wooden castle 1537), Magome and Tsumago on the former Nakasendo route (Edo-Kyoto), Takayama 2h by train (preserved Edo old town), Shirakawa-go (UNESCO, traditional gassho-zukuri thatched-roof houses). Try miso katsu (breaded pork in Nagoya miso sauce), tebasaki (fried chicken wings), hitsumabushi (grilled eel 3 ways), kishimen (flat noodles).
matsuyama:en: Matsuyama, capital of Shikoku (the smallest of the 4 main islands of Japan) on the Seto Inland Sea: 510,000 inhabitants. Largest city in Shikoku, cultural capital of the island 4 times on the list of the most beautiful castles in Japan. Place of the most famous hot spring in Japan = Dōgo Onsen = the oldest in operation in the country (3000 years according to tradition, mentioned in the "Nihon Shoki" imperial chronicle 720 AD). Direct inspiration of the Studio Ghibli animated film "Spirited Away" 2001 (Hayao Miyazaki was inspired by Dōgo Onsen for the spirit baths in the film). May is my favourite month (7.7/10): 14-25°C, 18% rain, ideal temperatures with wisteria + rose blooming. March-April-October-November stay excellent (7.4-7.6/10). From March to May and October-November, these are the best seasons (spring + autumn signatures of Japan, sakura cherry blossom in April, momiji red maple in November). From June to September, it's the wet + hot season: 19-31°C with 67-78% precipitation ("tsuyu" Japanese monsoon season June-July), potential typhoons August-October. From December to February, expect 0-13°C and 13-31% rain: relatively mild Japanese cold (Shikoku warmer than northern Honshu). Matsuyama highlights: Matsuyama Castle (Matsuyama-jō, 1602-1603, one of the 12 original castles of Japan not rebuilt = ensemble of castles surviving fire and WWII bombings = national treasures, perched on Mount Katsuyama 132m in the heart of the city, panorama over Matsuyama + Seto Inland Sea, access by cable car-chairlift or 30-min hike), Dōgo Onsen Honkan ("main bath", historic 1894 wooden building on 3 floors, the postcard image of Matsuyama and Studio Ghibli inspiration, thermal bathing in 2 baths "Bath of the Gods" + "Bath of the Spirits" alkaline water 42°C, signature traditional Japanese experience, Tama-no-yu imperial salon where Emperor Hirohito stayed, open to the public on cultural visit), Bocchan Karakuri Clock (in front of Dōgo Onsen, mechanical clock opening every hour with characters from "Botchan" 1906 novel by Natsume Sōseki signature of the city), Ishiteji Temple (one of the 88 temples of the Shikoku pilgrimage, signature Japanese cultural = pilgrim circuit of 1200 km traversed on foot 40-60 days by the henro in traditional white garb = signature spiritual of Shikoku heritage of the monk Kūkai 9th century), Bansuiso Villa (French neo-renaissance villa 1922 inspired by French châteaux), Shiki Memorial Museum (museum of the haiku poet Masaoka Shiki born in Matsuyama 1867 = father of modern haiku). Shikoku excursions: Naoshima Art Island 2h by train + ferry ("contemporary art island" UNESCO Tentative, Tadao Ando architecture signature museums, Yayoi Kusama Pumpkin giant pumpkin = viral Instagram image of Japan), Imabari (capital of Japanese shipbuilding, Shimanami Kaido bridges connecting Shikoku-Honshu = famous cycle path 70 km on 6 spectacular bridges), Kotohira (Konpira-san Shinto shrine), Kochi (other original Shikoku castle), Iya Valley (isolated valley with traditional liana bridges). Try tai-meshi (rice with real sea bream signature of Matsuyama), Iyokan (local Ehime orange), gokoku barley shochu, Yamanashi Japanese wines rare in Shikoku. Matsuyama airport (MYJ).
raja-ampat:en: Raja Ampat, archipelago in the extreme east of Indonesia in West Papua province: 1500 islands, 64,000 inhabitants, considered the world epicentre of marine biodiversity. Over 1500 species of fish, 700 species of molluscs, 75% of the coral species known in the world (versus 50% for the Australian Great Barrier Reef). Preserved by its extreme remoteness and difficulty of access. August is my favourite month (6.5/10): 23-28°C, 85% rain. Raja Ampat never tops 6.5/10 (equatorial tropical climate with frequent rains year-round, but short and warm rains). From October to April, it's the wetter season: 85-95% rainfall. From May to September, it's the relatively drier season: 82-95% (Raja Ampat is one of the wettest regions in Indonesia). Optimal diving visibility October-April (calmer water), but locals prefer June-September for the climate. Highlights / Main islands: Waigeo (the largest, capital Waisai), Misool (in the south, spectacular karst landscapes, Misool Eco Resort 5*+ reference), Salawati, Batanta, Kri Island (one of the main diving bases, world record 374 fish species identified on a single site Cape Kri by Gerald Allen 2012), Wayag (in the north, signature landscape of Raja Ampat = panorama from the summit over dozens of emerald karst peaks in turquoise sea, the postcard image of the archipelago, difficult access by boat 4-6h from Waisai), Pianemo (more accessible alternative to Wayag, similar panorama), Arborek (traditional village on stilts), Pulau Gam (birds of paradise = endemic), Birds of Paradise Wilson's and Red Bird of Paradise (early morning observation 4am in the jungle of Waigeo and Gam). Diving: Raja Ampat is top 1 worldwide for many divers and operators: cult sites Cape Kri, Sardine Reef, Manta Sandy, Blue Magic, Mike's Point, sometimes strong currents (intermediate-advanced level diving required), liveaboard 7-12 days = ideal formula (1500-5000€/person all inclusive from Sorong). Snorkelling exceptional too (visibility 20-30m, multi-coloured corals), accessible to non-divers from homestays on Kri or Mansuar. Accommodation: Misool Eco Resort 5*+ (1500-2500€/night), Papua Diving (Sorido Bay, Kri Eco Resort), Balinese homestays on Kri/Arborek (50-80€/night full board). Audience: advanced divers, underwater photographers, well-off ecotourists. Try ikan bakar, Papuan sago (local starch), sambal sagu. Logistics: Sorong airport (SOQ) at the western end of Papua, flights from Jakarta 4h via Makassar or Manado, then public ferry from Sorong port to Waisai 2h (135,000 IDR ≈ 8€) or private speedboat.
labuan-bajo:en: Labuan Bajo, gateway port to Komodo National Park on the island of Flores in Indonesia: 7000 inhabitants, transformed in a few years (since 2015) from a small fishing village into a major tourist hub. Designated by the Indonesian government as one of the "5 new Bali destinations" to decongest Balinese tourism (4 others: Lake Toba, Borobudur, Mandalika Lombok, Likupang). August is my favourite month (8.8/10): 22-29°C, 23% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From June to September, it's the dry season: 22-30°C, this is the tourist high season, hotel prices x2-x3, golden landscapes (burnt savannah). From December to March, it's the rainy season: 84-94% rainfall, sometimes rough sea, greener landscapes. Labuan Bajo highlights: Cunca Wulang Waterfall 30 min away (waterfall in the jungle, swimming, jumps), Cunca Rami Waterfall (other waterfall), Batu Cermin Cave ("Mirror Cave", cave with stalactites lit by filtering sun, marine fossils indicating that the cave was underwater), Paradise Bar (panoramic sunset over the bay). Essential excursions = this is why one comes to Labuan Bajo: Komodo National Park by 1-day or 2-3 day boat tour (with dragons at Komodo or Rinca, Padar viewpoint, Pink Beach, Manta Point, Kanawa Island), 3-7 day Liveaboard in the park (ideal formula for divers and the complete experience, 200-1000€/day depending on category). Diving: Labuan Bajo is one of the world bases for Komodo diving (Top 5 worldwide), renowned centres Manta Rhei, Wicked Diving, Dive Komodo. Standard 1-day excursion Komodo: 550,000-1 million IDR (35-65€/person) with BBQ lunch, snorkelling, dragons at Rinca (more accessible than Komodo), Padar viewpoint, Pink Beach. Surrounding islands: Kanawa, Kelor, Sebayur, Pink Beach, Bidadari. Flagship hotels: Plataran Komodo Resort, Ayana Komodo Resort, Sudamala Resort, Le Pirate Boatel (floating hotel). Try ikan bakar (grilled fish), nasi goreng, kolak (coconut milk fruit dessert), arak Flores (very strong local eau-de-vie). Audience: international divers, ecotourists, wealthy on private yachts (the bay of Labuan Bajo has become in recent years a luxury mooring). Logistics: Labuan Bajo Komodo airport (LBJ) with direct flights from Bali Denpasar (1h), Jakarta (2h30), Makassar.
bhutan:en: Bhutan, small Himalayan kingdom between India and China in the foothills of the eastern Himalayas: 770,000 inhabitants. Last Himalayan Buddhist kingdom (Sikkim Tibet was annexed by India 1975, Tibet by China 1950). Country unique in the world measuring Gross National Happiness (GNH) instead of GDP since 1972 (philosophy of King Jigme Singye Wangchuck). Tourism strictly regulated: permit mandatory + minimum tariff 200-250 USD/day all inclusive (accommodation-meals-transport-guide-permit), commitment to "low volume - high value tourism" to preserve culture and environment (unique policy in the world). From November to December are my favourite months (5.0-5.3/10): 0-19°C, 6-9% rain, clear sky, ideal temperatures. Bhutan never tops 5.3/10 (Himalayan climate with monsoon and cold winter). From October to November and March-May, it's the tourist high season: -3/22°C, the clearest sky to see the Himalayan summits. From June to September, it's the monsoon: 49-72% rainfall, constant mists, greener landscapes. From December to February, expect -7/16°C: winter cold, snow possible at higher altitudes, many passes closed but high season of the Tiger of the Snows = trekking in the snow-covered Himalayas. Highlights: Tiger's Nest Monastery (Paro Taktsang, "Tiger's Nest Monastery", the most sacred Buddhist site in Bhutan, monastery perched at 3120m on vertical cliff 900m above the Paro valley, founded 1692 on the place where the Indian sage Padmasambhava allegedly meditated 3 years riding a tigress, 4-5h round-trip hike from 2600m, one of the most famous postcard images of Asia), Punakha Dzong ("fortress-monastery" of Punakha 17th century at the confluence of the Mo Chhu and Pho Chhu rivers, one of the most beautiful dzongs in Bhutan, former capital of the kingdom), Thimphu (capital, 100,000 inhabitants, one of the only capitals in the world without traffic lights — the white-gloved traffic agents have become signature, Buddha Dordenma = gilded Buddha statue 51m on panoramic hill, Tashichho Dzong = royal palace and seat of government), Paro Valley (main tourist valley with unique airport, preserved bucolic atmosphere), Bumthang Valley (4 sacred valleys of central Bhutan, ancient monasteries), Trongsa Dzong (in the centre of the country, the largest dzong in Bhutan, monumental). Trekking: Snowman Trek = one of the most difficult treks in the world (24-25 days, 5 passes 5000m+, spectacular Himalayan landscapes). No McDonald's, Starbucks or KFC sign in Bhutan (strict cultural policy = no international chain accepted). Paro Airport: one of the most difficult airports to land in the world (only 8 pilots authorised, landing in turn in the middle of Himalayan mountains), direct flights from Delhi, Bangkok, Kathmandu only. Try ema datshi (national dish, chillies and local cheese), red rice (Bhutanese red rice), suja (Tibetan butter tea), local Druk 11000 beer.
nepal:en: Nepal (Nepal), Himalayan country between India and China (Tibet): 30 million inhabitants. Roof of the world = the country shelters 8 of the 14 summits over 8000m in the world: Everest 8849m, Kanchenjunga 8586m, Lhotse 8516m, Makalu 8485m, Cho Oyu 8188m, Dhaulagiri 8167m, Manaslu 8163m, Annapurna 8091m. Former Hindu monarchy until 2008 (massacre of the royal family by the crown prince 2001, abolition of monarchy 2008, Federal Democratic Republic). Capital Kathmandu (already covered by BDW). April is my favourite month (8.3/10): 14-25°C, 42% rain, ideal temperatures before the monsoon. October-November stay excellent (8.0-8.1/10), ideal for trekking (the clearest sky to see Everest and other summits). From March to May and October-November, these are the best trekking seasons: 5-26°C. From June to September, it's the monsoon: 78-100% rainfall, frequent landslides, summits hidden by fogs. From December to February, expect 5-21°C: winter cold, high-altitude passes closed, but dry climate in the plain = good for visiting cultural sites Kathmandu-Pokhara. Highlights: Kathmandu Valley (UNESCO 1979, 7 monumental sites: Durbar Square Kathmandu + Patan + Bhaktapur, Boudhanath and Swayambhunath stupas, Pashupatinath and Changu Narayan temples), Pokhara 7h by bus (see dedicated review), Sagarmatha National Park (UNESCO 1979, Everest park, base camp at 5364m accessible by 12-14 day trek from Lukla), Lumbini (UNESCO 1997, birthplace of the Buddha born 563 BC according to tradition, place of international pilgrimage), Chitwan National Park (UNESCO 1984, Terai plain Indian border, safari on elephant back or 4x4 to observe one-horned rhinoceros, rare tigers), Bardia National Park (less frequented more traditional park). Signature treks: Everest Base Camp (12-14 days, 5364m max altitude, Everest view from Kala Patthar 5550m, the most famous trek in the world), Annapurna Circuit (15-18 days, 5416m Thorung La pass), Annapurna Base Camp (10-12 days, 4130m), Langtang Valley (8-10 days, less frequented alternative), Manaslu Circuit (14-16 days, wilder). 2015 tragedy: 25 April 2015 earthquake magnitude 7.8 = 9000 deaths, historic sites devastated (Durbar Square partly rebuilt since). Try dal bhat (rice lentils, national dish, base of Himalayan diet), momos (dumplings), thukpa (Tibetan noodle soup), sel-roti (fried rice ring), tongba (Himalayan millet beer), masala chai. Kathmandu Tribhuvan airport (KTM).
myanmar:en: Myanmar (Burma), country in South-East Asia between India, China, Bangladesh, Thailand: 54 million inhabitants. Mysterious and closed country for decades under military junta (1962-2011), brief democratic opening 2011-2021 with Aung San Suu Kyi 1991 Nobel Peace Prize, then 1 February 2021 military coup which overthrew Suu Kyi (still imprisoned), civil war since. Tourism now inadvisable by France Diplomacy for security reasons (ongoing fighting in some regions, international sanctions, heavy military checks). From January to February are my favourite months (6.9/10): 14-32°C, 7-9% rain. Myanmar never tops 6.9/10 (hot tropical climate + pre-monsoon heatwave). From November to February, it's the ideal dry season: 14-33°C. From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 18-39°C (sometimes 45°C in the central dry plain), impossible to visit at noon. From June to October, it's the monsoon: 60-86% rainfall. Highlights: Bagan (one of the most impressive archaeological sites in the world, 3000 Buddhist stupas and temples 9th-13th centuries scattered over 100 km², overflown by hot air balloon at sunrise = signature experience of Myanmar before Cambodia Angkor, landscapes with morning mist + golden temples emerging unique in the world), Lake Inle (altitude lake 880m, Intha people = traditional fishermen who paddle on a single leg surrounded by the oar = unique technique in the world, floating villages on stilts, floating gardens, signature visual), Mandalay (already covered by BDW, former royal capital), Shwedagon Pagoda in Yangon (already covered, the most sacred golden stupa in Myanmar), Mount Kyaiktiyo ("Golden Rock", miraculously balanced rock on cliff covered with gold leaves, major pilgrimage place), Pindaya Caves (limestone caves with 8000 Buddha statues accumulated over 200 years), Mrauk U (former 15th-century royal capital in the western Rakhine State = Rohingya conflict zone, inadvisable), Hpa-an (karst landscapes in the south-east, terrestrial alternative to Halong Bay similar to Ninh Binh Vietnam). Rohingya crisis: since 2017, systematic genocide against the Muslim Rohingya minority in Rakhine State, 1 million refugees in neighbouring Bangladesh, one of the worst humanitarian dramas of the 21st century. Important: extremely unstable political situation since February 2021, inadvisable France Diplomacy. If travel maintained: avoid central Yangon, Kachin/Karen/Shan/Rakhine fighting zones, strictly respect local instructions.
mongolie:en: Mongolia, landlocked country between Russia to the north and China to the south: 3.4 million inhabitants over 1,564,116 km² (density among the lowest in the world, 2 inhabitants/km²). Capital Ulaanbaatar, one of the coldest in the world. 30% of the population remains nomadic in yurts (ger), practises extensive livestock farming (sheep, goats, horses, yaks, Bactrian camels). Cradle of the Mongol empire of Genghis Khan (13th century, largest continental empire in history). June-August are my favourite months (8.1-8.5/10): 9-25°C, 31-40% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 4-25°C, mild climate, green landscapes ("flower season"), Naadam Festival mid-July (3 days of archery + traditional bökh wrestling + 30 km children's horse races, national festival UNESCO intangible heritage 2010). From December to February, expect -23/-9°C and 2-7% rain often as snow: extreme continental winter (sometimes -40°C, -50°C in remote areas, blizzard, dzud climate catastrophes killing millions of livestock). Regions to know: Gobi Desert in the south (1.3 million km², 2-humped Bactrian camels, Khongor sand dunes 100m, Yolyn Am icy gorge oasis even in summer, dinosaur fossils discovered by Roy Chapman Andrews in the 1920s inspired Indiana Jones), Orkhon Valley (UNESCO 2004, historic valley of the Mongol emperors, former capital Karakorum 13th century today ruined), Lake Khövsgöl ("Mother Mongolia" or "Blue Pearl", 2nd largest lake in Mongolia, 2% of world freshwater, pure waters, Tsaatan reindeer-breeding people in the north), Central Mongolian steppes (infinite landscapes, stay with nomadic locals in yurt = signature experience of Mongolia), Terelj National Park 70 km from Ulaanbaatar ("Easy Mongolia", spectacular landscapes Turtle Rock, giant equestrian statue of Genghis Khan 40m the largest equestrian statue in the world 2008), Erdene Zuu Monastery at Karakorum (the oldest Buddhist monastery in Mongolia 1585), Ulaanbaatar (capital, already covered). Try khorkhog (mutton cooked with hot stones in metal container, traditional nomadic dish), buuz (steam-cooked dumplings, Mongol equivalent of dim sum), khuushuur (fried turnover), aïrag (fermented mare's milk, alcoholic), suutei tsai (salted milk tea), Mongol vodka Chinggis (named after Genghis Khan).
ella:en: Ella, small mountain village in the heart of Sri Lanka in the Hill Country: 1500 inhabitants at 1041m altitude in the heart of the tea plantations. Has become one of the most popular stages of the Sri Lanka circuit for its picturesque train, its temperate climate and its accessible hikes. February-July are my favourite months (8.1-8.2/10): 16-28°C, 50-77% rain, ideal temperatures at altitude. From December to August, it's the tourist high season: 17-28°C, pleasant temperate climate despite occasional rain. From September to November, it's the 2nd inter-monsoon season: 65-89% rainfall, but stable temperatures (18-27°C, fresh in the morning/evening). Unique altitude climate: Ella never tops 28°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 16°C in minimum average, ideal tropical "Eternal Spring" climate that contrasts strongly with the stifling heat of Colombo and the coast. Highlights: Nine Arches Bridge ("Bridge of the Nine Arches", British colonial railway bridge 1921 in stone and concrete without iron, 91m long and 24m high, crossed by the cog train 4-5 times/day, the most viral image of Sri Lanka on Instagram, free access via 30-min path from Ella, panoramic view above the bridge), Little Adam's Peak (1141m, easy 30-min hike from Ella, panorama over signature Ella Gap, sunset observation point), Ella Rock (1041m, moderate 4h round-trip hike through tea plantations, the summit is not marked so local guide recommended 1500-3000 LKR ≈ 5-10€, panorama over Hill Country more impressive than Little Adam's Peak), Ravana Falls (waterfall on the Ella-Wellawaya road, 25m, glacial swimming possible), Demodara Loop (loop railway unique in the world where the railway crosses on 3 levels in the same mountain, designed by the British 1921 to gain 30m of vertical drop without tunnels), Halpewatte tea plantations (free visit with tasting, purchase of local Ceylon tea Ceylon Tea), Lipton's Seat 1h away (viewpoint 1970m where Sir Thomas Lipton himself came to sit and contemplate his tea empire created 1890, hairpin road access, 6 km uphill hike from Dambatenne tea factory). Kandy-Ella or Nuwara Eliya-Ella train = the signature experience (3-7h depending on departure, one of the most picturesque trains in the world, view of terraced tea plantations, viaducts, waterfalls, derisory price 1000-3000 LKR for 1st class Observation Car, ticket to buy in advance as full), Adam's Peak 4h away (Sri Pada, 2243m, sacred mountain for 4 religions — Buddhists/Hindus/Muslims/Christians — footprint of Buddha/Shiva/Adam depending on the religion, 5500 steps night ascent for sunrise at the summit December-May, marking spiritual experience). Try local Ceylon tea (Pekoe or Orange Pekoe), kottu roti, rice and curry, hoppers, fresh seafood (fish brought from the coast), Lion Lager beers. Logistics: Ella 7h from Colombo by train (via Kandy + Nuwara Eliya), 2h by bus from Wellawaya south-east side.
cambodge:en: Cambodia (Kingdom of Cambodia), country in South-East Asia between Vietnam, Laos, Thailand: 16.5 million inhabitants. Constitutional kingdom, King Norodom Sihamoni since 2004. Country marked by the genocide of the Khmer Rouge 1975-1979 (Pol Pot, 2 million deaths or 25% of the population, one of the worst genocides of the 20th century, intellectual elite systematically murdered), reconstruction since 1993. Capital Phnom Penh, main attraction Angkor Wat. January is my favourite month (6.9/10): 22-31°C, 12% rain, this is the ideal dry season. Cambodia never tops 6.9/10 (hot tropical climate). From November to March, it's the tourist high season: 22-33°C, dry climate and cool in the morning (sometimes 18°C in December, surprisingly cool for tropical Asia). From March to May, it's the pre-monsoon heatwave: 25-36°C (sometimes 42°C+), impossible to visit Angkor at midday without risk of heatstroke. From May to October, it's the monsoon: 65-92% rainfall, but milder temperatures (24-33°C) and the greenest landscapes. Highlights: Angkor (UNESCO 1992, the largest religious complex in the world 400 km², 1000 Khmer temples 9th-15th century, capital of the Khmer empire 9-15M inhabitants at its 12th-century apogee = the largest pre-industrial city in history, rediscovered by Henri Mouhot 1860 even if never forgotten by the locals, already covered by BDW Siem Reap), Angkor Wat (the signature temple, 162m × 215m, the largest religious monument in the world, dedicated to Vishnu then Buddha, on the Cambodian national flag = the only time a monument features on a flag in the world), Angkor Thom (fortified city 9 km², Bayon temple with 216 giant faces), Ta Prohm ("Tomb Raider Temple" 2001, giant kapok roots invading the temple), Banteay Srei ("woman's fortress", the most delicate pink sandstone temple). Phnom Penh capital (1.7 million inhabitants, urban Cambodian atmosphere, Royal Palace + Silver Pagoda, S-21 Genocide Museum = former Tuol Sleng prison of the Khmer Rouge where 17,000 people were tortured, poignant testimony, Killing Fields Choeung Ek 15 km away where 9000 bodies were exhumed, memorial with stupa containing 8000 skulls), Battambang 4h away (already covered by BDW), Sihanoukville and Koh Rong Islands (Cambodian beaches, Sihanoukville in full Chinese boom-bust 2017-2020 discouraging, Koh Rong remains paradisiacal), Kampot (charming French colonial town in the south, famous for its IGP black pepper), Kep ("crab capital" and flower), Mondulkiri and Ratanakiri (wild provinces in the north-east, ethical elephant sanctuaries). Try amok (fish curry in banana leaf, national dish), beef lok lak, num pang sandwich (French heritage), Cambodian pho, prahok (signature fermented fish paste), bia angkor.
laos:en: Laos (Lao People's Democratic Republic), landlocked country in South-East Asia between Vietnam, Cambodia, Thailand, Myanmar, China: 7.5 million inhabitants (the least populated in continental South-East Asia). 65% Theravada Buddhist country, ruled by the Lao Communist Party since 1975. Nicknamed "Vietnam before mass tourism" for its preserved atmosphere and quiet pace. Laos received more bombs during the Vietnam War than the entire Second World War combined (Secret War 1964-1973 = 2 million tonnes of US bombs dropped or 1 air sortie every 8 min for 9 years, 80 million unexploded "bombies" cluster bombs still present today = main cause of accidental mortality in some rural regions). February is my favourite month (9.1/10): 17-32°C, 1% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January-March stay excellent (8.5-9.0/10). From November to February, it's the cool dry season: 14-32°C. From March to April, expect 21-37°C: pre-monsoon heatwave + agricultural slash-and-burn polluting the atmosphere of the north ("Burning Season"). From May to October, it's the monsoon: 39-86% rainfall, the greenest landscapes. Highlights: Luang Prabang (UNESCO 1995, one of the most beautiful towns in South-East Asia, former royal capital of Laos until 1975, preserved Franco-colonial atmosphere mixed with Buddhist architecture, 33 wat temples, morning alms ceremony 5:30am with saffron monks parading in single file to receive offerings from the faithful, already covered by BDW), Vang Vieng 4h south of Luang Prabang (spectacular karst landscapes similar to terrestrial Halong Bay, Nam Song river, tubing = inflatable rubber-ring descent signature of backpackers since 2000, sometimes excessive and dangerous prohibition of bars on the river since 2012, today wiser and family-friendly), Vientiane (calm and bohemian capital, already covered by BDW), Bolaven Plateau in the south (multiple waterfalls, Bolaven coffee plantations highlands 1200m), 4000 Islands (Si Phan Don, Mekong archipelago Cambodian border, Irrawaddy dolphins, extremely tranquil atmosphere), Plain of Jars at Phonsavan (UNESCO 2019, archaeological plain with 2000 megalithic stone jars 2500-3000 years of unknown origin, still debated theory: probable funeral ossuaries), Wat Phou (UNESCO 2001, pre-Angkorian Khmer sanctuary 5th-8th in the south of the country). Try laap (chopped meat salad, Lao national dish), tam mak hoong (spicy papaya salad), khao niaw (typical Lao hand-eaten sticky rice), or-lam, sai oua sausage, Beer Lao (the national pride, 90% of the national market).
philippines:en: Philippines (Pilipinas), archipelagic country in South-East Asia: 113 million inhabitants on 7641 islands. 3rd English-speaking country in the world by number of speakers (American colonial heritage 1898-1946), 80% Christians (Spanish colonial heritage 1565-1898) = only majority Christian country in East Asia with East Timor. Unique mix of Malay + Spanish + American + Asian cultures. January is my favourite month (8.3/10): 22-29°C, 8% rain, this is the ideal dry season. February-March-December stay excellent (8.0-8.1/10). From November to May, it's the dry season "tag-init" + "tag-tagaraw" hot summer: 22-32°C, sea at 27°C, this is the tourist high season. From June to October, it's the wet + cyclone season "tag-ulan": 47-77% rainfall, real typhoon risk. Highlights / Regions: Luzon (see dedicated review, main island), Visayas central region (paradisiacal archipelagos): Cebu, Bohol with Chocolate Hills = 1268 grassy conical hills browning in the dry season unique signature visual in the world + Philippine tarsiers = 10 cm primates the smallest endemic primates in the world, Negros, Panay with Boracay (already covered), Siquijor, Leyte, Samar, Palawan western region (already covered = El Nido + Coron + Puerto Princesa = top world karst landscapes), Mindanao southern region (inadvisable for Abu Sayyaf-MILF Islamist terrorism, but Davao + Siargao = capital of Filipino surf are accessible). Signature activities: Sulu Sea diving (one of the world cradles of marine biodiversity, Tubbataha Reefs UNESCO 1993 in the Sulu Sea = top 10 worldwide for diving but accessible only by liveaboard cruise March-June, Apo Reef, Anilao = capital of underwater macro-photography), swimming with whale sharks (Donsol = ethical November-May vs Oslob = controversial because sharks artificially fed), Siargao surf Cloud 9 legendary spot, archipelago sailing (rental of bangka = traditional Filipino boat with outriggers), Filipino festivals = among the most colourful in Asia: Sinulog Cebu January (1 million spectators), Ati-Atihan Kalibo, Pahiyas Quezon May (façades covered with fruits-vegetables-leaves signature), MassKara Bacolod October ("festival of smiles"). Safety: Mindanao + Sulu archipelago inadvisable (Abu Sayyaf-MILF Islamist terrorism, Western kidnappings), but Luzon-Visayas-Palawan globally safe, urban crime Manila with dangerous Tondo quarters. Filipino hospitality = legendary, nicknamed "Pearl of the Orient", most welcoming people in Asia according to many traveller rankings. Try adobo (national dish), sinigang (sour soup), lechon (roast pig), pancit (noodles), halo-halo (icy dessert), San Miguel beer ("SMB" Filipino institution). Main airport Manila NAIA (MNL).
luzon:en: Luzon, largest island of the Philippines and the 17th largest island in the world: 50 million inhabitants on 109,965 km² = 50% of the total Filipino population. Main island sheltering the capital Manila + Quezon City + spectacular landscapes: Banaue UNESCO terraced rice paddies + Palawan beaches (technically Palawan = other island administratively) + active volcanoes (Pinatubo 1991 explosion 2nd most powerful of the 20th century). March is my favourite month (8.1/10): 22-29°C, 9% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January-February-April-December stay excellent (7.7-7.9/10). From November to May, it's the dry season "tag-init": 19-32°C, this is the tourist high season. From June to October, it's the active cyclone season "tag-ulan": 50-80% rainfall, real typhoon risk (Philippines = country in the world most affected by typhoons, 20+ per year of which 5 major on average, Yolanda/Haiyan 2013 = 6300 deaths in neighbouring Visayas). Luzon highlights: Manila capital (already covered by BDW, 14 million inhabitants in urban area, chaotic-cosmopolitan-polluted megacity), Banaue Rice Terraces (UNESCO 1995, Ifugao terraced rice paddies built 2000 years ago at 1500m altitude by indigenous Cordilleras peoples, nicknamed "8th wonder of the world" by Filipinos, spectacular landscapes covering 10,000 km² mountainous, Batad village = the most picturesque accessible 30 min hike from Banaue town, Bangaan, Hapao, in danger according to UNESCO as the rice paddies collapse due to lack of maintenance from peasants leaving for the city), Sagada (Cordillera mountains, Igorot indigenous people, hanging coffins = ancestral funeral tradition unique in the world of coffins hung from cliffs, cultural signature), Vigan (UNESCO 1999, preserved 17th-19th century Spanish colonial town, one of the only completely preserved Hispanic colonial towns in Asia, unique Hispano-Filipino atmosphere), Bohol Chocolate Hills (technically Bohol = Visayas, but often included Philippines tour from Manila), Mount Pinatubo (active volcano June 1991 eruption = 2nd most powerful of the 20th century after Mt Katmai 1912, turquoise blue crater lake visible today, 4h hike one way from Capas + 4x4), Mt Mayon (perfectly conical volcano 2462m "most beautiful volcanic cone in the world" according to Filipinos, in Albay province south-Luzon), Donsol (whale shark snorkelling with whale sharks November-May, ethical alternative to Oslob), Batanes Islands (extreme north archipelago, improbable Scottish-Irish atmosphere in tropical, lush green hill landscapes, unique Ivatan peoples), Pagsanjan Falls (waterfalls by traditional dugout boat "shooting the rapids" signature). Safety: Mindanao (other island in the south) inadvisable for Abu Sayyaf Islamist terrorism, but Luzon globally safe, urban crime vigilance Manila. Try adobo (chicken/pork in vinegar-soy sauce, national dish), lechon (roast pig), sinigang (sour tamarind soup), halo-halo (multi-layer icy dessert), San Miguel beer (institution), Tanduay Filipino rum. Manila NAIA (MNL) + Clark Pampanga (CRK) airports.
ipoh:en: Ipoh, capital of Perak state in north-western peninsular Malaysia: 660,000 inhabitants in urban area, 3rd largest Malaysian city after Kuala Lumpur and George Town Penang. Former tin mining centre 19th-20th century when Malaysia was the world's leading tin producer, preserved British colonial architectural heritage + unique Sino-Malay atmosphere thanks to massive 19th-century Chinese immigration for the mines. Town in tourist renaissance 2010-2025 thanks to signature street food + traditional café. February is my favourite month (6.9/10): 22-32°C, 19% rain, this is the relative dry season. Ipoh never tops 6.9/10 (humid hot equatorial climate year-round). From January to March-June-July-December, these are the best seasons: 22-33°C, moderate humid tropical climate. From April to May-October-November, it's the wet season: 36-43% rainfall. Highlights: Ipoh Old Town ("Old Town", preserved 19th-20th century British colonial architecture with Sino-Malay atmosphere, peranakan colourful shophouse façades, "Mural Art Trail" mural art since 2014 by Lithuanian artist Ernest Zacharevic = signature tourist of Ipoh = circuit of 7 signature street art frescoes in the city centre comparable to neighbouring Penang), Concubine Lane (Lorong Panglima, picturesque alley peranakan shophouses converted into trendy boutiques-cafés-restaurants since 2015), Birch Memorial Clock Tower (1909, tribute to British resident J.W.W. Birch assassinated by Malay nationalists 1875), Ipoh Railway Station (1917 signature Mughal-British architecture, nicknamed "Taj Mahal of Ipoh"), Town Hall (1916), Sam Poh Tong Temple (Buddhist-cave temple sculpted in a limestone cliff, one of the most impressive in Malaysia, Buddha statues + sacred turtle gardens), Kek Lok Tong (other limestone-cave temple with landscaped ponds), Perak Cave Temple (Perak Tong, cave-temple with 600 steps to the panoramic 100m summit), Lost World of Tambun (amusement park with natural hot springs + tigers in sanctuary), Kellie's Castle 30 min away (unfinished Scottish castle 1915 of William Kellie Smith in the Malaysian jungle = unique Gothic-tropical atmosphere in the world), Cameron Highlands 2h away (highlands at 1500m, Boh Tea tea plantations + preserved British colonial residences, temperate climate 15-25°C). Ipoh cuisine = major signature of the city: Ipoh white coffee ("Ipoh kopi putih" Malaysian-style coffee roasted with margarine then sweetened with condensed milk, signature culinary emblem of Ipoh, to taste at the legendary café Sin Yoon Loong or Nam Heong 1958), chicken rice Ipoh (different from Hainan, famous for "nga choi kai" bean sprouts), Hor fun (flat noodles in clear broth signature of Ipoh), dim sum Ipoh (one of the best outside Hong Kong according to local Chinese), caramel custard Sino-Malay, tau fu fa (sweetened soya milk). Excursions: Cameron Highlands 2h away (highly recommended, fresh tropical alternative), Kuala Lumpur 2h by train (already covered), Penang 2h away (already covered), Pangkor Island 1h30 away (beach island). Logistics: Ipoh Sultan Azlan Shah airport (IPH) modest, modern direct ETS Kuala Lumpur-Ipoh train 2h.
porto-rico:en: Puerto Rico is a US territory in the Caribbean: 3 million inhabitants, official Spanish + English, US dollar, beaches, tropical forest (El Yunque). January is my favourite month (8.3/10): 22-28°C, 47% rain, end of the American high season, mild temperatures. From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 56-74% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Maria 2017 category 4 devastated the island). From December to April, expect 22-29°C: American high season, prices x2-x3. Highlights: San Juan (capital, Old San Juan UNESCO, El Morro and San Cristóbal 16th-century Spanish forts), El Yunque National Forest (the only tropical forest in the US National Forests system, Mount Britton 1075m, La Mina Falls), Vieques and Culebra (1h islands by ferry from Fajardo, Flamenco Beach and Playa Carlos Rosario the most beautiful beaches), Bioluminescent Bay at Vieques (Mosquito Bay, one of the brightest in the world, night kayaking), Ponce (colonial old town, Toro Negro park), Camuy (limestone caves). Cuisine: mofongo (mashed plantain), arroz con gandules, tostones, lechón asado, piña colada (cocktail invented in San Juan in 1954). Bacardí (rum, distillery visitable near San Juan). Direct flight Paris-San Juan in 9h.
san-juan:en: San Juan, capital of Puerto Rico on the north-east coast: 320,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 2.3 million in urban area. Puerto Rico = unincorporated US territory since 1898 (passage from Spain to the USA after the Spanish-American war), unique political status: US citizens with US passports but without presidential voting rights or representation in Congress, 4 referendums on independence or statehood since 1967 without change. Unique Caribbean Hispanic atmosphere mixed with American influence. Hard hit by Hurricane Maria in September 2017 (3000 deaths, devastated infrastructure, slow reconstruction). January is my favourite month (8.3/10): 22-28°C, 41% rain, this is the ideal dry season. December-February stay excellent (7.9-8.3/10). From December to April, it's the tourist high season: 22-30°C, sea at 27°C. From June to October, it's the active cyclone season: 51-72% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Puerto Rico is on the Caribbean "Hurricane Alley"). From September, it's the cyclone peak. San Juan highlights: Old San Juan (Old San Juan, Viejo San Juan, UNESCO 1983 Spanish colonial old town, one of the oldest European cities in the New World founded 1521, paved alleys in blue adoquines = cobalt-blue cobblestones imported from Spain 16th-17th, colonial houses with pastel pink/yellow/blue/green façades), Castillo San Felipe del Morro ("El Morro", 16th-18th century Spanish fortress on the promontory defending the entrance to the bay of San Juan, one of the most imposing Spanish fortifications in the Americas, 6 levels, 360° panorama), Castillo San Cristóbal (other 18th-century fortress, larger than El Morro, defended the city on the land side, secret tunnels), Cathedral of San Juan (1521, contains the tomb of the conquistador Juan Ponce de León founder of Puerto Rico), Plaza de Armas and Plaza Colón (lively colonial squares), Paseo de la Princesa (seaside promenade in the ramparts), Condado Quarter (modern seafront with hotels, urban beach, restaurants), Isla Verde Quarter (longer urban beach, tropical atmosphere), Santurce Quarter (street art, local nightlife). Puerto Rico excursions: El Yunque National Forest 1h away (the only tropical forest in the American National Park system, waterfall hiking), Bioluminescent Bay (Mosquito Bay on the island of Vieques = one of the brightest bioluminescent bays in the world, night kayaking with Dinoflagellate micro-organisms that light up at movement), Vieques and Culebra neighbouring islands (beaches among the most beautiful in the Caribbean, Flamenco Beach Culebra = top 5 worldwide, accessible by 1h ferry from Fajardo), Cabo Rojo south-west (spectacular cliffs, pink salt pans), Camuy Caves (3rd largest cave system in the world), Ponce in the south ("Pearl of the South", colonial + Art Deco architecture). Try mofongo (plantain mash, Puerto Rican national dish), arroz con gandules (rice with pigeon peas), lechón asado (spit-roasted pork), pasteles, alcapurrias, tostones, piña colada (cocktail invented in San Juan in 1954), Medalla light beer (national beer), Puerto Rican coffee. Luis Muñoz Marín San Juan airport (SJU), the largest in the Caribbean after the Dominican Republic.
saint-barthelemy:en: Saint Barthélemy ("Saint-Barth" or "St. Barts" in English), French overseas community in the Lesser Antilles: 25 km², 10,000 inhabitants, one of the smartest and most expensive destinations in the Caribbean (the "tropical Saint-Tropez"). March is my favourite month (8.7/10): 24-25°C, 25% rain, sea at 26°C, end of the high season. From December to April, it's the tourist high season: 24-26°C, 25-42% rain, extreme hotel prices (Hôtel Manapany or Cheval Blanc Saint-Barth 1500-5000€/night, private villas 5000-50,000€/week), bookings 1 year ahead for Christmas/New Year (when the yacht of Bezos/Murdoch and the Russians dock at Gustavia, paparazzi on the lookout). From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 35-59% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Irma 2017 category 5 devastated the island at 70%), many hotels close September-October. Highlights: Gustavia (capital, yacht harbour, former Swedish colony 1784-1878 = French name/Scandinavian influence in the architecture, luxury boutiques Hermès/Cartier/Vuitton), Beaches: Saline (wild, no facilities), Gouverneur (wild), Saint-Jean (lively with Eden Rock, signature hotel, spectacular plane landing just above), Anse Colombier (accessible only on foot 30 min or boat, calm beach), Shell Beach (shells, in Gustavia centre), Lorient (surf), Flamands. Landing at St. Jean = one of the most dangerous in the world (650m runway, abrupt descent above the col de la Tourmente). Try grilled lobster, accras, ti-punch. Flight from Saint-Martin (10 min by plane) after direct flight Paris-Saint-Martin.
cayman-islands:en: The Cayman Islands are a British overseas territory: 3 islands (Grand Cayman, Cayman Brac, Little Cayman), 65,000 inhabitants, tax haven (one of the largest offshore banking centres in the world). January-February are my favourite months (8.0/10): 25-28°C, 37-47% rain, sea at 26°C, steady trade winds. From June to October, it's the cyclone season: 53-68% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Ivan 2004 category 4 devastated). From December to March, expect 25-28°C: American high season, very high hotel prices (tax haven = wealthy clientele). Highlights: Seven Mile Beach (signature beach of Grand Cayman, ranked among the most beautiful in the Caribbean), Stingray City (sandbank where one swims with semi-domesticated stingrays, boat excursion), Bioluminescent Bay at Rum Point (bioluminescent plankton at night), Hell (black limestone rocky formation with Hell Town post), George Town (capital, cruise port, duty-free shopping). Cayman Brac = cliffs, diving. Little Cayman = the calmest (170 inhabitants), Bloody Bay Wall for diving. Diving: among the best spots in the world (Bloody Bay Wall, Kittiwake wreck). Try conch fritters, turtle stew (Cayman Turtle Centre breeding). Currency: Cayman Dollar (KYD = 1.20 USD). Direct flight from London or Miami. Very expensive (tax haven).
bermudes:en: Bermuda is a British overseas territory in the North Atlantic (1000 km from North Carolina, closer to Halifax than to Miami): 138 islands, 64,000 inhabitants. May is my favourite month (8.3/10): 21-22°C, 35% rain, sea at 21°C (cooler than the Caribbean), end of spring. From June to August, expect 24-28°C: pleasant summer, sea at 26°C. From September to November, it's the hurricane season: 49-54% rainfall, real risk (Bermuda outside the main Caribbean trajectory but can be affected). From December to April, expect 18-22°C and 34-49% rain: too cool for swimming (sea at 18-20°C), but hotels at third-price, golf in full activity. Highlights: Hamilton (capital, colourful Front Street), Royal Naval Dockyard (West End, cruise port, National Museum of Bermuda), St. George's (UNESCO, former capital founded 1612, oldest continuous English colony in America), Crystal Caves (spectacular limestone caves), Horseshoe Bay Beach (pink sand, signature of Bermuda), Tobacco Bay (snorkelling). Pink-sand beaches: characteristic of Bermuda (foraminifera). Diving: 300+ wrecks in Bermudian waters ("world capital of wreck diving"). Bermuda Triangle = folklore subject, no tourist reality. Try fish chowder (national dish), rum swizzle, dark 'n' stormy. Outside the Caribbean zone = currency BMD (1:1 parity with USD). Very expensive: tax haven/financial.
bahamas:en: The Bahamas is the archipelago of 700 islands 80 km from Florida: Nassau (capital), Paradise Island (Atlantis Resort), Exumas (swimming pigs). March is my favourite month (8.7/10): 22-26°C, 27% rain, sea at 25°C, end of the American high season. From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 58-66% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Dorian 2019 category 5 devastated the Abacos). From December to May, expect 21-28°C: North American high season, hotel prices x2-x3. Highlights: Nassau (capital, Atlantis Resort at Paradise Island, Queen's Staircase, Pirates Museum), Junkanoo (Bahamian carnival, 26 December and 1 January). Islands to explore: Exumas (Pig Beach for the viral swimming pigs, Thunderball Grotto where James Bond filmed, Iguana Cay), Eleuthera (Pink Sands Beach, Glass Window cliffs), Andros (the largest island, Tongue of the Ocean diving, blue holes), Bimini (the closest to Florida, Hemingway lived here). Cruisers: Nassau is one of the most frequented ports in the world by cruises (several liners/day). Try conch fritters/salad (lambis), Bahama Mama cocktail, rock lobster. Currencies: USD accepted everywhere (Bahamian dollar 1:1 parity). Flight from Paris via Miami or Atlanta.
nassau:en: Nassau, capital of the Bahamas on New Providence Island: 280,000 inhabitants (70% of the country's population lives here). Bahamas archipelago = 700 islands in the tropical Atlantic, 80 km east of Florida. Independent since 1973 (Commonwealth realm), strong British cultural influence + Afro-Bahamian Caribbean. World's leading cruise destination. March is my favourite month (8.7/10): 22-26°C, 27% rain, sea at 25°C, end of the high season. From December to May, it's the tourist high season: 21-28°C, 27-54% rain, hotel prices x2-x3, daily liners (Atlantis Resort, Baha Mar Resort 5*+ with thousands of rooms). From June to November, it's the cyclone season: 54-66% rainfall, real hurricane risk (Dorian devastated Abacos and Grand Bahama in September 2019, 70 deaths, catastrophic damage). Nassau highlights: Bay Street (colonial commercial street, duty-free Cartier/Bulgari/Rolex), Parliament Square (pink colonial buildings), Fort Charlotte (18th century), Pirates of Nassau Museum (Nassau was the HQ of the "pirate golden age" Edward Teach "Blackbeard" early 18th century), John Watling's Distillery (rum), Fish Fry at Arawak Cay (local Bahamian street food), Cable Beach (long beach with hotels), Atlantis Paradise Island (themed resort with aquarium and waterpark). Beach excursions: Pig Beach (Big Major Cay, Exuma, ferry/private plane, beach with viral swimming wild pigs Instagram), Eleuthera and Harbour Island (pink sand unique in the world Pink Sand Beach), Exuma Cays (catamaran cruise), Andros (largest island, blue holes, diving). Try conch fritters/salad/cracked, rock lobster, johnnycake, sky juice (gin coconut cocktail), Kalik beer (Bahamian pride).
roatan:en: Roatán, the largest of the Honduras Bay Islands in the Caribbean Sea: 80,000 inhabitants over 65 km long and 5 km wide. Former British colony 1742-1859 then integrated into Spanish-speaking Honduras while keeping English as the main language (Caribbean English creole unique to the Bay Islands), British Caribbean atmosphere distinct from the rest of Hispanic Honduras. Renowned for diving on the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef (2nd largest barrier reef in the world after the Australian, 1000 km offshore from Mexico-Belize-Guatemala-Honduras). February is my favourite month (8.2/10): 22-29°C, 26% rain, this is the ideal dry season. March-April stay excellent (8.0-8.1/10). From January to April, it's the dry season: 22-31°C, sea at 26-27°C, this is the tourist high season (Americans mainly), hotel prices x2-x3. From May to October, expect 24-31°C with 29-49% rain. From November to December, expect 22-29°C with 50-67% rain: secondary cyclone season.
