Skiathos airport (JSI), famous for its spectacular landing above Megali Ammos beach = landing filmed by all Instagram avgeeks, similar to Saint Martin Princess Juliana.
kalamata:en: Kalamata, capital of western Peloponnese in Greece: 70,000 inhabitants on the Messenian Gulf. World capital of Kalamata olives (variety of black olive in the shape of an almond, with fleshy flesh, AOC since 1996 = the first olive AOC in Greece, exported worldwide) and Greek olive oil. Rebuilt after devastating 1986 earthquake (20 deaths, 80% of old buildings destroyed). June is my favourite month (9.1/10): 19-29°C, 16% rain, sea at 21°C, ideal temperatures. September stays excellent (8.9/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (22-33°C). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 10-33°C. From November to March, expect 6-20°C and 33-40% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting without crowds. Kalamata highlights: Old town (Kastro, on hill, 13th-15th century Venetian-Frankish castle, panorama), Kalamata Cathedral (Agios Apostoloi), Kalamata urban beach (3 km of pebbles), Central market (local institution to taste the Kalamata olives directly from the producers and IGP Kalamata dried figs), Military museum, Traditional costume museum. Essential Peloponnese excursions: Mystras 1h away (UNESCO, former Byzantine capital of the despotate of Morea 13th-15th, one of the most beautiful Byzantine medieval cities, impressive ruins on hill, 30 monasteries and palaces), Sparta 1h away (legendary ancient city-state, few visible vestiges), Olympia 2h away (UNESCO, place of the ancient Olympic Games 776 BC to 393 AD, palaestra, gymnasium, temple of Zeus, exceptional museum), Mycenae 2h away (UNESCO, capital of Agamemnon in the Iliad, Lion Gate, Treasury of Atreus), Epidaurus 3h away (UNESCO, best preserved ancient theatre in the world, exceptional acoustics still used for theatre in summer), Gythio and Mani peninsula 1h away ("Mani", the wildest region of Greece, unique stone Maniote towers, preserved beaches, austere atmosphere), Monemvasia 2h30 away ("Eastern Gibraltar", medieval fortified town on islet connected by sand strip, unique atmosphere in the Mediterranean, one of the postcard images of the Peloponnese), Pylos and Methoni 1h away (Venetian fortresses, Bay of Navarino where the Ottoman fleet was destroyed in 1827 = the decisive event of the Greek war of independence). Beaches: Voidokilia 1h away near Pylos (beach in regular arc circle in a semi-circular bay, one of the most beautiful beaches in Greece and Europe according to many rankings). Try IGP Kalamata olives (the famous black almond-shaped olives, to taste at the local producers), IGP Kalamata olive oil (the most prestigious in Greece with that of Crete), IGP Kalamata dried figs, thyme honey, lalagia (sweet beignets in olive oil), Mantineia AOC moscofilero white wine. Kalamata airport (KLX) with charter flights from Europe in season.
izmir:en: Izmir, 3rd city of Turkey and largest port on the Aegean Sea: 4.3 million inhabitants. Former Smyrna, one of the oldest cities in the world (5000 years of continuous history), cultural capital of the Turkish Aegean, more liberal and secular city than the Turkish average. Rebuilt after the great 1922 fire during the Greco-Turkish war that drove out the Greek population. May-June-September are my favourite months (9.0-9.2/10): 16-32°C, 7-21% rain, ideal temperatures. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the extreme heat (24-36°C). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 12-36°C. From November to March, expect 6-20°C and 12-36% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, ideal for visiting. Highlights: Kemeraltı Bazaar (the largest covered traditional bazaar in Turkey after the Grand Bazaar of Istanbul, 1639 boutiques, traditional oriental atmosphere, few tourists), Konak Square (central square with Clock Tower 1901 gift from Sultan Abdul Hamid II = signature visual of Izmir), Alsancak Quarter (night and culinary centre, terraces-restaurants), Kordon (8 km seaside promenade, one of the longest in the Aegean, to see at sunset), Asansör ("the elevator", public elevator 1907 connecting 2 quarters at different levels, invented by a Syrian Jewish industrialist Nesim Levi to facilitate the life of the poor, panoramic view at the top), Kadifekale ("Velvet Castle", ancient fortress on hill, panorama over Izmir and its gulf), Ancient agora of Izmir (Roman Smyrna 2nd century, excavations), Izmir Archaeological Museum. Essential excursions: Ephesus 1h away (UNESCO, one of the best-preserved ancient cities in the world, 2nd-century Library of Celsus, 25,000-seat Roman theatre, Temple of Artemis = one of the 7 ancient Wonders), House of the Virgin Mary at Selçuk (place of final retreat of Mary according to Catholic tradition, place of pilgrimage), Şirince picturesque village, Pergamon 2h away (UNESCO, ancient acropolis with altar of Zeus today in Berlin), Pamukkale 3h away, Çeşme and Alaçatı 1h away (kitesurf beach resorts), Bodrum 3h away. Try kumru (regional sandwich), boyoz (typical Izmir Sephardic Turkish pastry, heritage of the Iberian Jews driven out of Spain 1492 and settled in Smyrna), söğüş (sheep's head sandwich), midye dolma (mussels stuffed with rice), şambali (semolina cake), boza (fermented drink). Izmir Adnan Menderes airport (ADB).
alacati:en: Alaçatı, smart beach resort of the Çeşme peninsula in south-western Turkey: 9000 inhabitants. Former Greek town of Καλά Ακτή ("beautiful beaches"), deserted after the 1923 population exchange, rediscovered in the 2000s by upper-middle Istanbul classes. Preserved Greek-Ottoman architecture, bohemian-smart atmosphere, capital of windsurfing and kitesurfing in the eastern Mediterranean (constant thermal northerly winds May-September, the bay is sheltered by a peninsula creating an ideal water plan). June is my favourite month (9.7/10): 21-29°C, 7% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. September stays excellent (9.6/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (23-32°C, but refreshing thermal winds). From November to March, expect 7-19°C and 14-41% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many establishments close off-season. Highlights: Old centre (cobblestone streets between Greek-Ottoman stone houses with blue shutters and signature bougainvillea, unique Mediterranean atmosphere in Turkey, smart restaurants-bars-boutiques, Kemalpaşa Caddesi street is the main shopping street), Hacımemiş Mosque and Pazaryeri Mosque (former Greek churches converted into mosques 1923), Saturday market ("Cumartesi Pazarı", one of the most beautiful fresh markets in Turkey, Aegean fruits and vegetables, ot kınık herbs local specialties), Alaçatı beach (5 km from the old town, shallow bay 1m on 100m = ideal for windsurf and kite beginners, restaurants-cafés on the seafront, nightlife), Iliada Beach, Çark Plajı, Delikli Koy (wild coves to the south of the peninsula). Activities: windsurfing and kitesurfing (the main attraction, international schools: ASPC, Kalipso, Myga; constant thermal winds 18-25 knots daily in season, optimal sessions 11am-7pm, equipment rental 50-80€/day, beginner courses), SUP (paddle), diving (modest). Excursions: Çeşme 8 km away (other beach resort, ferry to Chios Greece 1h), Izmir 1h away, Ephesus 1h30 away. Audience: Istanbulites 30-45 years in the majority, Turkish expats, few Western foreigners (essentially Israelis). Restaurants: Asma Yaprağı (signature vine décor, refined Mediterranean cuisine), Köfteci Halil (traditional köfte balls), Imren Lokantası. Try kumru sandwich Çeşme, local mastic (neighbouring Chios = world capital of mastic), enginar dolması (stuffed artichokes), Aegean figs, Turkish raki. Izmir airport (ADB) 1h30 away.
transylvanie:en: Transylvania, historic region of central-northern Romania: 7 million inhabitants on 100,000 km², 40% of Romanian territory. Nicknamed "The land beyond the forests" ("Trans-silva" in Latin). World-famous thanks to Bram Stoker's novel Dracula 1897 (fictional vampire inspired by Prince Vlad III the Impaler 1431-1476 = cruel voivode of Wallachia), although Vlad lived little in Transylvania. Unique mix of Romanian + Hungarian + Saxon (German) + Tzigane cultures inherited from 1000 years of migrations. August is my favourite month (9.0/10): 13-26°C, 28% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (8.8/10). From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 7-26°C. From November to March, expect -7/9°C and 30-38% rain: harsh continental cold, abundant snow (ideal skiing Poiana Brașov, Predeal), unique atmosphere under mysterious fogs. Highlights: Brașov (the signature medieval Saxon city, council square with painted houses 17th-18th, Black Church 14th-century Gothic the largest Gothic church in Eastern Europe, paved alleys, preserved atmosphere), Sibiu (UNESCO 2004 European cultural capital 2007, square with houses with "eyes" = roof skylights resembling eyes, Germanic atmosphere preserved by the Transylvanian Saxons), Sighișoara (UNESCO 1999, 14th-century medieval fortified citadel the best preserved in Eastern Europe, birthplace of Vlad III the Impaler 1431, 14th-century clock tower), Cluj-Napoca (cultural capital of Transylvania, university town, 100,000 students), Bran Castle 30 km from Brașov ("Dracula's Castle" of tourism, in reality 14th-century Gothic-Renaissance medieval fortress, Queen Marie of Romania resided here early 20th, real Gothic atmosphere even if Dracula link essentially marketing), Peleș Castle at Sinaia (the most beautiful neo-Renaissance castle in the Balkans, summer residence of the kings of Romania 1873-1947, spectacular interiors), Corvin Castle at Hunedoara (more impressive 14th-century Gothic than Bran, real medieval fortified castle, place where Vlad III was imprisoned), Seven Saxon Fortresses UNESCO 1993 (Biertan, Viscri visited by future King Charles III who has a house in the village, Calnic, Darjiu, Prejmer, Saschiz, Valea Viilor), Bucovina Monasteries in the north (UNESCO, Voronet/Sucevita/Moldovita exterior frescoes 15th-16th painted in signature blue). Carpathians: mountain range crossing Transylvania, brown bears in high density (Transylvania has the largest brown bear population in Europe with 6500 bears, observation at Bran-Brașov-Zarnesti), Transfăgărășan (legendary 90 km road from Sibiu to Pitesti in the Făgăraș mountains 2042m, open June-October, Top Gear ranked it the best panoramic road in the world 2009). Try sarmale (stuffed cabbage), mămăligă (polenta), papanași (fresh cheese blueberry beignets), țuică (local plum eau-de-vie), Transylvanian Frankincense (Transylvanian red wine). Logistics: Cluj-Napoca (CLJ), Sibiu (SBZ), Brașov (BSV recent 2023) airports, direct Bucharest-Brașov train 2h30.
wild-atlantic-way:en: Wild Atlantic Way, coastal itinerary of Ireland on the Atlantic west coast: 2500 km of coastal roads from Inishowen Peninsula (Northern Ireland border) to Kinsale (Cork) passing through 9 counties (Donegal-Sligo-Mayo-Galway-Clare-Limerick-Kerry-Cork) = one of the longest coastal tourist itineraries in the world, created in 2014 by Tourism Ireland as a route of discovery of wild Ireland. May is my favourite month (7.1/10): 7-15°C, 40% rain, ideal temperatures with Irish spring blooming. June-August stay excellent (5.9-6.6/10) despite rains. From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 5-19°C, long Atlantic days (18-19h of day at the June solstice), ideal visiting conditions. From November to March, expect 1-9°C and 35-46% rain: mild for the North Atlantic thanks to the Gulf Stream, but windy and rainy, short days (8h day at the December solstice), mystical-melancholic atmosphere. Highlights on the Wild Atlantic Way (north to south): Inishowen Peninsula Donegal (extreme north, Cape Malin the northernmost point of Ireland, spectacular cliffs), Slieve League Donegal ("Cliffs of Slieve League", 596m = among the highest in Europe after Croaghaun Achill 688m, higher than Cliffs of Moher but less frequented, exceptional panorama), Glenveagh National Park, Ben Bulben Sligo (Sligo signature flat-top mountain, place of burial of poet W.B. Yeats at Drumcliff 1948 + his lines "Cast a cold eye on life, on death, horseman pass by"), Mullaghmore Head Beach (giant winter waves 30m+ extreme surf reserved for pros), Achill Island Mayo (the largest Irish island, 5 blue flag beaches, Keem Bay signature visual), Croagh Patrick Mayo (sacred mountain of Saint Patrick 764m, annual pilgrimage "Reek Sunday" last Sunday of July), Connemara National Park Galway (wild "Connemara mountains" landscapes signature visual of romantic Ireland, many songs by Michel Sardou "Les Lacs du Connemara" 1981), Kylemore Abbey (19th-century neo-Gothic abbey at the foot of Connemara, signature visual), Aran Islands (Inis Mór + Inis Meáin + Inis Oírr, preserved traditional Gaelic atmosphere, Dún Aonghasa signature prehistoric fortification at the edge of the cliff), Cliffs of Moher Clare (UNESCO Geopark 2011, the most visited image of Ireland 1.5 million visitors/year, 214m cliffs overlooking the Atlantic over 8 km signature visual, filming location "Princess Bride" 1987 + "Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince" 2009), Burren (UNESCO Geopark, unique karst landscape in Europe), Dingle Peninsula Kerry ("most beautiful peninsula in the world" according to National Geographic 2011, preserved Gaelic atmosphere, Slea Head the westernmost point of Europe, Blasket Islands), Ring of Kerry ("Ring of Kerry", 179 km circular road around the Iveragh Peninsula, signature Ireland tourist), Killarney National Park (lakes of Killarney + 19th-century Muckross House), Mizen Head Cork (the southernmost point of Ireland), Kinsale Cork (picturesque old town + culinary capital of Ireland). Itinerary: 2-3 weeks by car to fully cover, but individual sections 3-5 days possible (Connemara + Cliffs of Moher in 5 days = signature route). Try Irish Stew (signature lamb-potato stew), Boxty potato pancakes, soda bread, fish and chips Atlantic fish, Fish Pie, Galway Bay oysters, fresh draught Guinness, Irish whiskey Jameson + Bushmills + Tullamore + craft. Logistics: Dublin (DUB) airports 4h by car from the departure points + Shannon (SNN) Clare in the heart of the Wild Atlantic Way + Kerry (KIR) extreme south.
bansko:en: Bansko is the cheapest ski resort in Europe: Bulgaria, Pirin Mountains, prices 30-50% lower than the Alps. Day pass at 30-40€, in-resort meal at 10-15€. July is my favourite month (9.3/10): 14-26°C, 18% rain, good for hikes in Pirin National Park. From December to March, expect -3/8°C: this is ski season, snow guaranteed thanks to altitude (gondola up to 2600m). The area remains small (75 km of pistes) but well suited to families and beginners. Jazz and opera festivals in summer in the old town (Bulgarian Renaissance architecture of the 18th and 19th centuries). Sofia 2h30 by bus, Plovdiv 3h. Direct charter flights from Europe in ski season. Population that goes from 8000 inhabitants to 30,000 in high season.
hampi:en: Hampi, UNESCO archaeological site of Karnataka in southern India: former capital of the Vijayanagara empire (14th-16th century = one of the largest empires in Indian history), destroyed by the Muslim sultans of the Deccan in 1565 during the battle of Talikota after 6 months of plundering and fire. UNESCO 1986 for its 1600 monuments scattered over 41 km² in the middle of a unique geological landscape of red granite blocks. January is my favourite month (9.1/10): 19-31°C, 2% rain, this is the ideal dry season. December stays excellent (9.1/10). From November to February, it's the tourist high season: 19-32°C, ideal dry climate for exploring. From March to May, expect 23-38°C: extreme heatwave (sometimes 42°C), impossible to explore at midday. From June to September, it's the south-west monsoon: 54-67% rainfall, but Hampi is in a rain shadow zone (relatively little rain). Highlights: Virupaksha Temple (the only active temple for 700 years, dedicated to Shiva, signature 50m gopuram, accessible to visitors including non-Hindus), Vittala Temple (architectural masterpiece with its famous Stone Chariot = stone chariot in the form of a miniature temple with sculpted wheels that could originally turn, signature visual of Hampi on the 50 Indian rupee note, and with its musical pillars = pillars that produce different tones when struck gently), Lotus Mahal (palace of the queens in the form of a lotus), Elephant Stables (stables of the royal elephants, 11 vaulted chambers), Hazara Rama Temple (private royal temple with sculpted Ramayana frieze), Achyutaraya Temple (in ruins), Matanga Hill (panoramic viewpoint over the whole site at sunrise 4am, 30-min ascent), Hemakuta Hill (sunset), Pampa Sarovar and Anjaneya Hill (Anegundi side, birthplace of Hanuman according to the Ramayana, 575 steps), Sanapur Lake (swimming in natural pool, traditional round coracle-basket rides), Tungabhadra River (the sacred river that separates Hampi into 2 zones: temple zone to the south + rural zone Anegundi-Virupapur Gadde to the north with bohemian atmosphere). Atmosphere: Hampi has attracted international backpackers and bohemians for 50 years (Virupapur Gadde north zone notably), budget guesthouses, bohemian atmosphere. Visit recommended 2-3 days minimum, scooter or bicycle rental indispensable, local guide to understand Vijayanagara history. Try South Indian thali (complete vegetarian platter), masala dosa, idli sambar, filter coffee. Logistics: Hubli airport (HBX) 3h away, alternative Hospet rail 30 min away with trains from Bangalore (8h overnight train) or Goa (1 train/day).
shimla:en: Shimla, capital of Himachal Pradesh, is the former summer capital of the British Raj. At 2200m altitude, temperate climate that makes it an escape from the Indian heat. The Kalka-Shimla Railway (UNESCO) remains the most charming way to arrive. May is my favourite month (8.3/10): 12-24°C, 43% rain, soft light on the Mall and Christ Church. From June to August, it's the monsoon: 56-93% rainfall, fogs that cling to the pines, possible landslides on the main road. From December to February, expect -1/13°C: possibility of snow (rare but photogenic). Many Indians go there to see snow for the first time. October-November remain an excellent dry season. Many colonial accommodations converted into heritage hotels (Wildflower Hall, Oberoi Cecil). Plan 8h from Delhi by car or 12h by train.
gulmarg:en: Gulmarg, in Indian Kashmir, houses the highest cable car in the world: 3979m at the summit of Mount Apharwat. Wild back-country skiing, few marked pistes. September is my favourite month (7.3/10): 8-18°C, 57% rain residual from the monsoon, wild flowers in the meadows. From January to March, expect -8/3°C: ski season. Exceptional conditions for freeride (up to 14m of snow per season some winters), but advised freeriders only (no systematic patrol, frequent avalanches). The Kashmir security context remains to be checked before any travel: special permits may be required depending on periods, visible military presence. Flights from Delhi to Srinagar then 2h by road. Accommodation in chalet-style wooden hotels, affordable prices (50-150€/night). Variable opening period depending on winters and political situation.
kunming:en: Kunming, capital of Yunnan province in south-western China at 1900m altitude: 8 million inhabitants. "City of Eternal Spring" (春城 "Chūnchéng") = one of the only Chinese cities with temperate climate year-round thanks to tropical altitude, one of the most climatically pleasant Chinese destinations, cultural capital of Yunnan ethnic minorities = 26 different ethnicities out of 56 officially recognised in China. April is my favourite month (8.5/10): 12-22°C, 12% rain, ideal temperatures. March-May stay excellent (8.0-8.4/10). From March to May and September-November, these are the best seasons: 8-23°C, ideal temperate climate. From June to August, it's the wet season "monsoon": 50-67% rainfall, but stable temperatures 16-25°C thanks to altitude. From December to February, expect 4-17°C: mild for China (vs Beijing -10°C in January), little rain. Permanent climate: Kunming never tops 25°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 4°C in minimum average thanks to its altitude 1900m, ideal "Eternal Spring" tropical climate strongly contrasting with Beijing (-10°C / +35°C) or Shanghai (1°C / 33°C). Kunming highlights: Lake Dianchi (the 6th largest freshwater lake in China 300 km², on the edge of the city), Western Hills (Xishan, panoramic hills over Lake Dianchi with Dragon Gate Long Men Taoist temple sculpted in the cliff), Stone Forest (Shilin) 100 km away (UNESCO 2007, karst park with 30m-high limestone pillars forming a unique-in-the-world "stone forest", signature visual of Yunnan), Green Lake Park (Cuihu, urban park with overwintering Siberian seagulls November-March signature of Kunming), Yunnan Provincial Museum (museum of Yunnan ethnic minorities, traditional Yi-Bai-Naxi-Hani-Dai costumes), Yuantong Temple (8th-century Buddhist temple, one of the oldest in Yunnan), Cuihu Lake, Bird and Flower Market (picturesque traditional market). Essential Yunnan excursions = this is why one comes to Kunming: Dali 4h by train (UNESCO 2013 Tentative, former Bai kingdom capital 7th-13th century, preserved old town, signature 3 pagodas, spectacular Lake Erhai), Lijiang 6h away (UNESCO 1997, perfectly preserved medieval Naxi old town, signature tourist of Yunnan, Yulong Snow mountain 5596m visible), Shangri-La 8h away ("Zhongdian" renamed by Chinese marketing 2001 after the novel "Lost Horizon" James Hilton 1933, Tibetan atmosphere at 3200m altitude, signature Songzanlin monastery), Tiger Leaping Gorge (Hutiaoxia, one of the deepest gorges in the world 3900m deep, 2-3 day signature trekking hike in China), Yuanyang Rice Terraces (UNESCO 2013, Hani terraced rice paddies over 16,000 hectares, signature photography at sunrise), Xishuangbanna in the south (tropical region bordering Laos-Myanmar, Theravada Buddhist Dai people, preserved jungle), Jiuxiang Caves. Try guoqiao mixian ("crossing-the-bridge noodles", signature noodle soup of Yunnan), Yunnan ham (prestige Chinese ham equivalent of Italian Parma ham), Pu'er tea (fermented Pu'er tea from Yunnan, the most prestigious of Chinese teas, ages like wine), Yunnan tea, edible flowers (Yunnan signature).
qingdao:en: Qingdao, coastal city of Shandong on the Yellow Sea in China: 9.5 million inhabitants. Former German concession 1898-1914 which bequeathed unique colonial architecture in China + the Tsingtao brewery (1903 by the Germans, today the 2nd largest brewery in China and the most internationally exported Chinese brand = signature of Qingdao). Summer capital of Chinese seaside tourism ("China's Switzerland" marketing nickname). September is my favourite month (8.7/10): 17-26°C, 13% rain, ideal temperatures. May-October stay excellent (7.7-8.7/10). From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 13-28°C, sea at 22°C in August. From June to August, expect 18-30°C with typical sea fogs ("haiwu" Yellow Sea fogs signature of the climate of Qingdao), the wettest season. From December to March, expect -3/9°C and 5-10% rain: dry winter cold of north-eastern China, snow possible. Highlights: Qingdao Old Town (preserved 19th-20th century German colonial architecture, unique atmosphere in China = looks more like Munich than a Chinese city), St Michael's Cathedral (Catholic cathedral 1934 German neo-Romanesque signature visual), Governor's Mansion (1908 residence of the German governor of Qingdao, become museum), Xinhaoshan Park ("Signal Hill Park", signature panorama over Qingdao + Yellow Sea + beaches), Tsingtao Beer Museum (Tsingtao brewery founded 1903, visit + tasting = signature tourist of Qingdao, the international beer festival of Qingdao August = Chinese Oktoberfest equivalent, 6 million annual visitors), Qingdao beaches = the main summer beach attraction: No.1 Bathing Beach, No.2-3-6 Bathing Beaches, golden sand, Yellow Sea water 22°C in summer (warmer than European North Sea), lively local atmosphere July-August with millions of domestic Chinese tourists, Zhanqiao Pier ("1892 historic jetty", signature visual of Qingdao with octagonal Huilan pavilion at the end 440m), Mt Laoshan 30 km away ("sacred mountain of Taoism", 1133m, highest point of coastal Shandong, panorama over the sea + Taoist temples Taiqing Palace 9th century), May Fourth Square (commemorative square of the May Fourth Movement 1919 = anti-Versailles Chinese nationalist movement triggered in Qingdao when the Allies returned the city to Japan rather than China, political signature of modern Chinese nationalism, red sculpture "Wind of May" signature visual), Olympic Sailing Center (2008 Olympic centre where the sailing events of the 2008 Beijing Olympics were held = Qingdao co-host of the Olympics thanks to its favourable bay). Excursions: Beijing 4h by high-speed train (already covered), Shanghai 6h by train, Tai Shan 4h away (UNESCO, the most prestigious sacred mountain of Taoism in China), Confucius Temple Qufu 4h away (UNESCO, birthplace of Confucius). Try fresh draught Tsingtao Beer (the famous "plastic bag beer" = local institution where the inhabitants buy their beer in sealed plastic bag to take home), Yellow Sea seafood (Qingdao = capital of seafood in northern China), shengjianbao fried dumplings, Shandong hot pot, Chinese Changyu wines (founded by neighbouring Qingdao). Qingdao Jiaodong airport (TAO).
harbin:en: Harbin, capital of Heilongjiang province in the extreme north-east of China near the Russian border: 5.5 million inhabitants. "Moscow of the Orient" = former Russian city built by the Tsarist Empire 1898 for the Southern Trans-Siberian connecting Vladivostok-Beijing via Chinese Manchuria, preserved Russian architectural atmosphere + unique mixed Slavic-Chinese culinary culture. World capital of the Ice Sculpture Festival = the largest international ice sculpture festival in the world since 1985. May is my favourite month (8.1/10): 7-21°C, 14% rain, ideal temperatures. June-September stay excellent (7.7-8.0/10). From May to September, it's the summer tourist high season: 7-26°C, mild and pleasant temperatures. From January to February, it's the paradoxical winter high season thanks to the festival: -25/-12°C on average (sometimes -40°C at night = among the coldest temperatures in a major Chinese city), very cold equipment mandatory but unique signature experience. From July to August, expect 18-26°C with 47-54% rain: humid but pleasant summer season. Highlights: Harbin International Ice and Snow Sculpture Festival ("Harbin Ice and Snow World", January-February, the largest ice festival in the world, 600,000 m² of monumental sculptures illuminated in multi-coloured LED, full-scale recreations of world monuments in ice = Eiffel Tower, Colosseum, Notre-Dame, Taj Mahal, signature visual experience absolutely unique in the world, entry price 330 RMB ≈ 45€, 1 million visitors over 2 months, very cold equipment -30°C mandatory), Sun Island Snow Sculpture Festival (parallel, sculptures on snow rather than ice, free), Saint Sophia Cathedral ("St. Sophia Cathedral", 1907, former Russian Orthodox cathedral with green Byzantine domes = signature visual of Harbin = the largest Russian Orthodox church in the Far East outside Russia, transformed into architecture museum after 1945 expulsion of the Russians), Central Street (Zhongyang Dajie, 1.4 km pedestrian street with preserved Russian architecture + Slavic-Chinese cuisine restaurants + Madieer Ice Cream shop 1906 = local Russian institution), Stalin Park (park on the Songhua river Soviet heritage), Volga Manor (Russian theme park with reproduced Tsarist architecture), Daoli District (historic Russian old quarter), Siberian Tiger Park 30 min away (the largest Siberian tiger park in the world 1000+ tigers, semi-free animals in large spaces, observation from armoured buses, ethical controversy but legitimate genetic conservation of the last 500 wild Siberian tigers worldwide), Yabuli Ski Resort 200 km away (the largest ski resort in China, host of the 1996 Asian Winter Olympics, alternative to Niseko Japan or Yongpyong Korea). WWII heritage: Unit 731 Museum (memorial of Unit 731 = Japanese bacteriological research unit 1936-1945 having practised atrocious human experiments on 250,000 Chinese-Russian-Korean-Mongolian prisoners including vivisections without anaesthesia, the largest crime against humanity of the Second World War Asia, equivalent of Japanese Nazism, overwhelming memorial). Excursions: Yabuli ski, Russian border Suifenhe 5h away, Mount Changbai 8h away (crater lake shared with North Korea). Try hong sausage (signature Russian-Chinese red sausage), Harbin borscht (Russian heritage), Russian dumplings, Slavic-Chinese hybrid jiaozi pelmeni, Harbin beer (the oldest brewery in China 1900). Harbin Taiping airport (HRB).
chongqing:en: Chongqing, largest municipality in China in the heart of the south-west on the Yangtze River: 32 million inhabitants in metropolitan area + direct municipality status (equivalent Beijing-Shanghai-Tianjin) = largest urban zone in the world by administrative area 82,000 km² (larger than Austria). "Mountain-City" (Shancheng 山城) built on steep hills overlooking the Yangtze-Jialing confluence, cyberpunk cinematographic atmosphere with skyscrapers on stairs, monorail crossing buildings, urban escalators. September is my favourite month (7.7/10): 19-30°C, 41% rain, ideal temperatures. April-May stay excellent (7.6/10). From March to May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 12-30°C. From June to August, it's the humid heatwave: 22-37°C with 51-78% rain + fogs = Chongqing is one of the "3 Furnaces of China" with Wuhan + Nanjing (stifling river climate in summer). From December to February, expect 5-12°C with 34-66% rain: mild but foggy winter. Highlights: Hongyadong ("Hongya complex", 11 storeys of traditional Tujia stilt-house architecture on the Yangtze bank, illuminated at night in gold = signature cyberpunk visual of Chongqing, compared to the world of "Spirited Away" Studio Ghibli, viral Instagram photography), Liziba Light Rail Station (monorail station crossing DIRECTLY at the heart of a 19-storey residential building = unique-in-the-world urban planning experience signature visual), Jiefangbei ("Liberation Monument", central pedestrian square surrounded by modern skyscrapers), Eling Park (signature night panorama over the cyberpunk skyline of Chongqing at the confluence), Two Rivers Cruise (night cruise on the Yangtze + Jialing with view of the illuminated skyscrapers signature), Ciqikou Ancient Town (preserved Ming-Qing old town 30 min from the centre, traditional atmosphere + restaurants), Dazu Rock Carvings (UNESCO 1999, Buddhist-Taoist-Confucian rock sculptures 9th-13th century = one of the largest sets of rock sculptures in China after Longmen + Mogao + Yungang Buddhist Caves), 3 Gorges Cruise (3-4 day cruise Chongqing-Yichang on the Yangtze through the Three Gorges = spectacular landscapes + visit to the Three Gorges Dam = the largest hydroelectric dam in the world 22,500 MW, controversial for displacement of 1.4 million inhabitants 1993-2009, major Chinese tourist signature), Wulong Karst (UNESCO 2007, karst landscapes 3 natural bridges Tianlong-Heilong-Qinglong = filming location "Transformers 4" Hollywood 2014). Chongqing cuisine = signature: spicy hot pot (Chongqing hot pot = signature culinary = chilli red oil broth + extreme numbing Sichuan pepper "málà" = signature experience to taste at the famous Liuyishou Hot Pot or Xiaolongkan), Xiaomian (signature spicy noodles for local breakfast), Chongqing chicken ("chicken with chillies" dry spicy dish), Yangtze seafood. Excursions: Chengdu 1h30 by high-speed train (neighbouring Sichuan capital, already covered by BDW, alternative Sichuan cuisine + giant pandas), Yichang end of the Yangtze. Logistics: Chongqing Jiangbei airport (CKG), direct train major Chinese cities.
nha-trang:en: Nha Trang, main beach resort of south-central Vietnam: 420,000 inhabitants in the province of Khanh Hoa, 7 km urban beach bay. Modern city with hotel skyscrapers overlooking the sea, active nightlife, one of the Vietnamese destinations most frequented by Russians and Koreans. February-March are my favourite months (8.6/10): 22-29°C, 29-34% rain, this is the ideal dry season. From January to August, it's the dry season: 22-33°C, sea at 26-29°C. From September to December, it's the typhoon and north-east monsoon season: 61-83% rainfall (the central Vietnamese coast is hit by 4-6 typhoons per season on average, of which some devastating: Damrey 2017, Molave 2020). Highlights: Nha Trang Beach (7 km of urban beach in the heart of the city, golden sand, palm trees), Vinpearl Land (amusement park on Hon Tre island, access by Vinpearl Cable Car = one of the longest cable cars over the sea in the world 3320m, opened 2007 + park with roller coasters, aquarium, water park, private beach), Po Nagar Cham Towers (8th-13th centuries, the most important Cham sacred complex in southern Vietnam, still active as place of worship), Nha Trang Cathedral (Notre-Dame, neo-Gothic 1934 French heritage, panoramic view of the city), Long Son Pagoda (with giant white Buddha 14m on the back hill), Bac My An Beach, Mud baths (Thap Ba Hot Springs or I-Resort = mineral mud baths + sulphurous hot water, popular experience for the skin, 200,000 dong ≈ 8€), Dam Market (central market, fruits and seafood). Excursions: 4 Islands Tour (Mun Island, Mot Island, Tam Island, Mieu Island, snorkelling, BBQ lunch on boat), Hon Mun Marine Reserve (marine reserve, diving), Doc Let Beach 1h to the north (fine white sand, calmer), Ninh Hoa and Whale Island, Da Lat 3h by bus (Highlands). Diving: Nha Trang is one of the main diving bases in Vietnam (PADI Open Water 250-300€). Try nem nuong (grilled pork rolls), bun cha ca (fish soup), banh canh, sea urchin, bird's nest soup (Nha Trang is one of the world capitals of edible bird's nest, very high price 1500-3000€/kg). Cam Ranh airport (CXR) 30 min away.
hemsedal:en: Hemsedal, Norwegian ski resort in Buskerud in south-eastern Norway: 2300 inhabitants at 700-1500m altitude. One of the main ski resorts in Norway with Trysil and Geilo, paradise of Scandinavian alpine skiing (Norway is the country that invented modern skiing, but paradoxically mainly cross-country not alpine, Hemsedal is an exception with more rugged relief). Renowned for its legendary après-ski ("Scandinavian Aspen") with dynamic nightlife. June-July are my favourite months (7.2-7.5/10): 6-15°C, 44-56% rain, mild temperatures for hiking, long days (18h of day at the June solstice, midnight sun possible at higher altitudes). Hemsedal never tops 7.5/10 (cold Scandinavian alpine climate). From June to August, it's the summer high season: 0-15°C, hiking, mountain bike, 9-hole golf course. From December to April, expect -11/-1°C and 31-49% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Exceptionally long ski season: Hemsedal generally has skiable snow from late November until late April (5 months) thanks to the maintained cold climate and altitude. Pistes: 53 km Hemsedal Skisenter (modest vs Alps: 600 km 3 Valleys or Portes du Soleil), 56 pistes of which 35% intermediate, 30% expert, altitude 700-1500m, vertical drop 800m, 21 lifts including 4 detachable chairlifts. Signature pistes: Reidarløypa (the longest red piste 6.2 km), Tinden (summit at 1450m, panorama over Hallingdal valley). Off-piste: Hemsedal has 75 km² of accessible off-piste with its Hemsedal Adventure Skiing programme, skin hiking to reach preserved places. Snowpark: one of the best in Scandinavia. Norwegian particularity: the culture of "Nordic ski touring" Telemark style is still very present in Hemsedal (you'll see many skiers with free heels and flexible knee turns Norwegian signature, technique invented in neighbouring Telemark province in the 19th century). Legendary après-ski: Hemsedal Skisenter pub, Garasjen, Stadium Bar (surprisingly active night clubs for such a remote resort). Audience: Norwegians on Oslo weekends (3h by car), Swedes (Stockholm 5h by car thanks to the bridge connecting the 2 capitals), Danes thanks to charter flights, few British or Western Europeans. Hotels: Skarsnuten Hotel (4* on the mountain), Skogstad Hotel (3*+ village centre 1900), Quality Hotel Hemsedal, 150-500€/night. Try reindeer, elk, fårikål (mutton cabbage, Norwegian national dish), brunost (typical caramelised brown cheese), aquavit (Norwegian eau-de-vie), Norwegian craft beers Lervig/Nogne. Logistics: Hemsedal 3h by car from Oslo, Oslo Gardermoen airport (OSL) then Hemsedal Express bus or car, station relatively far from the main cities (preserved charm but difficulty of access).
geilo:en: Geilo, Norwegian ski resort on the Oslo-Bergen railway in Hallingdal: 2400 inhabitants at 800m altitude between Hardangervidda and Hallingskarvet national parks. Oldest tourist ski resort in Norway = developed from 1909 when the Oslo-Bergen railway was inaugurated crossing Geilo, more traditional and family atmosphere than neighbouring more festive Hemsedal. June-July are my favourite months (7.2-7.5/10): 1-17°C, 35-48% rain, ideal temperatures for Norwegian alpine hiking with long days (18-20h of day at the June solstice). Geilo never tops 7.5/10 (cold Scandinavian alpine climate). From June to August, it's the summer high season: -7-17°C, Hardangervidda hiking = the largest altitude plateau in Europe 8000 km², mountain bike, salmon fishing, observation of the last wild reindeer of Europe in their natural state. From December to April, expect -10/4°C and 32-49% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 39 km Geilo Skisenter (modest vs Alps), 2 sectors (Geilo + Vestlia) + 220 km of cross-country trails ("langlauf" = Norwegian national sport, more practised than alpine skiing locally). Signature pistes: Tjødnaheis (summit 1178m), Vestlia family side. No sporty off-piste at Geilo (gentler relief than at neighbouring Hemsedal), ideal for beginner families. Nordic winter activities: dog sledding (husky safari), snowmobile, cross-country skiing (the largest cross-country area in Norway), ice fishing on frozen lakes, Northern Lights safari (Geilo is at 60°N so at the southern limit of the Northern Lights observation zone, uneven conditions unlike Finnish Levi 67°N). Highlights: Hardangervidda National Park 30 min away (the largest altitude plateau in Europe, lunar landscapes of alpine tundra, last wild reindeer of Europe in herds, "most beautiful Norwegian hike" Trolltunga 27 km 4h from Geilo), Vøringfossen Waterfall (one of the most impressive in Norway 182m, accessible 1h by car), Kongsberg Mining Museum 2h away (17th-century royal silver mine). Oslo-Bergen train = the most picturesque railway in Norway, ranked among the world top 10, crosses Geilo in 4h from Oslo or 3h from Bergen. Audience: Norwegians in the majority (Oslo 3h by train, traditional family atmosphere), Swedes, Danes, few foreigners (the real international ski resorts in Norway are Lillehammer + Hemsedal + Trysil). Hotels: Vestlia Resort (4*+), Highland Hotel, prices 150-500€/night. Try reindeer ("reinsdyrkjøtt"), elk, Norwegian Atlantic salmon, brunost (signature Norwegian caramelised brown cheese), Norwegian aquavit, local Hansa beer.
are:en: Åre, main ski resort of Sweden in Jämtland province in central Scandinavia: 1400 permanent inhabitants (15,000 in tourist season). Largest ski resort in the Nordic countries with 89 pistes and 41 lifts (in competition with Trysil/Hemsedal Norway). Host of several FIS World Cups and the 2007 and 2019 Alpine Ski World Championships. June-July are my favourite months (7.3/10): 8-17°C, 50-54% rain, fresh temperatures ideal for Scandinavian hiking and midnight sun (Åre is far enough north to benefit from long summer days 21h of day at the June solstice, but not the complete midnight sun unlike Finnish Levi). From June to August, it's the summer high season: 6-17°C, fjälls Scandinavian mountain hiking, mountain bike, fishing, kayaking. From December to April, expect -8/4°C and 36-53% precipitation often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 89 km Åre Skistar (modest vs Alps 600 km, but one of the largest Scandinavian areas), altitude 380-1420m (Mt Åreskutan 1420m), vertical drop 890m. Signature pistes: Åre Olympia (World Cup descent), Susabacken, Hummelliften (highest lift at 1274m). Cultural particularity: Åre is one of the smartest alpine resorts in Scandinavia, more festive atmosphere than Hemsedal Norway or Levi Finland, lively après-ski. Snow Park excellent for freestyle. Off-piste: Åre offers accessible back-country with short skin hiking, wild descents in the fjälls, possibility of heli-ski exceptionally practised in Sweden (rare authorisation in Europe, contrasting with Alps where heli-ski is forbidden in France-Germany but authorised in Italy-Switzerland). Nordic winter activities: dog sledding (husky safari, more accessible than Finnish Lapland from European airports), snowmobile, cross-country skiing (Sweden = national country of langlauf, signature Swedish Olympic sport), snowshoeing, Northern Lights safari (Åre is at 63°N so right at the southern limit of the Northern Lights observation zone, less reliable conditions than Finnish Lapland 2h further north, observation possible November-March in good conditions only, about 30-40% chance per stay of 5 days), reindeer and moose safari ("elk safari", the moose = Swedish animal signature, observation early morning by bus). Swedish sauna = cultural institution practised but less traditional than in Finland, more contemporary. Highlights: Tännforsen 30 km away (the largest waterfall in Sweden 32m high 60m wide, magnificent in winter frozen as ice cathedral), Mt Åreskutan (1420m summit accessible by Åre cable car, 360° panorama over the Scandinavian fjälls), Åre Old Church (Åre Gamla Kyrka, 12th century, one of the oldest stone churches in Sweden), Åreälven (Åre river, salmon fishing in summer). Hotels: Copperhill Mountain Lodge (5*+ Swedish design Bjørklid architects 2008), Holiday Club Åre, Hotel Diplomat Åre, 200-1200€/night in high season. Audience: Swedes in the majority (70%), Norwegians, Danes, Finns in the minority, few non-Scandinavian tourists (Germans, British) unlike the Alps. Try renskav (sliced reindeer), gravlax (Swedish marinated salmon), köttbullar (meatballs IKEA-style but artisanal), Swedish moussaka (kålrullar = stuffed cabbage), surströmming (ultra-smelly fermented herring signature Swedish = extreme culinary experience reserved for the brave), kanelbullar (cinnamon buns), aquavit (Swedish caraway eau-de-vie), Swedish craft beers Mariestad/Norrlands. Logistics: Åre 1h by train from Östersund airport (OSD) or 6h by train from Stockholm-Arlanda, direct European charter flights in season.
bratislava:en: Bratislava, Slovak capital: 475,000 inhabitants on the Danube, compact baroque old town (to see in 1 day), often combined with Vienna (1h by train) or Budapest (2h30). July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 18-28°C, 30% rain, terraces on Hlavné námestie, Danube cruises. August stays excellent (9.1/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 13-28°C, 27-36% rain. From November to March, expect -2/9°C and 23-32% rain: dry continental cold, Christmas market on Hlavné námestie late November-December (one of the most traditional in Central Europe, without the crowds of Vienna or Prague), prices x2. Highlights: Compact old town (Hlavné námestie main square, Maximilian's fountain), Bratislava Castle (on the hill, panoramic view over the Danube and Austria-Hungary in the distance), Saint Martin's Cathedral (former coronation place of the kings of Hungary), Saint Michael's Gate (only preserved medieval gate), SNP Bridge (Most Slovenského Národného Povstania, nicknamed "UFO" for its tower-restaurant in flying saucer shape), Devín Castle (Slavic ruins 15 min by bus, Danube-Morava confluence). Jewish Quarter Židovská. Try bryndzové halušky (sheep cheese gnocchi, national dish), kapustnica (cabbage and sausage soup), Tatratea (liqueur). Bratislava is cheaper than Vienna or Prague (beer 1.5-2€, meal 8-15€).
jasna:en: Jasna, in the Slovak Low Tatras, is the largest ski resort in the country: 50 km of pistes, Chopok summit at 2024m. Rates among the lowest in Central Europe (day pass 35-50€). August is my favourite month (7.3/10): 7-16°C, 49% rain, good for hiking in the Low Tatras national park. From December to March, expect -12/0°C: ski season. Reliable snow thanks to altitude and continental climate. The resort belongs to the Tatry Mountain Resorts group (which also owns Vysoké Tatry nearby). From November and April, it's the slow shoulder season. Bratislava 4h by car, direct flight Poprad-Tatry from Krakow or Vienna. Ideal for a low-cost ski weekend from Western Europe. Local cuisine (bryndzové halušky: sheep cheese gnocchi) to discover in the koliba (traditional chalets).
bruxelles:en: Brussels, Belgian capital and capital of the European Union: 1.2 million inhabitants in urban area, bilingual French/Dutch city, cultural mosaic (1/3 of residents are foreigners). July-August are my favourite months (8.3/10): 14-23°C, 35-36% rain, terraces, Brussels-les-Bains (urban beach on the canal mid-July to mid-August). From November to February, expect 2-10°C and 42-45% rain: mild for Northern Europe, Plaisirs d'Hiver Christmas market mid-November to early January (Grand-Place, Place Sainte-Catherine, Marché aux Poissons). From April to June, expect 5-22°C: spring often rainy but pleasant. Highlights: Grand-Place (UNESCO, one of the most beautiful squares in the world, 15th-century Town Hall, 17th-century Guild Houses), Manneken Pis (60 cm, always disappoints by its smallness), Atomium (102m, opened for Expo 1958, lift in the central sphere), Saint Michael and Saint Gudula Cathedral, Sablon Quarter (antique dealers, chocolatiers), European Quarter (European Parliament visitable), Mont des Arts. Museums: Magritte (the most surreal), Royal Museums of Fine Arts, Comic Strip Museum (Tintin, Smurfs, Astérix), Horta Museum (Art Nouveau). Try moules-frites (at Léon since 1893), waterzooi, carbonade flamande, fries (Maison Antoine), Liège waffles, chocolate (Pierre Marcolini, Neuhaus, Godiva), Trappist beer (Westvleteren, Chimay, Orval).
ghent:en: Ghent (Gent in Dutch), 3rd city of Belgium and capital of East Flanders: 265,000 inhabitants. Often eclipsed by more touristic Bruges, Ghent nevertheless offers an equally exceptional medieval atmosphere with on top a real student urban life (75,000 students Ghent University). In the 14th century, Ghent was the 2nd largest city in Northern Europe after Paris (65,000 inhabitants), major centre of the cloth industry. June is my favourite month (8.3/10): 13-22°C, 36% rain, ideal temperatures. July-August stay excellent (8.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 13-23°C. From November to March, expect 2-11°C and 41-45% rain: humid cold, snow rare, unique atmosphere under winter mist. Highlights: Saint Bavo Cathedral (14th-16th century Gothic, houses the famous Ghent Altarpiece by Hubert and Jan van Eyck 1432, considered one of the absolute masterpieces of early Flemish painting and one of the most stolen and recovered paintings in history — 13 partial thefts and recoveries since 1432 including by the Nazis 1942, the "Just Judges" panel stolen in 1934 never recovered, recent restoration 2012-2020 revealed the original human Bible hidden under the overpaintings, paid access 12.5€), Belfry of Ghent (UNESCO 1999 with the other belfries of Belgium and France, 91m 14th-15th century, ascent by lift), Castle of the Counts of Flanders (Gravensteen, 12th-century fortified castle by Philip of Alsace, one of the last intact fortified castles in Europe, keep, panorama from the ramparts), Saint Nicholas Church (13th-century Scaldian Gothic), Korenlei and Graslei (the 2 medieval quays facing each other on the Lys = the signature visual of Ghent, former wheat and grain port warehouses, popular terraces, illuminated in the evening), STAM Stadsmuseum (urban history museum), SMAK Stedelijk Museum voor Actuele Kunst (contemporary art museum), Patershol (picturesque labyrinth medieval quarter), Werregarenstraat ("graffiti street", the only street in Ghent where graffiti is legal, paintings change constantly), Kouter (flower market square since the 18th century, Sunday morning Ghent institution). Excursions: Bruges 30 min by train (UNESCO, already covered by BDW), Antwerp 1h away (Rubens, port, fashion), Brussels 30 min by train. Try waterzooi (Flemish stew with chicken or fish cream), cuberdon (Ghent specialty conical violet candies with strawberries since 1873, to buy at "Cuberdon Noses" Place du Marché Vendredi), Ghent pralines, jenever (juniper eau-de-vie, the ancestor of gin), Belgian Trappist beers (Westvleteren, Chimay, Orval). Major festival: Gentse Feesten ("Ghent Festivities", 10 days late July, 1.5 million visitors, one of the largest free urban festivals in the world, theatre, concerts, food).
luxembourg:en: Luxembourg City, capital of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg: 130,000 inhabitants (700,000 in the country), seat of several European institutions (Court of Justice of the EU, Court of Auditors), major financial centre (banks, investment funds). Trilingual French/Luxembourgish/German city, one of the highest GDP per capita in the world. July is my favourite month (8.3/10): 14-23°C, 30% rain, mild temperatures. From June to September, it's the high season: 11-23°C, temperate oceanic climate. From November to March, expect 0-9°C and 38-47% rain: dry to humid cold, Christmas market (Wantermaart) on Place d'Armes and Place Guillaume II late November-December. Highlights: Old town (UNESCO, perched on a plateau above the Alzette and Pétrusse gorges, unique urban landscape in Europe, to discover at sunset from the Adolphe Bridge), Bock Casemates (UNESCO, 23 km of underground galleries dug in the rock in the 17th century, WWII air-raid shelter), Grand Ducal Palace (official residence of Grand Duke Henri, changing of the guard in summer), Notre-Dame Cathedral (17th century), Place d'Armes, Grund Quarter (down in the valley, village atmosphere at the foot of the cliffs, restaurants on the edge of the Alzette), Kirchberg Quarter (modern European city, MUDAM Museum of Modern Art in Pei building), Solidarity Memorial (Gëlle Fra, gilded statue signature). Excursions Luxembourgish Little Switzerland to the north-east (Mullerthal, gorges, hikes), Vianden in the north (medieval castle), Moselle Valley (Riesling/Gewurztraminer/Pinot white wines). Try Judd mat Gaardebounen (smoked pork collar), Gromperekichelcher (potato pancakes), Diekirch/Bofferding beers. Very expensive (one of the most expensive capitals in Europe).
vaduz:en: Vaduz, capital of Liechtenstein (Principality of Liechtenstein): 5500 inhabitants. 4th smallest country in Europe after Vatican, Monaco, San Marino = 160 km², 40,000 inhabitants in total, monarchical constitutional principality ruled by the House of Liechtenstein since 1719 (one of the oldest dynasties in Europe still reigning, originating from Lower Austria). Alpine country enclaved between Switzerland and Austria, no own currency (uses Swiss franc), no airport (the closest: Zurich), only accessible by car-train-bus from Switzerland. Historic tax haven (eased since 2009). June-August are my favourite months (7.7/10): 14-24°C, 56-63% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking. From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 11-24°C. From December to March, expect -3/11°C and 45-50% precipitation often as snow: skiing possible at Malbun (1600m, modest 23 km of pistes only). Highlights: Vaduz Castle (Schloss Vaduz, perched on cliff 120m above the town, official residence of the reigning Prince Hans-Adam II and his family = signature visual of Liechtenstein, 12th-17th century castle, closed to the public as private residence but visible from outside, one of the last castles in Europe still really inhabited by a royal family), Centre of Vaduz ("Städtle", pedestrian street with town hall, LGT Bank, restaurants, duty-free boutiques, smart small town atmosphere), Liechtenstein Art Museum (Kunstmuseum Liechtenstein, opened 2000, discrete black-grey cubic architecture, modern and contemporary art collection including works lent by the princes of Liechtenstein — their private collection is one of the most important in the world), National Museum of Liechtenstein (history of the principality), National Treasure (Schatzkammer, opened 2015, princely regalia + diplomatic gifts + unique works of art from the princely collection on rotating display), Saint Florin Cathedral (1873 neo-Gothic), Red House (Rotes Haus, signature medieval house with red façade), Tourist Office (where you can have your passport stamped for 3 CHF = collector souvenir). Activities: Liechtenstein Trail (hiking trail crossing the whole country over 75 km in 5 stages, one of the unique tourist particularities of Liechtenstein), Malbun 30 min away (only ski resort in the country, modest but spectacular landscapes), Triesenberg (Walser mountain village with unique dialect, hikes). Excursions: Zurich 1h away (Switzerland), Innsbruck 1h30 away (Austria, already covered by BDW). Try Käsknöpfle (signature alpine cheese gnocchi), Ribel (corn semolina), Rebstöcke wines from Liechtenstein (microscopic production, princely AOC), Vaduzer Eistee (local iced tea), kirsch cherry eau-de-vie. Logistics: Vaduz accessible by bus from Sargans (Switzerland, 30 min from Zurich by train + 25 min bus), no direct rail connection.
saint-marin:en: San Marino, micro-state enclaved in central Italy near Rimini: 33,000 inhabitants on 61 km². Oldest existing republic in the world (founded according to tradition in 301 AD by the Christian stonecutter Marinus of Rab fleeing the persecutions of Diocletian), only republic to have kept its independence in the face of Italian unification 1861. UNESCO 2008 for the historic centre and Mount Titano. July is my favourite month (9.2/10): 16-28°C, 17% rain, ideal temperatures. June and August stay excellent (8.6-9.0/10). From April to October, it's the tourist high season: 5-28°C. From November to March, expect -2/13°C and 24-39% rain: mountain cold, snow possible (San Marino reaches 749m). Highlights: 3 Towers (signature visual of San Marino on the country's coat of arms, 3 medieval fortresses perched on the peaks of Mount Titano: Guaita 11th century the oldest, Cesta 13th century the highest 755m with ancient weapons museum, Montale 14th century inaccessible but visible, connected by panoramic path with view over the Adriatic 25 km to the east and Apennines to the west), Piazza della Libertà (main square, Palazzo Pubblico 14th-1894 neo-Gothic = parliament, republican guard in traditional white-red-green uniforms, changing every 30 min), Basilica di San Marino (neo-classical 1838, houses the relics of the founder Saint Marinus), State Museum (millennial history of the Republic), Curiosities Museum (unusual), Weapons Museum, Cava dei Balestrieri ("Crossbowmen's Quarry", place where the national festival is held every 3 September with crossbow competition in medieval costumes), Historic City Coque (pedestrian old town within the ramparts, paved alleys with duty-free boutiques = San Marino has its own VAT lower than Italy). Particular: San Marino issues its own euro stamps and coins with national face (popular collection), passport stamped free at the tourist office. Excursions: Rimini 30 min away (Italian Adriatic coast, 15 km of beaches), San Leo (other neighbouring Italian medieval village on rocky peak), Urbino 1h away (UNESCO, ducal Renaissance town). Try piadina (Romagna tortilla), strozzapreti, cacciatella (hunter's rabbit), torta tre monti (signature San Marino cake = 7 layers of chocolate wafers), Sangiovese di Romagna DOC. Logistics: San Marino 30 min from Rimini by bus from Rimini airport (RMI), no own airport.
gibraltar:en: Gibraltar is 6 km² of British territory in the south of Spain: Rock at 426m, free Barbary apes, pound sterling, right-hand driving. Marine Mediterranean climate. July is my favourite month (9.8/10): 20-28°C, 2% rain, Levante wind that refreshes. From December to March, expect 9-17°C and 28-41% rain: humid and windy. The border crossing from La Línea (Spain) can take 5 minutes or 2h depending on the time and the diplomatic tension of the moment (post-Brexit, reinforced controls). The Rock cable car, the Saint Michael caves, and the crossing of the siege fortifications fill a day. The macaques (Barbary macaques) are wild and opportunistic: never show them food. The ferry to Tangier (Morocco) leaves from Tarifa 30 minutes by car. Gibraltar Airport has a runway that crosses the main road, a curious phenomenon to observe.
minsk:en: Minsk, capital of Belarus: 2 million inhabitants. Former Soviet republic 1922-1991, ruled with an iron fist since 1994 by Alexander Lukashenko ("last dictatorship in Europe"), rigged elections 2020 having triggered massive demonstrations severely repressed. Unwavering ally of Putin's Russia, logistical support to the Russian invasion of Ukraine 2022. June is my favourite month (8.4/10): 13-22°C, 29% rain, ideal temperatures. July-August stay excellent (8.2-8.4/10). From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 12-24°C. From November to March, expect -6/3°C and 31-46% rain: harsh continental Soviet cold, abundant snow. Highlights: Independence Square (Niezaliežnasci Square, one of the largest squares in Europe 7 hectares, monumental Stalinist urbanism signature), Independence Avenue (Niezaliežnasci Avenue, 15 km one of the longest main avenues in Europe, magnificently preserved Stalinist architecture 1944-1956 = Minsk was destroyed at 80% during the Second World War then rebuilt in grandiose Stalinist architecture), Victory Square (Pieramohi Square, monument to the Soviet dead of the Great Patriotic War), National Library of Belarus (modern architecture in the form of a polygonal diamond 2006, 73m high, panorama from the roof accessible to visitors), Museum of the Great Patriotic War (opened 2014 by Lukashenko and Putin together, one of the largest post-Soviet military museums, Russo-Soviet perspective on the Second World War), Trinity Suburb old town (Trojeckaje Pradmiescie, the only old quarter preserved in Minsk, 19th-century coloured houses on the Svislatch river, picturesque atmosphere rare in this rebuilt city), Holy Spirit Cathedral (17th-century Polish Baroque Orthodox cathedral), Saints Simon and Helena Church ("Red Church", 1910 neo-Romanesque red bricks), Komarovka Quarter (Kamarouski Rynak, traditional central market). Belarus excursions: Mir Castle 100 km away (UNESCO 2000, 16th-century Gothic-Renaissance castle, one of the best preserved in Central Europe), Niasvij/Nieswiez (UNESCO 2005, castle of the Radziwill magnates 16th-19th century), Brest (heroic Second World War fortress, Polish border), Białowieża Forest (UNESCO shared with Poland, last primary forest in Europe, last European bison in the wild). Safety: Belarus political situation makes any tourist visit inadvisable since 2020 (international sanctions, direct flights from Europe cancelled, international payments blocked, political activists imprisoned, Western travellers risk arbitrary detention), France Diplomacy formally advises against. Try draniki (potato pancakes, Belarusian national dish), machanka (pork sauce), kvas (fermented Slavic drink), Belarusian vodka Belaja Vieza, local pierogi. Minsk National airport (MSQ) under EU sanctions since 2021.
chisinau:en: Chișinău (Kishinev in Russian), capital of Moldova: 530,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 800,000 in urban area. Country among the poorest in Europe (lowest GDP per capita in Europe), former Soviet republic 1940-1991. Population shared between Romanians-Moldovans (75%) and Russian-Ukrainian-Gagauz minorities. Frozen conflict in Transnistria (pro-Russian separatist territory in the east, 500,000 inhabitants, internationally unrecognised). Very little touristic. August is my favourite month (9.2/10): 18-29°C, 15% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (9.0/10). From May to September, it's the tourist high season: 11-29°C. From November to March, expect -4/9°C and 21-24% rain: continental cold, little snow (drier climate than Belarus), unique atmosphere. Highlights: Great National Assembly Square (Piaţa Marii Adunări Naţionale, main square with triumphal arch Arcul de Triumf 1840 + Orthodox Cathedral Naşterea Domnului 19th-century neo-classical Russian), Ștefan cel Mare Avenue (main avenue 4 km named after the great 15th-century Moldovan prince Ștefan cel Mare = heroic defender against Ottomans, national hero), Ștefan cel Mare Park (central park with statue of the prince), National Museum of History of Moldova, Cathedral of the Transfiguration (Schimbarea la Faţă), Pre-war Jewish Quarter (memory of the 1903 and 1905 pogroms + Holocaust 1941 where 90% of the Moldovan Jewish community was exterminated), Doina Cemetery (where Maria Cebotari famous Moldovan soprano rests). Excursions = this is why one comes to Moldova: Orhei Vechi Caves 1h away ("Old Orhei", 13th-century troglodyte Orthodox monastery dug in the limestone cliff above the Răut river, mystical-bucolic atmosphere uniquely, signature visual of Moldova), Mileștii Mici Caves 30 min away (the largest wine cellar in the world 200 km of underground galleries with 1.5 million bottles, Guinness Book record, mandatory visit by car as distances large), Cricova Caves 30 min away (other large cellar 120 km of galleries, fine tasting), Purcari Caves 2h away (vineyards Negru de Purcari = legendary Moldovan wine served at the court of the Russian tsars 19th century + Queen Elizabeth II), Tipova 1h30 away (other spectacular troglodyte monastery), Soroca in the north (15th-century medieval fortress + Roma Tzigane capital of Moldova with kitsch palace-houses built by enriched Roma). Moldovan wines: Moldova produces more wine per capita than any other country in the world (3.8% of GDP, 25% of exports). Try mămăligă (polenta), sarmale (stuffed cabbage), plăcinte (cheese turnover), brânză (fermented sheep cheese), Moldovan wines Negru de Purcari/Feteasca Neagră/Riesling, Divin (Moldovan cognac), Kvas. Chișinău airport (KIV).
pristina:en: Pristina, capital of Kosovo: 200,000 inhabitants, young capital (independence 2008, recognised by 100 states including France, contested by Serbia and Russia). Not the prettiest but one of the youngest in Europe (60% of the population under 25). July is my favourite month (9.1/10): 17-28°C, 26% rain, terraces. August stays excellent (9.0/10). From June to September, it's the high season: 13-29°C, dry continental climate. From February to March drop to 1.9-2.0/10 due to humidity, grey sky, low temperatures. From November to March, expect -2/13°C: continental winter cold. Highlights: Bill Clinton Statue ("hero of Kosovo" for having launched the NATO bombings 1999 that ended the conflict, Bill Clinton boulevard), National Library (1982 polarising brutalist architecture, covered with 99 white domes and metal grids, one of the ugliest in the world according to some rankings — therefore to see), Mother Teresa Cathedral (Catholic, in completion, dedicated to the saint of Albanian origin), Newborn Monument (NEWBORN letters repainted each year according to a theme, erected on the day of independence 17 February 2008), Sultan Mehmet Fatih Mosque (15th century, Ottoman), Bazaar (Old Bazaar). Excursions: Prizren 1h30 away (the "real" cultural capital of Kosovo, charming Ottoman old town), Visoki Dečani Monastery (UNESCO, Serbian Orthodox monastery protected by KFOR, 14th-century frescoes), Rugovo Canyon near Peja (canyon, skiing in winter). Try burek, ćevapi, flija (pancake cake), raki (plum eau-de-vie). Capital among the cheapest in Europe (beer 1€, meal 5-10€).
ohrid:en: Ohrid, town in North Macedonia on Lake Ohrid (UNESCO double inscription natural AND cultural, one of the only mixed sites in the world): 42,000 inhabitants, 358 km² lake shared with Albania, one of the oldest lakes in the world (4 million years) and the deepest in Europe (288m). July is my favourite month (9.6/10): 19-28°C, 16% rain, ideal temperatures, swimming in the lake (water at 24°C). August stays excellent (9.3/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 15-28°C, Macedonian locals in the majority (Ohrid = the "Macedonian Riviera"). From December to March, expect 1-11°C and 25-37% rain: mild for the Balkans, calm winter atmosphere. Highlights: Saint John of Kaneo Church (13th century, perched on cliff above the lake, one of the most famous images of Macedonia), Old town (12th-13th century paved streets, traditional Macedonian wooden houses), Saint Sophia Church (11th century, exceptional Byzantine frescoes), Ancient Theatre (4th century BC, Hellenistic then Roman, still used for the Ohrid Summer Festival July-August), Saint Naum Monastery 30 km away (in the south, founded by the disciple of Cyril and Methodius, Saint Naum springs source of the Drim river), Samuel's Fortress (10th century, panorama over the old town and the lake), Bay of Bones (underwater museum reconstituting a Neolithic stilt village). Activities: cruises on the lake (to Saint Naum 4h), swimming at Lagadin/Trpejca, hiking Mt Galičica (national park between Ohrid and Lake Prespa). Try Belvica (endemic fish of the lake, 17 unique species in the world), tavče gravče, ajvar, Macedonian wines (Tikveš). Direct Wizz Air flight to Ohrid, or via Skopje (2h30 by bus).
krasnaya-polyana:en: Krasnaya Polyana ("Red Glade"), ski resort and holiday destination in the Russian Caucasus 60 km from Sochi: 5000 inhabitants at 600-2200m altitude. Main site of the 2014 Sochi Winter Olympics (all the mountain events were held here: alpine skiing, biathlon, luge, bobsleigh, snowboard, freestyle), modernised on a large scale (50 billion USD invested = the most expensive Olympics in history). Today ski complex 4 connected resorts: Roza Khutor, Gorky Gorod, Gazprom Ski Resort, Alpika. August is my favourite month (8.3/10): 16-26°C, 48% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (8.0/10). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 8-26°C, Caucasus hiking, mountain bike, paragliding. From December to March, expect -2/9°C and 52-59% rain often as snow: ski high season. Pistes: 100 km combined (modest vs Alps), altitude 540-2320m, vertical drop 1750m (one of the largest in Russia). Roza Khutor = the smartest and largest, designed by French architects from Megève, European atmosphere, 4-5* hotels. Highlights: Roza Plateau (2320m, 360° panorama over the Caucasus, Black Sea visible 60 km to the south-west), Gorky City (winter amusement park, ice rink, luge descent), Achipse Fortress (9th-10th century Byzantine-Georgian remains), Skypark AJ Hackett Sochi (the highest pedestrian suspension bridge in Russia 207m, signature 207m bungee jumping, 700m zipline), Mzymta River Valley (rafting, canyoning), 33 Waterfalls (33 waterfalls in canyon, hike). Excursions: Sochi 60 km away (Black Sea beach resort, palm trees and 28°C in summer paradoxically 60 km from skiing, already covered by BDW), Sochi National Park, Adler (Sochi suburb, airport, 2014 Olympic Park), Abkhazia 1h by car (separatist territory of Georgia recognised by Russia, border with Russian passport only). Safety: the Russian political situation makes any tourist visit to Russia inadvisable since the invasion of Ukraine in February 2022 (Western sanctions, international payments blocked, direct flights cancelled from Europe, Russian e-visa suspended for most Western nationalities). Logistics: Sochi-Adler airport (AER) 50 min away, direct Lastochka high-speed train from Adler 1h.
kazan:en: Kazan, capital of the Republic of Tatarstan in Russia: 1.3 million inhabitants 800 km east of Moscow on the bank of the Volga. Multicultural city unique in Russia: 50% Tatar Muslims + 45% Russian Orthodox cohabit in harmony for 5 centuries (model of religious integration highlighted by the authorities). Historic capital of the Khanate of Kazan (1438-1552 conquered by Ivan the Terrible), 3rd city of Russia for its economic importance. July is my favourite month (8.7/10): 16-25°C, 29% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 13-25°C, 22-29% rain, pleasant climate. From December to February, expect -13/-5°C and 36-38% rain often as snow (1m+/season): rigorous continental winter but fairy-tale charm with the snow-covered Kremlin, mixed Russian-Tatar Christmas markets unique. Highlights: Kazan Kremlin (UNESCO 2000, fortified ensemble unique in the world mixing Orthodox and Muslim architecture, 16 hectares), Kul Sharif Mosque (within the Kremlin enclosure, 2005, modern reconstitution of the mosque destroyed by Ivan the Terrible in 1552, 4 minarets of 58m, one of the largest in Europe), Annunciation Cathedral (16th century, next to the mosque, symbol of the cohabitation), Söyembikä Tower (17th century, leaning tower 58m of the Söyembikä dynasty), Bauman Street (pedestrian artery with historic buildings, restaurants, boutiques), Lake Kaban (legend of the khan's treasure swallowed in its waters), Old Tatar Sloboda Quarter (preserved traditional Tatar architecture), Orthodox Cathedral of Saint Peter and Saint Paul (1726). Excursions: Sviyazhsk (UNESCO 2017, monastic island founded by Ivan the Terrible 1551 as a base to conquer Kazan, accessible by boat or road), Bolghar (UNESCO 2014, ancient capital of the Volga Bulgars 9th-15th century). Logistics: Kazan 1h by plane from Moscow, 11h by night train (Sapsan rapid or traditional train), 2 daily flights. Try chak-chak (Tatar honey-fried dessert), echpochmak (triangular meat-potato turnover), peremyach, kystyby, Tatar tea with milk.
novossibirsk:en: Novosibirsk, 3rd city of Russia and capital of Siberia: 1.6 million inhabitants 3300 km east of Moscow on the Ob river, on the Trans-Siberian line. Young city (founded in 1893 during the construction of the Trans-Siberian), nicknamed "Scientific Capital of Siberia" thanks to Akademgorodok ("City of Academicians" 1957, Soviet scientific complex with 35 research institutes). June-July are my favourite months (8.3-8.5/10): 12-24°C, 32-39% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 6-24°C, 31-39% rain, mild climate. From December to February, expect -19/-10°C and 25-41% precipitation often as snow: extreme Siberian continental winter (sometimes -40°C, blizzard). Highlights: Opera and Ballet Theatre (1945, the largest opera theatre in Russia, larger than the Bolshoi in Moscow or the Mariinsky in Saint Petersburg, 1700 seats, characteristic dome), Ascension Cathedral, Lenin Square (with statue of Lenin and bronze of Stalin), Metro Bridge (the longest metro bridge in the world 2145m, connects the 2 banks of the Ob), Akademgorodok (30 km to the south, Novosibirsk State University NSU, research institutes in mathematics, nuclear physics, geology, archaeology), Trans-Siberian and Railway Museum, Novosibirsk Zoo (one of the largest in Russia). Excursions: Tomsk 4h away (former 19th-century historic capital with unique wooden architecture), Lake Karakol in the Altai 8h away (paradisiacal altitude lake), Belokurikha (Soviet thermal station), Sheregesh ski 5h away in the Kuznetsk Alatau mountains (the main ski resort in western Siberia). Logistics: Novosibirsk 4h by plane from Moscow, 50h by train (the 3rd important stop on the Moscow-Vladivostok Trans-Siberian after Yekaterinburg), Tolmachovo airport. Try Siberian pelmeni (probable origin of Siberia), pirogues, Siberian ukha (Ob fish soup), borscht, vareniki, omul (endemic fish of Lake Baikal), vodka, kvass.
ekaterinbourg:en: Yekaterinburg, 4th city of Russia on the Asian side of the Urals (natural Europe-Asia border, monument-obelisk 30 km from the city materialises this border): 1.5 million inhabitants. Historic capital of the Russian mining and metallurgical industry since Peter the Great (1723). Sadly famous for the massacre of the Romanov imperial family on the night of 16-17 July 1918 by the Bolsheviks (Tsar Nicholas II + his wife + their 5 children + 4 servants executed in the basement of the Ipatiev house, demolished in 1977 by Boris Yeltsin then first secretary of the Party in Sverdlovsk — irony of history). July is my favourite month (8.2/10): 14-23°C, 37% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to August, it's the tourist high season: 11-23°C, 28-40% rain. From December to February, expect -16/-8°C and 17-20% rain often as snow: rigorous continental winter. Highlights: Church on the Spilled Blood (Khram-na-Krovi, 2003, neo-Byzantine built on the site of the Ipatiev house where the Romanovs were assassinated, place of Orthodox and imperial pilgrimage), Ganina Yama Monastery (15 km away, complex of 7 chapels built in 2000 at the place where the Romanov bodies were thrown into a mine — canonised "passion-bearer saints" by the Russian Orthodox Church in 2000, become icons of contemporary Russian nationalism), 1905 Square (centre, Lenin monument), Boris Yeltsin Centre (modern museum 2015 dedicated to the 1st president of post-Soviet Russia 1991-1999, controversial as it tells the collapse of the USSR from a Yeltsinian point of view), Vysotsky Skyscraper (188m, panoramic observation deck), Saint John the Forerunner Cathedral, Literary Quarter, Computer Keyboard Monument (humorous sculpture 2005). Excursions: Europe-Asia Border 30 km away (obelisk, signature photo with feet in 2 continents), Olen'i Ruchi Reserve ("deer streams", 100 km north, Ural taiga landscapes), Verkhoturye 4h away ("Orthodox Jerusalem of the Urals"). Logistics: Yekaterinburg 2h by plane from Moscow, 26h by Trans-Siberian train (1st major stop after Moscow), Koltsovo airport. Try Ural pelmeni (claimed regional origin), shashlik, draniki, blinis, kvas, vodka.
vladivostok:en: Vladivostok, capital of the Russian Far East on the Sea of Japan: 600,000 inhabitants 9300 km from Moscow (the eastern terminus of the Trans-Siberian the longest continuous train journey in the world 6 days/7 nights), 100 km from the Chinese border and 700 km from Tokyo. Unique "Russian San Francisco" atmosphere (hills, spectacular bay, suspension bridges, Asian-Russian cultural mix). City closed to foreigners until 1992 (Pacific Fleet military base). July is my favourite month (7.7/10): 18-21°C, 31% rain, end of the cool oceanic season. Vladivostok never tops 7.7/10 (oceanic climate with persistent fogs). From June to September, it's the tourist season: 12-23°C, 29-49% precipitation, mild summer climate. From December to February, expect -14/-4°C and 6-11% rain: extreme dry winter cold with glacial Siberian winds, sea sometimes taken by ice (rare). Highlights: Russky Bridge (Russky Bridge, 2012, the longest cable-stayed bridge in the world 1104m main span, one of the symbols of Russian modernisation), Zolotoy Bridge ("Golden Bridge", 2012, on Golden Horn Bay), Russky Island (FEFU university campus 2012, APEC Summit 2012 sites — Vladivostok was previously forbidden to foreigners and the 2012 APEC summit justified massive infrastructure investments), Svetlanskaya Street (main historic centre artery, 19th-20th century buildings), Tokarevsky Lighthouse (1904, on peninsula, place of burial of local wild white cats), Funicular (1959, the only one in Russia outside Moscow-Saint Petersburg), S-56 Submarine Museum (WWII submarine transformed into museum), Arseniev Museum. Excursions: Russky Island Aquarium (one of the largest aquariums in the world 2016), Shamora Beach 30 min away (the main urban beach), Land of the Leopard Reserve (100 km away, last world population of Amur leopard 100 individuals). Logistics: Vladivostok 8h by plane from Moscow, direct flight from Seoul/Tokyo/Beijing. The Trans-Siberian arrives at Vladivostok after 9300 km from Moscow (among the most legendary train journeys in the world). Try pelmeni, ukha (fish soup), raw fish and sushis (strong Japanese and Korean influence), Sakhalin salmon, vodka.
georgie:en: Georgia (Sakartvelo), country in the South Caucasus between Russia, Turkey, Armenia, Azerbaijan: 3.7 million inhabitants. Country at the Europe-Asia crossroads, unique Georgian alphabet, isolated non-Indo-European language, Christianised in 327 (4th country in the world after Armenia 301). Independence 1991, conflicts with Russia (5-day war 2008, occupation of 20% of the territory Abkhazia + South Ossetia). Tourism boom 2015-2020. August is my favourite month (8.6/10): 17-26°C, 38% rain, ideal temperatures. July stays excellent (7.9/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 12-26°C. From November to March, expect -4/10°C and 33-46% rain: harsh continental cold, abundant snow (ideal skiing Gudauri/Bakuriani), unique atmosphere under Caucasus snow. Highlights: Tbilisi capital (1.2 million, picturesque old town on the Mtkvari, Old Narikala Fortress 4th-century panorama, Abanotubani sulphur baths = millennial thermal institution, Peace Bridge modern 2010), Mtskheta 30 min away (UNESCO, former Georgian capital, Svetitskhoveli Cathedral 11th-century burial place of the Georgian kings), Kazbegi-Stepantsminda (village at the foot of Mt Kazbek 5054m, Gergeti Trinity Church 14th-century perched at 2170m on a hill = signature visual of Georgia, postcard image of the Caucasus, access on foot 1h30 or 4x4), Vardzia Caves (12th-century troglodyte city-monastery with 600 chambers dug in the cliff over 13 levels), Davit Gareja (6th-century troglodyte monastery Azeri border, medieval frescoes), Svaneti (UNESCO 1996, isolated region of the high Caucasus mountains with villages with Svan defensive towers from the 9th-12th century = signature Georgian cultural, Mestia Svaneti capital, Ushguli highest inhabited village in Europe 2200m), Kakheti (wine region, Georgia = cradle of wine 8000 years ago with qvevri technique buried clay jars UNESCO 2013, orange and red wines Saperavi), Batumi (Black Sea beach resort, already covered by BDW). Try khachapuri (signature cheese bread, Adjarian version in open boat with egg signature visual), khinkali (Georgian dumplings to pinch with 1 hand), churchkhela (walnut sausages with grape juice), qvevri Saperavi/Rkatsiteli wines. Tbilisi airport (TBS).
gudauri:en: Gudauri, main ski resort of Georgia on the Georgian Military Way 120 km north of Tbilisi: 1500 inhabitants at 2200m altitude at the foot of the Greater Caucasus massif. Resort developed in the Soviet era 1960 then massively modernised 2010-2025 (Emirati-Azerbaijani investments), become one of the rising ski resorts in Eurasia thanks to exceptional snow + low prices. August is my favourite month (7.6/10): 9-19°C, 31% rain, ideal temperatures for Caucasian alpine hiking. Gudauri never tops 7.6/10 (Caucasian alpine climate with narrow season). From June to September, it's the summer high season: 5-21°C, Georgian Caucasus hiking, mountain bike (Gudauri Bike Park summer), paragliding (one of the most famous spots in the Caucasus for paragliding with panoramic spot), Mt Kazbek 5054m visible from Gudauri = 4-5 day alpine ascent reserved for confirmed mountaineers. From December to April, expect -10/0°C and 28-46% precipitation often as snow: ski high season (ski season among the longest in Europe November-May thanks to altitude 2200-3300m). Pistes: 70 km Gudauri (modest vs Alps: 600 km 3 Valleys, but one of the largest areas in the Caucasus and the former Soviet bloc with Krasnaya Polyana Russia), altitude 2200-3307m, vertical drop 1107m, 8 lifts including modern Doppelmayr cable cars. Signature pistes: Sadzele (summit 3279m, 360° panorama over Georgian-Russian Caucasus + Mt Kazbek), Bidara (main descent), Kobi (north side).
