From November to February, it's the cool dry season: 16-36°C, ideal dry desert climate. From March to June, it's the Sahelian pre-monsoon heatwave: 19-41°C (sometimes 47°C+ = among the highest temperatures in West Africa, extreme climate). From July to September, it's the short Sahelian rainy season: 15-53% rainfall. Niamey highlights: Niamey Central Market (Grand Marché, traditional Sahelian atmosphere), Boubou-Hama National Museum (one of the best museums in Sahelian Africa, Hausa-Songhai-Tuareg mask collection, Niger dinosaurs "Sahel saurus" + "Nigersaurus" signature paleontological), Central Mosque of Niamey, Notre-Dame Cathedral of Niamey, Kennedy Bridge (on the Niger river), Banizoumbou Quarter, Wadata craftsmen village. Essential Niger excursions (very inadvisable for security currently): Aïr Massif (UNESCO 1991, Saharan mountains 2000m highest point Mount Bagzane, prehistoric Tassili-Aïr rock paintings 8000 BC, traditional Tuareg peoples, spectacular desert landscapes, inadvisable for security jihadist zone since 2014), Ténéré Desert (the most signature Saharan desert after the central Sahara, Bilma dunes 100m+ high, Tree of Ténéré = legendary solitary tree in the most isolated desert in the world 400 km from any other tree, hit by a drunk truck 1973, replaced by a metal monument today), Agadez (UNESCO 2013, Tuareg capital of north-Niger, signature 15th-century mud mosque), W National Park (UNESCO 1996 cross-border Niger-Benin-Burkina Faso, West African savannah, elephants-lions-buffaloes, inadvisable for security). Safety: inadvisable France Diplomacy for the entirety of the country = unstable post-coup situation, active jihadist terrorism (Boko Haram south-east, AQMI/ISGS north-west), Western kidnappings possible, formally inadvisable travel. Try djerma (millet ball), Niger greasy rice, fonio, dolo, bissap-ginger juice, spiced Arabic coffee, ceremonial Tuareg tea 3 glasses signature cultural ("1st bitter as life, 2nd sweet as love, 3rd suave as death" according to tradition). Niamey Diori Hamani airport (NIM).
cotonou:en: Cotonou, economic capital of Benin on the Atlantic coast of the Gulf of Guinea: 800,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 2.4 million in urban area. Official capital = Porto-Novo (30 km east) but 90% of economic activity is concentrated in Cotonou. Benin = cradle of voodoo (vodun, traditional West African religion practised by 50% of the population, exported to Brazil/Haiti/Cuba via the transatlantic slave trade 15th-19th century). January is my favourite month (6.7/10): 26-30°C, 18% rain, this is the ideal "little dry season" + harmattan = dry dusty Saharan wind that refreshes. Cotonou never tops 6.9/10 (humid hot equatorial climate year-round). From December to February, it's the dry season: 26-31°C. From March to June, it's the great rainy season: 43-82% rainfall. From July to August, it's the relative "little dry hivernage season": 51-57% rain. From September to November, it's the small rainy season. Cotonou highlights: Dantokpa Market (the largest open-air market in West Africa 20 hectares, extraordinary chaotic West African atmosphere, voodoo fetishes, pagne fabrics, spices, meat, dried fish, to see in the company of a local guide), Place de l'Etoile Rouge (communist-Marxist memorial heritage of the People's Republic of Benin 1975-1990 under Mathieu Kérékou), Cathedral of Notre-Dame des Apôtres, Heritage and Tourism House, Cotonou Beach (urban, strong currents dangerous for swimming, more for the walk at sunset). Essential Benin excursions = this is why one comes to Benin: Ouidah 40 km away (historic capital of the slave trade on the "Slave Coast", embarkation port of millions of slaves to Brazil 1670-1850, Slave Route 4 km from the holding pen to the Gate of No-Return = sculpted seaside memorial, overwhelming memorial place, Ouidah History Museum, Kpassè Sacred Forest = sacred voodoo wood, Temple of Pythons = vodun institution where royal pythons are sacred and free), Ganvié 30 min away ("African Venice", lake village 30,000 inhabitants on stilts in Lake Nokoué, founded 18th century by the Tofinu fleeing the Fon slave hunters who could not approach the water for ritual reasons, still active and inhabited, accessible by pirogue from Abomey-Calavi), Abomey 130 km away (UNESCO 1985, former capital of the 17th-19th century Kingdom of Dahomey, royal palaces of the 12 successive kings with spectacular bas-reliefs telling the military history including the famous Dahomey Amazons = elite corps of female warriors), Pendjari National Park (safari park north of Benin, lions, elephants, hippopotamuses, one of the last places in West Africa with wildlife). Safety: Benin globally stable, but northern borders with Burkina Faso and Niger affected by jihadist terrorism since 2018, inadvisable France Diplomacy for the north. Try moyo (tomato-chilli-fish sauce), wagasi (Fulani cheese), akassa (fermented corn ball), cassava paste, fufu, plantain cream, sodabi (palm eau-de-vie), bissap. Cotonou Cardinal Bernardin Gantin airport (COO).
lome:en: Lomé, capital of Togo on the Atlantic coast of the Gulf of Guinea: 840,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 2 million in urban area. Togo = former German colony (1884-1914 = "Togoland" = model German colony colonial propaganda) then French-British partitioned 1922 (the British part is today in neighbouring Ghana), independence 1960, ruled with an iron fist by the Gnassingbé family since 1967 (Etienne Eyadéma 1967-2005 then his son Faure Gnassingbé since 2005). January-March are my favourite months (6.9/10): 25-32°C, 18-46% rain, this is the relative dry season + refreshing harmattan. Lomé never tops 6.9/10 (humid hot equatorial climate year-round). From November to February, it's the dry season: 25-31°C. From March to June, it's the great rainy season: 41-67% rainfall. From July to August, it's the "little dry hivernage season": 37-57% rain. From September to October, it's the small rainy season. Lomé highlights: Grand Market of Lomé ("Assigamé", traditional West African central market, Togolese trade signature, wax pagne fabrics, astonishing Nana Benz = Togolese matriarchs who made fortunes in wax fabric trade in the 1960s-1970s until they could afford Mercedes-Benz, becoming icons of African feminist capitalism), Akodessewa Fetish Market ("Sorcerers' Market" = the largest vodun market in West Africa, frightening stalls for Europeans: leopard-chimpanzee-elephant skulls, iguana skins, feathers, chicken legs for traditional rituals, distinctive cultural sign to see but respect), Sacred Heart Cathedral (1902 German colonial heritage), Congress Palace, Colonial Governance (former German buildings), Lomé Beach (urban, strong Atlantic currents dangerous for swimming, more for the early morning walk with local-fisherman atmosphere), Boulevard du Mono (main palm avenue along the sea), National Museum of Togo. Excursions: Togoville 30 km away (on the shores of Lake Togo, former slave embarkation village, founding place of German Togo 1884 where local chief Mlapa III signed the protectorate treaty with Gustav Nachtigal), Aného 50 km away (former Togolese capital, historic coastal town), Kpalimé 2h in the mountains (Kpimé waterfall, Womé waterfall, hill hikes, cooler atmosphere), Atakpamé 4h away (central Plateau capital), Sokodé and Tamale in the north (traditional regions, yam festival in September-October = main Togolese cultural celebration with traditional warrior dances), Fazao-Malfakassa Park (200,000-hectare national park, modest wildlife after poaching). Try fufu (hand-pounded cassava/yam ball), gboma dessi (signature Togolese spicy sauce leaf vegetable), akpan (fermented millet semolina with milk), ablo (steam-cooked corn cake), agboma ("Mono fish" smoked), sodabi (palm eau-de-vie), bissap. Lomé Gnassingbé Eyadéma airport (LFW).
malabo:en: Malabo, capital of Equatorial Guinea on the island of Bioko in the Gulf of Guinea: 250,000 inhabitants. Geographical particularity unique in the world = the capital is on an island (Bioko) 250 km from the African continent where the rest of the country is located, situation inherited from Spanish colonisation where Bioko = former Fernando Pó important island for cocoa + slavery 16th-19th century. Only Spanish-speaking country in sub-Saharan Africa = former Spanish colony 1778-1968 (with Western Sahara), official languages Spanish + French + Portuguese + Bantu African languages. Ultra-rich country thanks to offshore oil since 1995 (highest African GDP per capita) but massive corruption under President Teodoro Obiang Nguema (in power since 1979 = longest reign in the world after Queen Elizabeth II, family dictatorship, international controversy). January is my favourite month (8.6/10): 25-28°C, 65% rain, this is the relative dry season. December-February stay excellent (7.6-8.4/10). Malabo never tops 8.6/10 (extremely humid equatorial climate year-round). From December to March, it's the relative dry season: 25-29°C, refreshing Saharan harmattan in January-February. From April to November, it's the equatorial humid season: 80-95% rainfall (Malabo is one of the most watered African capitals with 2000 mm/year), Pico Basile volcano visible 3011m highest point Bioko. Malabo highlights: Place de la Indépendance (central square with preserved 19th-20th century Spanish colonial architecture), Saint Elizabeth Cathedral (1916 Spanish colonial heritage neo-Gothic), Presidential Palace of Malabo (President Obiang's residence), Central Market of Malabo, Arena Blanca Beach ("white sand beach", contrasting with typical Bioko volcanic black sand), Pico Basile 3011m (dormant volcano, highest point Bioko + Equatorial Guinea, 2-day hike for exceptional panorama over the island + Gulf of Guinea + island of São Tomé visible), Lake Biao (volcanic crater lake in the south of Bioko), Arena Negra Beach (volcanic black sand beach signature of Bioko). Equatorial Guinea excursions: Bata 1h flight (economic capital on the continent, more traditional sub-Saharan African atmosphere), Monte Alén National Park on the continent (preserved equatorial jungle with gorillas + chimpanzees + forest elephants + drills + mandrills, very difficult access), Bata beaches (continental Atlantic). Safety: Malabo and Equatorial Guinea = among the most difficult African countries to access for tourism (very restrictive visa, multiple authorisations required, journalism forbidden, photography of official buildings strictly forbidden prison sentences), moderate urban crime but ambient political repression, inadvisable travel except oil business. Try succotash (Africanised Spanish fricassee), chicken in moambé (neighbouring Congo heritage), sopa de pescado (fish soup Spanish heritage), yam fufu, malanga, Equato-Guinean Robusta coffee, imported Spanish wines. Malabo Saint-Isabel airport (SSG).
monrovia:en: Monrovia, capital of Liberia on the Atlantic coast of West Africa: 1.1 million inhabitants. Country unique in sub-Saharan Africa = colony of former American freed slaves sent from the USA by the American Colonization Society 1822 ("Liberia" = "Land of Freedom"), independence 1847 = the oldest republic in sub-Saharan Africa. Capital named after the 5th American president James Monroe 1816-1825. English-speaking country, flag identical to USA + 1 star instead of 50. Marked by the bloody civil war 1989-2003 under Charles Taylor (250,000 deaths, child soldiers Kalashnikov-armed kids horrific signature of the conflict, ritual cannibalism of the "generals" Butt-Naked + Mosquito fighters), reconstruction since. February is my favourite month (7.9/10): 24-29°C, 56% rain, this is the relative ideal dry season. January-December stay excellent (7.8/10). Monrovia never tops 7.9/10 (very humid equatorial climate year-round). From December to March, it's the dry season: 23-29°C, refreshing Saharan harmattan in January-February. From April to October, it's the West African humid season: 86-99% rainfall (Monrovia is one of the most watered African capitals with 5000 mm/year, one of the world records for a capital). Monrovia highlights: National Capitol (American architecture inspired by the Washington Capitol), Executive House (presidential residence), National Museum of Liberia (modest ethnographic collection, americo-liberian heritage), Waterside Market (traditional West African central market), Trinity Cathedral (Episcopalian, americo-liberian heritage), Central Mosque of Monrovia, Monrovia Beach / ELWA Beach (urban Atlantic beach, strong currents dangerous for swimming), Ducor Hotel (former 5* colonial hotel abandoned since the civil war, preserved post-apocalyptic atmosphere signature visual of Monrovia, ascent of the roof for panorama), Providence Island (historic island of the Mesurado river, landing place of the first americo-liberian settlers 1822 = founding memorial place). Liberia excursions: Sapo National Park (the largest national park in Liberia, equatorial jungle with chimpanzees + pygmy hippopotamuses + forest elephants + jaguars, very difficult access), Robertsport in the north (one of the most respected surf spots in West Africa, clean Atlantic waves, bohemian atmosphere international surfers). Safety: political situation stabilised since 2003 under presidents Ellen Johnson Sirleaf 2006-2018 (1st African woman president elected, Nobel Peace Prize 2011) then George Weah 2018-2024 (former footballer Ballon d'Or 1995), moderate urban crime in Monrovia, increased France Diplomacy vigilance. Try jollof rice (West African signature), pepper soup, palm butter (signature red palm nut sauce), cassava leaf, fufu, fried plantain, palm wine (local fermented palm wine), local Club beer. Monrovia Roberts International airport (ROB).
freetown:en: Freetown, capital of Sierra Leone on the Atlantic coast of West Africa: 1.2 million inhabitants. City founded in 1792 as a refuge for freed slaves repatriated from North America and the Caribbean by British abolitionists ("Province of Freedom", first organised abolitionist establishment in Africa = major symbol of the fight against slavery). English-speaking country (British colonial heritage 1808-1961), Krio creole = unique syncretic vehicular language. Country marked by the bloody civil war 1991-2002 (50,000 deaths, child soldiers, war diamonds = "blood diamonds" subject of the Leonardo DiCaprio film 2006), reconstruction since. March is my favourite month (6.9/10): 25-29°C, 10% rain, this is the ideal dry season. Freetown never tops 6.9/10 (extremely humid equatorial climate, one of the most watered capitals in Africa). From December to April, it's the dry season: 25-30°C, humid temperate climate. From May to October, it's the extreme West African monsoon: 74-98% rainfall (Freetown = 3500 mm/year, among the rainiest capitals in the world, July-September = near-continuous rains), severe landslide risk in the hills (the Mount Sugarloaf tragedy 14 August 2017 = 1141 deaths from mudslide after torrential rains = one of the worst African natural disasters of the 21st century). Freetown highlights: Cotton Tree ("Cotton Tree", giant centenarian tree at the heart of Freetown, place where the first freed settlers gathered in 1792, signature visual and historical of the city, but unfortunately fell during a storm in May 2023 = major symbolic loss for the nation), King's Yard Gate House (1817, historic gate where freed slaves were registered on arrival), Bunce Island (historic island on the Sierra Leone river, former 17th-19th century British slave trading post = memorial place of the transatlantic slave trade to the Carolinas-Georgia USA, Gullah-Geechee community of South Carolina descends from Sierra Leoneans deported here), Fourah Bay College (the oldest university in West Africa founded 1827, nicknamed "Athens of West Africa" 19th century for its educational influence), National Museum, Big Market Freetown, Saint George Cathedral (1828 British Anglican heritage), Aberdeen and Lumley Beach (urban beaches), Tacugama Chimpanzee Sanctuary 30 min away (sanctuary of chimpanzees rescued from poaching, ecotourism signature of the country). Sierra Leone excursions: Banana Islands in the south (paradisiacal archipelago), Bunce Island slave memorial, Tiwai Island (chimpanzee wildlife sanctuary), Preserved Atlantic beaches (the beaches of the Freetown peninsula are among the most beautiful in West Africa but little known = River No. 2 Beach, Bureh Beach, Tokeh Beach). Try jollof rice (West African signature), cassava leaf (cassava leaf in sauce), groundnut soup (peanut sauce), fufu, plasas, rice and stew, palm wine (local fermented palm wine), local Star Beer. Lungi Freetown airport (FNA), particularity = airport on the north bank of the Sierra Leone river, access to Freetown by 30-45 min ferry or 15 min helicopter, unique signature arrival experience.
conakry:en: Conakry, capital of Guinea on the Atlantic coast of West Africa: 2 million inhabitants in urban area. Former French colony (1898-1958), first colony in Africa to say "no" to the De Gaulle 1958 referendum and obtain independence immediately (under Sékou Touré, Marxist dictator 1958-1984), country rich in bauxite (1st world producer) but among the poorest in Africa. Military coup in September 2021 by Colonel Mamady Doumbouya (overthrowing authoritarian President Alpha Condé). February-January-March are my favourite months (9.2-9.3/10): 24-30°C, 0-2% rain, this is the ideal dry season + refreshing Saharan harmattan. From November to May, it's the tourist high season: 24-30°C, pleasant dry and hot climate, refreshing Atlantic trade winds. From June to October, it's the intense West African monsoon: 37-99% rainfall (Conakry = one of the most watered African capitals in monsoon, July-August = near-continuous torrential rains, minimal infrastructure flood risk). Highlights: Madina Market (the largest market in West Africa according to locals, chaotic-extraordinary atmosphere), Niger Market, Faisal Grand Mosque (one of the largest mosques in West Africa, capacity 12,000 worshippers, financed by King Faisal of Saudi Arabia 1980), Saint Mary Cathedral (French colonial heritage), Palace of the People (Soviet brutalist architecture, Sékou Touré heritage 1960-70), National Museum of Guinea (Sandervalia, Baga-Soussou-Malinké ritual masks), Kaloum Quarter (main urban peninsula), Conakry Lighthouse, Loos Islands 30 min by boat ("Los Islands", archipelago of 3 preserved islands with wild Atlantic beaches, former Portuguese then British colony before retrocession to France 1904, paradisiacal atmosphere rare in West Africa). Essential Guinea excursions: Mount Nimba (UNESCO 1981, Liberia-Ivory Coast border, exceptional primate biodiversity, rich iron, hiking), Fouta Djallon ("water tower of West Africa", mountain massif where the Niger-Senegal-Gambia rivers are born, spectacular landscapes, traditional Fulani people, Dalaba-Mali-Pita hiking 6h from Conakry), Kindia 2h away ("bride's waterfall", chimpanzees in park), Kankan 12h by bus (Upper Guinea, former pre-colonial Mandingo commercial centre), Boké (Atlantic coast, bauxite). Safety: fragile political situation since the 2021 coup, increased France Diplomacy vigilance, high urban crime in Conakry (frequent armed robberies). Try Guinean greasy rice (rice with red palm oil, signature national dish), fouti (peanut sauce), mafé skewers, fried plantain, bissap, ginger juice, dolo, Guinean Robusta coffee. Conakry Gbessia airport (CKY).
bissau:en: Bissau, capital of Guinea-Bissau on the Atlantic coast of West Africa: 500,000 inhabitants. Former Portuguese colony (1446-1973 = the longest European colonisation in sub-Saharan Africa), independence by bloody liberation war under Amílcar Cabral (pan-African intellectual-revolutionary assassinated 1973). Country among the poorest in Africa, chronic political instability (8 successful coups since 1980). Tourism near non-existent. December-January are my favourite months (8.5-8.7/10): 21-33°C, 0-1% rain, this is the ideal dry season + refreshing Saharan harmattan. From November to May, it's the dry season: 21-34°C, pleasant dry and hot climate. From June to October, it's the West African monsoon: 32-92% rainfall. Bissau highlights: Old town Bissau Velho (decrepit but preserved Portuguese colonial atmosphere, paved alleys, colonial house facades collapsing = unique melancholic atmosphere), Amura Fortress (18th-century Portuguese, today houses the military garrison + Amílcar Cabral's tomb), Notre-Dame Cathedral of la Candeleria, Central Mosque of Bissau, Bandim Central Market (lively West African atmosphere), National Ethnographic Museum, Pidjiguiti Memorial (1959 massacre of striking dockers by Portuguese police = trigger of the liberation war), Praia de Bissau Beach (urban, not very clean). Essential Guinea-Bissau excursions = this is why one comes to Guinea-Bissau: Bijagos Archipelago 1h by boat from Bissau (UNESCO biosphere reserve 1996, archipelago of 88 islands of which 21 inhabited, traditional matrilineal animist peoples unique in Africa = the woman chooses her husband by Bijagos tradition, the islands of Bubaque, Orango, João Vieira, Caravela, Carache are accessible with preserved fauna endemic salt water hippopotamuses + green turtles nesting January-May + rare African manatees + saltwater crocodiles), Bijagos Islands National Park (the largest national park in Guinea-Bissau), Cacheu ("first European city in sub-Saharan Africa" founded 1588 by Portuguese as slave trading post, historic fort, slavery memorial). Safety: Bissau globally calm but urban crime, chronic political instability, very basic tourist infrastructure. Try jollof rice (greasy rice), Caldou (fish in sauce), Guinea-Bissauan feijoada (Portuguese heritage), galinha à cafrial (Portuguese spicy chicken), bagre (catfish), Portuguese coffee, vinho português, Pampa beer (national beer). Bissau Osvaldo Vieira airport (OXB), flights from Lisbon TAP Portugal direct, from Dakar neighbouring Senegal, very limited infrastructure.
banjul:en: Banjul, capital of The Gambia on the Atlantic coast of West Africa: 31,000 inhabitants intra-muros, 400,000 in urban area with Serekunda. Smallest country in continental Africa = 11,300 km², 2.5 million inhabitants, micro-state surrounded by Senegal on 3 sides + 80 km Atlantic frontage. English-speaking country (British colonial heritage 1888-1965) at the heart of francophone West Africa. More peaceful and accessible atmosphere for Western visitors ("The Smiling Coast of Africa" official nickname, one of the most welcoming African destinations for tourism). March-April-May are my favourite months (9.3/10): 21-30°C, 0-1% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January stays excellent (9.0/10). From November to May, it's the tourist high season: 21-32°C, pleasant dry and hot climate, refreshing Atlantic trade winds, harmattan Saharan wind December-February = relative cold and dusty. From June to October, it's the rainy season (West African monsoon "hivernage"): 11-77% rainfall. Highlights: Arch 22 (Arch 22, 35m high monument 1996 commemorating the coup of 22 July 1994 of President Yahya Jammeh = controversial memorial atmosphere as Jammeh became authoritarian), Albert Market (traditional West African central market, wax pagne fabrics, signature Gambian sculpted wood, batik), National Museum of The Gambia, Notre-Dame Cathedral of the Assumption, Historic Banjul Quarter (preserved British colonial old town), Atlantic beaches = the main Gambian tourist attraction: Senegambia Beach + Kotu Beach + Cape Point 15-30 km from Banjul, golden sand, cool Atlantic water 22-25°C thanks to the cold Canary current, moderate surf. Essential Gambia excursions: Abuko Reserve 1h away (the most accessible of the Gambian wildlife reserves, observation vervet monkeys-crocodiles-iguanas, simplified African experience for families), James Island/Kunta Kinteh Island (UNESCO 2003, former 16th-19th century transatlantic slave embarkation port, popularised worldwide by the novel "Roots" by Alex Haley 1976 telling the story of his ancestor Kunta Kinteh kidnapped in The Gambia 1767, place of African-American Roots Heritage Tour pilgrimage, island eroded by the sea in progressive submersion), Juffureh (Kunta Kinteh's village of origin according to Roots, memorial atmosphere), Makasutu Cultural Forest (private forest reserve, ecotourism), River Gambia National Park (river cruise on the Gambia river crossing the whole country = "Gambia Bird Trail" one of the best bird-watching spots in West Africa, 600 bird species including endemic Abyssinian hornbill). Tourist particularity: The Gambia has become famous for "bumster" tourism = improvised local guides soliciting Western tourists on the beaches, sometimes male prostitution towards Northern European women (mainly British), significant social phenomenon since the 1990s. Try domoda (chicken peanut sauce, Gambian national dish), benachin (spiced fish rice), yassa (chicken with onion lemon, neighbouring Senegalese heritage), thieboudienne (West African signature fish rice), bissap (hibiscus juice), JulBrew Beer (Gambian national beer). Banjul Yundum airport (BJL).
lilongwe:en: Lilongwe, capital of Malawi in southern Africa: 990,000 inhabitants. Planned capital built ex-nihilo in the 1970s (previous capital = Zomba and Blantyre remains the economic capital), calmer atmosphere than Lagos-Nairobi-Kampala. Country among the poorest in Africa but one of the most peaceful and stable, nicknamed "The Warm Heart of Africa" for the legendary hospitality of Malawians. September is my favourite month (9.5/10): 13-29°C, 4% rain, this is the ideal austral spring. July-October stay excellent (8.4-8.7/10). From May to October, it's the cool dry season: 7-29°C thanks to altitude (Lilongwe at 1050m), ideal temperate-tropical altitude climate. From November to April, it's the rainy season: 27-72% rainfall, but stable temperatures 15-29°C, greener landscapes. Permanent climate: Lilongwe never tops 29°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 7°C in minimum average thanks to its altitude 1050m, ideal "Eternal Spring" tropical altitude climate. Lilongwe highlights: Lilongwe Wildlife Centre (wildlife sanctuary saved from poaching, tourist signature of Lilongwe), Old Lilongwe / Old Town (local atmosphere, traditional central market), Capital City (new administrative quarter planned 1970s, large verdant avenues), Kamuzu Mausoleum (mausoleum of founding President Hastings Kamuzu Banda 1898-1997 dictator 1964-1994 then overthrown by first multiparty elections), Malawi Parliament, Tobacco Auction Floors ("Tobacco Exchange", Malawi is the largest African exporter of tobacco, spectacular morning auctions April-October economic signature). Essential Malawi excursions = this is why one visits Malawi: Lake Malawi / Lake Niassa (UNESCO 1984, the 9th largest lake in the world 29,600 km², the 3rd in Africa after Victoria and Tanganyika, nicknamed "Lake of Stars" by David Livingstone 1859, paradisiacal freshwater beaches golden sand + crystal water + 1000+ colourful endemic cichlid species = top world for aquariophily, Cape Maclear + Nkhata Bay + Likoma Island = main lake beach destinations), Liwonde National Park (accessible safari park, elephants + lions + leopards + hippopotamuses + buffaloes, reintroduction of cheetah 2017 = beautiful conservation success), Majete Wildlife Reserve ("African Parks" remarkable rehabilitation success, complete Big Five reintroduced), Mount Mulanje in the south (endemic Mulanje Cedar, 3002m granite plateau hike signature), Nyika Plateau in the north (surprising alpine landscapes at 2200m altitude, pastures as far as the eye can see with antelopes), Zomba Plateau (former colonial capital, panoramic plateau 1800m). Safety: Malawi among the safest African countries, moderate urban crime in Lilongwe. Try nsima (signature southern African corn porridge, food base), chambo (Lake Malawi grilled cichlid signature), kachumbari (salad), thobwa (fermented millet drink), Carlsberg Malawi (the only Carlsberg brewery in sub-Saharan Africa, heritage of Scandinavian development aid links), local Kuche Kuche beer. Lilongwe Kamuzu airport (LLW).
mbabane:en: Mbabane, administrative capital of Eswatini (formerly Swaziland until renamed 2018 by King Mswati III) in southern Africa: 95,000 inhabitants at 1200m altitude. Country among the smallest in Africa (17,364 km²), landlocked between South Africa and Mozambique. Last absolute monarchy in Africa = King Mswati III rules without constitution since 1986, controversy over personal wealth vs national poverty (HIV rate the highest in the world 27% adult population). Preserved cultural country with Swazi traditions and annual royal ceremonies. May-June-July are my favourite months (7.9-8.2/10): 8-21°C, 5-13% rain, this is the ideal dry season thanks to altitude. August-September stay excellent (8.0-8.2/10). From May to September, it's the dry season: 8-22°C, temperate African climate, clear sky for wildlife observation. From October to April, it's the wet season: 27-68% rainfall, but stable temperatures 14-25°C. Permanent climate: Mbabane never tops 25°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 8°C in minimum average thanks to its altitude 1200m, ideal temperate African climate contrasting with subtropical Cape Town South Africa. Highlights: Mbabane Centre (modest little touristic African capital in itself), Swazi Plaza and Swazi Market (local atmosphere, Swazi craftsmanship), Ezulwini Valley 15 km away ("Valley of the Sky", the main tourist area of the country, former Swazi kingdom, 4-5* casino hotels, livelier atmosphere than Mbabane), Mantenga Cultural Village ("traditional Swazi cultural village", reconstruction of a traditional Swazi village with traditional dance demonstrations, reconstituted Swazi wedding, culturally informative pedagogical atmosphere), Sibebe Rock ("Bald Rock", granite monolith 2nd largest in the world after Uluru Australia, 3km panoramic ascent), House of Fire (cultural-gallery centre with annual festivals), Big Bend and Lobamba (official royal and legislative capitals, royal palace Embo State Palace, parliament). Swazi royal ceremonies = the great cultural attraction of Eswatini: Umhlanga / Reed Dance (August-September, 100,000 virgin young girls present reeds to the Queen Mother and the King in millennial ceremony = unique world Swazi cultural signature, international controversy as the king can choose a new wife from among them), Incwala / First Fruits Ceremony (December-January, sacred royal ceremony forbidden to foreigners). Essential Eswatini excursions: Hlane Royal National Park (national park, lions, elephants, rhinoceros, Big 4 except leopards), Mlilwane Wildlife Sanctuary (more accessible reserve, hiking-bike-horse safari), Mkhaya Game Reserve (black and white rhinoceros), Malolotja Nature Reserve (panoramic mountains with Malolotja Canopy Tour suspended walkway). International excursions: Kruger National Park South Africa 2h away, Maputo Mozambique 2h away. Try sishwala (corn porridge), siphuphe (vegetables), nyama yenkomo (grilled beef braai), umngqusho (corn beans), imported South African wines, local Sibebe Beer. King Mswati III airport at Manzini (SHO).
maseru:en: Maseru, capital of Lesotho in southern Africa: 330,000 inhabitants at 1600m altitude on the Caledon river bordering South Africa. Only country in the world entirely above 1000m altitude ("Kingdom of the Sky", highest point Thabana Ntlenyana 3482m), royal enclave entirely surrounded by South Africa. Constitutional kingdom under King Letsie III since 1996, majority Basotho people = historic warriors with mokorotlo hat = national cultural signature + traditional Basotho blankets. September-October are my favourite months (8.8/10): 7-26°C, 11-20% rain, this is the ideal austral spring. January-November-December stay excellent (8.4-8.5/10). From September to March, it's the hot summer season: 12-27°C with altitude storms. From May to August, it's the cool winter season: 0-21°C with snow at altitude (Lesotho has the only ski resort in sub-Saharan Africa = Afriski at 3050m, season June-August). Unique permanent climate: Maseru never tops 27°C in maximum average and descends to 0°C in minimum average in winter, exceptional cold-temperate altitude climate for Africa. Highlights: Maseru Centre (modest capital, relaxed Basotho atmosphere), Central market (Basotho craftsmanship, signature pointed mokorotlo hats, colourful Basotho blankets), Royal Palace (royal palace, no visit access but exterior photo), Notre-Dame des Victoires Cathedral, Cathedral of the Holy Trinity, Khotso House Quarter (Father of the Nation memorial), Lephole Pony Trekking (tours on Basuto ponies = endemic breed of robust ponies adapted to Lesothan mountains, signature experience). Essential Lesotho excursions = this is why one comes to Lesotho: Sani Pass ("highest drivable road in sub-Saharan Africa" 2876m, southern border with South Africa, vertiginous switchback road in the Drakensberg, accessible by mandatory 4x4 = signature experience to undertake from Underberg South African side, 360° panorama over Lesothan mountains at the summit, Sani Mountain Lodge "highest pub in Africa"), Maletsunyane Falls (192m, one of the highest single-drop waterfalls in Africa, accessible by 6h hike or heliport, abseil rappel descent = signature activity "longest abseil in the world" 204m Guinness Book), Thaba-Bosiu ("Mountain of the Night", birthplace of the Lesotho kingdom with King Moshoeshoe I 19th century, considered the spiritual cradle of the Basotho nation), Lesothan Drakensberg (UNESCO 2000 Maloti-Drakensberg Park cross-border with South Africa, San rock paintings = prehistoric Bushmen art 4000 years, hiking), Katse Dam (the largest dam in sub-Saharan Africa 185m, hydroelectric project that exports water to Johannesburg), Afriski Mountain Resort (3050m, only sub-Saharan ski resort in Africa with Tiffindell South Africa, modest 3 pistes but unique experience). Try papa (signature Sotho corn porridge), moroho (green vegetables), nyama (grilled meat), seswaa (sliced neighbouring Botswanan beef), mokoenya (Sotho beignet), local Maluti Beer. Logistics: Moshoeshoe I International Maseru airport (MSU), also accessible by car from Johannesburg South Africa 5h via Maseru Bridge border.
brazzaville:en: Brazzaville, capital of the Republic of the Congo ("Congo-Brazzaville") on the Congo river: 1.8 million inhabitants. Facing Kinshasa DRC rival capital = 2 capitals of different countries separated only by the river = only place in the world where 2 capitals face each other directly, river crossing by ferry 15 min between the 2 cities. Former French colony "Middle Congo" 1880-1960 (founded 1880 by Pierre Savorgnan de Brazza Franco-Italian explorer who gave his name to the city), capital of French Equatorial Africa 1910-1958 then independence 1960. Country rich in offshore oil but massive corruption under President Denis Sassou-Nguesso (1979-1992 then 1997-today = one of the longest reigns in Africa). June-July are my favourite months (9.1-9.2/10): 19-29°C, 0-2% rain, this is the ideal dry season. May-August stay excellent (8.0-8.6/10). From May to September, it's the dry season: 19-30°C, dry and cool tropical climate (cacimbo). From October to April, it's the equatorial humid season: 47-65% rainfall. Brazzaville highlights: Notre-Dame du Rosaire Cathedral / Saint Anne Basilica (1949 French colonial heritage, modern architecture), Place de la Liberté (central square), Pierre Savorgnan de Brazza Mausoleum (2006, monument to the explorer founder of the city, controversial modern mausoleum for its 10 million euros while the city is in poverty), Total Market (traditional central market), Quay of the Congo river (river atmosphere on the edge of the Congo facing Kinshasa with Kinshasa skyscrapers visible 4 km away), Nabemba Tower (skyscraper 106m, signature visual of modern Brazzaville), Massamba-Débat Stadium, National Museum of Congo. Essential excursions: Pointe-Noire 1h flight (economic capital on the Atlantic coast, preserved beaches, port atmosphere), Lésio-Louna Gorilla Reserve 4h away (sanctuary of lowland gorillas reintroduced to the wild after captive breeding), Lefini Reserve (chimpanzees). International excursion: Kinshasa 15 min by ferry (ferry crossing the Congo between the 2 capitals = unique world experience of river border crossing between 2 capitals of different states), customs formalities and visa required. Safety: Brazzaville more stable than neighbouring Kinshasa, moderate urban crime, increased France Diplomacy vigilance. Try saka saka (cassava leaves in sauce), moambé fish (Bantu Congo + DRC shared signature culinary, red palm nut sauce), moambé chicken, cassava fufu, fried plantain, local Ngok + Primus beers. Brazzaville Maya-Maya airport (BZV).
maun:en: Maun is the logistical base of the Okavango Delta, one of the largest inland deltas in the world, listed UNESCO. International airport, agencies for mokoros and safaris. May is my favourite month (9.5/10): 13-28°C, 0% rain, just after the rains when the delta is in full flooding. From June to August, it's the cool dry season (mornings 9-13°C, days 25°C): the great safari season. Animals concentrated around permanent water points, maximum visibility. From September to November, expect 17-36°C: the heat becomes overwhelming, the first rains arrive. From December to March, it's the green season: 20-30°C with 26-47% rain, photogenic landscapes, prices divided by 2-3, but some tracks become impassable. Mokoro (traditional pirogue) remains the signature of the delta. Expect 350-800$/night for a camp in the concession. Domestic Air Botswana flight from Gaborone or direct charter flight.
tofo:en: Tofo (Praia do Tofo), small fishing village on the south coast of Mozambique: 5000 inhabitants 22 km east of the town of Inhambane. Diving destination world-renowned for the giant manta rays and whale sharks visible year-round in local waters. November is my favourite month (9.0/10): 23-28°C, 28% rain, sea at 27°C, end of the dry season, ideal temperatures. From June to November, it's the dry season: 18-28°C, 17-30% rainfall, blue sky and refreshing trade winds, this is the best period. From December to April, it's the rainy season: 34-61% rainfall, but stable temperatures (22-29°C), real cyclonic risk (Cyclone Idai 2019 killed 1300 people in Mozambique, Freddy 2023 directly hit Tofo). Major activities: Whale shark diving (Rhincodon typus, the largest fish in the world 12-18m, present year-round at Tofo, one of the only places in the world allowing such frequent encounters - 40-50% of outings), Manta ray diving (Manta alfredi coastal + Manta birostris giant oceanic 5-7m, largest world population of Manta alfredi at cleaning station at Manta Reef and Giant's Castle), Surfing (consistent waves Tofinho Point for advanced surfers, beach break Tofo for intermediates), Big game fishing (sailfish, marlin), traditional dhow boutre excursion at sunset. Beaches: Praia do Tofo (the liveliest, 7 km of white sand), Praia da Rocha, Praia da Barra (7 km north, smarter and privatised by resorts), Closeau. Hotels and lodges: Tofo Mar Sea View Hotel, Mozambeat Motel, Praia da Barra resorts (Barra Lodge, Flamingo Bay), 50-300€/night. Public: international divers, surfers, young backpackers, South Africans on weekends (Tofo 8h by car from Johannesburg). Try Mozambique prawns LM (giant grilled prawns with local piri-piri), peri-peri chicken (Portuguese heritage), matapa (cassoulet cassava-peanut-coconut leaves), 2M beer ("Dois M" national pride), Tipo Tinto rum.
nosybe:en: Nosy Be ("big island" in Malagasy), main tourist island of Madagascar off the north-west in the Mozambique Channel: 110,000 inhabitants on 312 km². The archipelago includes Nosy Be + 7 other islets (Nosy Komba, Nosy Iranja, Nosy Tanikely marine reserve, Nosy Sakatia, etc.). Nicknamed "the perfume island" for its ylang-ylang plantations (Madagascar is the 1st world producer with 80% of the global market, luxury perfumery Chanel n°5). July is my favourite month (9.0/10): 22-28°C, 20% rain, sea at 26°C, this is the full ideal dry season. From May to October, it's the tourist high season: 22-30°C, 31-57% rain, dry climate, refreshing trade winds. From November to April, it's the rainy and cyclonic season: 70-94% rainfall, sea sometimes rough, real cyclonic risk (Madagascar is one of the most cyclone-exposed countries in the world). Highlights: Mont Passot (329m, highest point of Nosy Be, 7 sacred volcanic lakes visible from the summit, 360° panorama at sunset), Hell-Ville (capital and main port, central market, former French colonial houses — the island was called "Nosy Be" then renamed "Helleville" in honour of Admiral de Hell during the French protectorate 1841-1960, return to local name after independence), Nosy Be beaches: Andilana (the most beautiful in the north-west), Madirokely, Ambondrona, Ambatoloaka (hotel zone), Ylang-ylang plantations (traditional distillery visit), Cocoa plantation, Lokobe Reserve (primary forest with black lemur endemic species). Neighbouring island excursions: Nosy Komba 30 min by boat ("lemur island", tame macaco lemurs climb on visitors), Nosy Iranja 1h30 (double island connected by a white sand bank visible only at low tide, signature photo, sea turtles), Nosy Tanikely 30 min (snorkelling marine reserve with multicoloured fish and turtles), Nosy Sakatia (snorkelling with green turtles). Activities: diving exceptional (visibility 20-30m, manta rays, whale sharks November-March), whale shark cruise, humpback whale watching July-September. Try grilled fish, lobsters, romazava, THB beer.
socotra:en: Socotra is one of the most difficult destinations in the world to access: Yemeni island of the Indian Ocean, flights only from Abu Dhabi (Air Arabia, 1 time per week), mandatory guides for most sites. October to April is the workable period. February is my favourite month (9.3/10): 16-27°C, 5% rain, calm sea to reach Detwah Lagoon. The dragon trees (Dracaena cinnabari) of the Diksam plateau are the absolute signature, complemented by the bottle trees and endemic desert roses. From May to September, the south-west monsoon brings violent winds, some flights suspended, access to north beaches cut off. It's when the bougainvilleas bloom. The logistics remain rudimentary: accommodation in eco-camps, little hot water, generator electricity, USD cash payment only. Expect 1500-2500€ for an 8-day trip. Destination reserved for prepared travellers.
fujairah:en: Fujairah, one of the 7 United Arab Emirates and only emirate on the east coast on the Gulf of Oman: 250,000 inhabitants. Only emirate without coast on the Persian Gulf = major climatic benefit (cooler water, ocean breezes, less humid) + spectacular Hajar mountain landscapes. "Nature and diving" alternative to Dubai for Emirati locals and expatriates. December-February-March are my favourite months (9.0/10): 16-29°C, 7-11% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January-November stay excellent (6.8-9.0/10). From November to April, it's the tourist high season: 16-35°C, sea at 22-26°C. From May to October drop to 3.6-6.9/10 due to extreme heatwave + coastal humidity (29-39°C, sometimes 47°C+). Highlights: Fujairah Beach (finer sand and more turquoise water than on the Persian Gulf side Dubai/RAK thanks to position on Indian Ocean-Sea of Oman), Fujairah Fort (16th-17th century, one of the oldest forts in the UAE), Sheikh Zayed Mosque Fujairah (2015, main mosque of the emirate), Al Bidya Mosque (the oldest mosque in the UAE, 15th century, tradition saying it was built by a man in a single day, unique design with 4 ornamental mud domes signature), Wadi Al Wurayah (national park, one of the only UAE wadis with permanent water, waterfalls, 4x4 hike), Hatta (Dubai enclave in the Hajar mountains 30 min from Fujairah, accessible from Fujairah, picturesque green lakes, kayaking, traditional Hatta Heritage Village), Snoopy Island (rocky islet shaped like Snoopy the dog, signature of Fujairah, snorkelling from the beach with nearby coral reef), Khor Fakkan (Sharjah enclave in Fujairah, urban beach, fishing port, more local atmosphere). Activities: Sea of Oman diving (Fujairah is the main UAE diving base, major sites Martini Rock, Dibba Rock, Inchcape 1 and 2 wrecks = wrecks voluntarily sunk for artificial reefs, hammerhead sharks possible October-December, one of the international Gulf divers' bases), preserved Snoopy beaches, Al Aqah, Hajar Mountains hiking, Friday-fish market at Khor Fakkan. Hotels: Le Méridien Al Aqah Beach Resort, Fujairah Rotana Resort, Iberotel Miramar Al Aqah, prices 30-40% lower than Dubai. Excursions: Dubai 2h by car (motorway crossing the desert and Hajar mountains), Sharjah 1h30 (already covered by BDW), Muscat Oman 4h by car (Hatta border + car, already covered by BDW), Khasab Oman (Musandam Peninsula, spectacular fjords, dolphins, accessible in 1h from Fujairah). Try Emirati machboos, balaleet (sweet vermicelli eggs), harees, luqaimat. Fujairah International airport (FJR) modest, alternative Dubai DXB or Sharjah SHJ airport 1h-1h30 more practical for international flights.
ras-al-khaimah:en: Ras Al Khaimah (RAK), northernmost emirate of the United Arab Emirates 100 km north-east of Dubai: 350,000 inhabitants. Calmer and more traditional emirate than Dubai/Abu Dhabi, preserved natural landscapes (Hajar mountains, beaches, deserts) = "nature" alternative to Dubai skyscrapers 1h by road. March is my favourite month (9.2/10): 20-28°C, 7% rain, this is the ideal dry season. December-February stay excellent (8.7-9.0/10). From November to April, it's the tourist high season: 16-32°C, sea at 22-25°C, hotel prices x2-x3. From May to October drop to 3.2-6.8/10 due to extreme heatwave (28-40°C, sometimes 50°C+ with oppressive coastal humidity). Highlights: Jebel Jais (1934m, highest mountain in the UAE, accessible by 35 km panoramic switchback road from the village of Jais, climate 10°C cooler than at sea level = unique in the UAE = snow possible exceptionally in winter, houses the Jebel Jais Flight = the longest zipline in the world 2.83 km Guinness Book, speed 150 km/h, signature experience of RAK), RAK Old Town (more local traditional old town than Dubai, preserved historic Arab atmosphere), National Museum of RAK (in former fort), Dhayah Fort (16th-century mountain fort, panorama), Al Jazeera Al Hamra (former pearl fishing village abandoned 1968 when the inhabitants moved after UAE unification, memorial atmosphere unique in the UAE, preserved architectural ghost), Desert dunes around RAK (4x4 dune-bashing more accessible and less touristic than in Dubai), RAK public beaches (white sand, turquoise water, less frequented than Dubai/Abu Dhabi), Suwaidi Pearl Farm (modernised pearl farm, demonstration of traditional pearl fishing which was the main historical pre-oil UAE industry), Khatt Hot Springs (hot springs), Wadi Shawka (spectacular wadi for hiking). Activities: Jebel Jais Flight zipline (signature, 650 AED ≈ 165€), Jais via ferrata (the 1st via ferrata of the Gulf), Jebel Jais hiking (recent trails), desert safari 4x4 + night in Bedouin camp with dinner-belly dance show, Sea of Oman diving (RAK is on the Persian Gulf but 1h from Fujairah diving sites on the Sea of Oman). Hotels: Waldorf Astoria RAK, Rixos Bab Al Bahr, Hilton Marjan Island (5*+ all-inclusive on the artificial Al Marjan Island), prices 30-50% lower than Dubai for equal quality. Wynn Resort Casino = first legal casino in the UAE under construction on Al Marjan Island, opening planned 2027, major transformation of RAK to become a Vegas-style Middle East destination. Try Emirati machboos, harees, luqaimat (date beignets), Arabic coffee. Ras Al Khaimah airport (RKT) with European charter flights, alternative to Dubai DXB 1h by car.
majuro:en: Majuro, capital of the Marshall Islands (Republic of the Marshall Islands) in the central Pacific Ocean: 28,000 inhabitants on a sand-strip atoll 64 km long and only 200m maximum wide. Pacific micro-state archipelagic country with 1100 islands scattered over 1.9 million km² of ocean (the lowest world habitation density by maritime surface area). Independence from the USA 1986 but remains in "Compact of Free Association" = free military-monetary-passport association with USA (Marshallese citizens can live/work in the USA). Country among the most exposed in the world to sea level rise due to global warming (average altitude 2m, several islands already disappeared). February is my favourite month (7.8/10): 26-28°C, 65% rain, this is the relative dry season. January stays excellent (7.7/10). From January to March, it's the dry season: 26-28°C, sea at 28°C. From May to November, it's the tropical wet season: 86-90% rainfall, but stable temperatures. Extremely stable climate: Majuro never tops 28°C in maximum average and rarely descends below 26°C in minimum average = annual range among the smallest in the world. Highlights: DUD Atoll (Delap-Uliga-Djarrit, urbanised part of the Majuro atoll, capital + administration), Majuro beaches (white sand, turquoise water, snorkelling from the shore with nearby coral reef, free public beaches but few amenities), Alele Museum (national museum with traditional Marshallese artefacts notably stick charts = wood and shell stick charts used by traditional Marshallese navigators to cross the Pacific guided by ocean currents without Western instrument, classified UNESCO intangible heritage), Capitol Building, WWII Memorial (World War II memorial, the Marshalls were the theatre of intense battles 1944 between US Marines and Japanese, codename "Operation Flintlock"), Laura Beach 50 km away (western tip of the atoll, the most beautiful beach in Majuro, paradisiacal atmosphere), Aquarium-reef of Majuro Atoll. Essential excursions = this is why one comes to the Marshalls: Bikini Atoll (UNESCO 2010, memorial site of the 23 American nuclear tests 1946-1958 including the Castle Bravo test 1954 = the largest thermonuclear explosion ever conducted by the USA 15 megatons, 1000x Hiroshima, lasting radioactive contamination, displaced population still in exile today = major human and ecological tragedy of the 20th century, diving on the wrecks of the American fleet sunk by nuclear tests = top 5 world for wreck diving, USS Saratoga aircraft carrier, USS Arkansas battleship, IJN Nagato Japanese battleship, very limited access, liveaboard expeditions 9-12 days 6000-12000 USD), Arno Atoll (neighbouring atoll of Majuro 30 min by boat), Mili Atoll and other more remote atolls. Sport diving: Majuro and Bikini = top world for WWII wreck diving and nuclear tests. Try lo-poke (raw fish in coconut milk, Marshallese signature), ulanga (taro), pwiwur (plantain porridge), grilled fish, fresh coconut milk, local Marshall Beer. Logistics: Majuro Amata Kabua International airport (MAJ), flights from Honolulu (5h via legendary United Airlines "Island Hopper" which serves several atolls), Tokyo, Manila. Cost: Majuro very expensive for Pacific micro-state standard (costly imports), 200-400 USD/day minimum.
tarawa:en: Tarawa, capital of Kiribati on the South Tarawa atoll in the central Pacific: 65,000 inhabitants. Archipelagic micro-state country of 33 atolls scattered over 3.5 million km² of ocean ("largest archipelagic country by marine surface area in the world"), 119,000 inhabitants in total, independent of the United Kingdom since 1979. Country split between 3 different time zones thanks to its dispersion = only country in the world in all hemispheres 24h/24 (equator crosses Kiribati, international meridian 180° also). Very little touristic (3000 visitors/year, among the least visited countries in the world). Country at the highest world risk with Tuvalu of disappearance by sea level rise. March is my favourite month (8.7/10): 27-28°C, 29% rain, this is the ideal dry season. January-February stay excellent (8.3/10). From January to April, it's the dry season "Aumeang": 26-29°C, sea at 28°C. From May to December, it's the wet season "Aumaiaki": 60-94% rainfall. Highlights: Battle of Tarawa November 1943 Memorials (Tarawa = theatre of one of the most violent battles of the Pacific in 76 hours 20-23 November 1943, 1000 US Marines killed + 3000 Japanese out of 4500 defenders, the deadliest amphibious operation of the Pacific War as the marines had underestimated tidal currents and Japanese fortifications, modest memorials scattered on the atoll), Bairiki Beach (capital beach, white sand), South Tarawa and North Tarawa (2 distinct V-shaped parts of the atoll, boat access), Japanese military vestiges (rusty cannons, concrete bunkers still visible in the jungle, ghostly resemblance to Iwo Jima), Maneaba ("community house" traditional Kiribati = large pandanus-covered building for village meetings, signature Gilbert islands cultural, to see in the outer village of Tarawa). Activities: diving and snorkelling (spectacular living reefs, manta rays, reef sharks, PADI training possible but limited infrastructure), traditional fishing with local fishermen, tour of neighbouring atolls by boat (Abaiang 30 min by lancha), traditional Kiribati dances ("te bino", village performances during ceremonies, legendary Kiribati hospitality). Cultural particularities: Christmas Island (Kiritimati in Kiribati, one of the largest coral islands in the world 388 km², eastern part of Kiribati, world-renowned bonefish fishing, marine bird watching), Banaba (Ocean Island) (phosphate island exploited until exhaustion by British 1900-1979 then abandoned, population transferred to Rabi Fiji, become almost uninhabited island). Safety: peaceful Kiribati, near non-existent crime but great poverty, very basic tourist infrastructure, few accommodations (3-4 hotels-guesthouses on Tarawa). Try tuna sashimi (fresh fish, the main industry of the country = tuna fishing licences sold to foreign fishermen), te kabubu (traditional pandanus porridge), te bero (coconut pancake), grilled fish, coconut milk, te kamaimai (fermented coconut palm syrup). Logistics: Tarawa Bonriki airport (TRW), Fiji Airways flights from Suva 3h or Air Niugini from Brisbane via Honiara, among the most difficult to access in the world, high prices.
palikir:en: Palikir, capital of the Federated States of Micronesia (FSM) on the island of Pohnpei in the central Pacific: 7000 inhabitants. Archipelagic country of 607 islands scattered over 2.9 million km² of ocean, independent of the USA since 1986 but in "Compact of Free Association" with USA (free military-monetary-passport association). January-February are my favourite months (7.8/10): 25-28°C, 62-63% rain, this is the relative dry season. Palikir never tops 7.8/10 (very humid equatorial climate year-round). From December to March, it's the dry season: 25-28°C, sea at 28°C. From April to November, it's the wet season: 71-94% rainfall, but stable temperatures 25-29°C. Extremely stable climate: Palikir = one of the world capitals with the most regular climate, annual range less than 2°C, 25-28°C year-round. Highlights: Nan Madol (UNESCO 2016, megalithic archaeological site unique in the world = "Venice of the Pacific", complex of 92 artificial islets built in columnar basalt blocks between 1100-1628 AD on coastal reefs, former ritual capital of the Saudeleur dynasty of Pohnpei, mysterious atmosphere with jungle invading the ruins, accessible only at high tide by kayak or boat, one of the most impressive archaeological sites in the Pacific), Sokehs Rock (signature rock of Pohnpei shaped like "Diamond Head Hawaii", 200m high, 2h hike for 360° panorama over Pohnpei + Palikir + coral reef), Liduduhniap Twin Falls (waterfalls in the jungle), Kepirohi Waterfall, Pohnpei Surf Club (one of the most remote surf spots in the world with exceptional reef waves, accessible to advanced surfers only, preserved atmosphere), Pohnpei Microsstate Capital (simple federal government), Kolonia Market (the largest town on Pohnpei 7 km from Palikir, traditional Micronesian island atmosphere). Activities: diving and snorkelling (exceptional living reefs, manta rays, seasonal whale sharks, top world for WWII wreck diving especially on the Truk/Chuuk Atoll 1h by plane = one of the largest wreck cemeteries in the world after the US attack of Operation Hailstone February 1944 which sank 60 Japanese ships at 30-50m), kava ("sakau" in Pohnpeian, traditional Pacific drink made of pounded then filtered Piper methysticum root, light relaxant-narcotic effect, to drink in the local sakau bars in the evening = signature experience of Pohnpei). Excursions: Chuuk Lagoon 1h by plane (top world WWII wrecks), Yap 2h by plane (traditional island preserving the stone money = round stones 4m in diameter still used as currency, unique world cultural signature, manta ray diving at Mil Channel top world), Kosrae (the 4th main island, more traditional). Try chukenap (raw fish in coconut milk), pohpei (cooked taro), jackfruit, ceremonial kava-sakau. Logistics: Pohnpei International airport (PNI), unique legendary United Airlines "Island Hopper" flight 5x weekly connecting Honolulu-Majuro-Kwajalein-Kosrae-Pohnpei-Chuuk-Guam-Manila in 12h.
larnaca:en: Larnaca (Larnaka), 3rd city of Cyprus on the south-east coast: 90,000 inhabitants. Former Phoenician-Roman port Citium = birthplace of the Stoic philosopher Zeno of Citium (334 BC, founder of Stoicism). Main airport of Cyprus = gateway for the majority of tourists. More traditional and local atmosphere than Paphos or Limassol. May is my favourite month (9.5/10): 16-26°C, 7% rain, sea at 21°C, ideal temperatures. June and October stay excellent (8.8-9.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 18-32°C. July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-32°C). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 18-37% rain: mild for the Mediterranean. Highlights: Saint Lazarus Church (Agios Lazaros, 9th-17th century Byzantine and Baroque, contains the tomb of Lazarus = the brother of Mary and Martha resurrected by Jesus according to the Gospels, who allegedly fled Bethany for Cyprus where he was the first bishop of Citium and would have died and is buried here, one of the most sacred Christian sites in Cyprus, place of international pilgrimage), Hala Sultan Tekke (mosque founded 8th century on the tomb of Umm Haram, foster aunt of the Prophet Muhammad died here during an Arab raid 649, place of Muslim pilgrimage, located on the edge of the Larnaca Salt Lake populated in season by 2000-12000 migratory pink flamingos, ideal photo January-March), Foinikoudes Promenade ("Palm Tree Promenade", 600m of seaside with palm trees, restaurants-cafés, urban beach), Mackenzie Beach (the main urban beach, grey-golden sand, lively atmosphere), Larnaca Fort (14th century, former Venetian-Ottoman fort), Pierides District (pedestrian shopping streets, traditional market), Lefkara 30 min away (typical inland village, famous for its traditional lace and embroidery "lefkaritika" UNESCO 2009 in the list of intangible heritage, and its silver gold work, Leonardo da Vinci would have bought an embroidered handkerchief for the high altar of Milan Cathedral in 1481). Excursions: Stavrovouni Monastery 30 min away (monastery on summit 668m founded according to tradition by Saint Helen mother of Constantine 4th century bringing fragment of the True Cross, one of the oldest monasteries in the world, access forbidden to women), Choirokoitia 40 min away (UNESCO, Neolithic site 7000 BC, one of the oldest human dwellings in the Mediterranean), Aiya Napa 30 min away (festive youth resort), Cape Greco (spectacular cliffs, sea caves), Nicosia 50 min away (capital, already covered). Try Cypriot meze, kleftiko (lamb slow-cooked in closed pot), shoeftalies, koupes (stuffed wheat balls), loukoumades (honey beignets), Cypriot brandy. Larnaca airport (LCA), the largest in Cyprus, direct European flights.
paphos:en: Paphos (Pafos), former royal capital of Cyprus in the south-west of the island: 35,000 inhabitants. UNESCO 1980 for its exceptionally rich archaeological site. According to Greek mythology, birthplace of Aphrodite (the goddess of love would have been born from the sea foam at Petra tou Romiou 25 km from Paphos). European Capital of Culture 2017. June is my favourite month (9.8/10): 19-29°C, 1% rain, sea at 23°C, ideal temperatures. May-October stay excellent (8.6-9.4/10). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (24-32°C). From November to March, expect 8-19°C and 13-37% rain: mild for the Mediterranean. Highlights: Paphos Archaeological Park (UNESCO 1980, former Nea Paphos = capital of Cyprus 4th century BC to 4th century AD, destroyed by earthquake 332 AD, vestiges still being excavated, main attraction = exceptional Roman mosaics in the villas House of Dionysus, House of Aion, House of Theseus, House of Orpheus, 3rd-4th century AD, among the best preserved in the Mediterranean world), Tombs of the Kings (UNESCO, Hellenistic-Roman necropolis 4th century BC with monumental tombs sculpted in the rock, similar to Petra but more modest), Byzantine Castle of Saracen (13th century on the medieval port of Kato Pafos), Saint Paul/Pierre Pillar Church (where Saint Paul was allegedly flogged on the column in 45 AD according to Christian tradition, place of pilgrimage), Saint Salomon Catacombs (early Christian catacombs), Baths of Aphrodite 35 km away in Polis (natural pool in the rock where the goddess would have bathed according to mythology, 30 min easy trail hike), Petra tou Romiou 25 km away (legendary birthplace of Aphrodite, beach with signature rocks, signature landscape of Cyprus), Avakas Gorge (spectacular gorge 2h round trip hike), Akamas Peninsula (wild peninsula at the extreme west, hikes with endemic Cyprus tulip orchids, Lara beach turtle nesting), Coral Bay (main sand beach of the region, turquoise water), Lara Beach (protected green turtle nesting beach May-August). Excursions: Limassol 1h away, Troodos Mountains 1h30 away (mountains with UNESCO painted churches), Kykkos Monastery 1h30 away, Larnaca 1h30 away, Nicosia 2h away. Try Cypriot meze (institution), grilled halloumi, souvla skewers, sheftalies, commandaria (ancient sweet wine), zivania (grape eau-de-vie), Cypriot coffee. Paphos airport (PFO) with direct European charter flights, one of the main airports for the south-west coast of Cyprus.
trogir:en: Trogir, medieval town of central Dalmatia in Croatia: 13,000 inhabitants on islet connected to the continent by 2 bridges, 30 km west of Split. UNESCO 1997 for its exceptionally preserved historic centre (one of the best-preserved medieval fortified cities in the Adriatic). Venetian atmosphere (Venice controlled Trogir 1420-1797). June is my favourite month (9.4/10): 19-27°C, 14% rain, sea at 22°C, ideal temperatures. May and September stay excellent (8.4-9.0/10). From June to September, it's the tourist high season: 13-29°C, large crowds (cruise passengers stopping over from Split port, hotel prices x2-x3). July-August drop to 6.9/10 due to the heat (21-30°C). From November to March, expect 5-15°C and 30-49% rain: mild for the Mediterranean, many establishments close. Highlights: Saint Lawrence Cathedral (Sv. Lovro, Gothic 13th-16th century, architectural jewel with the Radovan Portal = Romanesque portal 1240 sculpted by Croatian master Radovan, one of the most important Romanesque works in the Balkans, monumental biblical figures and zodiac signs, panorama tower 47m), John Paul II Square (main square, former agora, Venetian atmosphere with 15th-century town hall), Trogir Loggia (15th-century Venetian with clock), Cipiko Palace (15th-century Venetian Renaissance, dwelling of the great Cipiko family who governed Trogir), Kamerlengo Fortress (15th-century Venetian quadrangular, panorama over the bay), Saint Mark Tower (15th century), Small Loggia, Saint Sebastian Church (15th century, delicate facade), Saint Nicholas Benedictine Convent (12th century with relief of Kairos = Greek god of luck). Riva Quarter (picturesque seafront with yachts, restaurants on the water, lively atmosphere in the evening). Trogir-Čiovo Bridge and Čiovo Island (on the other side of the canal, coastal beaches, fishing villages). Beaches: Pantan Beach 5 min away (rare sand in Croatia), Okrug Gornji Beach on Čiovo (the longest beach in the area 2 km, popular), Slatine Beach. Excursions: Split 30 min by bus (UNESCO, larger city with Diocletian Palace 3rd century, already covered by BDW), Hvar and Brač islands 1h by ferry from Split (already covered), Krka National Park 1h away (waterfalls, swimming allowed), Plitvice National Park 4h away (UNESCO, already covered), Šibenik 1h away (UNESCO, exceptional 15th-16th century Saint James Cathedral). Try pasticada (Dalmatian beef), peka (meat/fish cooked under bell in embers), grilled fresh fish (Adriatic), pršut prosciutto, Pag cheese, Plavac mali wine (Croatian grape variety). Logistics: Split airport (SPU) 5 km from Trogir = the closest airport to a UNESCO site in Europe, taxi transfers 5-10 min.
poznan:en: Poznan (Posen), 5th city of Poland in Greater Poland: 540,000 inhabitants. Cradle of the Polish state = it is here that Mieszko I, the first historical sovereign of Poland, was baptised in 966 thus founding the Polish nation. Commercial economic capital (MTP Poznań fair since 1925, one of the largest in Central Europe). Student city (140,000 students out of 540,000 inhabitants). June is my favourite month (8.1/10): 14-24°C, 35% rain, ideal temperatures. From June to August are the tourist high season: 12-25°C. From November to March, expect -1/9°C and 26-41% rain: continental cold, snow possible. Highlights: Stary Rynek (Old Market Square, one of the most beautiful squares in Poland, 4th largest after Wroclaw / Krakow / Łódź, background of colourful Renaissance bourgeois houses, lively student atmosphere), Town Hall (Renaissance 1551-1560 by architect Giovanni Battista Quadro, signature visual of the square with its arcades + 61m tower + clock with 2 mechanical billy goats coming out at noon hitting their heads "Koziołki Poznańskie" since the 16th century, legend of the 2 billy goats fighting during the governor's banquet, saving the kitchen on fire), Poznan Cathedral (Ostrów Tumski, on the cathedral island, place of Mieszko I's baptism 966, tombs of the first Polish sovereigns Mieszko I died 992 and Boleslaw I died 1025), Imperial Castle (neo-Roman palace 1905-1910 built by German Emperor William II, occupied by Hitler as Bunker residence in 1939-1945, controversy over its conservation), Saint Martin Croissants ("rogale świętomarcińskie", IGP UE 2008, Poznan speciality = croissants with almond paste and white poppy, tasted on 11 November Saint Martin festival patron of the city, 250 tonnes consumed each year), Croissant Museum (rare and amusing), Lake Malta (artificial lake for World Championship water sports, urban beach), Stary Browar ("Old Brewery", international award-winning commercial complex 2008 in a former brewery). Excursions: Gniezno 50 min away (1st capital of Poland, Saint Adalbert tomb-cathedral), Biskupin 1h30 away (archaeological site of a prehistoric Polish city 700 BC, life-size reconstruction of stilts on the lake), Berlin 2h45 by train, Wroclaw 2h by train. Try rogale świętomarcińskie (St. Martin croissants), pyzy (potato dumplings), kaczka po poznańsku (Poznan-style duck with red cabbage), Lech beer (brewed in Poznan).
grandvalira:en: Grandvalira, largest ski area of Andorra and the Pyrenees: 210 km of pistes connecting 6 resorts in the eastern Pyrenees, on the Franco-Spanish border. Andorra = principality co-ruled by the French president and the Catalan bishop of Urgell, one of the last vestiges of the European feudal system. July is my favourite month (7.7/10): 9-21°C, 36% rain, ideal temperatures for alpine hiking. From June to September, it's the summer high season: 4-21°C, Pyrenees hiking, mountain biking, paragliding. From December to April, expect -7/4°C and 39-49% precipitation often snow: this is the ski high season. Pistes: 210 km Grandvalira (210 km, one of the largest ski areas in the Pyrenees), 6 connected sectors: Pas de la Casa (French gateway, 2100m, the highest), Grau Roig, Soldeu (the smartest), El Tarter, Canillo, Encamp, altitude 1710-2640m, drop 930m, 130 pistes, 67 lifts including 4 gondolas. Legendary pistes: Avet (Alpine Skiing World Cup descent giant slalom, one of the most difficult on the circuit), Slalom at Soldeu. Assets: prices much cheaper than the Alps (lift passes 50-60€/day vs 70-90€ in France/Switzerland, hotels 30-50% cheaper), Andorran duty-free = alcohol and tobacco very cheap, snow conditions guaranteed thanks to high altitude and excellent artificial snow making. Public: Spanish (Catalans and Madrilenians) in the majority, French from the south (Toulousains, Montpelliérains, Bordelais), numerous British thanks to Barcelona/Toulouse flights + bus, few Italians or Germans. Pas de la Casa = the French border sector, very international festive atmosphere, low prices, cheap accommodation. Soldeu = the smart sector with 4-5* hotels (Hermitage 5* overlooking the village). Andorra principality sites: Andorra-la-Vella capital (highest European capital in altitude 1023m, old town Casa de la Vall = Andorran parliament 16th century), Caldea (the largest thermal spa in South-Western Europe, 6000 m² of thermal water at 70°C, signature of Andorra, spectacular night illuminations), Madriu-Perafita-Claror Valley (UNESCO 2004, preserved Pyrenean cultural landscape, 9% of Andorra's territory), Meritxell Sanctuary (patroness of Andorra, basilica rebuilt 1976 by Ricardo Bofill after fire). Try escudella (Andorran stew, national dish), trinxat (potato cabbage gratin), cunillo (rabbit), embotits (Pyrenean charcuterie), Vins du Pirineu (rare Andorran appellation, altitude vineyards 1200m+). Logistics: Grandvalira 3h by car from Barcelona, Toulouse, or by shuttle from airport (no airport in Andorra).
annaba:en: Annaba, port town of north-east Algeria on the Mediterranean coast 600 km east of Algiers: 460,000 inhabitants. Former Roman Hippo founded by the Phoenicians 7th century BC, Numidian then Roman capital, place where Saint Augustine was bishop 396-430 AD = one of the most influential Church Fathers ("Confessions" + "The City of God"). Coastal town preserved from international tourism, traditional Algerian atmosphere. May is my favourite month (8.7/10): 14-22°C, 12% rain, this is the ideal Mediterranean spring. April-June-October stay excellent (8.0-8.5/10). From April to June and September-October, these are the best seasons: 12-29°C, ideal Mediterranean climate. From July to August, expect 22-31°C with warm sea 25°C, local seaside season. From November to March, expect 6-19°C and 30-44% rain: mild for the Mediterranean. Highlights: Saint Augustine Basilica (1900 on the hill of Hippo, neo-Byzantine, one of the most beautiful churches in North Africa, houses a relic of Saint Augustine's forearm), Hippo Ruins (Roman vestiges from the 1st-5th century AD, early Christian basilica where Saint Augustine officiated, Roman theatre, forum), Cours de la Révolution ("Annaba Course", main pedestrian shaded avenue lined with 19th-century French colonial arcades), Place de l'Indépendance, Sidi Bou Merouane Mosque (11th century), Salah-Bey Mosque, Rizi Amor Beach (Mediterranean urban beach), Cape de Garde (north-east cape with lighthouse and preserved beaches), Hippo Museum (Roman + Carthaginian archaeological collection). Excursions: Constantine 2h away (see dedicated review), Tipaza 7h away (UNESCO 1982 Roman-Punic ruins), El Kala National Park 1h away (RAMSAR wetland, lakes and migratory birds), Tunisian border Tabarka 1h away. Safety: Annaba globally safe, but Algeria in general under France Diplomacy vigilance for residual terrorism (weakened but present AQMI movement in southern Saharan regions), moderate urban crime in Annaba. Visa required for most nationalities. Try Algerian couscous (national signature), chorba frik (lamb barley soup), méchoui (spit-roast lamb), makroudh (semolina-date pastry), mint tea, Arabic coffee with cardamom. Annaba Rabah Bitat airport (AAE).
batna:en: Batna, town of the High Plateaus 425 km south-east of Algiers in Algeria: 290,000 inhabitants at 1040m altitude. Berber Chaoui capital = the town is at the heart of the Aurès region, historic stronghold of the Chaoui Berbers ("Imazighen"), people who triggered the Algerian War on 1 November 1954 by the first coordinated FLN attacks at Batna and surroundings. Modest town tourist-wise but access base to the Aurès and Timgad UNESCO. September is my favourite month (9.1/10): 16-30°C, 17% rain, ideal temperatures. May-June-October stay excellent (7.5-8.6/10). From April to October, it's the relative tourist high season: 9-34°C, semi-arid altitude climate. From July to August, expect 19-34°C with possible heatwave 40°C+ despite the altitude. From November to March, expect 1-15°C and 11-33% rain: cold semi-arid altitude with snow possible at the neighbouring mountains. Highlights: Timgad 35 km away (UNESCO 1982, one of the best-preserved Roman cities in the world, founded 100 AD by Emperor Trajan as a colony for veterans, nicknamed "Pompeii of Africa", regular grid plan with Trajan's Arch + theatre 3500 places + library + temples + baths, buried under Saharan sand since the 7th century then cleared 1881-1960 = major Algerian tourist signature), Lambèse / Lambaesis 30 km away (other major Roman site, former military camp of the Legio III Augusta 2nd century AD, praetorium = best-preserved Roman building in North Africa), Aurès mountains (Saharan Atlas chains up to 2326m Mt Chélia, wild landscapes, spectacular gorges Tighanimine + Rhoufi, traditional Chaoui villages Menaa + Tkout in stone), Tighanimine Gorges (spectacular 600m deep canyon), Ghoufi Balcony (panorama over Aurès troglodyte villages), Batna city centre (Algerian working-class atmosphere, central market, mosque). Safety: the Aurès region is globally calm, increased France Diplomacy vigilance for Algeria in general. Try chakhchoukha (Chaoui national dish = crumbled bread in sauce signature of the Aurès), Chaoui couscous with dried vegetables, méchoui spit-roast lamb, Aurès honey, Deglet Nour dates, mint tea. Logistics: Batna Mostefa Ben Boulaid airport (BLJ), also accessible by train or by taxi from Constantine (2h) or Algiers (6h).
irkoutsk:en: Irkutsk, Siberian town on the Angara river 70 km from Lake Baikal: 620,000 inhabitants 5000 km east of Moscow. Nicknamed the "Paris of Siberia" in the 19th century (preserved characteristic carved wood architecture, theatres). Former exile town for the Decembrists (noble officers revolted in December 1825 against Tsar Nicholas I, exiled here with their wives — the wives brought European salon culture to Siberia). Access base to Lake Baikal. June-July are my favourite months (8.2/10): 11-25°C, 34-36% rain, this is the short continental summer. From June to August, it's the tourist high season for Baikal: 6-25°C, 32-36% rain, pleasant climate. From December to February, expect -23/-10°C and 8-15% rain: extreme Siberian continental winter (sometimes -45°C in January), but the entirely frozen Baikal allows expeditions on the crystal blue ice (1m+ thickness) in March — unique world spectacular phenomenon. Irkutsk highlights: 19th-century carved wood houses (130 Kvartal quarter entirely rebuilt-restored 2011, traditional atmosphere), Volkonsky and Trubetskoy Decembrist house-museum (poignant memorials of exile families), Cathedral of the Epiphany (18th century, Siberian Baroque), Polish Catholic Church (heritage of Polish exiles in Siberia), Central market (dried fruits, smoked omul), Promenade along the Angara. THE major site: Lake Baikal 70 km east: deepest lake in the world 1642m, oldest lake in the world 25 million years, 20% of world surface fresh water, UNESCO 1996, unique endemic fauna (nerpa seal = only freshwater seal in the world, omul). 2 main bases: Listvyanka 70 km away (the most accessible, Baikal museum, smoked fish market), Olkhon Island 5h by bus + ferry (the largest island of the lake, Buryat shamanic landscapes, signature Shamanka rock), Bolshie Koty (Grand Trail Baykalskaya hike). Logistics: Irkutsk 6h by plane from Moscow, 78h by Trans-Siberian train from Moscow. Try smoked Baikal omul (endemic fish, to buy at the exit of traditional smoke-houses at Listvyanka), Siberian pelmeni, vareniki, sagudaï (Siberian omul sashimi), pinia (cedar drink).
achgabat:en: Ashgabat, capital of Turkmenistan: 1.2 million inhabitants at the foot of the Kopet Dag Mountains on the Iranian border. Exceptionally strange capital (ultra-authoritarian post-Soviet regime, personality cult of Saparmurat Niyazov "Türkmenbaşy" 1990-2006 then Berdimuhamedov, nicknamed "white marble capital": 543 white marble buildings 4.5 million m², Guinness world record 2013). Country among the least open to tourism (very difficult visa, mandatory guide, limited access). September is my favourite month (9.2/10): 20-31°C, 1% rain, ideal temperatures after the heatwave. From May and September-October, these are the best seasons: 12-31°C, 1-9% rain, ideal dry climate. From June to August, it's the extreme heatwave: 24-38°C (July 38°C+), totally inadvisable. From December to February, expect 1-11°C: mild. Highlights (essentially grandiloquent regime architecture): Neutrality Monument (75m, golden statue of President Türkmenbaşy at the top which pivoted to follow the sun until 2010 when it was moved by his successor), Türkmenbaşy Ruhy Mosque (15 km south, the largest in Central Asia, 10,000 places, entirely marble interior, the Quran and the words of "Ruhnama" book of President Niyazov engraved together on the walls — which shocked the Islamic world), Presidential Palace, Yntalanan Yyly (bizarre Wedding Palace, giant 70m cube topped with a terrestrial globe), Independence Park (massive statues of national heroes), White marble quarter. Excursions: Darvaza gas crater 270 km away in the Karakum desert ("Gates of Hell", 70m diameter, burns continuously since 1971 when Soviet geologists set fire to burn toxic gases after collapse of a drilling hole — bizarrely still active 50 years later; fantastic night spectacle, organised 4x4 visit with guide from Ashgabat 5h), Konye-Urgench (UNESCO, medieval ruins), Nisa (UNESCO, Parthian ruins), Avaza (Caspian coast, beach resort), Mary and Merv (UNESCO, former Seljuk capital). Try Turkmen plov, shashlik, dograma (bread-mutton-onion soup), gutab (pancakes), green tea.
equateur:en: Ecuador, smallest Andean country: 18 million inhabitants on 283,000 km² crossed by the geographic equator (hence its name). 4 distinct regions: Sierra Andean, Costa Pacific, Oriente Amazonian, Galápagos. From July to September are my favourite months (7.6-7.7/10) at altitude: 7-19°C, 32-46% rain, this is the Andean dry season. From June to September, it's the tourist high season for the Sierra: 7-19°C, clear sky. From November to May, it's the rainy season in the Sierra: 79-93% rainfall, green landscapes but often in fog. On the coast, reversed seasons (dry December-May, rainy June-November).
